title: Consumer Conversion Prediction Via Heterogeneous Graph Networks and Sparse Attention Learning
authors: Shaojia Liu, Yushan Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Consumer Conversion Rate (CVR) prediction is challenged by the complex integration of high-dimensional, noisy time-series behavior, multi-source heterogeneous graph relationships, and the inherent lack of model interpretability. To address these issues, this paper proposes HTSA-Net, a novel framework for CVR prediction. HTSA-Net is designed with a Hierarchical Time-Series-Structure Collaborative Learning paradigm. Specifically, it incorporates a Transformer Sequential Feature Encoding Module to capture dynamic user intent over long-range behavior sequences, and a Hierarchical Heterogeneous Graph Network Module to model structural associations and collaborative signals within the heterogeneous entity network. Crucially, the model introduces a Sparse Attention Learning and Fusion Module, leveraging the SparseMax activation function to perform explicit, interpretable feature screening and noise suppression during multi-source feature fusion. Experiments on a real-world e-commerce dataset demonstrate that HTSA-Net consistently outperforms strong baselines, achieving 81.72% AUC and 11.41 % AUPRC. Extensive ablation studies further confirm the complementary effectiveness of SFEM, HGNNM, and SALFM, providing a robust, high-precision, and interpretable solution for marketing and recommendation optimization.

title: A Lightweight and Adaptive Method for Dynamic Feature Suppression in Visual SLAM
authors: Jie Xia, Jianan Jiang, Dechao Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: In dynamic scenarios, the core challenge confronting Simultaneous Localization and Mapping (SLAM) technologies lies in the interference of moving objects with system stability. Existing approaches based on semantic or instance segmentation often suffer from high computational costs, limiting real- time performance on resource-constrained platforms. In this work, a dynamic SLAM framework based on lightweight object detection is presented. The YOLOv8 model is optimized with modules including ADown, Ghost convolution, and C2f-GB to improve multi-scale feature fusion and support edge deployment via the NCNN framework. Object motion states are estimated by integrating optical flow tracking and epipolar constraints. An adaptive dynamic feature point removal strategy based on depth information is further designed to distinguish dynamic points from static background. The proposed system is integrated into ORB-SLAM3, forming a real-time, multithreaded SLAM pipeline that improves localization robustness and accuracy under dynamic conditions. Experiments on dynamic datasets show that the proposed method significantly enhances both accuracy and efficiency compared to existing dynamic SLAM systems.

title: AD-YOLO: An Animal Object Detection Algorithm in Complex Scenes Based on Improved YOLO11
authors: Xiangzhen Meng, Lei Liu, Binbin Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To solve the problems of low detection accuracy and high processing demand in animal detection under complex scenes, we propose an improved algorithm, AD-YOLO, based on YOLO11n. First, a novel efficient downsampling module, DADown, is introduced, in which Deformable ConvNet v2 (DCNv2) replaces conventional convolutions and incorporates an improved channel attention mechanism to enhance downsampling performance. Second, the DRB module is introduced into the bottleneck structure of the C3k2 module to enhance adaptability to multi-scale objects and complex backgrounds. Finally, a lightweight and efficient detection head is designed using group convolution and parameter-sharing strategies, substantially reducing both the number of parameters and computing complexity. Experimental results demonstrate that, compared with YOLO11n, AD-YOLO achieves improvements of 2.4 % in mAP{@}50 and 2.5% in mAP{@}50:95 on the AP-10K dataset, while reducing parameters to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 9 5 M}$</tex> and computational cost to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{4.2}$</tex> GFLOPs.The experimental results validate the feasibility and generalization ability of the proposed method, which realizes model lightweighting while enhancing detection performance.

title: Vertically Partitioned High-Dimensional Data Publishing with Differential Privacy
authors: Junming Zhang, Jingru Wang, Shigong Long, Shengguang Bai, Lun Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Ensuring data security and availability is crucial in high-dimensional data privacy releases, but the effectiveness of dimensionality reduction algorithms and the allocation of privacy budgets often pose challenges. Data usability encompasses accuracy, completeness, and association properties. To address these issues, the rough set-based personalized privacy-preserving Bayesian network algorithm (RSPPrivBayes) is proposed. This algorithm leverages the advantages of rough sets and Bayesian networks: first, it preprocesses the data using rough set theory to retain key correlations while eliminating redundancy for efficient dimensionality reduction; next, according to user privacy requirements, it differentially injects Laplace noise into the categorized data to balance privacy and data correlation; finally, it employs a Bayesian network to construct the released data, thereby protecting privacy while preserving dependency relationships and statistical properties. Experiments confirm that RSPPrivBayes performs well in both privacy protection and data quality, verifying its effectiveness and efficiency.

title: Evaluating Functional Coverage of Accelerator Design for DT-based ML Implementations
authors: Ayush Dixit, Raushan Kumar, Abhishek Yadav, Binod Kumar
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: This work explores the functional coverage assessment of Decision Tree(DT)-based classification accelerator designs. Our investigation demonstrates that decision tree algorithms can be efficiently implemented in hardware with minimal resource utilization while maintaining high classification accuracy. The proposed accelerator design achieves 96.66% accuracy on the benchmark Iris dataset using Python libraries (Pandas, Scikit-learn, and Matplotlib) while consuming only 0.96 W of power in Vivado 2022.1 and utilizing just 0.07% of available look-up tables (LUTs) and 0.01% of flip-flops (FFs) on the Basys3 Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) platform. Comprehensive functional coverage analysis using the Synopsys VCS tool confirms 100% coverage across all variables by incorporating a new reference model for coverage collection in addition to the conventional universal verification methodology (UVM) testbench architecture, ensuring robust verification. The findings highlight the potential of decision tree-based accelerators for resource-constrained applications where power and area constraints are critical. Codes and subsequent files are available at GitHub Repository.

title: A Bottom-Up Olympic Medal Prediction Framework Based on Multiple Machine Learning and Statistical Methods
authors: KeYuan Zhang, ChenXing Li, Bin Zheng, Jingdong Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a bottom-up Olympic medal prediction framework to improve the robustness and accuracy of national-level medal forecasts. It integrates athlete-level LightGBM classification, country-level random forest regression with Bayesian optimization, and MAPIE-based uncertainty quantification. A Monte Carlo sampling method using the Poisson distribution is applied to discretize predicted medal counts. Experimental results on Olympic datasets 2024 show that the proposed model achieves a Mean Absolute Error (MAE) of 3.71, Root Mean Squared Error (RMSE) of 7.21, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.88 in medal count prediction. The model significantly outperforms traditional statistical models.

title: Research on Classification and Recognition of Traditional Chinese Medicines Based on YOLOv8-MSDA
authors: Guihua Zhang, Junchao Dai, Yue Guan, JiaNing Li, Yin Liu, Jiashu Sun
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Aiming at the recognition difficulties of Chinese herbal medicines due to the variety of species, similar morphology and sparse features of small targets, this study proposes a classification and recognition model based on improved YOLOv8. The traditional recognition methods have the problems of low efficiency and high misclassification rate, and the existing YOLOv8 model has insufficient feature extraction ability for multi-scale targets in complex backgrounds, especially for small targets. To this end, the study introduces the multi-scale null attention mechanism (MSDA) and deformable convolution (DCNv2) to strengthen the model's ability to capture complex features: MSDA realizes multi-scale feature fusion by focusing on local sparse feature interactions while reducing the computational redundancy; and DCNv2 strengthens the efficiency of extracting small targets and geometrically deformed features by adapting to adjusting the convolution kernel offsets. Experiments on a self-constructed dataset of 9709 Chinese herbal medicine images show that the improved algorithm achieves good results in the Chinese herbal medicine dataset, with a 2.4% improvement in mAP compared with the original model, and in specific categories, the recognition accuracy of herbs such as Lycium barbarum and Angelica dahurica is improved by a range of 1.2% to 3.9%. The precision rate and recall rate are all improved. The model effectively enhances the ability to capture complex features while taking into account the computational efficiency, providing technical support for the application of automated recognition of Chinese herbal medicines in scenarios such as quality control of drinking tablets.

title: Balancing Innovation and Integrity: Comparative Policy Frameworks for AI Governance in Australian Higher Education
authors: Tharindu Attanayake, Gayathri Goonaratne
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) technologies are transforming higher education through personalised learning and automated assessment while challenging academic integrity and governance. This study examines AI policies across six Australian institutions three public universities and three private providers through a comparative analysis of policy documents, focusing on scope, principles, governance, and enforcement. Findings reveal that public institutions employ comprehensive governance structures and mandatory AI literacy training, whereas private providers emphasize breach definitions with decentralized oversight. A critical gap in regulating staff AI use was identified across both sectors. We propose a hybrid governance model combining public-sector rigor with private-sector agility, featuring tiered penalties, mandatory AI literacy programs, and an AI usage registry. The framework aligns with international standards (UNESCO, EU AI Act) and offers actionable pathways for national policy harmonisation. This work equips education leaders with strategies for ethical AI integration while preserving academic rigor.

title: FPGA Implementation of Open-Set Specific Emitter Identification with Power-of-Two Quantization and Channel Reuse Acceleration
authors: Qihang Yan, Huamin Tao, Shanzhu Xiao, Qingjiang Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: This study presents the quantization optimization and hardware implementation of open-set Specific Emitter Identification (SEI). Mixed-precision and power-of-two quantization are combined with Quantization-Aware Training (QAT), replacing multiplications with shift operations and introducing a zero-skipping mechanism, effectively reducing computation and power consumption while maintaining open-set discriminative capability. A channel-reuse convolution architecture alternates between input-channel and output-channel reuse, enhancing data locality and on-chip bandwidth utilization. Experiments on the XCZU7EV FPGA demonstrate a throughput of 53.60 GOPs at 3.32 W power consumption, achieving an energy efficiency of 16.15 GOPs/W- $107 \times$ that of a CPU and 3.1× that of a GPU-surpassing existing FPGA implementations. The results validate the feasibility and high energy efficiency of open-set SEI on low-power, customizable hardware, providing a practical reference for real-time deployment of radio frequency fingerprint recognition systems.

title: LLM-Driven Fire Safety Assistant
authors: Shan He, Yuwei Hu, Hongyu Li, Ting Li, Yanyan Hu, Fengmei Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: We propose a novel fire safety assistant system based on InternLM2.5-chat-20B, integrating TTS, ASR, and digital human technologies for 24/7 interactive consultation. A domain-specific vector database was built from regulations and rescue manuals, annotated with metadata to enhance retrieval accuracy. Real-world incident Q&A data enriched the model's adaptability to dynamic rescue scenarios. Using QLoRA, we achieved efficient fine-tuning with reduced memory and GPU usage, enabling deployment in constrained environments. A scenario-based Chain-of-Thought prompting design further improved reasoning clarity and transparency. Experiments show that BLEU increased from 0.0358 to 0.5934, and BERTScore from 0.6768 to 0.9452, demonstrating significant gains in response fluency and semantic relevance.

title: Simulation Analysis of BPSK and QPSK Modulation Performance in Millimeter-Wave Communication Systems
authors: Ruizhe Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Millimeter-wave communication is an important part of future communication networks and can provide more bandwidth resources. In this context, achieving efficient millimeter-wave transmission within limited spectrum constraints has become a core scientific and engineering challenge that needs to be urgently solved in the current wireless communication field. This paper uses MATLAB to implement modulation and demodulation models for binary phase-shift keying (BPSK) and quadrature phase-shift keying (QPSK) in a Gaussian white noise channel. Furthermore, a composite path loss model based on the Friis transmission equation is simulated. Link performance in the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$28-100 \text{GHz}$</tex> frequency band is quantitatively analyzed in both line-of-sight (LOS) and non-line-of-sight (NLOS) scenarios. The performance of millimeter-wave signals in different scenarios and modulation schemes is evaluated by comparing parameters like the bit error rate (BER) and actual signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) of the two modulation schemes. The experimental simulation results validate the matching requirements between the millimeter-wave frequency band and the application scenario, providing a reference for millimeter-wave system design.

title: Comment on “Enigmas, etc.” in September and October 2025 Issues of IEEE Microwave Magazine [Educator’s Corner]
authors: Adam Abramowicz

title: Adaptive Learning Technologies in Chinese Vocational Education: Rethinking the Teacher's Role
authors: Jingyi Chen, Juanjo Mena, Elvira G. Rincón-Flores
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Vocational training in China is significant in the training of students with practical skills, as well as aligning training with the labor market requirements. However, the fast advent of adaptive learning technologies has changed the educational situation, posing some trouble in rearranging the role of the teacher from a standard trainer to a guide of personalized learning. This research explores the effects of adaptive platforms on student performance and teacher participation in Chinese vocational schools. The investigation aimed to regulate the relations between teacher engagement, instructional adaptation, technology perception, and student performance. The study involved <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0}$</tex> participants, including teachers and students across diverse vocational fields. The analysis was based on structured questionnaires, institutional records, and usage data obtained with adaptive platforms. The analytical relationships were investigated using quantitative tools such as multiple regression, Pearson correlation, and descriptive statistics. Findings revealed that teacher engagement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathrm{b}=0.36$</tex>, p <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.001)$</tex> and student platform engagement <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\mathbf{b} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 3 3}, \mathbf{p}<0.001)$</tex> were the most important predictors of learning results, but instructional adaptation and teacher perception also exerted small effects. The research attains the conclusion that adaptive technologies plus active teacher assistance are the approaches that improve the learning process. Reorganizing teacher roles so as to align with adaptive systems will guarantee long-term and progressive vocational education.

title: Development and Evaluation of Video-Based Learning Materials in Improving the Achievement Level in Science IV
authors: Apple Ghee B. Bueno
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study focused on the development and quality assurance of scripts and video-based learning materials to improve the achievement levels of Grade 4 learners in the Most Essential Learning Competencies (MELCs) in Science at San Gregorio Elementary School, La Paz District. The research employed a descriptive-comparative design, which, according to Villanueva (2013), involves evaluating two unmanipulated variables using a formal method to determine if a significant difference exists between them. Findings revealed that the content of the seven developed scripts for Grade 4 was rated as satisfactory, while the language used was rated very satisfactory. The format and technical design, as well as the presentation and organization of the video lessons, were assessed as satisfactory, and the accuracy and up-todatedness of the video content was rated very satisfactory. Overall, the developed Science IV video lessons received a very satisfactory evaluation. Learners' performance before the implementation of the video lessons was assessed as poor or needing improvement, while after the use of quality-assured video lessons, performance improved to a very satisfactory level. The difference between pretest and posttest results was found to be highly significant. There was also significant agreement among evaluators regarding the content and language of the scripts, as well as in the content, format/technical design, presentation and organization, accuracy, and up-to-datedness of the video lessons. However, the development and evaluation phases were not without challenges. These included limitations in technical resources, time constraints in video production, and the need for continuous alignment with curriculum standards. Despite these challenges, the study demonstrated that well-developed video-based learning materials can effectively enhance learner achievement.

title: Shift in Design Thinking of Educational Agents: From Technology Integration to Realization of Educational Value
authors: Zongmei Liu, Jianxin Tan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Amid the ongoing wave of educational informatization, educational AI agents, emerging from the deep integration of large language models and educational theory, have seen widespread adoption in classroom teaching, extracurricular learning, and personalized tutoring. However, existing implementations often fall short in fostering students) holistic development, particularly in affective and value-laden dimensions. This paper proposes a design shift that reframes AI agents as a “vehicle for nurturing” grounded in learning sciences. Using the “Fundamentals of Information Technology” course as a case study, we develop the Educational Vehicle Model (EVM): a structured framework that not only tailors the agent's functional modules to align with instructional objectives and student developmental goals but also establishes a comprehensive evaluation system to assess its pedagogical effectiveness. The aim is to construct an educational AI agent capable of delivering robust skills training while seamlessly integrating moral education, thereby offering a replicable implementation framework for curriculum-based instructional innovation.

title: DGA: Direction-Geometric-Aware selection strategy for NoC
authors: Zhao-Jun Suo, Xin Xi, Yamin Li, Tuo Li, Changhong Wang, Fen Guo, Kang Su
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Network on chips (NoCs) have become a critical interconnect architecture, especially for multiprocessor systems and high-performance computing. To enhance on-chip communication efficiency and overall performance, numerous routing algorithms have been developed. This paper proposes a Direction-Geometric-Aware (DGA) selection strategy for mesh NoCs. Designed to augment adaptive routing algorithms (e.g., Odd-even, West-first) that offer multiple available output directions, DGA enables the current router to assign directional weights to candidate directions based on the geometric relationship between the current node and hotspots. This establishes an effective mechanism for hotspot-aware adaptive routing. The DGA strategy improves NoC performance by reducing communication latency, increasing network throughput, and enhancing load balance, while also demonstrating strong robustness across diverse traffic patterns such as Random, Transpose, Butterfly, and Shuffle traffic. Compared to baseline selection strategies, DGA selection strategy achieves 12.0% reduction in average latency and 11.2% improvement in load balance under the configuration with three hotspots.

title: Research on Intelligent Metro Guidance Technology Based on Communication Data and Neural Network
authors: Rui Cao, Yan Liu, Zhiming Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: This paper focuses on the problem of passenger flow guidance in urban subway systems and proposes an intelligent guidance solution based on multi-source communication data and neural network technology. The research first systematically integrated multi-dimensional information from mobile communication network signaling, in-station Wi-Fi probes, train operation systems and mobile application data, and constructed a data basis that can accurately depict the spatio-temporal distribution and movement patterns of passengers. Based on this, a deep learning model integrating convolutional neural network (CNN) was innovatively constructed to mine the spatial correlation and temporal dependence of passenger flow data, achieving precise assessment of passenger flow parameters in key areas within subway stations and short-term future prediction. Ultimately, based on the dynamic assessment and prediction results of the model, a data-driven intelligent diversion decision-making mechanism was designed, providing scientific decision support for dynamic passenger flow guidance, transport capacity allocation and emergency management. The simulation results show that this technical solution significantly improves the real-time performance and prediction accuracy of passenger flow status perception, providing an effective way to alleviate subway congestion and ensure operational safety and efficiency.

title: Spatiotemporal dynamics of wildfires in the Sierras de Córdoba, Argentina (1986-2024): A remote sensing approach to burned area reconstruction
authors: J.V. Albornoz, C. Baldini, L.M. Bellis, M.C. Naval Fernández, J.P. Argañnaraz
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: -This study presents the most comprehensive and consistent reconstruction of wildfire history in the Sierras de Córdoba, Argentina, spanning the period 1986-2024. By integrating multiple satellite data sources (Landsat, Sentinel-2) and applying hybrid classification techniques in Google Earth Engine, a spatially explicit burned area dataset was developed. A total of 11,458 wildfire events were recorded, over the 39-year period studied, accumulating nearly 2.5 million hectares (ha). Large fires ($\gt1,000$ ha) represent 2.75% of all fire events and accounted for nearly 75% of the total burned area. Among these, megafires-defined as events exceeding $\mathbf{1 0, 0 0 0}$ ha-represented only $\mathbf{0. 3 4 \%}$ of all fires but were responsible for over 43% of the total area burned. Fire activity showed strong seasonality: over 77% of the total burned area occurred during the late winter and early spring, with peak activity in September and October. Nearly 45% of the Sierras de Córdoba experienced at least one fire event between 1986 and 2024, with recurrent hotspots in the central Sierras Chicas and southern Sierras de los Comechingones showing fire return intervals as short as $\mathbf{4 - 5}$ years. This long-term dataset provides a critical tool for understanding fire regime dynamics, supporting land and fire management, and assessing the ecological and socioeconomic impacts of wildfires in the context of climate change and expanding Wildland-Urban Interface (WUI).

title: Guided 3D Generation Through Depth Image Fusion Utilizing Diffusion Model
authors: Yifan Wei, Xingwang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Recent advances in text-to-3D generation have achieved impressive results, yet maintaining multi-view consistency remains a significant challenge. Existing methods often struggle with geometric coherence due to the limitations of 2Dbased priors or the scarcity of high-quality 3D training data. In this work, we introduce a depth-guided text-to-3D generation framework that leverages depth maps as structural priors to enhance 3D consistency. Depth maps naturally encode spatial information, serving as an effective constraint to mitigate geometric inconsistencies across different viewpoints. Our approach integrates depth priors into the optimization process, complementing 3D representations such as Gaussian Splatting or Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF). By refining geometry through depthguided constraints and enhancing details with 2D diffusion priors, our method significantly improves both structural integrity and multi-view consistency in generated 3D assets. Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves high-fidelity 3D reconstructions with improved coherence across different perspectives, providing a promising direction for efficient and high-quality text-to-3D synthesis.

title: Summer simulated biogenic emissions compared to Sentinel-5P observations in Argentina
authors: Anahi Bianco, María Fernanda García Ferreyra, Mario Agustín Sgró, Gabriele Curci, Maxi Alberto Burgos Paci
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Isoprene is a biogenic volatile organic compound with significant relevance in atmospheric chemistry, as it participates in the formation of tropospheric ozone and secondary aerosols. Argentina lacks a detailed inventory of biogenic emissions, as well as continuous measurements of isoprene or volatile organic compounds. This work aims to validate a simulation of isoprene emissions carried out with the MEGAN model for one summer week, using satellite observations of formaldehyde provided by Sentinel-5P. To do this, the spatial coherence between both datasets was mainly analyzed, seeking to verify whether areas with higher modeled isoprene emissions coincide with zones of high formaldehyde concentrations. Consequently, the results show elevated values in the northeast of the country, where vegetation, solar radiation, and temperature favor the emission of volatile organic compounds, especially isoprene. This preliminary analysis helps to evaluate the quality of the simulation and highlights the usefulness of satellite data for studies in locations with scarce field information.

title: Editor’s Note [Educator’s Corner]
authors: Robert H. Caverly

title: Attention-Enhanced Temporal Convolutional Network for Battery SOH Estimation
authors: Ao Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate estimation of lithium-ion battery SOH is essential for ensuring the safety and prolonging the service life of power battery systems. Conventional approaches often depend on manual feature engineering or single deep learning architectures, which struggle to effectively capture long-range dependencies in voltage sequences while extracting multi-scale features simultaneously. This leads to limited prediction accuracy and poor generalization performance. This paper proposes a multi-attention-enhanced temporal convolutional network for high-precision lithium-ion battery SOH estimation. First, fixed-length voltage sequences are extracted from constant-current/constant-voltage charging curves to form a 3D feature tensor. Then, three attention mechanisms-channel attention, temporal attention, and self-attention-are employed in parallel to extract features. A hierarchical fusion module efficiently integrates multi-scale information. Finally, global pooling followed by fully connected regression layers outputs the SOH value. Experimental results show that, compared with traditional methods, the proposed model reduces MAE to 0.3151 %, lowers RMSE to 0.3755 %, and improves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{\mathrm{2}}$</tex> to 97.83 % on the test set, achieving an additional 3.31 percentage points gain.

title: Research on Fault-Tolerant Methods for Matrix Multiplication in GPGPU
authors: Yingke Gao, Peidao Wang, Pei Wang, Zheng Yang, Shenglong Li, Bo Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This paper focuses on the reliability issue of generalpurpose computing (GPGPU) in radiation environments and studies a configurable matrix multiplication fault-tolerant algorithm. By comparing and analyzing the performance of the Block Diagonal Matrix Stacking fault-tolerant algorithm (BDMFT) and the checksum-based fault-tolerant algorithm (ABFT) in heavy ion radiation experiments, the fault-tolerant capabilities and limitations of both were quantitatively evaluated. The experimental results show that the ABFT algorithm achieves lightweight protection through the row-column checksum mechanism, has a 100% correction rate for single-row/singlecolumn errors and only introduces linear storage overhead. Although the BDMFT algorithm can shield single-replica errors through trimodal redundancy, its cube-level storage overhead intensifies the hardware load, leading to a significant increase in the risk of hard errors during high-dimensional operations. Based on the above findings, a hierarchical protection optimization scheme is proposed: ABFT is given priority to correct sparse SEU errors; When multiple row/column errors are detected, the BDMFT redundant voting is dynamically activated; If BDMFT fails, ABFT will be automatically executed in blocks. This strategy integrates the low-overhead detection advantage of ABFT with the complex error shielding capability of BDMFT, providing a new idea for high-reliability matrix computing of GPGPU in radiation environments.

title: The Effect of Slit Skew Angle on the Performance of Axially-Slitted Solid Rotor Induction Motors
authors: Batı Eren Ergun, Egemen Semerci, Mahmut Abaı, Mehmet Onur Gulbahce
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Axial slits in solid rotor induction motors (SRIMs) enhance torque production, yet the impact of skewing these slits remains insufficiently explored. This paper presents a finite element analysis (FEA) of an Axially-Slitted SRIM (AS-SRIM) with skew angles from 0° to 60°, evaluated under constant-speed operation. Key performance metrics—including torque, torque-tocurrent ratio, efficiency, and input current—were assessed alongside the air-gap flux density spectrum, overall flux distribution, and flux-line trajectories. The results indicate that an optimum skew angle not only mitigates space harmonics in the air-gap flux but also improves efficiency and torque-to-current performance. In this study, a 4 kW, 400 v/400 Hz AS-SRIM was analyzed, and the findings provide practical insights for skew angle selection in high-performance solid rotor induction machines.

title: Research on the Application of AI Vision Technology in Correcting the Forehand Stroke Action and Personalized Guidance of Teenagers in Badminton Teaching
authors: Liying Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study employs literature review, experimental observation, data collection, mathematical statistics, and logical analysis to investigate the application of AI vision technology in teaching badminton forehand techniques. Focusing on 60 adolescent players, it identifies and analyzes common errors in three key phases: preparatory stance, backswing motion, and the moment of impact. Leveraging AI's strengths in data analysis, the research decodes the root causes of these errors and develops targeted correction strategies with personalized coaching approaches. The findings provide practical insights to enhance teaching quality and optimize technical execution in youth badminton forehand instruction.

title: A Conceptual Model of an IoT/M2MS Interaction System For Designing A Digital Twin in a Robotized Workshop
authors: Kaldybek Makhambetov, Jelena Caiko, Baurzhan Belgibaev, Martins Bisenieks
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This paper proposes a conceptual model of an IoT/M2M interaction system for designing a digital twin of key equipment in a robotized workshop. As a demonstration case, a production line is considered, equipped with an intelligent online monitoring and process parameter control system. The proposed architecture integrates IoT devices, sensors, and video analytics components to enable real-time data collection, transmission, and analysis. The digital twin concept makes it possible to simulate and predict equipment behavior, optimize heating control processes, and synchronize interactions between workshop components. Special attention is given to implementing M2M communication, ensuring reliable connectivity between the cabinet oven, robotic delivery modules, and other elements of the production line. The paper describes only the model and architectural principles, emphasizing its role as a starting point for further research and prototyping. The proposed approach demonstrates the benefits of integrating IoT, M2M, and digital twins to increase energy efficiency, automate processes, and advance smart manufacturing in the food industry.

title: Architecture and Application Path for On-Device Deployment of Aviation Large Vision Models
authors: Xilin Zhang, Bo Liu, Ziliang Du, Yang Shao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the advancement of edge computing technology, aviation vision tasks demand higher real-time performance, privacy, and resource adaptability. This paper addresses the on-device deployment challenges of aviation large vision models by designing a task-oriented architecture and optimization path. The architecture introduces a data flow-model flow separation mechanism combined with modular design principles, significantly enhancing the flexibility of model updates and system maintenance. For model optimization, structured pruning, mixed-precision quantization, and knowledge distillation are comprehensively employed to enable efficient operation of large vision models such as Vision Transformer (ViT), Segment Anything Model (SAM), and Contrastive Language-Image Pre-Training(CLIP) on resource-constrained edge devices. Through analysis of typical application scenarios, the proposed system demonstrates advantages in real-time performance, accuracy, and energy efficiency. Additionally, this paper presents an intelligent Agent-based deployment strategy, providing a unified framework for rapid adaptation and evolution of multi-task aviation vision systems. The research offers a systematic solution for edge intelligence deployment of large vision models in the aviation domain, with significant engineering and academic value.

title: The Anti-Progressive Collapse Performance of External Prestressed Steel Strand Reinforced Steel Frame Structures Based on Intelligent Monitoring Systems
authors: Junpeng Li, Yemin Liu, Fen Ye, Lang Yu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This paper investigates the application of intelligent monitoring systems in the assessment of the anti-continuous collapse performance of steel frame structures reinforced with externally prestressed steel strands. By constructing a monitoring framework that integrates intelligent perception, edge computing, and decision control, real-time perception and analysis of the structural mechanical response are achieved. The research focuses on proposing intelligent early warning and performance evaluation methods based on deep learning and machine learning, and establishing data-driven prestressing optimization and control strategies. Case verification shows that the system can effectively identify changes in the structural state, provide intelligent decision support for prestressing and steel strand layout, and significantly enhance the structure's anti-continuous collapse capability and safety monitoring level.

title: Analysis of Proactive Uncoordinated Techniques to Mitigate Interference in FMCW Automotive Radars
authors: Alessandro Bazzi, Francesco Miccoli, Fabrizio Cuccoli, Luca Facheris, Vincent Martinez
abstract: Modern vehicles increasingly rely on advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS), with radars playing a key role due to their cost-effectiveness and reliable performance. However, the growing number of radars operating in the same spectrum raises concerns about mutual interference, which could lead to system malfunctions and potential safety risks. This study focuses on a scenario in which all vehicles are equipped with frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radars, and it assesses the impact of interference on radar functionality — expressed in terms of probability of failure — by considering both direct and reflected signals. The radars may employ one of the following proactive mitigation methods to reduce the impact of interference, all of which require no inter-vehicle coordination but differ in complexity: (i) random carrier-frequency hopping on a frame-by-frame basis, (ii) random carrier-frequency hopping on a chirp-by-chirp basis, and (iii) a directional, compass-based method specifically addressing interference from opposite directions, which can be combined with either of the two previous methods. In this work, we assume realistic simulated road traffic scenarios and develop a novel model that captures correlated interference and accounts for the main radar setting parameters. Results reveal that dense scenarios pose a high risk of radar malfunctions. Among the analyzed methods, chirp-by-chirp frequency hopping emerges as the most effective approach to mitigate interference and ensure system reliability, but only when combined with a sufficiently large bandwidth. The compass-based method, on the other hand, shows limited effectiveness and appears not worth the additional system complexity.

title: Event-Triggered Distributed Secondary Control With Preassigned Finite-Time Performance Constraints for Islanded Microgrids
authors: Jinzhu Yu, Wei Sun, Yang Xiao, Chanjuan Zhao, Zhenglong Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper proposes an event-triggered (ET) distributed secondary control method with preassigned finite-time performance (PFTP) constraints for islanded microgrids (MGs). The method ensures fast voltage recovery while also guaranteeing robust transient performance and efficient utilization of communication resources. First, a finite-time extended state observer is designed to estimate the uncertain term in the linearized MG system. Second, a barrier Lyapunov function incorporating a PFTP function is developed to ensure that the system satisfies the preassigned performance constraints. Based on this, a backstepping-based secondary controller with an ET mechanism is established, where a robust compensation term is integrated to mitigate chattering. Third, theoretical analysis demonstrates that all signals remain bounded and the synchronization error converges to a prescribed region within a preassigned finite time, while avoiding the Zeno phenomenon. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed method is validated through MATLAB/Simulink simulations and hardware-in-the-loop experiments. Its robustness under communication delays is further demonstrated, where enhanced chattering suppression effectively minimizes voltage deviations. Comparative results verify that the proposed method achieves fast voltage recovery with superior transient performance.

title: Powering a Reliable and Resilient Tomorrow, Together at the 2026 IEEE Pes T&D Conference and Exposition: A Welcome Message [President’s Message]

title: Boundary Aligned Superpixel Guided Dual Stream Vision Transformer for Pneumonia Detection in Chest CT and X-ray
authors: Sulav Baral, Rabindra Bista, Subindra Karki, João C. Ferreira
abstract: Pneumonia continues to be a significant cause of death around the globe, and quick and accurate diagnosis based on chest X-rays (CXR) and computed tomography (CT) images is of utmost importance. While transformer-based models that process CXR and CT images have been developed, models often make use of fixed patch tokenization, which can lead to a loss of anatomical boundaries and reduce interpretability from a clinical perspective. We fill this gap with a boundary-aware dual-stream vision transformer to leverage both pixel-level detail for images and region-level superpixel representations to make the architecture adaptable to both modalities. The framework was validated on two publicly available datasets (Kaggle) and one dataset from Dhulikhel Hospital, with accuracies on Kaggle CXR = 98.21%; Dhulikhel CXR = 98.74%; Kaggle CT = 99.73%; and Dhulikhel CT = 97.3%. Furthermore, per-class precision, recall, and F1-scores all exceeded 96%, and all ROC–AUC values exceeded 0.99. In addition to more typical metrics, boundary-aware evaluation revealed good alignment with clinicians with edge-alignment cosine scores ranging from 0.27 to 0.34 for CXR and 0.48 to 0.49 for CT and intra-region variance below 0.015, while observing focused attention distributions as evidenced by Gini coefficients ≈0.58 for CXR, and ≈0.33 for CT. Statistical robustness was also confirmed with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals, all reaching significance over single-stream baselines, and particularly emphasizing reduction in false negatives of pneumonia positive cases. These results show that the proposed approach fills the important gap between high diagnostic accuracy and preserving anatomical boundaries, improving clinical trust and understanding in pneumonia detection.

authors: N/A

title: Curvilinear Dynamics of Performance Shortfalls and Digital Innovation: The Moderating Role of IT Expertise_supp1-3668050.pdf
authors: Xiaowei Liu
abstract: This study explores the impact of performance feedback on digital innovation. While prior research often focuses on rational capability-based perspectives, we adopt a behavioral strategy lens to examine how performance feedback shapes digital innovation decisions. Using a sample of Chinese manufacturing firms from 2004 to 2021, we find an inverted U-shaped relationship between performance shortfalls and digital innovation, and board and CEO IT expertise flatten the inverted U-shaped curve. These findings are supported by a rigorous empirical design, including extensive potential endogeneity analysis and robustness checks. This study contributes to the literature by bridging behavioral strategy and digital innovation, offering new insights into how performance feedback, moderated by IT leadership, shapes strategic innovation decisions. It also extends the understanding of IT leadership in mitigating cognitive biases and enhancing firms' ability to respond strategically to performance signals in high-risk, long-term digital initiatives.
