title: Landscape Dynamics in the Piedmont and Escarpment Front of the Sierras de Comechingones: A Spatiotemporal Analysis
authors: Juan Pablo Zbrun Luoni, Nestor Lattari, Mariela Aguilera Sanmaritano, Facundo Reynoso
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: This study evaluates landscape dynamics in the piedmont and escarpment front of the Sierras de Comechingones (San Luis, Argentina) between 1984 and 2025, using remote sensing (Landsat) and landscape metrics to quantify land cover changes and fragmentation patterns. The research addresses two key questions: (1) How has land cover evolved over time $(1984,2005,2025)$ at a $1: 250,000$ scale? and (2) What is the degree of landscape disturbance? The results indicate a significant reduction in native forests (from 35.2% to 17.4%) and an expansion of grasslands (from 19.6% to 48.4%), driven by agricultural intensification, urban sprawl, and recurrent wildfires. Landscape metrics reveal increased fragmentation during the first period (1984-2005), followed by homogenization in the second period (2005-2025), with a decrease in Shannon diversity index values from 1.317 (1984) to $1.413(2005)$ and $1.268(2025)$, and an increase in the contagion index from $56 \%(1984)$ to $48 \%(2005)$ and 59% (2025). These metrics suggest a simplification of landscape complexity. The study highlights the role of anthropogenic pressures (agricultural and urban expansion) in transforming the piedmont ecosystem into a more homogeneous scenario, dominated by grasslands and croplands. It underscores the urgent need for integrated landscape planning to mitigate environmental degradation.

title: Research on Spectral Defense Optimization Method of Backdoor Attack Based on Singular Value Analysis
authors: Siyu Dong, Zhenzhen Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The deep learning model faces the serious threat of backdoor attack in practical application. The attacker implants the backdoor through poisoning training data to make the model behave normally on normal data, but outputs malicious results when the backdoor is triggered. This paper proposes an optimized spectral defense method for two typical backdoor attacks, BadNets attack and LabelConsistent attack. This method is based on Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) to analyze the feature space differences of the model, and improves the defense performance through Principal Component Analysis (PCA) dimensionality reduction, adaptive anomaly detection, K-means clustering assistance, robust fine-tuning and regularization technology. Experiments on MNIST data sets show that in the range of 10%~30% poisoning rate, compared with the original spectral defense, the optimized spectral defense keeps the average TCA (Test Clean Accuracy) of 99.18% almost unchanged, and the ASR (Attack Success Rate) after the two kinds of attack defense is stably controlled at about 11.4%, with a decrease of more than 88%, which is much better than the volatility performance of the original spectral defense, significantly improving the stability and generalization ability of the defense. The deep feature analysis of singular value spectrum, KL divergence and correlation further confirmed that the optimized spectral defense can more effectively eliminate the backdoor correlation in the model feature space, providing a more reliable guarantee for the model security.

title: Research on Cooperative Switching Control for Connected Autonomous Vehicles in Mixed Traffic Flows
authors: Huisheng Zhang, Chenqi Hu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This paper presents a technically integrated framework for cooperative lane-switching control of connected autonomous vehicles (CAVs) in mixed traffic environments. This study aims at the urgent need for safe and efficient coordination between CAVs and human-driven vehicles, especially during lanechanging maneuvers in heterogeneous traffic. By enhancing multi-agent learning and real-time vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication, the method enables each CAV to perceive its surrounding environment, predict the intentions of neighboring vehicles, and collaboratively discuss safe lane-changing operations. The distributed optimization algorithm translates the complex team decision into accurate longitudinal and lateral control commands, while considering real-time traffic conditions and dynamic constraints. The effectiveness of the framework is evaluated thru comprehensive simulations on an urban expressway model with various traffic flows and different CAV penetration rates. The results show that the proposed method can improve traffic throughput by 18%, reduce lane-changing conflict rate by more than 50%, and reduce overall fleet energy consumption by 20% compared with the baseline strategy. In addition, the framework has excellent traffic flow stability and anti-interference ability. These findings lay a solid foundation for the application of intelligent cooperative control strategies in mixed traffic scenarios. These strategies will greatly improve traffic safety, efficiency and energy sustainability.

title: Development and Usability of the KitGebra Game in Learning Mathematics
authors: Noor Fahisyah Binti Mohd Latip, Seuk Yen Phoong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The declining interest of students in mathematics is often attributed to the difficulty in grasping abstract mathematical concepts. To address this challenge and foster greater engagement, game-based learning has emerged as an effective strategy for making mathematical learning more enjoyable. This study focuses on the development of a game learning tool, KitGebra Game, designed to support students' understanding of Algebraic Expressions. Employing a quantitative research design, the study utilized a questionnaire to gather data from a sample of 32 randomly selected secondary school students in Melaka, Malaysia. The development process followed the ADDIE instructional design model to ensure a systematic and effective creation of the game. The study also evaluated the game's usability to determine its potential in classroom implementation. Findings show that all the items related to the usability of KitGebra Game ranked excellent level. KitGebra Game demonstrates potential as a valuable educational tool that aligns with curriculum standards, enhances student engagement, and promotes interactive, fun learning. This study offers practical insights and recommendations for educators seeking to integrate game-based learning into mathematics instruction.

title: Evaluating and Adapting Monocular Depth Models for Canopy Height Estimation in Semi-Urban and Forested Areas
authors: Loϊck G. Hodonou, Jorge A. C. Martinez, Matheus Pinheiro Ferreira, Raul Queiroz Feitosa
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Accurately measuring vegetation height is a key challenge in environmental monitoring, biomass estimation, and sustainable land management. Recent advances in monocular depth estimation from RGB imagery have introduced models capable of predicting canopy height without relying on costly LiDAR data. However, these models are typically trained on geographically constrained datasets, raising concerns about their ability to generalize to different ecosystems and landscapes. In this study, we assess the performance of a pre-trained monocular depth estimation model when applied to a dataset composed of natural forest areas and a semi-urban environment characterized by a mixture of trees and built structures. Our evaluation reveals a significant degradation in accuracy when the model is used directly without any adaptation, highlighting the limitations of cross-domain transfer in depth-based canopy height estimation. To address this issue, we perform a targeted fine-tuning using our dataset, which results in considerable improvements across key metrics, including Mean Absolute Error (MAE), and Intersection over Union (IoU). These findings demonstrate that even lightweight adaptation strategies are effective for tailoring monocular depth estimation models to distinct environmental contexts, reinforcing the importance of local calibration for accurate and reliable canopy height mapping. The source code used in this study is publicly available at: https://github.com/Geo99pro/depth-any-canopy

title: Research on Countermeasures for Improving the Digital Competence of Normal University Students
authors: Xingzhen Wang, Jia Tao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The Third Plenary Session of the 20th Central Committee of the Communist Party of China emphasized promoting educational digitalization, empowering the construction of a learning society, and strengthening the guarantee for lifelong education. Against the backdrop of the educational digitalization transformation to drive the construction of an educational powerhouse, teachers' digital competence should be an important part of teacher professional development. As future teachers, normal university students' digital competence cultivation has become one of the key factors in future education development. By assessing the current status of normal university students' digital competence, this article proposes countermeasures to enhance their digital competence from four aspects: strengthening the national top-level design, deepening multi-stakeholder collaboration, restructuring the training system of universities, and stimulating individual intrinsic motivation, aiming to provide useful references for optimizing the cultivation of normal university students.

title: Machine Learning–Based Cooling Energy Prediction and Intelligent Control in Data Centers
authors: Hongwei Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Data centers are core infrastructure for the digital economy, yet cooling systems still account for about $\mathbf{4 0 - 5 0 \%}$ of total energy use in many facilities, and have become a key bottleneck for green, low-carbon operation. Traditional set-point and rule-based strategies depend heavily on operator experience and often suffer from low prediction accuracy, delayed response to thermal disturbances and insufficient energy optimization. This paper proposes a machine learning-based cooling energy prediction and intelligent control method for an air-cooled internet data center. First, we construct a multi-source feature system that fuses room-level environmental parameters, IT load indicators and outdoor weather conditions. An improved random forest (RF) model with feature re-weighting and asymmetric loss is trained to predict short-term cooling power at the plant and CRAH levels. On this basis, we model server-room thermal dynamics using a long short-term memory (LSTM) network and embed it in a model predictive control (MPC) framework that optimizes chilled-water supply temperature, CRAH fan speed and temperature deadbands under comfort and safety constraints. A year-long dataset from a large internet data center is used for validation. The proposed RF model achieves 94.8% prediction accuracy, improving mean absolute percentage error by 15.2 percentage points over a backpropagation neural network baseline. In field deployment, the intelligent control strategy reduces cooling energy by 18.3% and improves PUE from 1.45 to 1.28 while maintaining room temperature within $\pm 0.5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ across low, medium and high IT-load scenarios. The method enhances both efficiency and intelligence of data center cooling systems and provides a practical technical route for green operation aligned with “dual-carbon” goals.

title: Assessing the potentiality of UFSAT-1 in TOA radiance and BOA reflectance for inland waters
authors: Raul Rubio, Sofía Paná, Francisco Nemiña, Alice Fabbretto, Anabella Ferral, Mariano Bresciani, Claudia Giardino
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Satellite monitoring of inland water bodies is an essential tool for assessing water quality, including the detection of water pollution, eutrophication and algal blooms. The success and accuracy of satellite measurements are highly dependent on rigorous calibration and validation (Cal/Val) processes using in situ reference data. The UFSAT-1 educational mission proposes to bring this monitoring capability to a more accessible scale, including a multispectral camera (Arrok) aimed at detecting algal blooms in coastal and inland waters. Validation of data from new sensors, especially those with specific spectral and spatial characteristics requires efficient methodologies. In this regard, previous studies have performed extensive Cal/Val work for hyperspectral sensors (PRISMA) over various water bodies, including Italian lakes such as Lake Garda and Lake Trasimeno, using validated in situ measurements. These lakes present varied optical characteristics and trophic states different from the eutrophic to hypereutrophic state of Lake San Roque, Argentina, which makes them valuable test sites to evaluate the potential of remote sensing in different aquatic environments. Based on the importance of $\mathbf{C a l} / \mathbf{V a l}$ and the availability of validated datasets over Garda lake, this work proposes to evaluate the capability of the Arrok camera for water quality monitoring using a simulation approach and analysis of existing data. Validated PRISMA reference hyperspectral data will be used to simulate the spectral response that the UFSAT-1 multispectral camera bands would record over these water bodies. By analyzing how the UFSAT-1 bands would capture the characteristic spectral signatures of water quality indicators (such as Chlorophyll-a, Total Suspended Solids) in the various trophic and optical states present in Garda, we seek to validate the proposed monitoring methodology for UFSAT-1 and characterize its operational capabilities. This approach allows to evaluate the Arrok camera in aquatic environments with properties different from its initial area of focus (San Roque lake), taking advantage of robust validation frameworks and reference data collected in previous studies on this Italian lake. The results will contribute to optimize processing algorithms for UFSAT-1 data and guide its future applications in global water quality monitoring.

title: A Fault-Tolerant Die-to-Die Interconnect with Retry and Repair Mechanisms for Chiplet-Based Systems
authors: Yan Qiao, Bi Wang, Shuai Hao, Wei Zhuang, Yong Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the slowdown of Moore's Law, chiplet-based integration has emerged as a promising strategy for improving system performance and yield in the post-Moore era. The die-todie (D2D) interconnect serves as the critical interface enabling communication among chiplets. However, the use of dense microbump arrays and advanced packaging technologies increase the risk of contact failures, thereby posing significant challenges to system reliability. This paper presents a lightweight and faulttolerant D2D interconnect that integrates a retry mechanism with a link repair scheme to ensure data integrity and maintain system availability. Implemented in Verilog and validated on a Xilinx XCVU440 FPGA, the proposed D2D interconnect achieves 14.76 Gb/s throughput and a 37.5% reduction in hardware resource utilization compared with a previously reported low-cost chip-to-chip interconnect.

title: A Lightweight YOLOv12n Object Detection Network for Underwater Degraded Environments
authors: Fengmin Peng, Qiong Gao, Dan Zhang, Guojin Xiao, Wenjie Huang, Mingshen Huang, Weihao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Due to the characteristics of light propagation underwater,underwater object detection models face challenges such as blurred object edges that are easily confused with complex backgrounds and difficulties in detecting small objects, which leaves much room for improvement in accuracy. This paper proposes the Convolution-Depthwise Separable Convolution-Max Pooling (CDM) module and develops the enhanced YOLOv12nCDM network based on the YOLOv12n architecture. By replacing the C3K2 module in the shallow layers of the original network with the CDM module,YOLOv12n-CDM addresses the limitations of C3K2 in distinguishing blurred object edges from background interference. The CDM module uses standard convolution and depthwise separable convolution for basic feature extraction and enhancing generalization ability,utilizes max pooling to filter out regions where edges and backgrounds are confused, focuses on prominent object features,and introduces residual connections to retain key features while improving model robustness. This module also enhances attention to small objects. Experimental results show that YOLOv12n-CDM achieves mAP@. 5 and mAP@.5: metrics. On the DUO public dataset,it scored 84.4% and 64.8% respectively,which are 0.8% and 1.2% higher than the baseline model. The model has 2.65 million parameters and a computational complexity of 8.2 GFLOPs,maintaining efficient detection performance while effectively controlling complexity.

title: Attention-Enhanced CNN-LSTM for Ship Intention Prediction Using AIS Data
authors: Bo Yang, Xiao Liu, Zhenya Wang, Shui Yu, Yunbo Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the increase in maritime traffic density, predicting ship intentions, especially in encounter scenarios, has become crucial for collision avoidance and traffic optimization. Traditional trajectory prediction methods often demonstrate reduced accuracy in complex, nonlinear maritime environments. To address this issue, the present study introduces a CNN-LSTM -attention framework for ship intention prediction, utilizing Automatic Identification System (AIS) data. This architecture incorporates convolutional layers for spatiotemporal feature extraction, LSTM for modeling sequential dependencies, and an attention mechanism for context-aware temporal weighting. A one-month AIS dataset was meticulously preprocessed, which included data cleaning, cubic spline interpolation for synchronization, and annotation based on the inherent embedding of topological concepts in Convention on the International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea (COLREGs) encounter types. Comparative evaluations against DWT-KNN, LSTM, GRU, TCN models demonstrate the superior performance of the proposed model across various metrics. The proposed model achieves an accuracy of 98.63%, surpassing DWT-KNN, LSTM, GRU, and TCN by 20.7%, 19.0%, 18.9%, and 9.8%, respectively. These findings highlight the effectiveness of the CNN-LSTMattention model in enhancing predictive capabilities for ship intentions.

title: Adaptive Resource Partitioning for Fractional Frequency Reuse in Multi-Cell Networks: A Heuristic and Optimisation-Based Approach
authors: He Sun
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Inter-cell interference remains a critical bottleneck in multi-cellular networks, significantly impacting the performance of edge users and resulting in notable throughput discrepancies between users near the base station and those at the cell periphery. This disparity not only diminishes user satisfaction but also poses challenges to the widespread implementation of 5G and future 6G networks. Fractional frequency reuse (FFR) emerges as a pivotal solution for alleviating inter-cell interference by partitioning the available spectrum into central and peripheral subbands and implementing distinct reuse factors. Nonetheless, the efficacy of FFR is heavily contingent upon subband delineation and power allocation strategies, presenting a continuing optimisation challenge. This paper introduces a novel adaptive fractional resource allocation (AFRA) technique for FFR in multicell networks. AFRA dynamically adjusts subband and power allocation based on network user density and interference levels to enhance average throughput while maintaining fairness for edge users. In contrast to existing adaptive approaches, AFRA is characterised by its simplicity, ease of implementation, and suitability for large-scale Monte Carlo simulations. Additionally, this paper presents a comprehensive MATLAB simulation platform, conducts a systematic comparison between heuristics and optimisation benchmark techniques, and incorporates reliability analysis using statistical methods. Simulation results demonstrate that AFRA strikes a favourable balance between overall system throughput and fairness, exhibiting lower computational complexity and greater scalability compared to state-of-the-art optimisation methods.

title: Object Detection Based on Centernet Small Object Information Enhancement
authors: Biao Wang, Ting Zhou, Hongxing Bai, Xi Yang, Ke Li, Lingyu Yan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Object detection is a fundamental task in computer vision, primarily used to identify the categories of objects within digital images. It finds extensive applications in intelligent transportation, human-computer interaction, and autonomous driving. We design an object detection algorithm based on CenterNet that enhances small object information, considering the informational characteristics of low-level features. First, the classic backbone network ResNet50 is employed for feature extraction. Drawing inspiration from feature pyramids, the output feature maps from the backbone network undergo multi-scale fusion to extract small object information from low-level features. Second, a feature selection module is added to model feature importance in deep feature maps, utilizing an adaptive feature fusion mechanism to reduce semantic gaps. Finally, a positional attention mechanism is applied to low-level features to capture contextual positional information. The performance of the improved CenterNet model is validated on the VisDrone2019 small object detection dataset for drones.

title: Moe-Tsqformer: Dynamic Context-Aware Query Generation for Multi-Label Animal Behavior Recognition Via Mixture of Experts
authors: Ruijie Li, Junwen Wu, Hongli Zhang, Yang Fu, Lu Li, Jingjing Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In-the-wild animal behavior recognition is challenged by fine-grained variations, multi-label co-occurrence, and dynamic backgrounds. Existing methods rely on static queries during inference, neglecting the dynamic visual context of videos. To address this limitation, we propose MoE-TSQFormer, which introduces a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) query generator. The proposed framework integrates dual-modal encoding with SigLIP2 and a sparse gating mechanism to adaptively generate sample-specific query vectors conditioned on both visual context and textual semantics. Experiments on the Animal Kingdom dataset demonstrate that MoE-TSQFormer significantly outperforms existing static-query models, validating the effectiveness of dynamic context-aware query generation.

title: Hierarchical Aggregation for Straggler Mitigation in In-Network Aggregated Distributed Training
authors: Wenlingxiao Li, Ruihan Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: In-network aggregation (INA) speeds up distributed deep learning by using programmable switches to aggregate gradients, but stragglers—slow workers—can severely delay synchronization and degrade training performance. We propose HSM, a hierarchical aggregation protocol that groups workers into multi-level clusters with varying sync frequencies. This localizes straggler effects while still incorporating delayed gradients to preserve accuracy. Experiments show HSM boosts training throughput over existing methods without sacrificing model accuracy, offering robustness in heterogeneous environments.

title: Cache-Augmented Multimodal Sentiment Detection Using Embedding Systems
authors: V Srilakshmi, G Uday Kiran, S Sai Sreeja, V Varun Reddy, V Karthik, P Sridar Chowdary
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: The exponential growth of multimodal content across digital platforms has exposed the limitations of traditional sentiment analysis systems that rely solely on text-based methods. This work presents a cache-augmented multimodal sentiment detection framework that integrates DistilBERT text embeddings (768D) and CLIP image embeddings (512D) through FAISS similarity search and LRU (Least Recently Used) caching mechanisms. The system addresses computational redundancy in real-time applications by implementing a retrieval-first pipeline that reuses previous computations before fallback MLP (Multi-Layer Perceptron) classification. Evaluated on MVSA-Single dataset (374 image-text pairs) and Twitter/Reddit corpus (600 text samples), the approach achieves 81.3% accuracy and 81.1% macro-F1 score. Key contributions include: (1) a FAISS-based retrieval pipeline reducing inference latency by 60% for repeated inputs, (2) adaptive LRU cache mechanism with 1000-entry capacity for computational efficiency, and (3) late fusion architecture combining normalized text and image embeddings for robust multimodal sentiment classification.

title: RDTAS-MAC: A Cross-Layer Robust Data Collection Protocol for AUV-Assisted UASNs Under Node Position Uncertainty
authors: Xiaofei Li, Qian Wei, Lin Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In complex marine environments, underwater sensor nodes exhibit continuous random drift driven by ocean currents, characterized by spatiotemporal correlations. This poses severe challenges, such as link interruptions and scheduling conflicts, for traditional AUV data collection protocols that assume static nodes or simple mobility models. To overcome the performance degradation caused by position uncertainty, this paper proposes a Robust Data Collection and Adaptive Scheduling MAC protocol (RDTAS-MAC). The protocol innovatively constructs a node drift model based on the Ornstein-Uhlenbeck (OU) process, which not only captures the mean-reverting characteristics of perturbations but also provides accurate prior distributions for subsequent state prediction. On this basis, the selection of the AUV's hovering position is modeled as a chance-constrained programming problem. A two-stage Monte Carlo search algorithm is designed to maximize the expected data collection utility while strictly guaranteeing probabilistic connectivity. Furthermore, addressing the random jitter in propagation delay, an uncertainty-aware scheduling mechanism is proposed. By dynamically mapping the error covariance of position estimates to time-domain guard intervals, collision-free parallel data transmission is achieved. Experimental results verify that the RDTAS-MAC protocol significantly improves throughput and reduces packet loss rates in strong perturbation scenarios. This study validates the necessity of incorporating physical layer position uncertainty into MAC layer design, providing a highly reliable solution for dynamic underwater acoustic networks.

title: A Secure Blockchain-Driven Model for Identity Preservation and Missing Person Tracing in Post-Disaster Scenarios
authors: Anil Gupta
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: In the aftermath of natural disasters or humanitarian crises, the urgent and safe identification and location of missing persons present a significant challenge. In this study I propose a novel blockchain-driven framework designed to enhance missing person tracing through the integration of intelligent warning systems, secure decentralized data storage, and biometric identity verification. The core of the system involves using secure, on-chain hash representations of biometric data, ensuring tamper-proof identity registration and verification processes. Complementing this, a decentralised metadata architecture facilitates distributed access and retrieval of crucial information while prioritizing anonymity. Automated procedures, including alert generation, status updates, and real-time stakeholder communications, are managed by smart contracts, enhancing response coordination. A novel mathematical framework, leveraging graph-based structures and safe hash functions, models’ identification relationships to enable efficient person-tracing logic. The system is implemented using Solidity and Ganache to simulate realistic post-disaster workflows. Performance evaluations demonstrate acceptable trade-offs between gas consumption, latency, and biometric matching accuracy. Scalability considerations and privacy-preserving mechanisms are discussed in detail. The findings indicate that the proposed architecture offers a resilient, transparent, and verifiable solution for managing identity and tracing in decentralized environments, making it a promising tool for emergency response and law enforcement applications in digital public safety infrastructure. Our work primarily contributes by proposing a blockchain-based architecture for identity preservation and missing-person tracking. The model incorporates a graph-based tracing technique along with optimizations tailored for specific disasters, distinguishing it from previous research.

title: DB-YOLO: An Efficient Steel Surface Defect Detection Model With Lightweight BiFPN and Adaptive Feature Fusion
authors: Yong Lu, Jie Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the challenges of low detection accuracy and excessive computational demands, we develop an improved model called DB-YOLO, which is based on the YOLOv8n architecture. We integrate the BiFPN-light module into the neck network to enhance multi-scale feature fusion and improve the flow of information across different layers. Furthermore, the DAFDetect module is employed in the detection head to conduct adaptive weighted fusion of feature maps. This enhances the model's sensitivity to defects across different scales. On the NEUDET dataset, DB-YOLO attains a mAP50 of 81.1 %. This represents a reduction in model parameters by 37.8 % and a decrease in computational cost (FLOPs) by 46.9 % compared to the original YOLOv8n. The model effectively balances detection accuracy with complexity, demonstrating strong potential for realworld industrial applications.

title: Temporal Knowledge Graph Reasoning With Dynamic Context-Aware Attention and Evolutionary Learning
authors: Qingkui Chen, Siling Feng, Mengxing Huang, Yingjie Feng
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Temporal Knowledge Graphs (TKGs) are critical for modeling dynamic knowledge with temporal semantics, but existing methods face challenges in capturing fine-grained structural evolutions and long-term temporal dependencies during entity and relation prediction. To address these challenges, this paper introduces DAEL (Dynamic Context-Aware Attention and Evolutionary Learning), a novel model that effectively tackles both dynamic structural dependencies and long-term temporal patterns through two complementary components: the Dynamic Context-Aware Graph Attention Layer (DCAGAT) and an LSTM-GRU joint evolution module. For structural modeling, DCAGAT enables dynamic neighborhood aggregation and multiperspective feature extraction via adaptive message weighting, dual-relation utilization, and multi-head attention. This design allows the model to prioritize critical neighbors based on temporal context and relational attributes, outperforming static aggregation methods in capturing evolving structural patterns. For temporal modeling, the LSTM-GRU module employs LSTM to model long-term temporal dependencies of relations and GRU to promote co-evolution of entities and relations, to enhance the model's capability to handle complex temporal dynamics. Experimental results on ICEWS14, ICEWS18, ICEWS05-15, and GDELT datasets show that DAEL achieves MRR improvements over the baseline model RE-GCN of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.86 \%, 0.33 \%, 1.10 \%$</tex>, and 0.32% in entity prediction tasks, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.83 \%, 0.22 \%, 0.98 \%$</tex>, and 0.14% in relation prediction tasks, respectively.

title: How Much is Enough? Assessing the Feature Dimensionality and Performance Trade-offs in Flood Classification Using Random Forest
authors: Aluizio Brito Maia, Camilo Daleles Rennó, Rogério Galante Negri, Evandro José Da Silva, Eduardo Moraes Arraut
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: In machine learning-based classifications, attribute selection plays a key role in building more efficient models for two main reasons: first, the selected attributes must adequately discriminate between classes; second, they must balance informational value and operational/computational cost. This raises a central question: What attributes and how many are truly essential to ensure an effective and resource-efficient classification? In the context of emergency flood mapping, this issue becomes even more critical, since rapid response depends on models that are accurate yet lightweight. To address this challenge, we assess the dimensional trade-offs involved in feature selection for flood detection using the Boruta algorithm and Random Forest models. The proposed approach evaluates spectral indices and altimetric descriptors, integrating their predictive accuracy with computational burden through the Multimetric Computational-Prediction Efficiency Index (MCPEI). The results demonstrate that reduced attribute sets-such as those based on a handful of top-ranked spectral and hydrological features-can deliver flood maps with quality comparable to models relying on extensive variable collections. This reduction not only decreases computational and operational costs but also enhances the feasibility of applying the method in disaster contexts where agility and resource economy are essential.

title: Energy-Aware 2D FIR Filter Design Using Approximate Multipliers and Circular Coefficient Symmetry
authors: Venkata Krishna Odugu, Harish Babu Gade, U Appala Raju, Kancharla Suresh, Manish Kumar Singla, Bharti Sharma
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Digital image processing applications such as image restoration, enhancement, and seismic data analysis often rely on two-dimensional (2D) digital filters (DFs). For VLSI implementations of such filters, achieving low latency, reduced area, and minimal power consumption is critical. This work introduces an optimized 2D FIR filter architecture by leveraging Circular Symmetry (CS) and hardware-efficient approximations. Specifically, a novel Approximate $4: 2$ Compressor (A42COMP) is developed by simplifying a conventional $4: 2$ compressor, reducing hardware complexity and delay. These compressors are integrated into a newly designed Approximate Multiplier (AMUL), which is then employed in the proposed 2D FIR Filter Architecture (2DFIRFA). The architecture is implemented in Verilog HDL and synthesized using Cadence Genus with $\mathbf{4 5 ~ n m}$ CMOS technology. Comparative analysis against state-of-the-art designs demonstrates improvements in area, power, and timing performance.

title: Flipped Classroom Adoption in Digital Education: The Roles of UTAUT Model, Learning Motivation and Satisfaction
authors: Ziqi Meng, Seuk Wai Phoong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study examines the factors influencing the adoption of flipped classroom by using an extended Unified Theory of Acceptance and Use of Technology (UTAUT) model. Key constructs including performance expectancy, effort expectancy, social influence, facilitating conditions, and learning motivation were analyzed for their effects on learning satisfaction and behavioural intention. There are two variables, learning motivation and learning satisfaction added to the UTAUT model to better understand students' intentions to use flipped classroom. This extension makes the UTAUT model more applicable to educational settings by adding students' learning motivation and satisfaction, which are important but often overlooked factors in flipped classroom adoption. Purposive sampling technique is used to collect data and the structural equation modeling based on 308 valid survey responses revealed that all five factors significantly and positively influenced learning satisfaction and behavioural intention, with learning satisfaction partially mediating these relationships. The findings highlight the influence of individual psychological factors and external conditions in shaping technology adoption in education. This study not only extends the theoretical scope of the UTAUT model but also emphasizes the critical roles of learning motivation and satisfaction in the flipped classroom context. The results offer practical implications for educators and policymakers seeking to enhance digital learning strategies in Chinese higher education.

title: Performance Evaluation of Sensitive Pattern Hiding Methods
authors: Peng Cheng, Jianghua Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Knowledge hiding in data mining, especially frequent pattern hiding, has made considerable progress over the past two decades. Given the current lack of evaluation and comparison of solutions on frequent pattern hiding, this paper provides a comprehensive empirical evaluation of state-of-the-art frequent itemset hiding methods, including heuristic (e.g., BBRH), border-based (e.g., WBA, Max-Min1, Max-Min2), and exact Integer Linear Programming (ILP) approaches (e.g., Max-Accuracy, Coeff. Max-Accuracy, BBMax-Accuracy), alongside the hybrid Heuristic-Coeff method. These algorithms are systematically evaluated on metrics: missing non-sensitive itemsets, the degree of data distortion, and time efficiency. Experiment results indicate that exact methods often achieve a balance of delivering a competitive minimization of side effects and data distortion while maintaining high time efficiency. In contrast, heuristic methods like WBA can achieve low side effects in specific scenarios but may suffer from scalability issues on dense datasets. The findings offer practical guidance for selecting appropriate hiding strategies based on application-specific requirements.

title: Zero-Shot Human-Object Interaction Detection with CLIP
authors: Xinyi Su, Song Wang, Zihao Zhang, Quanfu Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Zero-shot Human-Object Interaction (HOI) detection requires not only the identification of human and object instances with their interaction relationships, but also the recognition of unseen interaction types. However, existing CLIP-based methods often rely solely on global visual features from the final encoder layer, neglecting the fine-grained local representations embedded in intermediate layers that are crucial for HOI understanding. This limitation leads to insufficient accuracy in capturing detailed interactions. Moreover, current CLIP text encoders typically depend on manually crafted prompts, which tend to overfit seen categories during training and thereby hinder generalization to unseen classes. To overcome these challenges, we propose a zero-shot HOI detection with CLIP. Specifically, a multi-level feature aggregation module is introduced to integrate both local and global visual representations. A lightweight adapter is further employed to refine the aggregated features, aligning them more effectively with HOI tasks. In addition, we design a semantic consistency prompt learning module, which enhances generalization to unseen categories by enforcing semantic alignment between handcrafted and learnable prompts in the shared representation space. Extensive experiments demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach, HICO-DET and V-COCO, substantiating the superiority of the proposed method.

title: Intelligent Link Switching Assisted by Multi-Source Data Prediction for UAV Hybrid FSO/RF System
authors: Simeng Feng, Zhenghao Fang, Chao Dong, Lei Zhang, Qihui Wu
abstract: Hybrid free space optical (FSO) and radio frequency (RF) systems combine the high capacity of FSO links with the stability of RF links, providing a promising solution for unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) networks. In such hybrid systems, reliable link switching is essential for maintaining stable UAV communication. In this paper, we develop an intelligent link switching scheme assisted by multi-source data prediction (ILS-MDP). By fusing visible light images, meteorological data, positional information, and signal measurements, the scheme supports link quality prediction under dynamic UAV channel conditions, thereby enabling timely switching decisions. To adapt to FSO channel fluctuations, the prediction horizon is dynamically adjusted based on a channel fluctuation index, while an integrated error feedback mechanism monitors real-time prediction errors and triggers timely re-prediction when significant deviations occur. Simulation results based on real-world meteorological data show that the scheme effectively reduces outage duration through proactive link switching and improves the average throughput compared to existing schemes.

title: OpenUrban3D: Label-Free Open-Vocabulary Semantic Segmentation of Large-Scale Urban Point Clouds
authors: Chongyu Wang, Kunlei Jing, Jihua Zhu, Di Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Open-vocabulary semantic segmentation enables models to recognize and segment objects from arbitrary natural language descriptions, offering the flexibility to handle novel, fine-grained, or functionally defined categories beyond fixed label sets. While this capability is crucial for large-scale urban point clouds that support applications such as digital twins, smart city management, and urban analytics, it remains largely unexplored in this domain. The main obstacles are the frequent absence of high-quality, well-aligned multi-view imagery in large-scale urban point cloud datasets and the poor generalization of existing three-dimensional (3D) segmentation pipelines across diverse urban environments with substantial variation in geometry, scale, and appearance. To address these challenges, we present OpenUrban3D, a 3D open-vocabulary semantic segmentation framework for large-scale urban scenes that operates without aligned multi-view images, pre-trained point cloud segmentation networks, or manual labels. Our approach generates robust semantic features directly from raw point clouds through multi-view, multi-granularity rendering, mask-level vision-language feature extraction, and sample-balanced fusion, followed by distillation into a 3D backbone model. This design enables zero-shot segmentation for arbitrary text queries while capturing both semantic richness and geometric priors. Extensive experiments on large-scale urban benchmarks, including SensatUrban and SUM, show that OpenUrban3D achieves significant improvements in both segmentation accuracy and cross-scene generalization over existing methods, demonstrating its potential as a flexible and scalable solution for 3D urban scene understanding. The implementation code is available on.

title: Enhancing Data Augmentation Diversity: A Diffusion Model-based Approach for Few-Shot Specific Emitter Identification
authors: Dongli Zhang, Guoru Ding, Junning Zhang, Yutao Jiao, Peng Tang, Yufan Chen, Maomao Zhang, Jiabao Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Specific emitter identification (SEI) separates the radio frequency fingerprint (RFF) from signals, which is of great significance in solving Internet of Things (IoT) security problems. However, the scarcity of high-quality, diverse, and labeled data in real-world scenarios limits the application of SEI. Under such conditions, the SEI is referred to as few-shot SEI (FS-SEI). To surmount this challenge, we propose a diffusion model-based data augmentation method capable of generating a substantial volume of diverse, high-quality data. Specifically, we develop a multi-scale convolutional block attention module denoising diffusion probabilistic model (MSCBAM-DDPM), which enhances feature capture capabilities, laying the foundation for the generation of diverse data. Furthermore, we propose an adaptive two-stage multi-domain loss function that guides the model to learn the characteristics of the original data and further derive other similar features, thereby achieving the goal of generating diverse and high-quality data. Finally, we theoretically derive the feasibility of the proposed loss function and further demonstrate the excellent diversity and quality of the data generated by our method, as well as its considerable gain for FS-SEI, through extensive experiments on real-world signal datasets.

title: Electrothermal Design of Silicon-Doped Microheaters for Optical Phase Change Memory_supp1-3667672.pdf
authors: Georges Pavlidis
abstract: The growing interest in optical phase change materials (PCMs) has spurred the development of programmable photonics systems with zero-static power. Their optical properties make them well-suited for nonvolatile retention and reversible phase switching. PCMs can be switched using optical pulses or integrated microheaters. Microheaters provide Joule heating to indirectly induce the phase change. This mechanism offers the most promising route to achieving scalability through CMOS integration and reliable multilevel modulation. However, optimizing the design and modulation of microheater devices has become challenging due to the complex electrothermal dynamics, which require high temporal and spatial resolution techniques for accurate assessment. This article presents an electrothermal co-design of silicon-doped microheaters through advanced thermal characterization, which can reveal temperature distributions, on the submicrosecond scale, not readily obtainable through computational methods. We evaluate the geometrical and electrical parameters dependence on the transient thermal transport to maximize the hotspot temperature and quenching rates, as well as the impact of the pulsewidth on the multilevel response and energy consumption. High resolution transient thermoreflectance imaging (TTI) was used to measure and evaluate the temperature distribution under pulsed biasing (0.2–<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$10~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s). Reduced doped region lengths (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lt 6~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m) enable higher current densities to maximize the temperature and quenching rates. Additionally, shorter pulsewidths (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\lt 1~\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>s) enable well-defined multilevel temperature profile responses and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\approx 13$</tex-math> </inline-formula> times reduction in energy consumption.

title: Reliable AI Accelerator Design Using Multiple Fault-Tolerance Techniques on SRAM-Based APSoCs
authors: Eduardo Marañon, Fabio Benevenuti, Nilberto H. Medina, Nemitala Added, Vitor Ângelo P. Aguiar, Saulo G. P. N. Alberton, João Vitor Schervenin, Matheus S. Pereira, Fernanda L. Kastensmidt
abstract: As Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) implemented on All Programmable System on Chips (APSoCs) are increasingly adopted in space mission-critical systems due to their flexibility and parallelism, selecting a fault-tolerant design that balances reliability, area, and power is essential to address radiation-induced faults and resource constraints. However, the literature offers limited exploration of the combined use of multiple fault-tolerance techniques within a single AI accelerator, making it difficult to identify the most effective strategies. To address this, we propose a generalizable design flow and apply it to the ZynqNet accelerator which is generated using High-Level Synthesis (HLS). ZynqNet implements a 4-layer CNN trained on the SAT-6 dataset for aerial image classification. The goal is to evaluate the impact of several fault-tolerance strategies applied at different levels of architectural granularity based on Triple Modular Redundancy (TMR), including Modular TMR (MTMR), Coarse-Grained TMR (CGTMR), and Distributed TMR (DTMR), targeting bit flips in the configuration memory (CRAM) of the Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA). Additionally, memory scrubbing was implemented to correct and prevent the accumulation of CRAM faults over time, while error detection and correction codes (EDAC) were employed to protect volatile data stored in caches. The evaluation was validated through both fast, low-cost emulated fault injection and real heavy-ion irradiation experiments. Results show that the combination of DTMR, EDAC, and CRAM scrubbing at a reliability above 90% improves the expected mission image by 4.8× in emulation and 6.31× in irradiation.

title: Low-Profile Double-Sided Metal-Backed H-Slot Folded-Patch Tag with Resonance Tuning Capability for On-Metal UHF RFID
authors: Fuad Erman, Slawomir Koziel, Eng-Hock Lim, Shaker Alkaraki, Muthukannan Murugesh, Rawan Soboh, Mohammad Kouali
abstract: This research article presents a miniature, simple in construction, low-profile, two-sided metal-backed folded patch antenna with a resonance tuning capability for on-metal UHF tag. A flexible single-layer polyethylene terephthalate (PET) substrate is wrapped around two pieces of polyethylene foam to support the fabrication of the designed tag. The top and bottom conductive layers are fully laminated with copper, enabling two sides of the tag to be placed against the metal surface without compromising its performance. An H-slot is etched in the middle patch, and the UCODE 8 flip chip is bonded across the center of the embedded patch. The middle patch and top conductive layers are shorted to the bottom conductive layer using inductive shorting stubs. The top conductive layer and the middle patch radiate effectively as a combined structure when the bottom conductive layer is mounted on a metal surface. Conversely, the bottom conductive layer and middle patch jointly radiate effectively when the top conductive layer is placed on the metal surface. Rigorous constrained optimization using the penalty function approach was employed to realize optimal performance with ideal impedance matching at the desired resonant frequency. Concurrent adjustment of all antenna parameters enabled the enhancement of operating parameters beyond what is possible using traditional means, especially in terms of miniaturization. The tag resonance can be tuned to the Lower European (LEu) band with minimal adjustment using single parameter tuning techniques: adjusting the horizontal parallel slots length (Tag-B) or adjusting the width of the shorting stubs (Tag-C). The tag’s volume and size are remarkably small (49.76 mm×18.14 mm×1.177 mm) while its high performance is sustained. The measured read performance on-metal surface is 9.5 m for 4 W EIRP in the NA spectra, whilst it is 7.36 m (Tag-B) and 6.79 m (Tag-C) for 3.24 W EIRP in the LEu spectra. The tag’s reading performance stays stable after being flipped on a metal surface.

title: A Trustworthy Agentic Multi-LLM Network: Challenges, Solutions, and a Use Case
authors: Haoxiang Luo, Gang Sun, Yinqiu Liu, Dusit Niyato, Hongfang Yu, Mohammed Atiquzzaman, Schahram Dustdar
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) demonstrate strong potential across a variety of tasks in communications and networking due to their advanced reasoning capabilities. However, since different LLMs have different model structures and are trained using distinct corpora and methods, they may offer varying optimization strategies for the same network issues. Moreover, the potential maliciousness of its hosting device can result in LLM responses with low confidence or even bias. To address these challenges, we propose a collaborative framework that organizes distributed LLMs into an Agentic Multi-LLM Network (Agentic MultiLLMN). This novel architecture transforms individual network nodes into collaborative autonomous agents equipped with LLMs. Through agentic functionalities such as autonomous planning, reasoning, and collaborative decision-making, the framework can provide high-quality responses for complex network optimization problems. Specifically, we first review related work and highlight the limitations of existing LLMs in collaboration and trust. We then introduce a workflow of the proposed Trustworthy Agentic MultiLLMN framework, which leverages blockchain to provide a verifiable audit trail for autonomous agent interactions. Given the severity of False Base Station (FBS) attacks in wireless communications, we present FBS defense as a case study. In this scenario, each Legitimate Base Station (LBS) acts as an agentic LLM node, collaborating to optimize power allocation and mitigate attacks. The simulation shows that this framework can more effectively resist this attack compared to other solutions. Finally, we outline promising future research directions in this emerging area.

title: A Wearable Platform for Real-Time Control of a Prosthetic Hand by High-Density EMG_supp1-3670500.mp4
authors: Leonardo Abdala Elias

title: Dynamic Network Reconfiguration for Mitigating Price Integrity Attacks in Grid-Aware Price-Based Demand Response
authors: Vipin Chandra Pandey, Charalambos Konstantinou, Barry P. Hayes
abstract: The potential vulnerability of demand response (DR) to cyber-attacks represents a significant challenge that can have detrimental impacts on the technical and economic performances of the distribution system (DS). This issue is addressed in the proposed grid-aware price-based DR framework under price integrity attack (PIA) as a bi-level defender-attacker problem. The bi-level comprises a distribution system operator (DSO) at the upper-level, and demand response providers (DRPs) and customers at the lower-levels. It uses a grid-aware, distribution locational marginal price as an interaction in DR pricing. The defender-attacker problem is constructed when the attacker devises an adversarial DR attack on the DRP’s price using PIA to manipulate the load consumption of the customers. In response, the DSO uses dynamic Network Reconfiguration (NRE) as a defender action to change the grid-aware price for mitigating adverse DR. The proposed problem is tested on the 33-bus and 119-bus DS and is solved using the nested reformulation & decomposition method. Numerical analyses demonstrate that the proposed methodology effectively shows the substantial change in economic and technical performance of DR under attack and defender action. It shows the significant impact of PIA which reduces DR contribution from 9.60 to 2.15 %, and then improves to 5.30 % with the defender action using dynamic NRE for 33-bus. For the 119 bus, it changes from 9.90 % to 3.73 % then to 7.49 %. Further, the proposed model shows its scalability and tractability on small- to large-scale systems effectively.

title: Nonlinear Transformed Low-Rank Quaternion Tensor Total Variation for Multidimensional Color Image Completion
authors: Liqiao Yang, Yexun Hu, Tai-Xiang Jiang, Yimin Wei, Guisong Liu, Michael K. Ng
abstract: Completing multidimensional color images is a fundamental challenge in image processing and computer vision. However, some tensor-based methods often treat RGB channels as independent modes, thereby neglecting their intrinsic correlations. To address this limitation, we represent RGB values as pure quaternions and organize them into a quaternion tensor for holistic modeling that preserves chromatic relationships. To better capture the nonlinear characteristics inherent in visual data and to improve the compactness of low-rank representations, we propose a nonlinear transformation within the quaternion domain. This design enables more expressive modeling compared to conventional linear approaches. In addition, we introduce two novel regularization terms that jointly encode global low-rankness and local smoothness, with the nonlinear transformation further enhancing the exploitation of structural priors. The overall model is optimized via a nonlinear alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM), with theoretical guarantees of convergence. Extensive experiments on several datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art low-rank tensor and quaternion tensor recovery techniques in multidimensional color image completion tasks.

title: 6G‑Enabled Digital Twin–Driven Predictive Security Framework for University Research Management and Big Data Protection
authors: Jiajia Liu
abstract: Digital twin (DT) technology real-time digital counterparts of physical assets has advanced rapidly across critical sectors. In the 6G era, the integration of DTs with ultra-low latency communication, edge intelligence, and artificial intelligence (AI) promises predictive control, enhanced collaboration, and resilient research ecosystems. Yet, this same convergence expands the attack surface: physical tampering, edge compromise, model hijacking, and adversarial AI pose risks that current security standards only partially address. Existing frameworks such as ISO/IEC 27001, 3GPP SA3, ETSI PDL, GDPR, and NIST AI RMF each contribute, but none fully cover end-to-end DT synchronisation, AI governance, or federated research data protection. This article presents a layered predictive security framework for 6G-enabled DTs in university research management and big data protection. The framework integrates provenance anchoring, anomaly detection, risk forecasting, and explainability dashboards with secure network slicing and federated identity management. We map threats to controls, assess coverage of international standards, identify critical gaps, and propose future standardisation directions. A university case study illustrates practical deployment. The work highlights the urgency of harmonising security and AI standards to ensure interoperable, trustworthy, and privacy-preserving DT ecosystems in next-generation communication systems.

title: Fuzzy Decoupled Adaptive Impedance Control for a Five-Wheel Wall-Climbing Robot on Discontinuous Wall Surfaces_supp3-3670440.mp4
authors: Lei Chen
abstract: Aiming at the problems such as insufficient driving force, body oscillation, and contact instability that easily occur when wheel-based wall-climbing robots operate on discontinuous wall surfaces (such as corners and obstacles), this paper proposes an adaptive control strategy integrating joint impedance control and fuzzy decoupling compensation for a Five-Wheel Wall-Climbing robot(FWWC-robot). This strategy optimizes impedance parameters (mass, damping, and stiffness) online through an adaptive parameter update mechanism based on force error to minimize magnetic gap fluctuations and ensure stable adsorption. Meanwhile, a Mamdani fuzzy controller is used to dynamically compensate for the coupling interference of the front and rear swing arms, realizing real-time adjustment of joint compliance and improving the robot’s motion stability and dynamic adaptability when encountering abrupt changes in wall structures. Simulation and experimental results show that, compared with the traditional impedance control, this method reduces the attitude error by 60%, controls the magnetic gap fluctuation within 0.3 mm, achieves a 100% success rate of obstacle crossing under the working conditions of 63° corner and 30 mm obstacle, and keeps the attitude stability within ±1.2°. It provides an efficient and robust solution for the compliant motion of robots in complex wall environments, and can directly support the automated applications in high-risk operation scenarios such as ship derusting and large oil tank inspection.

title: Deep Reinforcement Learning and Fuzzy Logic Based Adaptive Shift Control Method of Driving Robot System
authors: Gang Chen, Tongqi Tang, Liangmo Wang, Weigong Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: In order to improve the fuel economy of the test vehicle manipulated by the driving robot, an adaptive shift control method based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL) and fuzzy logic of driving robot system is proposed. The driving robot system (DRS) is installed in the cockpit of a vehicle, which does not require any modifications to the vehicle. It includes a physical robot that mechanically operates vehicle and a controller for an automated driving. Firstly, the dynamic model of driving robot system is established. Then, a deep reinforcement learning model of gearshift strategy is established. Aiming at fuel economy, the throttle opening, vehicle speed and vehicle acceleration are used as the observation parameters of DRL model for gearshift strategy. Then, a nonlinear disturbance observer is established to observe the parameters of the DRL model. Furthermore, an adaptive shift controller consisting of gearshift strategy and fuzzy logic is proposed considering the Gaussian pulse secondary impact load model during the shifting process. The continuous control of DRS is realized by the fuzzy logic controller. Finally, the stability proof of the proposed shift control method is conducted. The proposed shift control method is verified through testing in different driving cycle tests. Test results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed shift control method.

title: Bridging the Subpopulation Gap: A New Paradigm for Robust Fault Diagnosis in Rotating Machinery_supp1-3575130.pdf
authors: Ziyue Qiao
abstract: This article introduces a ground breaking approach to rotating machinery fault diagnosis by addressing the critical, yet unexplored challenge of subpopulation shift. We present the first study to consider this in the domain, introducing a novel framework combining label propagation with time–frequency consistency regularization. Motivated by limitations of existing domain adaptation methods, we propose a unique dataset partitioning strategy that models subpopulation structures within fault categories. Our approach leverages a bridging distribution to facilitate knowledge transfer across domains with different subpopulation compositions. Theoretical analysis provides performance guarantees, while experiments on real-world bearing datasets demonstrate superior performance across various transfer learning scenarios. The proposed method consistently outperforms state-of-the-art techniques in multiple adaptation settings. By pioneering subpopulation shift consideration and introducing an innovative dataset preparation method, this work significantly advances rotating machinery fault diagnosis, offering a more reliable solution for complex industrial applications. The proposed framework directly addresses critical industrial challenges by enabling robust fault diagnosis across varying operating conditions, which helps reduce maintenance costs and prevent unexpected equipment failures in manufacturing plants.

title: Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Osteoporosis: A Review of Imaging-Based and Clinical Data Applications
authors: Anni Tang, Haiwen Xu
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning

authors: Yanling Qi, Fanfan Jia, Huichuan Dai
