title: Understanding Academic Risk in Blended Learning: A Machine Learning Approach with Chinese College Students
authors: Zenghe Jiang, Zhanjie Wu, Jixin Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Academic risk remains a pressing issue for higher education, especially under blended learning where students' engagement varies considerably. This study compared the performance of three machine learning (LightGBM, SVM and Logistic Regression) to predict and explain academic risk among first-year undergraduates in China. To enhance interpretability, SHAP analysis was applied to identify the most influential predictors. Results showed that LightGBM outperformed other models, achieving an accuracy of 92.8%. Learning responsibility, regular performance, and short-term learning achievement emerged as the strongest predictors of academic risk. Furthermore, learning engagement played a central role, while attendance was not significantly related to academic risk, and classroom interaction exhibited limited predictive power. The innovation of this study lies in incorporating multi-source process data from blended learning in China, demonstrating the robustness of LightGBM with interpretable SHAP analysis in small-sample contexts, and extending the CLASS framework into a student-centered structure that challenges conventional assumptions about predictors such as attendance and collaboration. Future research should incorporate longitudinal data and a broader range of factors to enhance the stability and applicability of risk prediction.

title: A Student Academic Early Warning Model Integrating Social Behavior Information and Graph Neural Networks
authors: Qinglie Yuan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Rapid advancements in educational informatization have prompted the adoption of Student Academic Early Warning (SAEW) systems, making them an imperative tool for modern, intelligent education management. However, existing models often suffer from several limitations, including reliance on limited data sources, an overemphasis on historical grades, inadequate handling of multi-source heterogeneous data, and a failure to capture meaningful feature interactions all of which restrict predictive accuracy. To address these issues, this study proposed a self-supervised comparative learning framework for academic early warning that integrates social behavior information with graph neural networks (GNNs). The model consists of three key modules: graph construction that integrates grade and social behavior data via GNNs, graph optimization using a virtual node generation strategy to mitigate data imbalance, and selfsupervised node-level contrastive learning to enhance contextual representation. Experiments conducted on a real-world dataset of undergraduate students over six years demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms benchmark methods in terms of AP and F1 scores across four different courses. Ablation studies further confirm that the integration of social behaviors, interestrelated behaviors, and historical grades yields the best performance. The proposed approach enhances early warning accuracy and offers effective support for educational management.

title: Intelligent Warning System for the Elderly Living Alone Based on OpenPose and YOLOV8
authors: Yuyang Fang, Bin Lu, Yusheng Cai, Zhen Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This paper introduces an innovative YOLOv8-based embedded fall warning system, designed specifically for elderly individuals living alone. By incorporating the advanced YOLOv8 model into our original system, we significantly enhance its ability to discern falls accurately, thereby reducing false alarms. This integration not only elevates the system's detection performance from 87.68% to a remarkable 98.28% but also ensures robustness in varied scenarios. Deployed on a Raspberry Pi 4B platform, this system marries high-tech monitoring with the practical needs of in-home care, offering a new level of security and peace of mind for the elderly. Its successful application demonstrates a significant stride in using embedded systems for critical health monitoring, marking a key advancement in the field of elder care technology.

title: Optimization of Sepsis Drug Delivery Based on Continuous State Space Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Zhiyu Ren, Yiwei Liu, Peiyu Fang, Mengting Ni
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Sepsis is one of the leading causes of death centralisedworldwide, and optimization of its treatment strategies is essential to improve patient outcomes. Treating sepsis is extremely challenging because individual patients respond very differently to medical interventions, and there is no universally recognized treatment for sepsis. In this work, we present a novel approach to derive optimal treatment strategies for patients with sepsis through the use of continuous state space models and deep reinforcement learning. By introducing Norepinephrine Equivalent Dose (NEE) as a unified quantitative standard, the model effectively integrates the dose adjustment of multiple vasoactive drugs to achieve precise treatment in complex and variable clinical scenarios. We demonstrate that, on average, the treatments recommended by the model are more valuable and reliable than those recommended by clinicians. In a large validation dataset, we found that patients whose clinician's actual dose matched the AI decision had the lowest mortality. Our model provides personalized and clinically explainable treatment decisions for sepsis to improve patient care

title: Blockchain-Enabled Process Management and Learning Record Trustworthiness Enhancement Mechanism Design for Mock Trials
authors: Jianxun Guo, Meng Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Mock trials have become an essential component of legal education, providing a practice-oriented platform that bridges legal theory with professional competence. Yet, current teaching practices face persistent challenges, including irregular processes, opaque case management, fragmented student records, and weak evaluation mechanisms. These deficiencies hinder fairness, consistency, and the credibility of competency evidence, particularly in cross-institutional or industry-linked contexts. This paper explores blockchain technology as a solution to these governance and credibility gaps. Leveraging immutability, traceability, and distributed consensus, blockchain enables transparent case allocation, standardized role rotation, and full-chain evidence management. Smart contracts automate assignments, evaluations, and incentives, reducing subjectivity while ensuring procedural fairness. A hybrid storage architecture anchors essential metadata-timestamps, hashes, and digital signatures-on-chain, while retaining detailed records off-chain, thus balancing auditability with privacy protection. Beyond process automation, blockchain supports multi-party collaboration by integrating universities, courts, and law firms into a consortium chain, allowing external experts to participate in evaluation and record verification. This ensures that student performance is securely documented, objectively assessed, and recognized across institutions. Case studies from domestic and international pilots further confirm the feasibility and effectiveness of blockchain-based education management. By enhancing transparency, credibility, and interoperability of mock trial records, this framework contributes to the digital transformation of legal education and strengthens the foundation for cultivating competent legal professionals in a data-driven era.

title: Conductivity Gradient Polarity Identification Algorithm for Magneto-Acousto-Electrical Tomography
authors: Guozhang Ai, Zhishen Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Magneto-acousto-electrical tomography (MAET) using linearly frequency-modulated (LFM) or non-linearly frequency-modulated (NLFM) ultrasound stimulation endures the problems of longitudinal resolution decrease and being unable to recognize the conductivity gradient polarity automatically. This work proposes an improved algorithm to solve the above problems by applying the idea of signal segmentation. The algorithm improves the longitudinal resolution of MAET using LFM and NLFM ultrasound stimulation signals, and recognizes the polarity of conductivity gradients automatically and correctly, which paves the way for a better conductivity gradient recognition using LFM or NLFM ultrasound stimulation.

title: Lighting System Design Challenges for Vertical Farming: Part 2. Perspective and Initial Tests of DC-Grid-Based LED Lighting System for Indoor Farming.
authors: Ansis Avotins, Peteris Apse-Apsitis, Martins Marcinkevics
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Lately, the climate is changing, making it more complicated for berry growers to achieve the planned yield. Indoor or Vertical farming is one approach to mitigate this issue, yet the electrical consumption (LED lighting, HVAC, etc) takes 40-60% of running costs, thus this paper introduces the DC-Grid LED lighting system concept that will be tested in two pilot sites growing Frigo strawberry plants. Author’s previous research revealed challenges in spectrum and LED luminaire design, and DC-Grid system implementation gives a reduction of lighting system installed power of $\mathbf{8. 5 \%}$. Also further testing results and light quality is tested in plant growing conditions.

title: Optimizing an Auxiliary Detection and Classification Model for Parkinson's Disease Based on Speech Signals
authors: Minghao Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This study developed and optimized a classification model utilizing speech signal features to assist in the detection of Parkinson's disease (PD) based on machine learning methods. The data preprocessing workflow included feature standardization, chi-square test feature selection, and random oversampling to address class imbalance. Subsequently, a hierarchical 5-fold cross-validation approach combined with a two-stage hyperparameter tuning strategy was employed to systematically train and evaluate three classifiers: logistic regression, support vector machines (SVM), and XGBoost. Experimental results indicate that the tuned XGBoost model demonstrated optimal performance, achieving a cross-validation AUC of 0.9824 and exhibiting robust discriminative capability. This study confirms that systematic hyperparameter optimization significantly enhances model performance, offering a viable solution for non-invasive auxiliary diagnosis of Parkinson's disease. By comparing baseline and traditional methods, the effectiveness of speech features was validated. The proposed technical framework demonstrates clinical application potential in early screening and disease progression monitoring, providing new insights for developing intelligent medical diagnostic systems.

title: Method for Identifying Abnormal Behavior in Aircraft Maintenance Practice Teaching Based on Enhanced Virtual Technology
authors: Mingfei Qu, Linjie Li, Yaping Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Conventional approaches predominantly depend on either manual inspection or basic sensor information to identify irregular practical teaching behaviors, suffering from limitations like inadequate recognition precision and subpar real-time capabilities. Consequently, an innovative technique for detecting anomalies in aircraft maintenance practical instruction, utilizing advanced virtual technology, has been introduced. Using augmented virtual technology, an aircraft maintenance teaching environment is created for standardized abnormal recognition training; a human-computer interaction unit with a virtual ray mechanism enables dynamic responses in maintenance; teaching behavior data, including actions, timing, and intensity, are collected; a 3D action-time-force feature space is built, using fusion algorithms to quantify behaviors into vectors; machine learning models detect anomalies in real-time by inputting these vectors for feedback. The results show that this method has a low false detection rate and a short recognition response time, whether facing the same dataset or different datasets. It has significant advantages in recognition accuracy and efficiency.

title: Digital Resource Integration and Sharing Method of New Media Advertising Course Under Blockchain Horizon
authors: Tingting Chen, Siqi Lv
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: In the traditional digital resource sharing mode, when facing a large number of synchronized online users, there are often problems such as significant system performance degradation, serious resource waste, difficulty in cross platform integration, and low sharing efficiency. These problems seriously restrict the efficient utilization and dissemination of digital resources in new media advertising courses. To address the aforementioned pain points, this study proposes a method for integrating and sharing digital resources of new media advertising courses based on blockchain technology. Specifically, firstly, by systematically collecting and meticulously categorizing various digital resources related to new media advertising courses, a hierarchical and logically clear directory structure is constructed, thereby forming a comprehensive and orderly resource library. Subsequently, relying on the decentralized and tamper proof features of blockchain technology, the digital resources of new media advertising courses will be deeply integrated and effectively consolidated to ensure the authenticity and security of the resources. Finally, the attributes of digital resources are finely divided, and the content information of resources is deeply excavated and identified. A scientific and reasonable mechanism for sharing digital resources is designed, and smart contracts are developed based on the characteristics of new media advertising courses to achieve automation, convenience, and efficiency in resource sharing. The experimental results show that this method not only significantly improves the efficiency and stability of digital resource sharing, effectively solves the problem of data loss in traditional methods, but also performs well in resource integration speed and application effects, providing strong support for the optimization and efficient utilization of digital resources in new media advertising courses.

title: Research on Software Development Quality Evaluation and Risk Prediction Algorithm Based on CMMI5 Model
authors: Zhang Baoliang, He Jian, Hui Chen, Sun Congrui
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Addressing issues such as the lag in traditional software quality evaluation methods, reliance on experience for risk prediction, and underutilization of CMMI5 process data, this paper proposes an integrated software development quality evaluation and risk prediction algorithm system incorporating CMMI5 quantitative management principles. First, a three-dimensional evaluation model integrating “process maturity × quality characteristics × lifecycle stage” is constructed. Quantifiable metrics are extracted from CMMI5's key process areas, and a dynamic scoring algorithm based on sliding windows and exponential weighted moving averages (EWMA) is designed to enable real-time quality status monitoring and trend forecasting. Second, integrating LSTM neural networks with XGBoost models, the LAX ((LSTM-Attention-XGBoost)) hybrid prediction model incorporates attention mechanisms to focus on critical process phases. This model outputs risk probabilities, while SHAP values explain risk factor contributions to guide process improvement priorities. Experiments on 25 CMMI5-certified projects and over 15,000 weekly granularity data points demonstrate a significant negative correlation between quality scores and future defect density (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$r=-0.72, p<$</tex> 0.01). <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{p}<0.01$</tex>). The LAX hybrid model achieved an AUC-ROC of 0.93 in risk prediction, significantly outperforming traditional models. This research effectively bridges the causal chain between CMMI5 process execution and quality outcomes, providing theoretical foundations and practical tools for software organizations to achieve data-driven continuous improvement and proactive risk prevention.

title: Innovative Practice of Artificial Intelligence Teaching Under the Three-Stage Progressive Teaching Mode
authors: Dong Zhao, Xin Chen, Dan Zhang, Xiujuan Ma, Jiawei Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: In response to the problems existing in the course, such as students' “high interest - low action”, teachers' “high demand - weak ability”, and resources' “high standards - few supporting facilities”, we propose a three-stage progressive teaching model: self-study of theoretical knowledge on the MOOC platform before class + application of regional characteristic cases during class + scientific research training at the practical training base after class. Teaching innovation takes “rooting in the west and serving the plateau” as the main line and “Interdlsciplinary and practice-oriented” as the characteristics, significantly enhancing students' ability to apply AI technology to solve practical problems in the region, and building a new model for AI talent cultivation in western universities that can be replicated and promoted.

title: Using a Custom AI Chatbot Platform to Foster Higher-order cognitive thinking in Programming Education
authors: Otemis Madina Begymbetkyzy, Jelena Caiko
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This article explores the potential for developing higher-order cognitive thinking in learners through the strategic teaching of programming languages. It demonstrates that such cognitive growth can be fostered and structured through the use of a custom-designed educational platform integrated with an AI-powered chatbot. This approach enables a gradual and measurable enhancement of cognitive skills while providing real-time assessment of students’ cognitive levels. Traditionally, identifying cognitive development required extensive manual coding and long-term research. However, by leveraging artificial intelligence capabilities alongside Bloom’s Taxonomy, this study introduces a method for automatically assessing learners’ cognitive levels in parallel with improving their programming proficiency. The findings offer instructors actionable insights into student performance and the opportunity to adjust teaching strategies accordingly. The paper also discusses practical applications and advantages of incorporating AI chatbots into the learning process.

title: Research on Generative AI-Empowered Smart Education in Automotive Structure Course
authors: Xiuqin Han, Xiaohui Xing, Hongliang Yang, Yu Jiang, Jianfeng Lyu, Licui Shi
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the rapid development of automotive technologies, the knowledge system of Automotive Structure course is updating quickly, involves large volumes of information, and exhibits significant interdisciplinary characteristics, making traditional classroom teaching insufficient to meet students' learning needs. This paper proposes a smart education model driven by generative AI, integrating AI-generated dynamic knowledge presentation, problem-based reasoning, and exploration of cutting-edge technologies to construct personalized, layered learning paths for students. Combined with the problem-based learning (PBL) approach, platforms such as ChatGPT and DeepSeek are employed to assist students in analyzing structural principles, exploring design solutions, and engaging in inquiry-based learning. Empirical analysis shows that this model significantly improves students' knowledge mastery, engineering problem-solving skills, and innovation abilities, while enhancing learning initiative and immersion. This study provides a systematic framework for reforming Automotive Structure course teaching and offers a replicable paradigm for AI-empowered education in engineering courses.

title: Simulation of PV Side DC Coupled Energy Storage for PV Park
authors: Evaldas Benosenko, Kristaps Vitols, Ansis Avotins
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: this paper explores a novel system topology in which energy storage is integrated into the DC side of existing PV park infrastructure. By connecting a battery to the MPPT (Maximum Power Point Tracking) inputs of conventional string inverters via bidirectional DC-DC converters, the proposed architecture enables grid-compliant energy storage without the need for costly inverter replacement. The paper investigates the proposed topology viability using MATLAB Simulink environment, with focus on creation of testbed for energy management system development.

title: An Optimized Lightweight Deep Neural Architecture for Precise Localization and Classification of Oral Squamous Cell Carcinoma Lesions
authors: Shalini Nehra, Sudershan Singh Negi, Satyam Srivastava, Gaurav Purohit
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Early detection of oral squamous cell carcinoma (OSCC) is crucial, yet current diagnostic methods-primarily visual examination and biopsy-suffer from subjectivity, invasiveness, and delays, particularly in resource-constrained environments. In this study, we propose an integrated imagebased screening pipeline optimized for point-of-care deployment. Raw intraoral images undergo standard preprocessing: resizing to $224 \times 224$ pixels; conversion to a uniform color space; contrast enhancement via ContrastLimited Adaptive Histogram Equalization (CLAHE); and Gaussian smoothing with a $5 \times 5$ kernel to suppress highfrequency noise while preserving structural features These steps standardize illumination, enhance tissue contrast, and stabilize downstream analyses. For classification, handcrafted texture (angular moment, GLCM, LBP) and color features are fused with deep representations from a MobileNetV3-Small backbone and fed into logistic regression and support vector machine classifiers. For lesion localization, a U-Net3+ variant is employed to segment irregularly shaped oral lesions, leveraging multi-scale feature fusion and dense skip connections. The entire framework is implemented using a lightweight Python Tkinter GUI. Extensive evaluation on annotated datasets demonstrates that our system approaches benchmark accuracy levels yet remain fully deployable on desktop and low-power devices, addressing a critical gap in accessible oral cancer screening tools.

title: Design of Digital Technology Application Process and System Implementation Based on the Integration of Science and Education
authors: Qian Xu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: With the continuous development of technology, the application of digital technology is becoming increasingly important in the integration of science and education. This article starts from the design of the application process of digital technology based on the integration of science and education, mainly discussing the importance of data management, intelligent testing, and the sharing of scientific research data, as well as the system implementation. This system enhances the efficiency of education and teaching and the effectiveness of scientific research by analyzing students' learning behaviors, decision-making, and feedback mechanisms. The usability and practicality of the system are analyzed and evaluated. The initial verification of this system to meet the requirements of educational teaching and scientific research effects can also play a positive role in the digital development of education and teaching.

title: Enhanced Partial Discharge Classification Using Ensemble Learning: A Comparative Analysis of Random Forest and XGBoost Approaches
authors: V C Rafeetha, Shashank H Joshi, G M Madhu, C Vyjayanthi
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: This paper presents an effective approach for partial discharge (PD) detection and classification in medium-voltage equipment using ensemble learning techniques. The experimental setup utilizes a High-Frequency Current Transformer (HFCT) in combination with the IEC 60270 standard setup for PD signal acquisition. The study focuses on three different types of PD defects and their combinations, with measurements conducted at various voltage levels (4-14 KV). Statistical features were extracted from the acquired signals, and feature selection was performed using mutual information and random forest techniques. Two ensemble learning algorithms, Random Forest and XGBoost, were implemented and compared for classification performance. The comprehensive evaluation using precision, recall, accuracy and F1-score metrics demonstrates that Random Forest classifier achieved superior performance in PD pattern recognition. This research contributes to the development of reliable PD detection systems for practical industrial applications, with potential for further enhancement through hyper parameter optimization.

title: Graph Attention Network for Industrial Equipment Fault Diagnosis Based on Learnable Mel Filters
authors: Hong Zhang, Bin Wang, Xi Kuang, Yuanyi Hou, Dawei Zhao, Hangyu Zou, Jianwei Zhang, Dekun Hu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Graph Neural Networks (GNN) have become a powerful geometric learning model capable of learning complex relationships between data and have been successfully applied in mechanical fault diagnosis. However, existing GNN-based diagnostic frameworks that rely on physical state variables such as vibration and temperature have a significant drawback: they all inevitably compromise the original structure of the equipment. In this paper, we propose using audio signals as the subject of study. We introduce a learnable Mel filter to extract adaptive Mel features and combine it with a Graph Attention Network to form the LMF-GAT framework for fault detection, which is designed to learn the complex time-frequency features of audio signals. Experiments on fault detection tasks for equipment such as UnloaderValve and PrimaryAirFan demonstrate that LMFGAT achieves good performance and exhibits robustness under imbalanced sample conditions, outperforming several baseline models. LMF-GAT also has an extremely low number of parameters, making it suitable for deploying fault detection networks in real industrial production. It can meet the needs of real-time computation and low-power edge devices.

title: ALOS-2 Long-Term Observation of the Legal Amazon - Monitoring Deforestation, Forest Loss, and Regrowth by L-band SAR
authors: Christian Koyama, Daniel M. Freitas, Edson E. Sano, Masanobu Shimada, Kazufumi Kobayashi, Nara V. Pantoja, Rodrigo A. Souza, Takeo Tadono
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: For nearly a decade, ALOS-2/PALSAR-2 has been monitoring the Amazon basin with a nominal revisit frequency of 42 days, achieving comprehensive coverage using its wide-swath (350 km) dual-polarization ($\mathrm{HH}+\mathrm{HV}$) ScanSAR mode. While the Long-Term Amazon Monitoring (LTAM) data archive provides amplitude data only due to processing and storage constraints, the coherent time-series data enables a range of forest applications with exceptional reliability. This paper highlights recent advancements in deforestation detection with the operational JJ-FAST early warning system, a powerful tool for mitigating tropical deforestation. Rigorous evaluation during the 2024 deforestation season confirmed JJ-FAST’s efficacy, with false alarm rates below 20% for deforestation sites larger than 10 hectares, establishing it as a scientifically validated and broadly applicable solution for reducing largescale biomass loss in the Amazon. Leveraging ALOS-2 timeseries forest/non-forest (TS-FNF) maps, we also track annual forest cover changes in the Brazilian Legal Amazon (BLA) offering improved accuracy compared to traditional optical remote sensing methods. Results indicate a net forest loss of approximately 2% in the BLA from 2018 to 2024. Furthermore, we explore the potential of ALOS-2 and ALOS-4 for monitoring forest regrowth, which is considered as one natural climate solution due to its CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> sequestration capacity. These developments underscore the critical role of $\mathbf{L}$-band synthetic aperture radar (SAR) in supporting sustainable forest management and climate change mitigation in the Amazon rainforest.

title: From Massive Supply to Personalized Services: The Practical Pathway of Generative AI in the National Smart Education Platform for Primary and Secondary Schools
authors: Ruyuan Peng, Jiaxin Deng, Siye He, Jiachao Lu, Yingdong Hu, Xiufang Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: As a key support for basic education public services amid the digital transformation of education, the National Primary and Secondary School Smart Education Platform has established a comprehensive large-scale resource supply system. However, it faces challenges such as resource homogenization, making it difficult to meet the personalized needs of teachers and students. Generative artificial intelligence (GAI), with its technical advantages, offers a practical pathway for transforming the supply of personalized and precise services. Based on user needs theory, this study constructs a ternary interaction model of “User-GAI-Platform” for personalized services, systematically exploring the mechanism and practical pathways through which GAI empowers personalized services on the National Platform. The aim is to provide references for the iterative optimization of the National Platform.

title: A Gated Convolutional Network for Autism Spectrum Disorder Classification and Functional Connectivity Analysis
authors: Hua Zhou, Jianbin Zhong, Liwei Fan, Yan Tang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: To address the critical limitation that conventional CNN-based methods fail to adequately exploit the inherent characteristics of functional connectivity matrices constructed from autism spectrum disorder (ASD) data, this study proposes a novel Gated Functional Connectivity Network (GFCNet) framework. It utilizes functional connectivity features from resting-state functional magnetic resonance imaging (rs-fMRI) data for accurate and interpretable ASD classification. GFCNet integrates gated convolutional modules and transfer learning strategies to effectively extract discriminative features from functional connectivity matrices. Additionally, Grad-CAM visualization is employed to identify key brain regions related to ASD classification decisions. Experiments on the multi-site ABIDE dataset show that our method outperforms mainstream CNN architectures, such as ResNet and DenseNet, achieving 73.5% accuracy and 87.3% sensitivity, surpassing several recent ASD studies. Additionally, the model successfully identifies abnormal functional connectivity in key brain regions, including the medial prefrontal cortex (mPFC), posterior cingulate cortex (PCC), and insular cortex, in ASD patients. Further experiments indicate that the Craddock 200 (CC200) atlas provides the best brain parcellation scheme for ASD diagnosis.

title: Research on Performance Optimization of SFP56 Optical Module with Integrated OTDR Function Based on Gray Code Assisted
authors: Di Ao, Huaqiang Xin, Mengming Xiao, Chengjun Zhu, Zihao Xia
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Aiming at the challenge of realizing highperformance OTDR (Optical Time Domain Reflectometer) function under SFP56 package size, this paper proposes and implements an optimization scheme based on Gray coding. Firstly, the inherent contradiction between the dynamic range and spatial resolution of the traditional monopulse OTDR scheme in the integrated environment is analyzed. Then, the principle of pulse compression to improve the signal-to-noise ratio by using the excellent autocorrelation characteristics of Gray coding is described in detail. The experimental results show that the dynamic range of the traditional single-pulse scheme with 100 ns pulse width is about 10.5 dB, and the spatial resolution is about 10 meters. The optimization scheme using 16-bit Gray coding can significantly increase the dynamic range to 13.2 dB while maintaining the spatial resolution of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0}$</tex> meters. The increase is about 2.7 dB, which effectively solves the performance bottleneck of integrated OTDR and provides an effective path for achieving high-performance online optical fiber monitoring.

title: Chest X-Ray Diagnosis with Joint Report Generation and Lesion Localization Using Multimodal Vision-Language Models
authors: Houxuan Zhang, Kuan Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Lung diseases remain a major global health concern, and chest X-ray imaging is a critical tool for early diagnosis and treatment. To support radiologists, automatic computeraided diagnosis (CAD) systems must detect abnormalities and accurately localize them within the image. However, developing such systems faces key challenges: annotated bounding box labels are scarce, and many models lack clear interpretability and visual grounding to support clinical trust. To address these gaps, we propose a unified multimodal diagnostic framework that combines a DenseNet-121 classifier with Grad-CAM-based pseudo-labeling and a powerful vision-language model (Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct). The DenseNet classifier is trained on pneumonia and pneumothorax cases from the MIMIC-CXR-JPG dataset, with Grad-CAM used to extract pseudo bounding boxes by thresholding activation maps. These pseudo-labels, combined with limited expert annotations, supervise the fine-tuning of Qwen2.5-VL, enabling it to generate structured radiology reports alongside spatial coordinates for pathological findings. Experimental results demonstrate that incorporating pseudo-labeled data significantly boosts localization performance, achieving <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{2 6. 0 9 \%}$</tex> recall at IoU <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\geq} \mathbf{0. 2 5}$</tex> compared to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{4. 3 5 \%}$</tex> using real labels alone. Meanwhile, report generation quality remains strong, with BLEU-4 and ROUGE-L scores comparable to state-of-the-art systems and with faster inference than models like LLaVA. This work illustrates how Grad-CAM-based pseudo-labeling and multimodal vision-language models can enhance interpretability and efficiency, providing a practical pathway toward integrated, explainable AI for chest X-ray diagnosis.

title: High-Efficiency Octa-Band Antenna for LTE/WWAN Unbroken-Metal-Frame Smartwatch Applications
authors: Xiaoxi Zhang, Jia-Qi Zhu, Yongtao Jia, Yi Chen Zhong, Yixiang Fang, Jin Zhang, Ying Liu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A high-efficiency octa-band antenna is proposed for LTE/WWAN unbroken-metal-frame smartwatch applications in this paper. By integrating the planar annulus, the radiation efficiency of the antenna is significantly improved. The principle of radiation efficiency improvement is analyzed based on the theory of characteristic modes. In addition, in order to optimize the reflection coefficient, a matching network composed of three matching circuit paths is designed. Through diode switching, three states are realized, thus achieving octa-band coverage with high efficiency. A prototype has been fabricated and tested with the integration of a wrist model. With the aforementioned planar annulus to improve radiation efficiency and the matching network to optimize the reflection coefficient, the octa-band (LTE700/GSM850/GSM900/DCS/PCS/UMTS/LTE2300/LTE2500) coverage is achieved with high efficiency. Specifically, the total efficiency in the low band is better than 5 % (average efficiency: 13.8%) and the average total efficiency in the middle and high band is 15.85 %. With the full band coverage capability and the high-efficiency characteristic, the proposed antenna is a promising candidate for smartwatch applications.

title: Time-Optimal Speed Planning for Open-Pit Mine Autonomous Driving based on segmented S-Curve
authors: Desheng Zhu, Yunchao Chen, Jun Hao, Qi Zhang, Zhipeng Huang, Kehu Yang
publish: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: To address complex open-pit mining scenarios featuring terrain constraints and dynamic demands, a segmented S-Curve-based time-optimal planning framework is proposed—one that effectively overcomes the limitations of traditional unconstrained planning approaches. The dynamic trajectory segmentation framework adaptively adjusts segmentation points through identifying geometric features, thereby decomposing the overall planning task into manageable subproblems. To tackle the real-time computational challenges posed by high-order asymmetric S-Curve trajectory planning, a Gröbner basis precomputation framework offsets algebraic complexity to the offline phase. To prevent planned trajectories from exceeding speed constraints, a dynamic path point insertion correction method is put forward by relaxing acceleration rates. Through comprehensive hardware-in-the-loop (HIL) simulations and scaled-down physical tests, the proposed method exhibits significant improvements in both efficiency and robustness. Experimental validation across five mining routes reveals an average 18.8% reduction in travel time while fully satisfying all constraints. The planner achieves real-time performance with a computation time of 15 ms or less, and maintains precise path tracking (0.22 m RMS error) under varying load conditions—verifying its practical applicability to mining operations.

title: Observer-based Nonlinear Consensus Control of Virtually Coupled Heterogeneous Trains Incorporating Train-following Interactions_supp1-3670326.pdf
authors: zhongqi li
abstract: To address the control challenges of virtual coupling (VC) for heterogeneous permanent magnetic maglev trains with time-varying delays and uncertain disturbances, an observer-based distributed nonlinear consensus controller is proposed for a third-order nonlinear model. Specifically, a state observer is utilized to estimate the leader’s acceleration information, thereby reducing the communication burden. To compensate for disturbances, a fixed-time-convergence disturbance observer is proposed and theoretically demonstrated. In particular, the train-following interactions are implemented to avoid negative spacing errors as well as unreasonable acceleration and deceleration. Subsequently, the single-train and string stability are analyzed, and an upper bound on the allowed communications delay is provided. Finally, simulations demonstrate that the proposed controller exhibits strong robustness and anti-interference performance, enabling the VC to operate safely, comfortably, and reliably in the presence of communication delays and disturbances.

title: Edge Service-Oriented Game-Theoretic Joint Optimization of UAV Deployment and Hybrid-NOMA Task Offloading in MEC Networks_supp1-3668786.pdf
authors: Ying Chen
abstract: In light of the frequent disaster events worldwide, the risk of communication network disruptions is increasingly prominent. Unmanned Aerial Vehicle (UAV) with flexibility and maneuverability is considered an effective solution for rapid service provisioning. Meanwhile, Non-Orthogonal Multiple Access (NOMA) enables simultaneous access for multiple User Equipments (UEs) on the same frequency band, enhancing spectral efficiency. This paper investigates a system that integrates NOMA with UAV-assisted Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) to provide efficient computational services. First, we construct a system model of UE task offloading and UAV deployment to reduce the overall service cost. Each UE and UAV strive to minimize their own cost. Then, the problem is modeled as the User Task Offloading and UAV Deployment Game (UTUD Game), and it is theoretically proven that at least one Nash equilibrium strategy exists for the offloading and deployment selection. Further, a decentralized algorithm based on game theory named Distributed Iterative Co-optimization for Offloading and Deployment (DICOD) algorithm is proposed to achieve this strategy. The performance of the algorithm is analyzed theoretically. Finally, we conduct experiments to analyze the upper bound of convergence time and evaluate the algorithm's performance in comparison with some other benchmarks.

title: Towards End-to-End Fault Diagnosis: An Adaptive Masked Modeling Framework with Retrieval-Augmentation
authors: Qijun Cheng, Wangpeng He, Zhihui Wen, Ye Tao, Chenxiao Feng
abstract: While fault diagnosis methods based on masked modeling significantly reduce reliance on manual data labeling, they shift the burden to a new manual task: tuning the optimal masking ratio. To alleviate the dependency, this study presents a fault diagnosis method based on adaptive masked modeling and retrieval-augmentation techniques, specifically designed for mechanical equipment fault diagnosis under cross-condition and few-shot scenarios. The core of this work is an adaptive masker, informed by feature distributions, that enables end-to-end mask optimization via the joint learning of intra- and inter-sample attention. Furthermore, a context retrieval module is employed to construct representative contextual samples through nearest-neighbor search. Experiments on two public datasets demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance in the majority of cross-condition and few-shot diagnosis tasks. Additionally, to verify the feasibility and robustness of the model, sensitivity and ablation analyses have been conducted on its key components.

title: Adaptive Federated Learning for Future IoV-Oriented IoT End-to-End Network Planning
authors: Jiaming Pei, Xiaoqing Xu, Lukun Wang, Saba Al-Rubaye, Sun Zhang, Anwer Al-Dulaimi
abstract: In the Internet of Things (IoT) domain, end-to-end (E2E) planning tasks require distributed devices to collaboratively train deep models under highly dynamic environments. However, existing federated learning (FL) methods often assume homogeneous communication conditions and static node reliability, leading to suboptimal aggregation performance when confronted with heterogeneous uncertainty sources such as sensing noise, prediction bias, and communication instability. To address this challenge, we propose FedUAP (Federated Uncertainty-Aware End-to-End Planning), a novel framework that dynamically adjusts client contributions based on multi-source uncertainty and network topology information. Specifically, each IoV vehicle node within the broader IoT system estimates three uncertainty factors—prediction uncertainty, sensing uncertainty, and communication uncertainty—to represent its model reliability and transmission stability. A topology-aware weighting module further refines the aggregation by incorporating node connectivity and link quality. In addition, a temporal smoothing strategy is introduced to stabilize weight evolution over successive communication rounds. Extensive experiments on various E2E IoV-centric IoT planning scenarios demonstrate that FedUAP achieves superior convergence stability, communication efficiency, and planning accuracy compared with existing adaptive aggregation and uncertainty-based FL baselines. The proposed approach provides a promising direction toward uncertainty-robust and topology-adaptive federated optimization in large-scale IoT and IoV networks.

title: Memory-Based TD3 for Autonomous Train Speed Trajectory Optimization Under Virtual Coupling
authors: Min Zhou, Xuan Liu, Hongwei Wang, Hairong Dong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Intelligent Transportation Systems
abstract: Virtual coupling is considered a key technology for increasing line capacity and enhancing recovery capabilities in emergencies. Real-time generation and optimization of train speed trajectory are fundamental to ensuring safe and efficient train operation under virtual coupling. The autonomous train enables autonomous request line resources and makes decisions, allowing for more flexible coupling and reliable and efficient operation. This paper constructs a speed trajectory optimization model for the autonomous train under virtual coupling, which meticulously considers the effects of line resources, such as switches and routes, on virtual coupling. A Twin Delayed Deep Deterministic Policy Gradient (TD3) is utilized to train the agent to optimize the train speed trajectory in real-time. By integrating the Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM), the agent has a longer history memory and learns a better policy. Moreover, two protection mechanisms involving safe following and switch protection are designed to ensure absolute operation safety of the autonomous train and improve training efficiency. Three numerical experiments based on real data from the Beijing-Shanghai High-Speed Railway are conducted. The proposed method can generate a higher quality train speed trajectory within seconds, achieving an average reduction of over 10% in the objective function compared to the commonly used driving strategy and commercial solver. The protection mechanisms always ensure the safety of the trains, even in unknown operating scenarios. Furthermore, the effect of memory and its length have been analyzed by comparing the proposed method with other deep reinforcement learning methods.

title: Power Minimization for Movable Antenna Aided Uplink NOMA System With Group-Level Successive Interference Cancellation
authors: Cheng Hu, Hong Wang
abstract: Movable antenna (MA) is a promising technology that provides additional degrees of freedom by altering antenna positions. In this letter, a transmission scheme is proposed to minimize total transmit power for MA-assisted uplink NOMA system, which accounts for group-level successive interference cancellation (GSIC) and residual interference stemming from the decoded groups. To address the joint optimization problem of MA positions, power control, and equalizers, a dual-loop iteration scheme based on particle swarm optimization (PSO) is developed. In the outer loop, the PSO method is invoked to optimize the MA positions, while the inner loop jointly optimizes the power control and the equalizers, thereby converting the formulated problem into a tractable one. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme significantly reduces the total transmit power of all users and outperforms the traditional fixed-position antenna (FPA) and the orthogonal multiple access (OMA) schemes in terms of power consumption.

title: A Graph Neural Network Approach for Hybrid Node-Edge Fault Diagnosis in Interconnection Networks under the HPMC∗ Model
authors: Xueli Sun, Shuangxiang Kan, Weibei Fan, Jin Qi, Zhenjiang Dong, Jianxi Fan
abstract: Fault diagnosis is crucial for ensuring the reliability of interconnection networks. Traditional diagnostic models usually assume that edges connected to faulty nodes are fault-free, which is unrealistic in practice where both node and edge failures can occur simultaneously. The recently proposed HPMC* diagnostic model provides a more realistic framework by considering both node and edge failures simultaneously, but existing diagnostic approaches under this model have significant limitations in handling complex fault scenarios. This paper proposes HYBRID-GNN, the first graph neural network-based approach for hybrid fault diagnosis under the HPMC* model. HYBRID-GNN employs an edge-enhanced GraphSAGE with comprehensive feature engineering that extracts diagnostic characteristics from HPMC* syndrome data and enables joint training for node and edge fault prediction. HYBRID-GNN learns complex fault patterns from syndrome data, overcoming traditional diagnosability constraints. Experiments on multiple interconnection network topologies show that HYBRID-GNN matches the traditional algorithm in node fault diagnosis (achieving over 99% accuracy within the hybrid diagnosability bound), while delivering substantially higher performance in link fault diagnosis (with accuracy above 97%). Even beyond the diagnosability bound, HYBRID-GNN remains robust, maintaining over 98% node accuracy and over 83% link precision under high fault rates. Furthermore, results on real-world networks further validate its practical effectiveness, achieving over 99% node accuracy and over 95% link accuracy.

title: Safe Multi-Agent Deep Reinforcement Learning for Privacy-Aware Edge-Device Collaborative DNN Inference_supp1-3670497.pdf
authors: zhipeng Cheng
abstract: As Deep Neural Network (DNN) inference becomes increasingly prevalent on edge and mobile platforms, critical challenges emerge in privacy protection, resource constraints, and dynamic model deployment. This paper proposes a privacy-aware collaborative inference framework, in which adaptive model partitioning is performed across edge devices and servers. To jointly optimize inference delay, energy consumption, and privacy cost under dynamic service demands and resource constraints, we formulate the joint problem as a Constrained Markov Decision Process (CMDP) that integrates model deployment, user-server association, model partitioning, and resource allocation. We propose a Hierarchical Constrained Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization with Lagrangian relaxation (HC-MAPPO-L) algorithm, a safe reinforcement learning-based framework that enhances Multi-Agent Proximal Policy Optimization (MAPPO) with adaptive Lagrangian dual updates to enforce long-term delay constraints. To ensure tractability while maintaining coordination, we decompose the CMDP into three hierarchically structured policy layers: an auto-regressive based model deployment policy, a Lagrangian-enhanced user association and model partitioning policy, and an attention-based resource allocation policy. Extensive experimental results demonstrate that HC-MAPPO-L consistently satisfies stringent delay constraints while achieving a superior balance between energy consumption and privacy cost, outperforming representative baseline algorithms across varying problem scales and resource configurations.

title: Exploration Car Based on STM32
authors: Chuixin Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the continuous development of technology, exploration vehicles have increasingly attracted the attention of researchers. However, some of the exploration vehicles studied by researchers still have shortcomings in practical applications, such as lack of lightweight, low cost-effectiveness, and impact on popularization. This study is based on STM32 and designs an exploration vehicle system. In order to improve the practicality and stability of the system, the system integrates video capture, mobile APP control, motor drive, and obstacle avoidance functions. The system uses a DC motor to drive the car. During the movement of the car, the OV2640 camera collects surrounding environmental data and displays it on the phone through WIFI, as well as on the car's OLED. By operating the mobile app, the car can control its forward, backward, left, right, and rotational movements. If the car encounters obstacles during operation, it will automatically avoid them. This system has good research value for hazardous environment exploration, and also has certain reference value for the study of autonomous driving.

title: From Iterative Denoising to Conditional Generation: A Survey on Diffusion Model-Driven Methods for Blind Image Super-Resolution
authors: Yuting Yi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Blind image super-resolution remains a significant challenge in recovering high-quality images from low-resolution inputs with unknown degradations. This paper systematically reviews diffusion models applied to this problem, leveraging their strong generative priors. We categorize methods into three pathways: iterative denoising and posterior sampling approaches (e.g., DDRM, DPS) that condition models during inference; methods utilizing pre-trained diffusion models as universal priors for zero-shot or tuned restoration (e.g., SDUP, DiffBIR); and end-to-end trained conditional diffusion models (e.g., SR3, ResShift). We detail the principles, innovations, and performance of each paradigm and discuss key challenges, including fidelity-perception trade-offs, computational efficiency, and generalization to real-world degradations. This survey provides a structured reference for future diffusion-based blind image restoration research.

title: Multi-Objective Alpha Evolution for Hardware and Software Co-Design of Heterogeneous Multicore Systems
authors: Hai Yi, Shanzhu Xiao, Huamin Tao, Shao Deng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Aiming at the multi-objective optimization problem in hardware and software co-design for heterogeneous multicore systems, a Multi-Objective Alpha Evolution (MOALPHA) algorithm is proposed in this paper. This method uniformly models system architecture design and hardware/software multidirectional partitioning as a multi-objective optimization problem. Processor resources allocation, bus resources allocation, and task assignment are integrated into the same candidate solution through a segmented encoding strategy, the Alpha operator is employed for evolution, and non-dominated sorting and an elite retention mechanism are introduced to optimize the convergence and diversity of the solution set. Experimental results demonstrate that MOALPHA achieves remarkable convergence and diversity in its solution sets, effectively balances exploration and exploitation, and provides an effective approach for early design space exploration of heterogeneous multicore systems.

title: Research on the Influence of Classroom Questioning Teaching Based on Smart Teaching Platform on Students' Learning Input
authors: Xing Wang, Huailong Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Classroom questioning has always been regarded as the core of effective teaching. With the intelligent teaching platform gradually becoming the standard layout of classroom teaching, the platform-based classroom questioning has also become the development trend of classroom teaching. While expanding the benefit of questioning, enriching the means of questioning and optimizing the feedback of questioning, this kind of questioning also has the problems of interrupting teaching coherence, causing cognitive slack and focus shifting. Therefore, based on the intelligent teaching platform of “Superstar Online Class”, a systematic questioning teaching design is carried out for the public course of “Modern Educational Technology”, and the questionnaire survey and interview method are used to explore the influence of this kind of teaching on students' learning input. The study found that the overall level of students' classroom learning input is relatively high, but low-input students still account for a certain proportion, the preference of students' questioning style has a positive impact on learning input, but it is necessary to further improve students' questioning concept, and external factors have become the main factors affecting students' learning input, at the same time, there is no linear relationship between academic performance and learning input.

title: Acceptance and Use of Personalized Learning Functions on International Chinese Smart Education Platforms: A TAM-Based Analysis
authors: Ziyi Feng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: AI-driven personalized learning functions are central to international Chinese smart education platforms, and their success hinges largely on learner acceptance. Grounded in the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM), this study investigates Chinese language learners' acceptance and usage behaviors toward these personalized functions. Data were collected via a questionnaire survey of 114 learners from 17 countries. The findings reveal that: (1) Perceived Ease of Use (PEOU) and Perceived Usefulness (PU) significantly and positively influence learners' Attitudes Toward Using (ATU), which in turn affects their Behavioral Intention (BI); (2) PEOU has a significant positive impact on PU; (3) Learners held positive attitudes, particularly recognizing the functions' usefulness, yet the relatively lower PEOU scores indicate room for improvement in platform interface and usability; (4) Interactive functions like AI pronunciation correction and intelligent speaking partners were the most frequently used (both >83 %), while advanced features like learning data reports were underutilized. This research enriches the application of TAM in international Chinese education, and provides practical insights for improving personalized learning in smart education platforms.

title: Research on Immersive Meta-Universe Interaction Design Based on User Behavior Perception
authors: Liu Mengyu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the rapid development of metaverse platforms, user interactions in virtual environments are becoming increasingly personalized and diverse. Accurately perceiving and responding to user behavior has become a key challenge in enhancing immersion and interaction efficiency. This study proposes a multimodal behavior perception model that integrates body tracking, voice recognition, and eye movement detection. Combined with machine learning algorithms, the model enables real-time recognition of user behavior patterns and adaptive feedback mechanisms. A prototype system was developed using the Unity engine, and empirical user testing demonstrated that behavior-driven interface responses significantly improve immersion, interactivity, and personalization in virtual environments. The study concludes by proposing a scalable design framework for immersive metaverse interactions, offering a reference paradigm for the next generation of multi-sensory intelligent spatial experiences.

title: Research on Financial Data Augmentation and Anomaly Detection Based on Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Yutong Ding
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: A hybrid framework integrating a Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP) and an Autoencoder-based anomaly detection module is proposed for financial time-series data augmentation and anomaly identification. The WGAN-GP component is engineered to generate high-fidelity synthetic market sequences that maintain the distributional complexity and rare-event structures of real financial records. Concurrently, the Autoencoder is optimized to measure reconstruction errors, providing a sensitive metric for distinguishing anomalous behaviors within both real and augmented data. Comprehensive experiments were conducted on the Limit Order Book System: The Efficient Reconstructor (LOBSTER) limit order book dataset to verify the fidelity of data augmentation by using advanced distribution similarity measures such as covariance analysis and maximum mean discrepancy (MMD). The composite anomaly score is an anomaly detection method that uses the reconstruction error of the autoencoder and the adversarial feedback of the discriminator. The results show that the hybrid model improves the F1 score by 12.7% compared with the traditional GAN and discriminant methods, and achieves a significant improvement in Area Under the Curve (AUC). Ablation studies confirm the importance of gradient penalty in stabilizing adversarial training and maintaining stable anomaly scores. The solution provides a scalable, technically rigorous standard for automated risk monitoring and fraud detection in the financial industry.

title: Analysis of the Current Application Status of Artificial Intelligence Based on Citespace in Wind Turbines
authors: Zhuoyu Lei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To clarify the research pattern of the application of artificial intelligence (AI) in the field of wind turbines, relevant literature from China National Knowledge Infrastructure (CNKI) from 2020 to 2025 was used as a sample to conduct keyword co-occurrence and clustering analysis using CiteSpace 6.4.5 tool, visualizing the research hotspots, correlation characteristics, and evolutionary trends in the field. Research has shown that a core research framework of “technical tools application scenarios support system” has been formed in this field, with core hotspots focusing on operation and maintenance aspects such as condition monitoring and fault diagnosis. Digital twin and predictive maintenance have become emerging frontiers in recent years. The research results can provide bibliometric references for the selection of future research directions and technological integration innovation in the field.

title: Acoustic Compensation Algorithm for Spatial Audio in Closed Helmets
authors: Yutu Dong, Yu Zhao, Yin Cao, Yi Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Spatial audio technology is mostly applied to devices such as headphones, with relatively scarce research focusing on closed helmets. During spatial audio experiments on pilots' closed helmets, we found that their enclosed structure induces acoustic distortions, including <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2-5 \text{kHz}$</tex> resonance and high-frequency attenuation above 8 kHz, which damage localization cues like Interaural Time Difference (ITD) and Interaural Level Difference (ILD), leading to inaccurate sound source localization. Traditional compensation algorithms struggle to balance distortion correction and cue preservation, resulting in poor adaptability. To address this, this paper proposes an innovative inverse filtering algorithm integrating “phase-constrained regularization and frequency band adaptive segmentation”. Combined with a self-recorded full-sphere Head-Related Transfer Function (HRTF) dataset, the algorithm achieves the coordinated optimization of distortion correction and localization cue preservation. Comparative tests in a complete test system show that the algorithm reaches a positioning accuracy of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{8 2. 1 \%} (\text{6. 9 - 1 4. 6}$</tex> percentage higher than the traditional algorithms) and a positioning time of 812 ms, with clear front-rear direction distinction and reduced directional confusion. It provides an efficient distortion compensation solution for the engineering application of helmet-based spatial audio systems.

title: Development of a Multi-Camera Vision-Based 3D Inspection System for Smart Manufacturing
authors: Jiaxin Wu, Yuming Liu, Jingfan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To meet the demands for efficient, non-destructive precision measurement in the era of Industry 4.0 smart manufacturing, this study developed a high-precision 3D measurement system based on multiple cameras. It overcomes the limitations of traditional contact-based measurement and addresses the challenges of accuracy and robustness in complex machine vision scenarios. The system adopts a layered modular architecture centered on Hikvision industrial cameras, integrating multiple optimization techniques: Image enhancement is achieved through Zhang Zhengyou calibration combined with pixel constraint calibration and Gaussian filtering; Feature extraction and contour fitting utilize OSTU segmentation and Canny edge detection; matching is performed using the SGBM algorithm (RANSAC outlier rejection) with multi-cost fusion integrating Census and BT methods; 3D models are constructed through triangulation and Poisson reconstruction. Through extensive testing, the system achieves an average measurement error of 0.49 % under optimal calibration conditions. Comparative evaluations across diverse test scenarios demonstrate a best-case measurement error of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{0. 4 2 \%}$</tex> for workpieces.

title: Evaluation of Electropollution in Non-Gaussian Channels Using Non-Free-Space Propagation
authors: M Ravi Sankar, Gavini Sreelatha, Sreedhar Kollem, T. Venkatakrishnamoorthy, D. Maithili, M Dharani
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Electropollution has become a major concern with the increasing growth of wireless communication systems, particularly in non-Gaussian channel conditions. In this work, Middleton Class A and Alpha-Stable noise distributions are incorporated into the high level Friis Free Space and Hata propagation models to assess electromagnetic exposure. With a transmission power ($P_{t}$) of up to 20 dBm, antenna gains ($G_{t}, G_{r}$) of 2.15 dBi, and distances ranging from 10 m to 1000 m, simulations were carried out throughout frequency ranges from 500 MHz to 2000 MHz. The results confirm that high user density, a stronger power supply and use of high-frequency signals will result in higher EPI. At 2000 MHz and 100 users, EPI increases to almost double its value compared to scenarios using a Gaussian channel. According to the Alpha-Stable model, impulsive noise caused many fluctuations in received power, proving that EM exposure in cities is unpredictable. The study results show it is possible to reduce electropollution through better wireless systems and to set appropriate regulatory limits.

title: Ad-hoc pre-tained models for multi-spectral satellite images
authors: Ferdinand Pineda, Victor Andres Ayma Quirita, Cesar Beltran
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: This study addresses the challenge of adapting pretrained models, originally designed for three-band (RGB) imagery, to multispectral data with more than three bands, a mismatch that often leads to suboptimal performance in remote sensing tasks. To overcome this limitation, we propose the development of pretrained multispectral deep learning models tailored to the spectral characteristics of the PeruSat-1 sensor for remote sensing applications. We evaluated several architectures from the ResNet family (ResNet34, ResNet52, ResNet101, ResNet152) and VGG16 using a curated dataset that preserves spatial-spectral priors. Each model was trained and tested on classification tasks involving 2, 3, and 4 land cover classes, using both RGB and multispectral inputs. The results show that the proposed multispectral models generalize well, particularly in low- and medium-complexity scenarios, supporting their suitability for transfer learning. These findings underscore the importance and feasibility of creating pretrained models specifically designed for multispectral imagery, enabling more accurate and efficient environmental monitoring, resource management, and other remote sensing applications, without relying on suboptimal adaptations of RGB-based models.

title: The Study of Roadside Visual Perception Based on Improved YOLOv5 and DSB-SORT
authors: Xiaohui Li, Peixiang Zhang, Yue Hu, Qin Xia
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Visual perception is a crucial technology for roadside perception in Vehicle-Road-Cloud systems. To address the low recognition and tracking accuracy caused by small or occluded targets, this paper proposes a novel image recognition and tracking method based on improved YOLOv5 and a new DSBSORT tracker. First, the accuracy of YOLOv5 is enhanced by incorporating MFE and an advanced loss function, while model complexity is reduced through network pruning. Building on this, a new DSB-SORT algorithm is proposed within the DeepSORT framework, which integrates the feature networks and joint similarity matrices of StrongSORT and Bot-SORT, and replaces Kalman filtering with polynomial fitting. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves higher accuracy and efficiency at high-traffic intersections, showing good application prospects in the Internet of Vehicles.

title: Maximizing Information in Domain-Invariant Representation Improves Transfer Learning
authors: Adrian Shuai Li, Elisa Bertino, Xuan-Hong Dang, Ankush Singla, Yuhai Tu, Mark N. Wegman
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: We propose MaxDIRep, a domain adaptation method that improves the decomposition of data representations into domain-independent and domain-dependent components. Existing methods, such as Domain-Separation Networks (DSN), use a weak orthogonality constraint between these components, which can lead to label-relevant features being partially encoded in the domain-dependent representation (DDRep) rather than the domain-independent representation (DIRep). As a result, information crucial for target-domain classification may be missing from the DIRep. MaxDIRep addresses this issue by applying a Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence constraint to minimize the information content of the DDRep, thereby encouraging the DIRep to retain features that are both domain-invariant and predictive of target labels. Through geometric analysis and an ablation study on synthetic datasets, we show why DSN’s weaker constraint can lead to suboptimal adaptation. Experiments on standard image benchmarks and a network intrusion detection task demonstrate that MaxDIRep achieves strong performance, works with pretrained models, and generalizes to non-image classification tasks.

title: Exploring the Mechanism of Treating Tendon Injury with the Seedling Medicine BAI CI Hua Based on Computer Network Pharmacology
authors: Bin Deng, Zhigang Ju, Chenggang Hu, Angxin Song, Shuai Liu, Zongren Xu, Yingyi Yu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This study used computer network pharmacy technology to construct a multi-layer heterogeneous model for muscle injury treatment. Based on the treatment system of white thorn flower, the relationship between chemical substances and targets is first encoded using a binary network, and then graph embedding learning and random walk algorithms are used to represent such high-dimensional data. Then, using Dijkstra's shortest path and dynamic flow control method, optimize the information transmission path, suppress the transmission delay caused by information overload, and apply Graph Attention Network (GAT) to adjust the transmission effect of important signals;And combined with spectral clustering method to divide functional modules, ensuring the coordination and stability of the entire system. The experimental results show that the system has high robustness and fault tolerance, and can maintain stable diffusion of pharmacological information under multi-target parallel activation conditions, providing mathematical models and theoretical support for multi-channel intervention measures and optimization of chemical substances.

title: Integrating Digital Co-Simulation into Blended Teaching of Robot Control Technology Education: A Curriculum Reform Case with Underwater Vehicles
authors: Mingzhi Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This paper reports on a curriculum reform that integrates MATLAB/Simulink and the UUV Simulator into a blended teaching model for robot control technology education. Taking the RexROV underwater vehicle model as a case study, a co-simulation framework originally developed for research has been adapted into a digital learning environment to support teaching and learning. The reform combines classroom instruction with simulation-based experimentation, enabling students to visualize abstract control theories and consolidate their understanding through hands-on practice. The approach is grounded in technology-enhanced learning design thinking, guiding students to iteratively explore trajectory tracking control strategies such as single-loop and cascaded PID controllers. In addition, the framework introduces gamified learning tasks (e.g., station-keeping challenges, linear tracking, and spiral trajectory missions) that foster motivation, collaboration, and active engagement. Evaluation through student feedback, project reports, and performance analysis indicates that this blended model enhances conceptual mastery, strengthens problem-solving ability, and cultivates critical thinking in controller design. The findings suggest that digital co-simulation platforms can be effectively embedded into curriculum design to increase inclusiveness, adaptability, and engagement, while also bridging the gap between theoretical knowledge and practical engineering skills.

title: An Algorithm for Informative Integration of Online Educational Resources Under Multi-Label Small Sample Learning
authors: Liqiao Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Traditional methods rely on text mining statistical principles to integrate resources, but they have problems such as low integration efficiency and limited quantity. In view of this, a research on online education resource information integration algorithm based on multi label small sample learning is proposed. This study first builds an integrated architecture for online educational resource informatization, providing support for subsequent algorithm design; Further extract semantic features of resources and use deep learning to mine deep semantic information; Then introduce multi label classification mechanism and small sample learning strategy to solve the problem of incomplete resource labels and inaccurate classification; Finally, build a comprehensive database and deeply integrate storage resources. Experiments have shown that after applying this algorithm, the average execution time is lower than traditional integration algorithms, and the integration scale is about 41.28% and 37.54% higher than traditional algorithms, respectively. The advantage of integration effect is obvious.

title: Generative AI Enabling Curriculum-Based Ideological and Political Education Integration Across Primary, Secondary, and Tertiary Levels: Mechanisms, Pathways, and Empirical Analysis
authors: Pingfan Xia, Xin Yang, Jinpei Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: As a transformative technology of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, generative artificial intelligence (AI) offers both technological scaffolding and practical pathways for the integrated construction of curriculum-based ideological and political education (CIPE) across primary, secondary, and tertiary levels. This study delineates the mechanisms and pathways through which generative AI facilitates CIPE integration and empirically examines its effectiveness. The enabling mechanisms are conceptualized along four dimensions: resource integration, learning situation analysis, and teaching integration. Correspondingly, the integration pathways include vertical integration, horizontal integration, technological enhancement, and competence enhancement. Using propensity score matching (PSM) on pooled data from Grade-12 physics students at a High School in China, we find that generative AIempowered CIPE yields a statistically significant and robust positive impact on students' overall academic performance across multiple matching specifications. These findings offer valuable empirical support and practical implications for promoting the integrated development of CIPE within educational system.

title: Near Field Synthetic Aperture Imaging Based on MIMO Millimeter Wave Radar
authors: Liuyang Tian, Penghui Chen, Yuanhao Wang, Yujing Bai, Jun Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: MIMO millimeter wave radar employs a multiple-input multiple-output array structure, which enables the synthesis of a large virtual aperture using a limited number of physical elements, thereby enhancing spatial resolution. However, the transmitting and receiving antennas in MIMO arrays are often non-uniformly distributed, and under near-field conditions, the equivalent phase center approximation becomes invalid. As a result, classical frequency-domain fast imaging methods cannot be directly applied to MIMO millimeter wave radar in such scenarios. This paper analyses the echo signal model of MIMO radar under near-field conditions and, based on this model, proposes a frequency-domain fast imaging method suitable for non-uniform arrays in the near field. Furthermore, a comparison is conducted between the time-domain imaging method and the improved frequency-domain method using measured data, demonstrating the effectiveness of the proposed method for near-field fast imaging with MIMO millimeter wave radar.
