title: Sustainable Poly(lactic acid) Smart Mattress Surface Fabrics with Integrated Capacitive Sensors for Real-Time Sleep Monitoring_supp1-3667922.doc
authors: Hong Hu
abstract: Smart mattress surface fabrics for real-time sleep monitoring have attracted significant interest, yet scalable and eco-friendly fabrication methods remain underdeveloped. In this work, a textile capacitive pressure sensor (TCPS) was seamlessly integrated into poly(lactic acid) (PLA) weft-knitted inlaid fabrics using a single-step intarsia knitting technique, eliminating the need for post-lamination or adhesives. The fabricated TCPS exhibited high sensitivity (1.73–0.09 kPa<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-1</sup> across 0–15 kPa), outperforming many previously reported textile-based capacitive sensors, while maintaining stable sensing performance under varied compressive pressures, compression rates, and repeated washing. The TCPS showed a hysteresis error of 14.02% at 6 kPa and maintained high repeatability over 5000 compression cycles at 15 kPa. Environmental tests indicated that baseline drift was mainly influenced by humidity, whereas the capacitance change ratio (ΔC/C<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">0</sub>) remained relatively stable. In addition, the PLA-based TCPS fabric demonstrated superior comfort properties, including thermal insulation, air permeability, and water vapor permeability, compared to commercial mattress surface fabrics. Furthermore, a 4 × 4 TCPS array was embedded into the PLA fabric and wirelessly interfaced with a data acquisition module to acquire and record capacitance signals in real time for sleep monitoring. As a proof-of-concept demonstration, an offline deep learning model achieved an overall accuracy of 88.2% for classifying five typical sleep behaviors and 96% for identifying ten participants, validated under strict Leave-One-Subject-Out (LOSO) and stratified 70/30 protocols, respectively. This work presents a promising and scalable route to sustainable smart textile systems for personalized sleep monitoring.

title: Contribution of Damp Cellulose Particles to the Breakdown Characteristics of Flowing Transformer Oil
authors: Haiyang Xu, Cheng Pan, Mengyue Yu, Ju Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: The contamination of damp cellulose particles in flowing transformer oil degrades its insulation performance. Classic “bridge” breakdown theory is established under static transformer oil, and its applicability under dynamic oil flow exhibits limitation. In this paper, the bridging process of flowing transformer oil with cellulose particles containing different moisture contents under both AC and DC voltages were observed until the final breakdown, and the breakdown voltage was tested. The results indicated that the increasing cellulose moisture content deteriorated the insulation performance in transformer oil under both AC and DC voltages. At AC voltage, the concentration of cellulose particles with 1% moisture content in the whole oil gap first increased and then decreased with rising flow velocity, while the trend of breakdown voltage was opposite. The oil flow velocity corresponding to extreme point was 0.04 m/s. When the moisture content increased to 4-7%, the variation of particle concentration and breakdown voltage was similar to those at 1% moisture content, but the corresponding flow velocity at the extreme point increased to 0.06 m/s. At DC voltage, with the rising flow velocity, the cellulose bridge width at 1-4% moisture content increased, while the breakdown voltage decreased. When the moisture content increased to 7%, the flow velocity corresponding to the maximum of bridge width and the minimum of breakdown voltage shifted from 0 m/s to 0.02 m/s compared to lower moisture contents. This paper will provide experimental support for the establishment of “bridge” breakdown theory in flowing liquid dielectrics

title: Satellite-Terrestrial Edge Networks for Intelligent Services: A Case Study of AIGC-Assisted Digital Watermarking
authors: Kongyang Chen, Jinteng Lin, Yikai Li, Wenjun Lan, Bing Mi, Shaowei Wang, Tiancai Liang
abstract: To enable smart consumer electronic applications, Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite communication is critical for future 6G communication networks by facilitating an integrated satellite-terrestrial network. In the satellite-to-ground network, LEO satellites serve as edge servers to offer intelligent task computation services to ground users such as consumer devices. Existing research primarily focuses on efficient task scheduling solutions for basic computation services. This study will integrate satellite edge networks with Artificial Intelligence-Generated Content (AIGC) technology to provide tailored AIGC services, such as personalized digital watermarking for consumer electronic applications. Initially, we propose a satellite-to-ground edge network architecture enabling bidirectional communication between visible LEO satellites and ground users. Each LEO satellite incorporates intelligent algorithms to support diverse AIGC-assisted digital watermarking technologies at varying precision levels. Subsequently, considering metrics like satellite visibility, communication stability, and digital watermark quality, we develop an AIGC-assisted digital watermarking model within the edge network framework. We then introduce a reinforcement learning-based task scheduling algorithm to optimize strategies. Experimental results validate the efficiency of our approach in balancing watermark generation time and user energy consumption, to meet ground users’ needs for consumer electronic applications.

title: Well-posed Global 3D Retrieval of Background Aerosol Optical Properties from the DQ-1/ACDL
authors: Linxin Dai, Feiyue Mao, Luhao Zuo, Weiwei Xu, Ge Han, Xiuqing Hu, Jiqiao Liu, Daniel Rosenfeld, Wei Gong
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Aerosols critically influence Earth’s energy balance and climate through direct radiation interactions and indirect cloud condensation nuclei effects. Background aerosols, typically undetectable due to low optical depths, hinder precise radiative forcing quantification. China’s DQ-1 satellite, equipped with the high-spectral-resolution ACDL lidar, enables the retrieval of these faint aerosols, but conventional algorithms struggle with weak signals. In this study, we address these challenges through two primary innovations: (1) the development of a uniquely tailored multi-scale denoising chain for HSRL data, and (2) the identification and subsequent correction of a systematic negative bias that occurs during the retrieval of backscatter data under low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) conditions. These advancements facilitate the first well-posed global 3D retrieval of background aerosol extinction, backscatter coefficients, and lidar ratios from spaceborne lidar, eliminating traditional assumptions. Validation against SAGE III/ISS occultation data confirms strong agreement. Bias correction reduces the absolute mean profile deviations by 7.2% and 26.3% for nighttime and daytime, with MAE values of 6.06×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup> and 7.71×10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-4</sup> for nighttime and daytime, respectively. Global stratospheric background aerosols average a lidar ratio of 45.0 sr, peaking near 30°N and reaching its minimum at 55°S. Tropospheric background aerosols average 36.9 sr, with significantly higher values over land than ocean, driven by seasonal biomass burning, human activities and phytoplankton cycles. Crucially, the algorithm captures the spatio-temporal evolution of highly diluted sulfate aerosols from the 2022 Hunga Tonga-Hunga Ha'apai eruption, demonstrating high sensitivity. This work provides a global 3D dataset to quantify background aerosol radiative forcing and constrain aerosol-cloud interactions in climate models.

title: Hand-Gesture Sensing Using Four Sequential Quadrature Channel (FSQC) Calibration With MIMO-FMCW Radar
authors: Ji-Xun Zhong, Iou-Heng Chen, Ming-Huan Hsu, Fu-Kang Wang, Ju-Yin Shih, Wei-Hsien Huang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a radar-based position-tracking system for contactless hand-gesture recognition using a 60-GHz multiple-input–multiple-output (MIMO) frequency-modulated continuous-wave (FMCW) radar with an L-shaped 2T4R antenna array. To address noise interference and limited spatial resolution, a sequential signal-processing framework is proposed, integrating a four sequential quadrature channel (FSQC) calibration method to mitigate phase errors and an extended virtual element (VE) method to improve angular resolution and reduce sidelobe interference. Simulation results confirm the effectiveness of the FSQC calibration in combination with the VE method for accurate position detection. Experimental results further demonstrate the ability of the system to accurately reconstruct complex trajectory patterns, including digits, geometric shapes, Japanese Hiragana, and multistroke Chinese characters. These findings highlight the potential of the proposed framework for human–machine interaction (HMI) applications requiring robust hand-gesture recognition.

title: Design and Application of a Knowledge Graph based Digitized Teaching Platform for Tax Law Courses Marketing
authors: Wenzhu Liu, Ruiqin Tang
publish: Proceedings of the International Conference on Digital Economy and Information Technology

title: Tripartite Evolutionary Game of AI Technology Diffusion: Dynamic Coordination among AI Firms, Manufacturing Enterprises, and Government
authors: Xuelian Wang, Yun Chen, Muge Zhang

title: Cultivating Civilizational Dialogue: A Digital Humanities Framework for Cross-Civilizational Education and Communication
authors: Yaqi Liang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: In an era where globalization demands intercultural literacy, education systems struggle to integrate diverse cultural narratives, often marginalizing non-Western heritage. Digital humanities (DH) offer a solution by leveraging technology to digitize and disseminate global cultural heritage, but existing initiatives rarely align with classroom needs or address technical barriers for non-Latin scripts. This paper presents an integrated framework that merges cross-civilizational heritage digitization with educational tools, designed to enhance cultural literacy in K-12 and higher education. The framework's three layers—Data, Computational, and Application—address both technical and pedagogical challenges: the Data Layer aggregates multi-modal heritage data (texts, artifacts, maps) using IIIF and CIDOC-CRM extensions, tagged with curriculum alignments for 20+ national education systems; the Computational Layer deploys low-resource NLP pipelines (e.g., simplifying Classical Arabic texts for middle schools) and GANs for artifact restoration, generating education-ready outputs; the Application Layer delivers VR classrooms with guided tours, teacher-friendly lesson generators, and blockchain for ethical content access, validated in 10 schools across 5 countries. Quantitative results show a 35% increase in student engagement with cross-civilizational topics (p<0.01), while Mayan glyph OCR tools enhance classroom literacy in Indigenous scripts. Challenges include scaling to diverse curricula and ensuring equitable access. This research advances global education by making digital heritage actionable for educators, fostering intercultural understanding through technology.

title: Enhancing the Robustness of AI-Generated Voice Detectors Against Data Poisoning Attacks
authors: Ian Miller, Ke Li, Dan Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: The rise of generative AI in fabricating or altering audio poses a significant risk to public confidence in what has traditionally been a reliable medium, amplifying the spread of synthetic content to an unprecedented degree. Safeguarding the integrity of media requires research dedicated to developing dependable methods for differentiating between authentic and AI-generated content. When combined with legislative measures that drive adoption on social platforms, these detection systems could help mitigate this concerning threat. This project focuses on strengthening such detectors against a specific challenge they may encounter in real-world deployment: data poisoning attacks. Our defense mechanism called Feature Inspection and Learning for Threat Examination and Removal (FILTER), is robust against two common types of data poisoning attacks: targeted label flipping attacks. and backdoor attacks.

title: A Novel NPC Control Method Suitable for Multi-Source Powered Mining Trucks
authors: Zhenhua Zhao, Jingbin Bi, Bing Hu, Zhaoyang Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Multi level converters have broad application prospects in multi-source power mining trucks due to their advantages of low harmonic content, good electromagnetic compatibility, energy efficiency, and low cost. In the three-level NPC control strategy, midpoint balance, narrow pulse suppression, and dead zone compensation are key issues that require attention. This article proposes a novel three-level NPC control method that integrates midpoint balance, narrow pulse suppression, and dead zone compensation. From a system perspective, three issues are considered and a novel comprehensive solution is presented. The experimental results of high-power motors have verified the effectiveness of the control strategy.

title: From “Free from Coercion and Inducement” to “Curriculum Development”: A Study on the Value Legitimacy of GAI in Curriculum Reform
authors: Haocong Ding, Guangtao Xu, Bingxu Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: In response to the potential crisis of educational legitimacy brought about by the integration of generative artificial intelligence (GenAI) into curricula, this study, based on the constitutive principle of education—“free from coercion and inducement”—proposes curriculum development principles encompassing three requirements: transparency and explainability, loss-free exit, and humanistic care. Building on this foundation, this study uses the high school physics topic ‘Weightlessness and Overweight’ as a teaching case to demonstrate the practical pathway of GenAI serving as an “intelligent scaffold” to support curriculum development and implementation. This provides a theoretical basis and design exemplar for safeguarding student agency and the legitimacy of the educational process in the era of artificial intelligence.

title: EmotionChat: Emotional Chain of Thought based MLLM for Dialogue Generation
authors: Yu Bai, Sirui Zhao, Min Zhang, Shifeng Liu, Yifei Zhu, Tong Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Emotional dialogue generation aims to equip dialogue systems with the ability to recognize user emotions and generate sentiment-aware responses based on conversational context. Although significant efforts have been made in emotional dialogue generation, existing methods still exhibit limited capacity for emotional reasoning and insufficient alignment across multimodal emotional cues, often resulting in emotionally inadequate responses. To address this, we propose EmotionChat, a novel framework that recognizes multimodal emotional signals and generates emotionally appropriate responses via a fine-tuned multimodal large language model (MLLM). Specifically, emotional features extracted from dialogue videos and audio empower EmotionChat to perceive contextual emotional cues. Afterwards, we introduce cross-modal attention mechanisms and a multimodal emotional chain of thought to further enhance emotional reasoning and response generation. Finally, comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that our proposed EmotionChat achieves improved emotion understanding and produces more emotionally appropriate responses.

title: Hydrological Analysis of the Middle and Lower Paraguay Basin Based on Water Balance and Storage Using Imerg, GLDAS, and Grace Products (2003-2023)
authors: Rossana Villalba, Anabella Ferral, Julian BaézGiven, Jorge Kurita, Matias Bonansea, Juan Carlos Bertoni
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: This study examines the hydrological behavior of the Middle and Lower Paraguay Basin using satellite data and in situ measurements. It analyzes trends in precipitation, evapotranspiration (ET), groundwater storage (GWS), and streamflow over a 20 -year period, emphasizing the basin’s sensitivity to climate variability, particularly to El NiñoSouthern Oscillation (ENSO) events. The use of GRACE data combined with cross-correlation proves effective in revealing the delayed groundwater response. The results highlight the hydrological dynamics and the basin’s sensitivity to interannual climate variability. Evapotranspiration remained relatively stable, while GWS exhibited high variability, reflecting seasonal recharge and depletion patterns. The analysis of monthly mean streamflow from 1931 to 2023 revealed extreme hydrological events-both floods and droughts-correlated with ENSO phases. Notably, 2015 and 2016 were identified as persistently wet years, whereas 2020 and 2021 marked widespread hydrological stress across all variables. Standardized anomaly analyses revealed synchronicity between surface and subsurface hydrological components. The calculation of the water balance and GWS anomalies allowed for spatial identification of vulnerable zones. A classification scheme based on the Standardized Groundwater Index (SGI) was applied to map dry (SGI $\lt-1$), intermediate ($-1 \leqslant S G I \leqslant 1$), and wet ($\mathrm{SGI}\gt1$) zones. Crosscorrelation analysis between the residual water balance ($\mathbf{P}- \mathrm{ET}-\mathrm{Q})$ and $\triangle \mathrm{GWS}$ indicated that groundwater storage responds with a one-month lag relative to surface hydrological fluxes. This finding highlights the buffering capacity of the subsurface and supports the use of GRACE data as an indicator of integrated hydrological response. Overall, the study emphasizes the need for continuous groundwater monitoring and streamflow control of river tributaries, as well as their interaction with climate-driven surface water processes in the region.

title: Bibliometric and Visual Analysis of Artificial Intelligence in Education Based on CiteSpace
authors: Lei Li, Xiangchun He
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The popularization of artificial intelligence is reshaping the educational environment. However, the current research on the application of artificial intelligence in education (AIED) is facing challenges such as the difficulty in systematically grasping the overall knowledge structure, research hotspots, and their dynamic evolution trajectory. There is also a lack of quantitative analysis and visual presentation in the field of AIED research. The study aims to conduct a visual analysis through CiteSpace to present the research landscape in the international AIED field and provide a reference for improving the research system. This study is based on the relevant literatures in the Web of Science core database from 2015 to 2025, and analyzes them in terms of spatio-temporal distribution, high-frequency keywords, research hotspots and research trends. The study has found that the number of publications in the AIED field has shown an upward trend in the past decade. The research hotspots are based on artificial intelligence technology, widely penetrating various educational scenarios. At the same time, they pay attention to the students' learning subjects and also involve the exploration of the adaptability of technology applications, demonstrating a research trend that is technology-driven, multi-scenario, and takes into account both the subjects and application mechanisms. The research trends present the characteristics of technology-driven and multi-field integration. Therefore, AIED research needs to focus on issues such as the absence of educational subjects, balance innovation and risk, and address challenges such as ethics and data privacy.

title: Visual Presentation Method for Digital Practical Teaching Based on Virtual Reality Technology
authors: Yan Zhao, Xucheng Wan, Xinhua Yin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Traditional digital practical teaching relies heavily on two-dimensional images and videos, which have limited visual expression and low student participation. Therefore, a research on visual presentation method for digital practical teaching based on virtual reality technology has been proposed. Firstly, construct visual elements in the virtual reality environment, such as 3D models, dynamic effects, etc., to enhance immersion; Design interactive visual presentation technology to enable students to interact with virtual environments through gestures, voice, and other means; Deeply integrate visual presentation with teaching content to ensure accurate communication of teaching information. The experimental results show that this method exhibits significant advantages in visual clarity, with high resolution, high color accuracy, and high contrast, significantly improving image clarity. At the same time, the accuracy of students' experimental operations has significantly improved, with scores reaching over 90 points. This method helps students better grasp the experimental principles and operational steps, and enhances teaching effectiveness.

title: Palm2Precision: Anemia Detection through Palm Images
authors: R. Girija, Manpreet Kaur, Maneesha Singh, Alok Kumar, Naveen Gill, Aditya Nagar, Tanishk
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Diagnosis of anemia is an important task, and non-invasive methods offer promising alternatives. A study by the World Health Organization shows that 42 percent of children under the age of 6 and 40 percent of pregnant women worldwide are anemic. Iron deficiency affects 33 percent of the world’s population. This study investigates the effectiveness of palm imaging for anemia diagnosis and uses machine-learning models to classify patients as anemic or non-anemic. In this analysis, various models, including AlexNet, VGG16, EfficientNetB0, MobileNet, Random Forest (RF), Gradient Boosting (GB), XGBoost, and Voting Classifier, are employed. With its eightlayer algorithm and effective use of max pooling with ReLU, AlexNet and VGG16 achieve outstanding results, demonstrating the ability of convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to extract and identify features from palm images and demonstrate stability with an accuracy of 77%, indicating its reliability in processing medical data. EfficientNet and MobileNet, known for their combination and computational efficiency, were included in the model together, achieving 60% accuracy. RF (Random Forest), GB (Gradient Boosting), XGBoost, and Voting Classifier demonstrated their power in handling complex data by outperforming all models, achieving an accuracy of 90%. This work demonstrates the potential of machine learning and CNNs to develop an accurate, non-invasive diagnostic tool to diagnose anemia using palm images.

title: Assessment of L-band passive soil moisture products over an arid agricultural region in Southern Mexico
authors: Alejandro Monsiváis-Huertero, Isaίas Barragán-Cruz, Héctor Ernesto Huerta-Báti, Enrique Zempoaltecatl-Ramίrez, José Carlos Jiménez-Escalona, Rodrigo Florencio Da Silva, Victor Manuel Sauce-Rangel, Daniel E. Constantino-Recillas, José Emilio Quiroz-Ibarra, Jorge Ángel González-Ordiano
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Soil moisture (SM) is a key parameter in micrometeorology, hydrology, and agriculture to estimate energy and moisture fluxes at the land surface and the water within the soil available for agricultural purposes. Observations at L-band $(1.2-1.4 \mathrm{GHz})$ are desirable to estimate SM due to their larger penetration depth. Currently, missions such as the NASA SMAP and the ESA SMOS provide periodically global coverage of brightness temperature (TB) information at L-band. Current satellite SM retrievals from TB observations are supplied at different spatial resolutions and their validation using ground measurements is challenging due to differences in the representativeness between satellite scales and site SM values. Because of the capability of satellites to cover extent areas, their SM products have been a valuable tool to monitor atypical drought conditions due to climate change. Satellite SM retrievals have been challenging in agricultural regions due to high variability of SM and phenological changes in vegetation. Particularly, agricultural areas in arid and semi-arid do not have reliable databases to calibrate SM retrieval algorithms. In this work, we conducted a field experiment and created an SM database that can be used. The database includes data from agricultural areas located in the Central Valleys of Oaxaca, Southern Mexico, during 2021 to 2024. In this study, we aim to compare different existing L-band passive satellite SM products with in-situ measurements for 3 years covering different growing seasons in the semi-arid agricultural region. The results show that the products from the SMAP mission provide closer SM estimates to in-situ measurements at 9 compared to SM products from other missions such as SMOS.

title: Unsupervised Identification Method for the Security Situation of Decryptable Traffic and Non-Decryptable Traffic
authors: Peng Zhou, Zichao Xu, Chenmeng Guo, Heng Tang, Xueyan Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In the process of in-depth analysis of decrypted traffic, a large amount of computing resources are consumed and the error is relatively high. To this end, an unsupervised identification method for the security situation of decryptable traffic and non-decryptable traffic is proposed. Combining the variational autoencoder and the generative adversarial network, the VAE-GAN model is proposed. The similarity function based on attributes estimates the similarity degree of security situation information. For decryptable traffic and non-decryptable traffic, combined with the fine-grained evaluation methods of severity, influence degree, node importance and node centrality, the security situation is divided into five levels and intervals, and the unsupervised clustering algorithm is used to identify the security situation. The experimental results show that the proposed method has the smallest exception threshold η obtained in the training stage, uses fewer hidden nodes, has the smallest average relative error value, and the memory occupancy rate under operation is 22.63%.

title: Edge-AI Empowered Self-Organizing Drone Swarms for Urban Underground Infrastructure Monitoring
authors: Hangfei Zhang, Lin Li, Wenan Yi, Dianyi Song, Liming Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Urban underground infrastructure systems, such as sewage networks, utility tunnels, and subway conduits, are critical yet challenging to monitor due to their confined, GPS-denied environments and dynamic structural complexities. Traditional inspection methods are labor-intensive, costly, and often fail to provide real-time insights. This paper proposes a novel theoretical framework for autonomous monitoring using Edge-AI empowered self-organizing drone swarms. By integrating edge computing with distributed artificial intelligence, the framework enables drones to collaboratively sense, process, and act on environmental data in real time without relying on centralized control or external connectivity. The self-organizing swarm leverages adaptive neural networks and decentralized decision-making algorithms to dynamically optimize formation, navigation, and task allocation while overcoming constraints such as limited communication bandwidth, obstacle avoidance, and resource constraints. The theoretical model emphasizes resilience, scalability, and energy efficiency, addressing challenges unique to underground settings through emergent swarm intelligence and lightweight on-device AI inference. This work lays a foundation for next-generation infrastructure monitoring systems, reducing human risk, operational costs, and downtime while enhancing urban resilience through proactive maintenance.

title: Construction and Empirical Research on Online Self-Regulated Learning Model Based on Visualization Technology*
authors: Fan Yang, Yue Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the advancement of information technology, self-directed online learning has become a prominent mode of education. However, it still confronts persistent challenges such as unclear learning goals, inappropriate strategies, and low efficiency. To address these issues, this paper proposes a Visualized Online Self-Regulated Learning (VOSRL) model aimed at enhancing learners' self-regulation and learning outcomes. Grounded in social cognitive theory and the triadic reciprocality framework of “person-behavior-environment,” the model integrates four dimensions: learner attributes, behavioral regulation, learning processes, and environmental support. A learning environment featuring visualization-based prompts, feedback, and recommendations was accordingly designed. An empirical study conducted in a Children's Literature course revealed that learners using the VOSRL model outperformed traditional-platform users in self-regulated learning ability, academic achievement, and behavioral engagement. Participants also reported that visualization significantly elevated their motivation and self- efficacy. The findings demonstrate that visualization technology can effectively support online self-regulated learning and provide both theoretical and practical foundations for designing personalized learning environments.

title: A Framework of Simscape-Based Digital Twin for Control System Design
authors: Shengzhi Du, Chunling Tu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of Internet of things (IoT) and AI-driven technologies, the digital twin (DT) technology is rapidly growing as a foundational technology in modern engineering systems. As a high-fidelity virtual replica of realworld systems, DT differs from normal simulations by remaining continuously synchronized through bidirectional data exchange. By integrating multi-domain models, sensor feedback, and realtime analytics, a DT enables accurate monitoring, simulation, and optimization of physical assets throughout their life-cycle. Despite these advantages, most existing DT research emphasizes diagnostics and predictive maintenance rather than control system design and validation. The integration of DT directly into control loops remains an open challenge due to issues such as model divergence, parameter inconsistency, and time delay. To address these gaps, this paper investigates the construction and synchronization of a DT for a brushed DC motor and explores its potential for DT-based control system design. The proposed framework establishes an interactive link between the physical motor and its virtual counterpart built in Simscape (a simulation environment) via a Raspberry Pi, ensuring parameter and state consistency through shared electro-mechanical properties. By introducing a Proportional-Integral-Derivative (PID) controller within the DT environment, this study demonstrates how control actions can be designed and validated in the virtual domain before deployment in the physical system. Multiple potential solutions are validated. The experimental results reveal that the DT-based PID controller effectively compensates for time delays and enhances control robustness. This work provides a feasible framework towards the implementation of DT-based control architectures, offering insights into next-generation cyberphysical systems that unify modeling, simulation, and real-time control.

title: Design and Implementation of a Configuration-Driven Intelligent Inspection System for Civil Aviation Meteorology
authors: Youwei Chang, Yumeng Zhou, Runnong Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Civil aviation meteorological IT infrastructure demands high availability and security, but traditional manual inspection faces inefficiencies, poor standardization, and security compliance challenges. To address these issues, this study designs and implements a configuration-driven intelligent automated inspection system integrated with a fortress machine security architecture. Built on Python, the system adopts a two-layer YAML configuration model to decouple inspection strategies from the execution engine, enabling dynamic definition of inspection targets, check items, and judgment logic. It supports standardized and customizable in-depth inspections across physical servers, MySQL/Oracle databases, and high-availability proxy services, while ensuring compliance with Level 3 Cybersecurity Classified Protection. Practical application shows the system reduces daily inspection time by over 92%, achieves 100% check item coverage, and maintains a false positive rate of less than 3%. Benchmarking against three state-of-the-art automated O&M systems (Zabbix, Prometheus+Grafana, and Ansible-based custom solutions) demonstrates its superior compliance coverage (100% vs. 75% 88%) and comparable efficiency, with a lower false positive rate than Zabbix and Ansible-based systems. This work provides a secure, scalable, and efficient reference for intelligent O&M transformation in critical information infrastructure.

title: Physical Information Neural Networks for Solving Nonlinear Schrödinger Equations
authors: Du Guifeng
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: A physics-informed neural network (PINN) framework is developed to address the computational challenges of solving the Nonlinear Schrödinger Equation (NLSE) in complex physical environments. The approach integrates the mathematical structure of the NLSE directly into the neural network's architecture and loss function, enabling the model to enforce physical laws while learning from observational or synthetic data. The technique uses automatic differentiation to efficiently compute high-order spatio-temporal derivatives. It also uses a composite loss that combines data fidelity, physical residuals, and boundary constraints to achieve this. Experimental validation on canonical NLSE benchmarks shows that the proposed PINN is able to accurately recover amplitude and phase, preserve conservation laws, and accurately simulate soliton propagation and nonlinear wave interaction. Qualitative analysis confirms the faithful reproduction of dynamic wave phenomena. On the other hand, the quantitative results show that the normalized mean square error is low and the agreement with the analytical solution is high. Ablation and sensitivity analysis show that the framework maintains accuracy and stability across many different network architectures and training strategies. The study shows that embedding physical information into neural networks provides a scalable, flexible and extremely accurate method for NLSE modeling. It also lays the foundation for the computational methods of nonlinear wave problems in the fields of science and engineering.

title: A CT Defect Detection Method for Additive Manufacturing Workpieces Driven by Digital Models and Virtual Simulation
authors: Li Ning, Zhiyu Gao, Haibin Lan, Baixiang Zeng, Linhai Xu, Wei Guan, Xiaolong Chen, Lindan Zheng, Qianni Wang, Bingyang Wang, Changsheng Zhang, Jian Fu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Additive manufacturing (AM) technology has been widely applied in key fields such as aerospace due to its high design freedom and material utilization rate. However, internal defects of parts are difficult to be captured by traditional non-destructive testing methods. Industrial computed tomography (CT) can provide three-dimensional visualization of parts, offering a high-precision means for defect detection. Nevertheless, the scarcity and high acquisition cost of real defect sample data limit the training and application of deep learning. To address this issue, an industrial CT defect detection method for AM workpieces driven by CAD digital models and virtual simulation is proposed. Firstly, typical internal pore defects are parameterizedly introduced into a defect-free CAD model to form a defective digital model. Then, CT projection and reconstruction simulations are carried out to generate a large-scale simulated CT dataset. The generated simulated data can be used not only for comparing defect-free simulations with real CT data to assist manual and traditional algorithm defect identification but also as a training set for deep learning models, improving the accuracy and generalization ability of defect detection. A case study of a 3D resin-printed blade part was conducted to verify the effectiveness of the proposed method. The simulation and real scanning results were compared and analyzed, and the application prospects of the method were discussed, providing a new technical approach for non-destructive testing of AM.

title: Real Time Object Detection using Yolo and Open CV
authors: Sumathi Pawar, K Suma, Geethalaxmi, Vasanth Nayak, K Ankitha, Prashanthi Ks
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Real time object detection is a challenged task in the field of autonomous applications which is part of computer vision and plays vital role in several areas. The research in this area as a regression problem indicates directly predicting bounding boxes in the frames that predicts the class of the images. This results in high-speed processing applications which are suitable for real-time applications. This research is designed to process live video feeds, utilizing a pre-trained YOLO (You Only Look Once) model for detecting objects within frames captured from a webcam. The OpenCV library facilitates video capture, image processing and the rendering of detection results. This implementation demonstrates the effectiveness of combining YOLO’s accuracy and efficiency with OpenCV’s versatile computer vision capabilities, achieving reliable object detection in real-time scenarios. The proposed system also demonstrates applying this system in watching classroom attentiveness with the help of SORT technique. The performance of the system is evaluated based on detection accuracy and frame processing rate, highlighting its potential for practical deployment in various real-time applications.

title: Research on the Dynamic Characteristics of OODA Heterogeneous Networks Based on the TWIA Algorithm
authors: Ruixin Huang, Guangjun Zeng, Kebin Chen, Xue Zheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: The dynamic evolution characteristics of OODA heterogeneous networks in modern warfare directly impact the effectiveness of combat systems. [1] However, the complexity of battlefield environments leads to incomplete data and prominent noise interference, making it difficult for traditional methods to accurately capture the time-varying characteristics of node states and the dynamic interaction patterns of networks. From a system perspective, this paper proposes a dynamic feature analysis method for OODA heterogeneous networks based on a two-stage inference (TWIA) algorithm. Within the OODA heterogeneous network model, state variables are defined across three dimensions: node strike frequency, destructive power, and precision. An automatic force term model for these state variables is established, leading to the derivation of a joint firepower strike dynamics model. The TWIA algorithm is optimized to adapt to the characteristics of OODA heterogeneous networks, enabling dynamic feature analysis of nodes. Research conducted using simulated datasets demonstrates the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Frequency Hopping Signal Generation Algorithm Based on Generative Adversarial Networks
authors: Tianyi Wei, Yong Li, Yingtao Niu, Liping Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In communication countermeasures, adversary reconnaissance aircraft can leverage radio frequency fingerprint identification (RFFI) technology to identify specific communication devices and deploy targeted jamming, thereby compromising the stability of particular communication links or networks. To counter this threat, this paper proposes a frequency-hopping signal generation algorithm based on a Local Feature Preserving Generative Adversarial Network (LFP-GAN). The objective is to generate decoy signals that closely resemble the original frequency-hopping signals while exhibiting distinct radio frequency fingerprint characteristics. This approach aims to deceive the adversary's RFFI models and protect the integrity of our friendly communication systems. Experimental results demonstrate that the signals generated by the proposed algorithm and the original signals maintain a Mean Amplitude Difference (MAD) greater than 0.03 across different Signal-to-Interference Ratios (SIRs), outperforming adversarial sample generation methods based on gradient ascent. Furthermore, when the SIR ranges from -10 dB to 5 dB, the Peak-to-Average Power Ratio (PAPR) of the signals generated by the LFP-GAN algorithm shows a significant deviation from that of the original frequency-hopping signals. This indicates that the proposed LFP-GAN can rapidly generate high-quality decoy signals.

title: Research on personalized learning path optimization of electrical control and PLC courses based on machine learning algorithm
authors: Jinlong Liu
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
abstract: None

title: Hyperspectral Image Classification via Dynamic Adaptive Graph Convolutional Network
authors: Xingwen Luo, Bing Tu, Bo Liu, Yan He, Lianhui Liang, Peng Wang, Jun Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Dynamic Graph Convolutional Network (DGCN) can represent temporal evolutionary features. Its compatibility with the spectral-dimensional characteristics of hyperspectral images (HSI), such as continuous gradients and local abrupt changes in spectral signatures, makes it valuable in this field. This study introduces dynamic graph modeling into HSI analysis. By mapping spectral dimensions onto virtual time series, we propose the Dynamic Adaptive Graph Convolutional Network (DAGCN). The core idea is to use dynamic graph evolution to simulate continuous variations and local abrupt changes in spectral sequences, thereby capturing subtle spectral features of the Earth’s surface. The framework includes three components: The Dynamic Graph Sequence Construction (DGS) strategy uses superpixel segmentation and spatio-temporal graph modeling to construct slice graphs for each spectral band. The Multi-Scale Adaptive Graph Convolution (MAGC) module dynamically generates multi-scale adjacency matrices through adaptive convolutions, learning pixel features within homogeneous regions while preserving multi-scale context. The Spatio-Temporal Graph Collaborative Fusion (STGC) strategy includes a Dynamic Segmentation-based Graph Update (DSG Update) module and a Multi-Spectral Channel Attention (MSCA) mechanism. DSG Update dynamically optimizes the graph structure of adjacent temporal phases using MAGC’s adjacency matrix, extending independent multi-graph learning to spatio-temporally coupled multi-graph learning for modeling spectral-spatial evolution. MSCA assigns self-attention weights to each temporal phase to enhance discriminative band selection. Experiments on public HSI datasets show that DAGCN outperforms state-of-the-art methods in classification accuracy, especially in capturing subtle spectral variations, confirming its effectiveness and superiority.

title: Thermally Stable, Low-Resistance Source/Drain Contacts in a-IGZO TFTs Using an Indium Interlayer
authors: Hoseok Lee, Heetae Kim, Heesu Kim, Chihun Sung, Jeho Na, Bada Kim, Sung Haeng Cho, Byung Jin Cho
abstract: This work presents indium interlayer insertion at the source/drain contacts of amorphous In–Ga–Zn–O (a-IGZO) TFTs to improve thermal stability under high temperatures to up 450°C. The indium interlayer not only effectively suppresses specific contact resistivity (ρc) degradation after a high temperature annealing but also further reduces it. The fabricated a-IGZO TFTs with the indium interlayer as a contact buffer in the source/drain contacts could maintain their electrical properties without any degradation even after post annealing at a temperature of 450°C.

title: A Generalized Framework for Quadratic Noise Modulation Using Non-Gaussian Distributions
authors: Hadi Zayyani, Mohammad Salman, Felipe A. P. De Figueiredo, Rausley A. A. De Souza
abstract: This letter generalizes noise modulation by introducing two voltage biases and employing non-Gaussian noise distributions, such as a mixture of Gaussians and Laplacian, in addition to traditional Gaussian noise. The proposed framework doubles the data rate by enabling discrimination in both the mean and variance of transmitted noise symbols. This novel modulation scheme is referred to as generalized quadratic noise modulation (GQNM). Closed-form expressions for the bit error probability are derived for the generalized Gaussian (GG) and generalized Gaussian mixture of Gaussians (GMoG) cases. Simulation results demonstrate the advantages of the generalized modulation scheme, particularly under non-Gaussian noise assumptions, highlighting its potential for enhanced performance in low-power and secure communication systems.

title: Late Breaking News CFP
authors: N/A

title: Continuous-Flow Data-Rate-Aware CNN Inference on FPGA
authors: Tobias Habermann, Michael Mecik, Zhenyu Wang, César David Vera, Martin Kumm, Mario Garrido
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Artificial Intelligence
abstract: Among hardware accelerators for deep-learning inference, data flow implementations offer low latency and high throughput capabilities. In these architectures, each neuron is mapped to a dedicated hardware unit, making them wellsuited for field-programmable gate array (FPGA) implementation. Previous unrolled implementations mostly focus on fully connected networks because of their simplicity, although it is well known that convolutional neural networks (CNNs) require fewer computations for the same accuracy. When observing the data flow in CNNs, pooling layers and convolutional layers with a stride larger than one, the number of data at their output is reduced with respect to their input. This data reduction strongly affects the data rate in a fully parallel implementation, making hardware units heavily underutilized unless it is handled properly. This work addresses this issue by analyzing the data flow of CNNs and presents a novel approach to designing data-rate-aware, continuous-flow CNN architectures. The proposed approach ensures a high hardware utilization close to 100% by interleaving low data rate signals and sharing hardware units, as well as using the right parallelization to achieve the throughput of a fully parallel implementation. The results show that a significant amount of the arithmetic logic can be saved, which allows implementing complex CNNs like MobileNet on a single FPGA with high throughput.

title: Wireless Aware Energy Efficient Federated Learning over Mobile Devices via Algorithm and Hardware Co-Design
authors: Rui Chen, Qiyu Wan, Xinyue Zhang, Xiaoqi Qin, Yanzhao Hou, Di Wang, Xin Fu, Miao Pan
abstract: Energy efficiency is essential for federated learning (FL) over mobile devices and its potential prosperous applications. Different from existing communication efficient FL research efforts, which regard communication energy consumption as the bottleneck, we have observed that with ever increasing wireless transmission speed (e.g., Wi-Fi 5 or 5G), the energy consumption of wireless communications for model updates in FL is significantly reduced and sometimes is smaller than that of local on-device training. Motivated by such observations, in this paper, we propose a high-speed wireless communications inspired energy efficient federated learning over mobile devices (EEFL), whose goal is to reduce the overall energy consumption (computing + communication). In particular, we design a novel energy-aware adaptive local update policy for mobile devices by jointly considering FL performance and energy saving of high-speed wireless transmissions. Furthermore, given the device’s local update policy in each FL global round, we advance the dynamic voltage and frequency scaling (DVFS) strategy to minimize local training’s energy consumption by keeping GPU and CPU working at appropriate frequencies without triggering thermal throttling. Extensive experimental results with various learning models, datasets, and wireless transmission environments demonstrate the proposed EEFL’s superiority over the peer designs in terms of energy efficiency.

title: Cyclic codes with even length from the ( C 1 + C 2 , C 1 — C 2 ) construction
authors: Daotong Qiu, Jian Gao, Fanghui Ma, Jiafu Mi, Fang-Wei Fu
abstract: Few-weight cyclic codes are widely used in the area of communication and cryptography due to their good algebraic structure. Let C1 be an [n, s1, d1]q cyclic code and C2 be an [n, s2, d2]q negacyclic code. Define (C1 + C2, C1 — C2) = f(C1+C2, C1—C2)lC1 ε C1, C2 ε C2}. Notice that (C1+C2, C1—C2) is a [2n, k ≤ s1 + s2]q cyclic code. In this paper, based on some structural properties of (C1 + C2, C1 ≤ C2), we determine the weight distributions of (C1 + C2, C1 — C2) in some special cases by Gauss sums. Several classes of distance-optimal or almost distance-optimal few-weight cyclic codes with respect to the Griesmer bound are derived. Furthermore, by some puncturing and shortening techniques on the cyclic code (C1 + C2, C1 — C2), we derive six classes of distance-optimal or almost distanceoptimal linear codes. At the end, we construct some explicit distance-optimal or best known few-weight cyclic codes with even length. As an application, we determine the locality of punctured and shortened codes, and obtained several families of distanceoptimal or dimension-optimal locally recoverable codes.

title: Internodal Distance Distributions for Static and Mobile Nodes in 2D/3D Wireless Networks
authors: Nicholas Vaiopoulos, Alexander Vavoulas, Harilaos G. Sandalidis, Konstantinos K. Delibasis, Dimitris Varoutas
abstract: This letter presents a unified analytical framework for deriving exact closed-form internodal distance distributions in both two- and three-dimensional wireless networks, covering all four node-pair combinations (static–static, static–mobile, mobile– static, and mobile–mobile) under uniform and random waypoint spatial models. Unlike existing works that typically address isolated scenarios or rely on numerical integration and simulations, the proposed approach provides a single tractable formulation that accommodates both unequal and equal region radii and captures mobility-induced spatial effects.

title: LGVF: A Low-Bitrate Generative Video Compression Framework with Spatio-Temporal Diffusion Model_supp1-3670748.pdf
authors: Zhi Liu
abstract: Low-bitrate video compression remains a fundamental challenge in video coding. Recent advances in generative technologies show great potential for addressing this problem, yet existing generative models often suffer from uncontrollability caused by error accumulation and unpredictable semantic mutation. To tackle these issues, we propose a novel low-bitrate generative video compression framework based on a spatio-temporal diffusion model. A quality comparator is designed to select the optimal feature during generation, which effectively suppresses error accumulation. In addition, we design a Semantic Mutation Feature Extraction Network (SMFN) to accurately predict frame sequences, thereby effectively handling semantically mutated features. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework achieves superior performance on FVD and LPIPS metrics, significantly reducing bitrate while preserving both visual fidelity and semantic consistency.

title: Minimum Shift Keying System Study: AWGN Baseline, Short-Delay Multipath, and Spectral Behavior
authors: Yi Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Minimum shift keying (MSK) is a continuous-phase binary modulation valued for its near-constant envelope and tight spectral containment, making it attractive for narrowband, battery-powered, and nonlinear radio-frequency (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{R F}$</tex>) links. However, a clean, reproducible baseline that ties the textbook model to measured occupied bandwidth and to performance under short-delay multipath is often missing. This paper builds such a baseline with an end-to-end MATLAB transceiver using the offset quadrature phase-shift keying (OQPSK) plus half-sine equivalence, an additive white Gaussian noise (AWGN) validation against the coherent reference, and a centered Welch estimate of the baseband spectrum. The study reports the two-sided 99% occupied bandwidth and the resulting spectral efficiency, and examines a three-tap short-delay channel with and without a frequency-domain minimum mean-square error (MMSE) equalizer. Under a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 0 ~ k b i t} / \mathrm{s}$</tex> setting with 8 samples/symbol <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{8 0 ~ k H z}$</tex> sampling), the occupied bandwidth is about 58 kHz, yielding a practical spectral efficiency near <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.17 \text{bit} / \mathrm{s} / \text{Hz}$</tex>; at 12 dB signal-to-noise ratio (SNR), the post-equalization constellation exhibits a root-mean-square error vector magnitude (RMS EVM) of roughly 19.6 %. The MMSE equalizer delivers the largest bit-error rate (BER) gains in the mid-SNR region yet leaves a residual floor due to noise enhancement around spectral notches. A modular, code-agnostic experimental design enables straightforward reproduction and adaptation. The baseline clarifies the trade-offs among spectral occupancy, equalization gain, and robustness, and it motivates extensions to Gaussian minimum shift keying (GMSK) shaping, time-varying fading with Doppler, fractionally-spaced decision-feedback equalizer / maximum-likelihood sequence estimation receivers, synchronization loops, and over-the-air software-defined radio validation.

title: The WIM Special Session at AWAP 2025: Empowering Women in Microwaves Across ASEAN
authors: Ngoc Gia Han Vu, Minh Thuy Le

title: Augmented Hybrid Cost Volume Network for Stereo Matching
authors: Chenglin Dai
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: As a classic estimation task, the Stereo Matching typically uses cost volume constructed by the left and right feature maps to estimate disparity. Although a recent study can make great progress, it is still difficult to achieve the goal of high precision due to the insufficient use of scale information, channel information, and the relevant feature map. Therefore, we firstly present the Two-Scale Hybrid Cost Volume Construction Module (THCVCM), which includes the Two-Scale Hybrid Cost Volume (THCV) to fully use the information of two-scale feature maps. And then we design the Residual-Block-Style Edge Aware Channel Attention Module (REACAM), which is the part of THCVCM applied to better utilize the channel-wise news from the low-scale feature maps. Finally, we adopt the Augmented Residual-Block-Style Attention Hourglass Module (ARAHM) to better aggregate the cost volume by introducing relevant feature maps. In conclusion, we build the Augmented Hybrid Cost Volume Network (AHCVNet) for end-to-end Stereo Matching. Compared to some state-of-the-art models, it is better and more competitive.

title: Key Node Identification for Complex Networks Based on Topological Potential
authors: Lidong Fu, Wenyang Xue
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Key node identification is a core problem in complex network analysis, with applications in enhancing network robustness, optimizing information propagation efficiency, and blocking disease transmission. Existing methods face limitations in handling nonlinear relationships and dynamic propagation capabilities in large-scale networks. The PCTP algorithm is proposed for key node identification, integrating topological potential and propagation characteristics. The main innovation is combining node propagation characteristics with impact radius, providing an efficient framework that considers both local and global propagation capabilities and their interrelationships. Experimental results based on SIR model disease propagation simulations show that the PCTP algorithm outperforms seven comparison algorithms in monotonicity, accuracy, propagation capability, and robustness, achieving accurate and efficient key node identification.

title: Trajectories in the Master’s in Spatial Information Applications
authors: María Fernanda García Ferreyra, Gastón González Krieguel, Carlos Marcelo Scavuzzo
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: The Master’s in Applications of Spatial Information (MAIE), jointly offered by CONAE and the National University of Córdoba through the Gulich Institute, represents a pioneering initiative in Latin America for the training of professionals in applied Earth observation. Over 16 years and with more than 100 graduates, the program has built a strong interdisciplinary foundation that integrates space technology with problem-solving approaches relevant to the Global South. This study presents a comprehensive analysis of the program’s evolution, including student demographics, funding mechanisms, and thesis topics. It highlights the program’s unique strengths, such as its alignment with the National Space Plan, personalized research mentorship, and partnerships with institutions like the Italian Space Agency. Thesis research spans diverse themes-ranging from environmental and disasters monitoring, epidemiology to food security and geospatial data science-employing varied satellite data sources. The findings underscore MAIE’s critical role in fostering technological sovereignty, sustainable development, and territorial intelligence, while also identifying areas for improvement in terms of equity and financial sustainability. In doing so, the paper positions MAIE as a key knowledge infrastructure for addressing socio-environmental challenges in Latin America and beyond.

title: Understanding University Students' Expectations for Pedagogical AI Agent's Support in Online English Learning
authors: Zexuan Chen, Chunhui Zhang, Lv Lin, Zixian Luo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study investigates university students' expectations for pedagogical AI agents' support in online English learning contexts. Based on survey responses from 186 students, we analyzed expectations within four key dimensions (Planning support, Process facilitation, Evaluation assistance, and Reflective adaptation), comprising 20 functions. Results reveal strong consensus with Process support as the highest priority. The top five functions emphasize personalization and immediacy: CET −4/6 exam preparation, immediate problem-solving guidance, intelligent note organization, mock test analysis, and language skills training. No significant differences emerged across gender, English proficiency, or AI usage experience, indicating universal preferences transcend demographic boundaries. These findings contribute to pedagogical AI agent research by providing evidence of convergent student needs in Chinese higher education contexts: prioritizing process support while incorporating exam-oriented features in online English learning. Meanwhile, the consensus across diverse user groups suggests universal design approaches rather than demographic-based customization strategies.

title: Research on a Lightweight Manhole Cover Defect Detection Method Based on an Improved YOLOv5 Approach
authors: Bei Xie, Kairu Peng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Damaged, displaced, or missing manhole covers not only compromise the safe operation of urban infrastructure but also pose potential risks to pedestrians and vehicles. Addressing the low efficiency of traditional manual inspections and the high deployment costs of IoT-based detection, this paper proposes a lightweight method for detecting manhole cover defects based on an improved YOLOv5. First, a specialized dataset encompassing five typical manhole cover defects is constructed. Raw samples are acquired via web crawling and undergo pixel-level semantic segmentation using a semi-automated annotation tool. For model design, the K-Means++ algorithm is employed for adaptive anchor clustering to enhance feature matching accuracy. The CSPDarknet53 backbone of YOLOv5 is replaced with the lightweight EfficientNetV2 to reduce computational complexity. The loss function incorporates the Focal-GIoU mechanism to mitigate sample imbalance and strengthen the model’s focus on challenging detection targets. Experimental results demonstrate that the improved model achieves significantly reduced computational requirements compared to the original YOLOv5 on our self-built dataset, while maintaining high mAP and detection speed, validating the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed approach.

title: Welcome to the April Issue of IEEE Microwave Magazine! [From the Editor's Desk]
authors: Robert H. Caverly

title: A Comparative Study of Traditional and Technology-Mediated Classrooms: Impacts on Student Motivation and Engagement
authors: Neil Cedric F. Awitan, Jasmine Nadja J. Pinugu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study explores the impact of classroom architecture on the academic motivation and engagement of college students. Using a cross-sectional predictive design, the research compared traditional classrooms with technology-enhanced learning spaces to assess how architectural and environmental factors—such as spatial arrangement, ergonomics, lighting, and technology integration—impact student outcomes. Results indicated that students in technology-mediated classrooms reported higher levels of motivation and engagement. Key factors contributing to these outcomes included ergonomic comfort, flexible spatial design, and the effective integration of digital tools, as well as improvements in acoustic, lighting and thermal conditions. The findings highlight the critical role of classroom architecture in shaping learning experiences, suggesting that well-designed, technology-supported environments can enhance student success. The study highlights the need for continued exploration into long-term effects and hybrid learning space designs to address the evolving needs of modern educational settings [11].

title: Novel Applications of Blockchain-based Timed Data Release
authors: Jingzhe Wang, Balaji Palanisamy
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: Timed Data Release (TDR) is a practical security primitive that protects data until a predetermined release time. In this paper, we expand the scope of Blockchain-based TDR by comprehensively exploring its potential applications. We propose and design three novel applications leveraging TDR. First, we introduce SR-BV, a single-round, blockchain-based privacypreserving voting protocol that allows voters to go offline once they have submitted their private ballots. Next, we present SR-BA, a single-round sealed-bid auction protocol on the blockchain. In SR-BA, bidders only need to participate in the bid submission phase, after which they can leave. Finally, we develop TV-TDR, a timed-release mechanism that supports progressively relaxed privacy guarantees under differential privacy. Interestingly, TV-TDR lets privacy protection loosen automatically as time advances. We implement and benchmark the three proposed protocols and our results demonstrate their efficiency and practicality. Together, these designs demonstrate that TDR has broad applicability and can be used effectively in real-world scenarios.

title: Design and Implementation of AIS Demodulation Algorithm Based on Digital IF Quadrature Sampling
authors: Weiyan Xia, Haoxun Wang, Ye Xiao, Yishen Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This paper presents an embedded automatic identification system (AIS) demodulation scheme based on a software-defined radio (SDR) architecture using digital intermediate-frequency (IF) quadrature sampling to implement. The proposed design eliminates the need for dedicated AIS demodulation chips and multi-stage analog circuit processing by realizing the complete GMSK demodulation chain—including digital down-conversion, frequency-offset estimation, symbol synchronization, and HDLC decoding—on a single GD32H759IMT6 microcontroller with its built-in ADC. Based on the DC-balance characteristics of AIS modulation, a lowcomplexity frequency offset estimation method is proposed, which shows good robustness and improved computational accuracy in noisy environments. Simulation and hardware results verify reliable real-time AIS reception, enhanced tolerance to frequency offset and channel noise, an approximate 2.6 dB improvement in BER performance, and a total power consumption below 0.4 W. With its stable performance, high programmability, and strong anti-interference ability, the proposed scheme has good engineering application prospects.

title: ICHBC 2025 Copyright Page
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)

title: Automation of cooling of power transformers at industrial enterprises
authors: Oleksandr Rubanenko, Milan Belik, Oleksandr Pidtychenko, Phaneendra Babu Bobba, Olena Rubanenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Research results indicate that nowadays powerful electric motors of coolers of power transformers of the cooling system work around the clock for years. The cost of electricity is increasing. Constant operation of cooling fans in the absence of automated control of their operation leads to excessive (unjustified) consumption of electricity for their power supply. This leads to excessive energy consumption for the operation of the cooling system. An automatic system for controlling electric motors of coolers is proposed, which will significantly reduce costs for cooling transformers. This is achieved by varying the fan speed depending on the radiator oil temperature and other factors. If the temperature is significantly exceeded, a larger number of fans may be turned on automatically.

title: A Deep Knowledge Tracing Model Integrating Multiknowledge Point Forgetting Characteristics and Item Response Theory
authors: Xichen Li, Biao Kong, Dongfeng Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Knowledge Tracing (KT) aims to track changes in a student's knowledge state based on their historical response records. With the rapid development of deep neural networks, they have been widely applied in the field of knowledge tracing. However, most existing deep knowledge tracing models assume that each question tests only a single knowledge component (KC), making them incapable of handling questions that involve multiple KCs. Additionally, these models often neglect the impact of forgetting behaviors during the learning process on knowledge state evolution. Furthermore, deep knowledge tracing models generally exhibit lower interpretability compared to traditional KT approaches. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel deep knowledge tracing model that represents questions using multiple KCs and incorporates two critical forgetting features: the time intervals between successive learning events of the same KC and the frequency of repeated learning of a KC. These forgetting features are integrated into both the prediction and knowledge state update phases of the model to enhance interpretability. In the prediction phase, the model combines Item Response Theory (IRT) to generate forecasts. Experimental results on public datasets demonstrate that the proposed model aligns more closely with real-world learning scenarios and achieves superior prediction accuracy.

title: Design of a Cross-Device Digital Furniture Exhibition System Integrating Lightweight WebGL with Multimodal Interaction
authors: Jiahui Tian
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This paper presents a cross-device digital furniture exhibition system that leverages lightweight WebGL rendering and advanced multimodal interaction to address the challenges of immersive online product visualization. The proposed system implements a unified interaction framework that seamlessly fuses gesture, speech, and gaze inputs through a cross-modal attention fusion network and a temporal synchronization protocol. The core algorithm improves the high-fidelity 3D display supporting crossheterogeneous platforms, and realizes reliable intention inference and real-time, device-independent semantic alignment. This is validated by experiments using multiple client devices and a complete 3D furniture dataset. Quantitative results show that task completion efficiency and error rate are significantly improved, user satisfaction is improved, and cognitive workload is reduced compared with traditional interfaces. The system architecture ensures performance and interaction reliability, and highlights the effectiveness of adaptive multi-modal fusion and scalable WebGL pipeline in digital commerce applications. This research has established a technical foundation for efficient, intuitive, and high-quality digital product display in complex real-world environments.

title: Density-Propagation Clustering and MLS Surface Reconstruction for 3D mmWave Radar Point Clouds
authors: Pengfei Wang, Shicong Ji, Baogang Ding, Yajun Li, Ding Zhang, Huizhe Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Millimeter-wave (mmWave) radar has become a key sensing technology for environmental perception and object detection under adverse weather and illumination conditions. However, radar point clouds are inherently sparse, noisy, and non-uniformly distributed, which makes reliable clustering and surface reconstruction challenging. This paper proposes a density-propagation clustering and surface reconstruction frame-work for mmWave radar point clouds that integrates K-nearest neighbor (KNN)-based local density estimation, KD-Tree acceleration, and Moving Least Squares (MLS) surface fitting. The method propagates cluster labels through local neighborhoods by jointly enforcing distance proximity and kNN-density similarity, enabling robust segmentation in heterogeneous radar data. The proposed framework was validated through both simulation and real-world experiments. In simulation, three synthetic soil mounds were generated to evaluate clustering accuracy and surface reconstruction quality. In real measurements, data were collected using a Texas Instruments AWR2243 four-cascade radar platform, where two soil mounds were reconstructed from top-down radar sensing. Experimental results show that the proposed algorithm achieves accurate and smooth 3D surface reconstruction, with sub-centimeter RMSE in simulation and centimeter-level accuracy in real radar data. Compared with DBSCAN, VDBSCAN, and REDBSCAN, the proposed method demonstrates improved robustness to density variations and measurement noise, while maintaining high computational efficiency, providing a practical solution for radar-based 3D modeling, terrain profiling, and environmental perception in autonomous and industrial applications.

title: Multi-Channel Spatial Representation Learning for FPGA Routability Prediction
authors: Lei Cai, Ning Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate routability prediction at the placement stage is crucial for efficient Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) physical design, because poor routability often leads to costly placement–routing iterations and prolonged design closure. Traditional approaches rely on fast but coarse congestion estimators that lack the capacity to model intricate spatial interactions among logic blocks, routing resources, and net topologies. Recent machine learning-based methods treat placement as an image and apply convolutional neural networks (CNNs) for routability forecasting; however, most of them use shallow networks and limited input representations. To address these limitations, we propose a deep learning framework based on ResNet that predicts continuous routability scores from a multi-channel composite representation. Our input encodes three complementary spatial feature groups, such as RUDY-based congestion map, density heatmaps, and device layout geometry, which jointly represent routing congestion, layout density, and geometric constraints. Leveraging pretrained ResNet weights and adapting the network for regression, the model captures hierarchical spatial patterns critical for accurate routability estimation. Experiments on commercial and open FPGA benchmarks demonstrate strong accuracy and improved generalization and scalability compared with conventional estimators and prior learning-based methods.
