title: Efficient Byzantine-Robust Privacy-Preserving Federated Learning via Dimension Compression
authors: Xian Qin, Xue Yang, Xiaohu Tang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) allows collaborative model training across distributed clients without sharing raw data, thus preserving privacy. However, the system remains vulnerable to privacy leakage from gradient updates and Byzantine attacks from malicious clients. Existing solutions face a critical trade-off among privacy preservation, Byzantine robustness, and computational efficiency. We propose a novel scheme that effectively balances these competing objectives by integrating homomorphic encryption with dimension compression based on the Johnson-Lindenstrauss transformation. Our approach employs a dual-server architecture that enables secure Byzantine defense in the ciphertext domain while dramatically reducing computational overhead through gradient compression. The dimension compression technique preserves the geometric relationships necessary for Byzantine defence while reducing computation complexity from O(dn) to O(kn) cryptographic operations, where k << d. Extensive experiments across diverse datasets demonstrate that our approach maintains model accuracy comparable to non-private FL while effectively defending against Byzantine clients comprising up to 40% of the network. Our approach also demonstrates substantial improvements in computational and communication efficiency. Experimental evaluation shows that the dimension compression technique achieves 25× ~ 35× reduction in computational overhead and 17× reduction in communication overhead compared to our non-compression version. When compared to state-of-the-art methods like ShieldFL [1], our approach demonstrates order-of-magnitude improvements in both computational and communication efficiency while maintaining equivalent privacy guarantees and achieving superior Byzantine robustness comparable to FLTrust [2]. These substantial efficiency enhancements make secure FL practical for deployment in large-scale neural networks with millions of parameters.

title: SG2D-AFnet: Structure-Guided Two-Dimensional Autofocus Network for Microwave Photon SAR
authors: Fulai Wang, Jianlai Chen, Hanwen Yu, Lei Zhang, Mengdao Xing
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Microwave Photon Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) enables large-scene remote sensing observations with centimeter-to even millimeter-level resolution. However, its ultra-high resolution characteristic poses notable bottlenecks for existing autofocus methods. Traditional autofocus methods based on physical or statistical models suffer from insufficient robustness or low efficiency. On the other hand, existing Deep Neural Network (DNN)-based autofocus methods neglect the imaging physical model due to their pure data-driven nature, resulting in suboptimal autofocus performance. To address these issues, this paper proposes a structure-guided two-dimensional (2-D) autofocus network tailored for ultra-high resolution microwave photon SAR. Its core breakthroughs are as follows: i) During the deep network training process, an innovative coupling relationship is established among the range defocus parameters, residual Range Cell Migration (RCM) parameters, and azimuth defocus parameters in the defocused image. By leveraging the analytical mapping relationship among these three, the 2-D (range-azimuth) parameter estimation is simplified to a one-dimensional (1-D) problem that only requires estimating the azimuth defocus parameters, significantly accelerating the stable convergence of the autofocus network. ii) It specifically solves the range defocus problem that existing 2-D autofocus networks commonly fail to consider, effectively improving the range resolution. The processing results of measured data from a Ku-band airborne microwave photon SAR with a resolution of 0.03 meters show that the proposed autofocus network achieves excellent focusing effects in two typical scenarios, i.e., the prominent point target scenario and the uniformly distributed target scenario. It effectively overcomes the limitations of traditional autofocus methods and existing DNN-based autofocus methods.

title: Three-Level Pressure-Based Authentication on Touch Screens_supp1-3663983.docx
authors: Jun Kong
abstract: Since pressure is invisible in nature, it has been applied to enhance the authentication security. However, previous work focused on only two-level pressure detection, which makes it feasible for an attacker to guess a pressure level from a user's pressing behavior in the shoulder surfing attack. To mitigate the above risk, this article extends pressure-based authentication from two levels to three levels. It systematically evaluates its usability and security with 98 young adults in a mid-west university in two user studies. The first study with 67 participants showed that a three-level pressure-based password did not increase the difficulty of memorization, and it was more resistant to the shoulder surfing attack than a two-level pressure-based password (two-level = 79.39% successful attacks versus three-level = 44.71% successful attacks). However, three-level passwords have a higher false negative rate than two-level passwords, which implies that users lack a consistent pressing pattern on the medium level. To address the above issue, we designed an adaptive training process that assists users in forming a consistent pressing pattern. The training process features an automatic detection of consistent pressing patterns, which potentially reduces the training length. The second user study, with 31 participants, indicated that the false negative rate in three-level passwords is decreased significantly after the training from 59.90% to 2.15%, while maintaining a reasonable false acceptance rate of 20%.

title: Technological Development and Application of Meteorological Unmanned Aerial Vehicles: A review
authors: Rui Liu, Shen Xue, Siyu Liu, Hong Yuan, Kaisheng Huang, Dongli Wu, Siyuan Mao
publish: IEEE Geoscience and Remote Sensing Magazine
abstract: To address the pressing need for high-precision and real-time meteorological monitoring, meteorological unmanned aerial vehicle (MUAV) technology has demonstrated significant advantages. However, the existing literature often examines specific technical aspects of MUAVs in isolation, lacking a systematic integration of the entire platform–payload–communication–protection technology chain. There is also insufficient analysis of the cross-disciplinary challenges MUAVs face in extreme environments and complex smart city scenarios. To bridge this research gap, this article presents a systematic review aiming to construct an analytical framework for the complete MUAV technological system, thereby guiding the promotion and synergistic application of related technologies. This review comprehensively examines the characteristics and applications of fixed-wing, rotary-wing, and tethered platforms; outlines the technological progress and integration strategies from traditional meteorological sensors to quantum sensors; analyzes data transmission and resilient communication technologies adapted to complex scenarios; and summarizes key advancements in wind resistance, deicing, and electromagnetic protection. The findings demonstrate that through the ongoing integration of technologies such as multi-UAV collaboration, lightweight sensing, and intelligent networking, MUAVs—leveraging their high resolution, 3D mobility, and strong environmental adaptability—are progressively evolving into a crucial technological platform supporting future smart cities and global refined meteorological monitoring systems.

title: A Wearable Platform for Real-Time Control of a Prosthetic Hand by High-Density EMG_supp2-3670500.mp4
authors: Leonardo Abdala Elias

title: A Distributed Two-Stage Framework for the Co-Scheduling of Power, Heat and Hydrogen Networks with Heterogeneous Frequency Supports
authors: Haizhou Liu, Zhizheng Fan, Yuanshi Zhang, Qinran Hu, Zaijun Wu, Mohammad Shahidehpour
abstract: This paper proposes a distributed two-stage frame-work for the co-scheduling of power, heat and hydrogen networks with primary and enhanced frequency supports. To address the bilinear terms inherent in the Nadir frequency constraint, a distributed two-stage framework in the form of a fully-distributed computational pipeline is proposed. In the first stage, FedOnce, a distributed learning architecture with one-shot communication, is proposed to coordinate the energy sectors to efficiently predict the total frequency reserves. These predictions serve as warm starts to generate accurate linearizations of the bilinear Nadir terms, which are then embedded into the second-stage convex optimization of power, heat and hydrogen networks through the decentralized alternating-direction method of multipliers with pre-keyed encryption. The underlying distributed computational pipeline helps resolve the dilemma between computational effi-ciency and information privacy, through both parallel learning and advanced encryption. Case studies demonstrate its significant improvements in computational efficiency (by 2 orders of mag-nitude), approximation gaps (by 4.2%) and frequency response characteristics.

title: Robust Driver State Assessment on an Embedded Platform Using a Fuzzy Belief Rule-Based Fusion of Eye Aspect Ratio and Voice Commands
authors: Chi-Lun Feng, Tzuu-Hseng S. Li
abstract: This research presents an Adaptive Driver Monitoring and Assistance System (ADMAS) built on a multimodal AI architecture to prevent accidents caused by critical driver states such as drowsiness, fatigue, and distraction. At its core, a Fuzzy Belief Rule-Based (FBRB) inference engine integrates multimodal data from camera and microphone sensors to ensure robust decision-making. The visual stream computes an innovative EAR4 feature derived from facial landmarks, achieving 89.4% recall in machine learning classification tasks. To enhance robustness, the system establishes a personalized adaptive threshold for each driver during an initialization phase by analyzing data from over 300 frames. Concurrently, a speech recognition module enables proactive, system-initiated assistance via a voice interface, allowing the system to deliver vocal prompts and manage functions based on conversational input, thereby minimizing driver distraction. The FBRB engine fuses the EAR-4 feature, head pose estimations, and voice intent to assess the driver's state, subsequently triggering timely drowsiness alerts and autonomous system adjustments. The system, deployed on an NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano for low-latency on-device processing, achieved reliable detection at 25 frames per second (FPS) under dynamic driving conditions, confirming its viability for real-world deployment.

title: A 1.5-2.0 TOPS/W 2T-Pixel CMOS Imager With Programmable In-Pixel Multi-Bit Feature Extraction
authors: Abubakar Abubakar, Bo Wang, Amine Bermak
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems I: Regular Papers
abstract: As the demand for embedding edge intelligence in vision systems continues to grow, future image sensors must become smarter and capable of performing efficient on-chip computation. This paper presents a hardware-friendly computational CMOS image sensor (C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>IS) that performs signed analog-domain multi-bit convolution directly within the pixel array to enable energy-efficient embedded vision. The proposed C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>IS uses a compact two-transistor (2T) pixel—without in-pixel weight memory or capacitors—while maintaining a 43% fill factor. By leveraging programmable column link switches and shared CTIA+SS-ADC channels, the system supports up to eight programmable convolution kernels per frame without requiring intermediate SRAM or DAC for partial-sum storage. Fabricated in a standard 0.18 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m CMOS process, the prototype achieves 1.5-2.0 TOPS/W performance (normalized to 1-bit), corresponding to 14–112 fps at 61.8–82.4 <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>W total chip power. A vision system incorporating the proposed C<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>IS demonstrates validation accuracy of 96.7% for handwritten digit recognition and 94.2% for hand gesture recognition. These results confirm the feasibility of this approach for low-power, high-density feature extraction, making it well suited for next-generation edge AI applications.

title: Amplitude-induced Phase Modulation And Its Application To High-Gain Full-Polarization-Tunable Transmitarray Antenna
authors: Yunhao Jiao, Hongyu Shi, Yujie Li, Xiaoming Chen, Jianjia Yi, Juan Chen, Anxue Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: In this work, an amplitude-induced phase control method is proposed to achieve phase modulation by performing vector synthesis of two components with a tunable amplitude ratio. With a fixed phase difference between the components, continuous phase tuning of the synthesized wave is achieved by adjusting their amplitude ratio. A dual-output element based on a guided-wave structure is designed, enabling both outputs to provide a full 360° phase shift. In this element, tuning the amplitude ratio also controls the phase difference between the two outputs, enabling polarization manipulation of the transmitted wave. A high-gain full-polarization-tunable transmitarray antenna is subsequently designed, fabricated, and measured. Simulated and measured results validate the effectiveness of the proposed phase control method. The transmitarray antenna demonstrates significant advantages in aperture efficiency and polarization tunability. Furthermore, the proposed method requires no active components, avoiding static power consumption and thus making it suitable for power-constrained scenarios such as satellite communications, UAVs, and and Internet of Things (IoT) applications.

title: Wideband High-Gain Polarimetric Metasurface-Integrated Antenna at Higher-Order Modes
authors: Meha Agrawal, Ravi Yadav, Slawomir Koziel, Stanislaw Szczepanski
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: A substrate-integrated waveguide (SIW)-based wideband and high-gain polarimetric metasurface integrated antenna is proposed in this paper for millimeter-wave (MMW) applications. The antenna operates on higher-order modes (TE130/TE310 and TE230/TE320) to achieve wide impedance bandwidth and enhanced gain. The radiation characteristics are shaped using strategically positioned slots with unequal spacing to suppress broadside nulls and control side lobe levels within a single-element structure. Characteristic Mode Analysis (CMA) identifies the modes and their coupling behavior, guiding further bandwidth and gain enhancement through effective mode combination. A dual-polarized metasurface composed of plus-shaped unit cells provides broadband reactive loading and polarization symmetry, improving radiation uniformity without increasing the design complexity. The fabricated prototype achieves a bandwidth of 20.2% operating within the frequency range from 24.9 to 30.5 GHz with isolation of 26 dB, a peak gain of 12.5 dBi, and stable co- and cross-polarized radiation across the band. The proposed dual-polarized antenna–metasurface configuration offers an efficient solution for advanced MMW applications requiring broadband, high-gain, and polarization-stable performance.

title: A Flexible Forwarding Scheme to Improve Latency-Bound Irregular P2P Communication in MPI
authors: Oguz Selvitopi, Nabil Abubaker, Erkin Aydin, Cevdet Aykanat
publish: IEEE Transactions on Parallel and Distributed Systems
abstract: We propose an algorithm to efficiently perform latency-bound communication scenarios that consist of many small messages. In these parallel scenarios, processes typically pass around a lot of small-sized messages of a few KBs of size. Performing communication operations with P2P MPI routines or collective MPI routines (including neighborhood collectives) in such scenarios may not always yield the optimal results and may not resolve the latency bottleneck. To this end, we develop a regular structure called virtual process topology (VPT) on which the messages can be communicated in a structured and controlled manner. Using parameters of this topology, one can tune the rate of aggression in tackling the latency costs. We demonstrate that our communication algorithm is preferable to MPI P2P and collective routines for latency-bound communication and it can easily be adapted only by replacing calls to MPI routines in a parallel application. We show how to adapt existing topology-aware mapping heuristics to address the volume overhead due to communicating messages on the VPT. Moreover, we propose a novel swap-based mapping heuristic to address this overhead by optimizing the maximum volume handled by a process. Experiments on synthetic communication graphs as well as real-world applications such as parallel Canonical Polyadic sparse tensor decomposition and parallel sparse matrix-dense matrix multiplication show that our approach is a powerful way of overcoming the bottlenecks posed by sparse and latency-bound irregular communication.

title: Simulating Muscle-Level Energetic Cost When Humans Walk With a Passive Biarticular Thigh Exosuit_supp1-3668912.pdf
authors: Vahid Firouzi

title: A Comparative Analysis of Deep Learning Approaches for Predicting Drug Target Interaction
authors: Shivani Bhargav, Surbhi Gupta
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Drug-target interaction (DTI) prediction is a critical step in drug discovery and development, with broad applicability across pharmaceutical research. However, experimental validation is costly and time-consuming, making computational prediction methods appealing alternatives. In this study, the performance of three widely used DL approaches i.e., CNNs, DNN, and autoencoders is evaluated in the current work on the benchmark dataset. The dataset used in this study is a publicly available Kaggle dataset on drug–target relations. Additionally, this work demonstrates the performance of all the three DL models. The DNN model achieved the highest performance, with an accuracy of 92.9%, precision of 94%, recall of 93.5%, and an MCC score of 0.86. The results of this study suggest the potential of deep learning to speed the discovery of drugs due to decreased experimental expense and increased predictive accuracy.

title: Dual-Mode Brain-Inspired High-Resolution Crack Detection
authors: Chunhan Wang, Deguang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This paper proposes Spiking Leaky Integrate-and-Fire Networks (Spiking-LIFN), a brain-inspired dual-mode Leaky Integrate-and-Fire (LIF) spiking dynamic designed for high-resolution image detection. The model is built upon a neuromorphic hybrid architecture that integrates lightweight convolutional networks with spiking neural dynamic encoding. Experimental results on the Concrete Crack Images for Classification dataset demonstrate that as the image resolution increases from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$32 \times 32$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$227 \times 227$</tex> pixels, Spiking-LIFN achieves a significant improvement in accuracy ranking, rising from 7th to 1st place. These results validate the breakthrough potential of neuromorphic architectures in co-optimizing efficiency and accuracy.

title: Power Management Strategy for Low-Power LoRa Terminal SoC Based on Energy-Aware Dynamic Voltage Regulation
authors: Jarvis Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the widespread deployment of LoRa IoT terminals in remote and energy-constrained environments, achieving efficient power management to ensure the long-term autonomous operation of SoCs has become an urgent issue. This paper proposes an energy-aware dynamic voltage regulation (EA-DVR) power management strategy. It integrates a two-layer control mechanism: the first layer is a workload state prediction scheduler based on a hidden Markov model (HMM), which anticipates system load fluctuations in advance. The second layer is a reinforcement learning-driven voltage regulation optimizer, which dynamically adjusts the supply voltage in real time according to energy harvesting conditions and task QoS requirements. Additionally, the model designs a hybrid power mode switching strategy, combining sub-threshold operation with a physical layer-MAC layer context-aware task offloading mechanism, achieving an adaptive balance between computational accuracy and energy consumption. Experimental results show that the proposed method can reduce overall energy consumption by 43.6% compared to the baseline DVFS strategy.

title: Study on Mango Disease Classification Based on YOLOv8 and Multidimensional Data Enhancement
authors: Yi Zhang, Bin Zheng, Yinbo Lin, Xiaoming Du
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of insufficient robustness in traditional methods and limited localization capabilities of deep learning models in mango disease classification, this study aims to enhance the performance of mango disease classification and provide technical support for intelligent prevention and control in the industry. An improved scheme based on the YOLOv8 architecture is proposed, constructing a mango disease dataset including three categories: healthy, Alternaria, and Aspergillus, employing mosaic enhancement, geometric transformation, and noise injection to expand data distribution. Combining pretrained weights to build a transfer learning framework, utilizing mosaic enhancement to mitigate over-fitting in small samples, and leveraging transfer learning to improve convergence speed and reduce training energy consumption. The research results show that the Precision-Recall (PR) value for Alternaria is 0.786, for Aspergillus is 0.598, and for health is 0.995. The mean average precision at 0.5 for all classes reaches 0.793. This verifies the synergistic effect of classical computer vision strategies and deep learning frameworks, providing a thought for lightweight deployment of models in the agricultural field. Although the robustness to complex backgrounds needs improvement, it offers crucial support for mango disease prevention and control.

title: The Design and Implementation of an Automatic Aiming System Based on K230
authors: Ming Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The automatic aiming system has extensive applications in the military field, autonomous driving,unmanned aerial vehicle applications and medical and health fields, and the application prospects are very broad. Based on this, an automatic aiming system based on K230 was designed and fabricated. This system is mainly composed of the MSPM0G3507 main control chip, grayscale sensor, K230 vision module, stepper motor and Hall encoder motor to form a closed-loop control system. After the aiming module is activated in the system, the K230 camera first identifies the target area and target point, calculates the error value using the dual-loop PID algorithm, compares it with the coordinates of the target point, and then transmits the data to the main control chip. Subsequently, the main control chip controls the stepper motor to rotate in a directional manner, and at the same time drives the laser on the pan-tilt to aim at the target point. This system can be mounted on the car, using gray sensor, to achieve the function of fixed track tracking and aiming at the target point.After testing, the camera module of this system can accurately identify the target area and target point, achieving the function of aiming at the target point within 2 seconds in a static state, aiming at the target point within 4 seconds in a dynamic tracking state, and continuously locking the target area and drawing target graphics in this state.

title: Adaptive Modeling and Evolutionary Simulation of Network Language Behavior Patterns
authors: Weimiao Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This article focuses on adaptive modeling and evolutionary simulation of network language behavior patterns. With the rapid growth of mobile Internet and social media, the platform generates a large number of user comments with rich emotions every day, which contains rich social emotions and public opinion trends. Internet language is not a static existence, but a continuously evolving and highly complex adaptive system. Artificial intelligence (AI) technology can deeply explore the behavioral patterns of online language, thereby accurately predicting the trend of online public opinion. Based on this, this article combines the pre trained language model BERT (Bidirectional Encoder Representation from Transformers) with Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN) to propose the BERTGCN model. This model utilizes BERT's text semantic representation ability to extract comment sentiment features, while combining GCN to model user interaction relationships, in order to capture the evolutionary patterns of language behavior in the network structure. The model can dynamically predict the development trend of public opinion by analyzing social media text data. The simulation experiment results show that the BERT-GCN model can not only efficiently mine the inherent patterns of network language behavior, but also accurately predict the trend of public opinion.

title: Two-Stage Feature Selection Algorithm Based on an Improved Grey Predictive Evolutionary Algorithm for Depression Detection
authors: Mengyuan Li, Shuhua Mao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Depression has emerged as a high profile mental health issue, particularly among students. This study obtained multisource heterogeneous data from the Kaggle open source platform. During the data preprocessing phase, an effective data dimensionality reduction method was proposed: a two stage feature selection approach combining significance statistics and an Improved Binary Grey Predictive Evolutionary Algorithm (IBGPEA). The proposed method employs a feature hierarchy mechanism in the first stage by integrating statistical concepts with the “jump degree” principle, while the second stage introduces a novel feature selection strategy based on grey predictive evolutionary algorithm to enhance prediction accuracy and accelerate convergence. Based on the selected features, a depression risk prediction model was constructed using TabNet. The model leverages attention mechanisms and sequential decision making processes to effectively capture complex relationships among features. Interpretability analysis reveals that the feature “Have you ever had suicidal thoughts” demonstrates significant contribution to depression risk prediction. Compared with Random Forest, SVM, KNN, Multi Layer Perceptron, and Adaboost, the TabNet model shows remarkable advantages in various performance indicators, enabling more accurate and reliable prediction of depression risk.

title: An xPatch Extension Model with Auxiliary Variable Modeling and Its Application in Power Load Forecasting
authors: Huaxia Li, Shengnan Zhao, Xingong Cheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Power load forecasting is a critical issue in power system dispatching and energy management. To address the prominent multi-scale characteristics of load sequences and the limitations of traditional models in utilizing auxiliary information, this paper proposes AG-xPatch (Auxiliary-Gated xPatch), a three-stream load forecasting model that integrates auxiliary information, based on the xPatch dual-stream forecasting framework. The model performs trend-residual modeling on load sequences and introduces independent auxiliary modeling streams, achieving adaptive fusion of multi-stream information through a gating mechanism. Experimental results on the Irish Commission for Energy Regulation load dataset demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms the original xPatch and multiple benchmark methods across different load scales, thereby validating its effectiveness in improving load forecasting accuracy.

title: Laser Holographic Image Segmentation and Recombination Processing Method Integrating Artificial Intelligence Technology
authors: Zhou Hong, Yang Chunqing
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a laser holographic image processing method integrating artificial intelligence technology, focusing on improving image quality through an optimized segmentation-recombination framework. The core innovation lies in the introduction of the Adaptive Genetic Algorithm (AGA), which dynamically adjusts crossover and mutation probabilities during image segmentation. Furthermore, the Otsu's method (maximum between-class variance method) is employed to determine the optimal threshold for holographic image segmentation. Finally, by combining the scale difference value with the spatial neighborhood edge energy fusion method, the image pixel sequence is extracted, and the positional difference between the processed image and the original image is corrected. The contour points with the maximum gray value are obtained, and the recombination of laser holographic images is realized through pixel information fusion. Experimental results show that compared with traditional methods and deep learning-based techniques, this method achieves better performance in segmentation accuracy (peak signal-to-noise ratio (PSNR) of 65.4 dB) and recombination efficiency (average registration error rate <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<0.15 \%)$</tex>. Computational complexity analysis indicates the core steps have a time complexity of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}(\mathrm{N})$</tex>, and GPU acceleration enables real-time processing (33 frames/s). Dataset validation (500 images covering medical, industrial, and natural scenes) confirms generalizability. These advancements verify the effectiveness of the proposed method in laser holographic imaging applications.

title: Robust Chinese Remainder Theorem for Polynomials with Unbounded Residue Errors
authors: Han Yang, Fuyou Miao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The Chinese Remainder Theorem (CRT) provides a powerful tool for reconstructing a solution from system of congruences. However, it is highly sensitive to residue errors; that is, even small perturbations in residues can lead to large deviations in reconstruction. To overcome this limitation, the Robust Chinese Remainder Theorem (RCRT) was introduced, which achieves approximate recovery with bounded errors by the redundancy of the system of congruences. However, existing RCRT algorithms over polynomial rings rely on complex multiplication and inversion operations, which is time-consuming. Moreover, for scenarios involving unbounded residue errors, existing methods are not well designed to handle such cases effectively. To address these issues, we propose new RCRT algorithms tailored to bounded and unbounded error scenarios, respectively. For bounded case, we accelerates reconstruction by adjusting deviations among residues to enforce consistency, thereby reducing computational overhead. For the unbounded case, based on the bounded algorithm, we further incorporate a graph-based consistency check mechanism. This mechanism increases the likelihood of correctly detecting unbounded errors and thus enhances the overall robustness of the algorithm. Finally, experimental results show efficiency gains under bounded-error conditions and enhanced robustness in mixed error environments.

title: A Resolution-Reconfigurable SAR-ADC for Computing-in-Memory to Break Power Bottleneck
authors: Tianyu Liu, Qingtao Zhang, Chenxu Li, Bo Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: In resource-constrained edge devices, computing-inmemory architectures have garnered significant attention because of advantages such as high integration, energy efficiency, and throughput. As a key module of computing-in-memory readout circuits, the resolution of SAR ADCs often limits overall computational accuracy, thereby affecting the precision of neural network deployment.High-resolution SAR ADCs often come with higher hardware overhead. To overcome this challenge, this paper proposes an 8-bit successive approximation register (SAR) analog-to-digital converter (ADC) for charge-domain CIM architectures. This design incorporates a redundancy and Capacitance splitting technique to effectively suppress noise and offset voltage. It relaxes the requirements for unit capacitance of digital-toanalog converter (DAC) module, reducing hardware overhead. Meanwhile, combined with resolution reconstruction technology, achieve the ADC resolution switching between 6-bit and 8-bit to adapt to diverse edge AI application scenarios such as adaptive sensing and multi-precision neural network inference. Based on analog performance, digital density, and cost trade-offs, this design employs the TSMC 40 nm process realization. The result shows this reconfigurable SAR ADC consumes 33.2% of the power and occupies 22% of the area in 8-bit mode within the CIM architecture.

title: DRH-PBFT: A Hierarchical PBFT Consensus Algorithm Based on Dynamic Reputation and Secure Slection
authors: Yuke Zhang, Jiayi Lin, Jiali Zheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the challenges faced by practical Byzantine fault-tolerant algorithms in adapting to large-scale consortium blockchains under <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">${O}\left({n}^{{2}}\right)$</tex> communication complexity, as well as the limitations of existing static hierarchical architectures—such as inflexibility and heavy communication overhead in flat reputation mechanisms—this paper proposes a hierarchical consensus algorithm (DRH-PBFT) based on dynamic reputation and secure election. This algorithm reconstructs the consensus process through a three-layer mechanism: at the physical layer, it employs adaptive K-medoids clustering to build an efficient hierarchical topology that adapts to network expansion; at the logical layer, it introduces a K-means++ mechanism to dynamically classify nodes into “high, medium, and low” credibility tiers based on historical performance; at the security layer, it combines verifiable random functions (VRFs) to achieve secure, anti-prediction leader election within a “high credibility” node pool for the backbone network. Experiments demonstrate that DRH-PBFT significantly outperforms standard PBFT and similar improved algorithms in throughput, latency, and scalability at the hundred-node scale. It also exhibits self-healing capabilities against malicious attacks, va

title: Data-Efficient Industrial Defect Segmentation with Multiscale Bidirectional Interaction Adapter
authors: Kai Gao, Qinjie Hu, Fei Qi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Detecting defects in Silicon Carbide-Carbon Fiber reinforced (Cf/SiC) composites using Computed Tomography (CT) is challenging due to intrinsic material heterogeneity and limited annotated samples. Vision Transformers (ViTs) model global context well, but their single-scale (isotropic) design lacks the hierarchical feature pyramid essential for dense prediction, which weakens spatial details. Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) reduces overfitting on small datasets, but common PEFT modules fail to compensate for this structural deficiency. To address this, we propose the Multiscale Bidirectional Interaction Adapter (MBI-Adapter). Our framework augments the frozen ViT backbone with a parallel branch to extract multi-scale features, employing a Bidirectional Feature Interaction Module (BFIM) to dynamically fuse global semantic priors with local spatial cues. Furthermore, we introduce a Lightweight MLP Adapter (LMA) to adapt the feed-forward networks with minimal computational overhead. Validated with a feature-aggregating decoder, extensive experiments on industrial composite datasets demonstrate that the MBI-Adapter outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving high-fidelity segmentation comparable to PEFT approaches with significantly fewer parameters.

title: Research on High-Precision 3D Positioning and Map Construction Technology of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles in GIS Environment of Substations
authors: Haoze Zhuo, Ke Du, Shenchao Jiang, Tailin Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: With the support of the GIS environment, the system integrates visual inertial navigation and depth feature extraction technologies to achieve high-precision three-dimensional positioning and map construction of the substation scene by unmanned aerial vehicles. In the working environment with complex metal structures and significant electromagnetic interference, the system adopts a multi-source information fusion method, effectively enhancing the accuracy and operational stability of three-dimensional positioning. Deep semantic segmentation is adopted to extract the structure of key facilities, ensuring that the core elements of map reconstruction have good recognition. The 3D mapping results are accurately aligned to the GIS coordinate system to achieve unified management and precise measurement of spatial information.

title:
Image Restoration by Reweighted ℓ
                    <sub>1</sub>
                    /ℓ
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    -Minimization Under Analysis Model
authors: Junhui Wang, Biaotong Kuang, Tao Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel reweighted $\ell_{1} / \ell_{2}$ minimization model under the analysis framework for image restoration. We employ a specific variable splitting scheme to solve this minimization model. In addition, we adopt the linearized ADMM combined with tight frame property to avoid matrix inversions or nested loops when solving the subproblem. Numerous experimental results demonstrate the superiority of our methods in image restoration.

title: A Robust OFDM Synchronization Scheme Based on Locally Normalized CP Timing and Two-Stage Frequency-Offset Correction
authors: Xiaodong Geng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This paper proposes a robust three-stage OFDM synchronization algorithm to address the challenges of high Doppler shift and strong adjacent channel interference (ACI). The algorithm first employs a Non-Data-Aided (NDA) symbol timing (STO) estimation algorithm based on locally normalized CP correlation and adaptive threshold peak searching. Subsequently, a hybrid integer frequency offset (IFO) estimation algorithm combines a robust noncoherent search (to lock onto a candidate window) with a high-precision coherent search within that window. Finally, the fractional frequency offset (FFO) estimation uses the CP phase as an anchor for coarse estimation and accumulates residual phase across multiple symbols for fine correction, thereby reducing the estimation variance. Simulations based on a 5 G NR TDL-C channel with approximately 300 Hz Doppler and carrier frequency offsets covering up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{\pm 1 3}$</tex> ppm demonstrate that the proposed scheme performs stably across all three metrics: symbol timing, integer frequency offset, and fractional frequency offset. The scheme maintains high pass rates and good implementation feasibility even under severe Doppler, strong ACI, and low SNR scenarios.

title: Strategic Incentivization for Locally Differentially Private Federated Learning
authors: Yashwant Krishna Pagoti, Arunesh Sinha, Shamik Sural
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: In Federated Learning (FL), multiple clients jointly train a machine learning model by sharing gradient information, instead of raw data, with a server over multiple rounds. To address the possibility of information leakage in spite of sharing only the gradients, Local Differential Privacy (LDP) is often used. In LDP, clients add a selective amount of noise to the gradients before sending the same to the server. Although such noise addition protects the privacy of clients, it leads to a degradation in global model accuracy. In this paper, we model this privacy-accuracy trade-off as a game, where the sever incentivizes the clients to add a lower degree of noise for achieving higher accuracy, while the clients attempt to preserve their privacy at the cost of a potential loss in accuracy. A token based incentivization mechanism is introduced in which the quantum of tokens credited to a client in an FL round is a function of the degree of perturbation of its gradients. The client can later access a newly updated global model only after acquiring enough tokens, which are to be deducted from its balance. We identify the players, their actions and payoff, and perform a strategic analysis of the game. Extensive experiments were carried out to study the impact of different parameters.

title: Cerebrovascular Disease Classification with LSTM Networks: A Deep Learning Framework for Discriminating Time-Series Signals
authors: Suryansh Malhotra, Nikita, Jyoti Yadav
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: This paper presents a Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network approach for identifying Cerebrovascular Disease (CVD) utilizing photoplethysmography (PPG) signals. LSTM networks are particularly suitable for efficiently capturing temporal patterns and dependencies in sequential data, making them effective for biomedical signal processing. The curated dataset, which includes patient attributes and time-series features, undergoes preprocessing including data normalization and slicing. The LSTM model differentiates between patients with and without cerebrovascular diseases. Performance metrics including accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, sensitivity, and specificity evaluate model performance. Results demonstrate that the LSTM model achieves high accuracy (99.57% training, 93.64% testing) and superior generalization compared to traditional machine learning methods. This suggests the LSTM model is promising for clinical decision support systems, enabling automatic, reliable, and early diagnosis of cerebrovascular diseases to assist practitioners in providing timely treatment and improving patient outcomes while ensuring resource-efficient healthcare delivery.

title: Flexibility as an Enabler for Value Creation: A Norwegian Perspective
authors: Hanne Sæle, Bjørn Harald Bakken, Kari Dalen
abstract: The Ongoing Green Transition is arguably the largest change in the European power system for several decades. Traditional dispatchable generation sources like coal and nuclear power plants are being replaced by massive amounts of wind and solar generation with less predictability and no inertia. These renewable sources give larger and faster changes in flow patterns and balancing, and the power system operation is moving from a “generation must follow load” to “load must follow generation” paradigm. In addition to this fundamental change in the physical properties of the power system, common European rules and regulations for markets and system operation are being implemented, including flow-based market clearing, 15-min time resolution in day-ahead and intraday markets, and increased cross-border trading of both energy and reserves.

title: Design of Embedded Voice Controlled Garbage Disposal Device for Smart Environmental Sanitation
authors: Zhangke Chen, Jiaxin Chen, Junnan Tian, Yuncai Yan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To enhance the intelligent level of urban waste classification, this paper proposes and implements an embedded voice-controlled garbage disposal device dedicated to smart sanitation. The system adopts an STM32 microcontroller as the core processing platform, integrating a voice recognition module, servo drive mechanism, infrared detection components, OLED display unit, WiFi communication module, and MQ-2 smoke sensor. It realizes functions including voice-command triggered lid opening and closing, monitoring of disposal status, real-time display of classification information, and remote alarm notification. In this work, the embedded voice recognition algorithm mainly performs keyword-based offline recognition through endpoint detection, feature extraction, and command mapping, enabling the microcontroller to accurately interpret user instructions. In terms of software design, the system employs an embedded voice recognition algorithm to parse voice input and coordinates multiple sensor data to achieve intelligent control over the garbage disposal process. Experimental results indicate that the device features fast response, high recognition accuracy, and stable operation. It effectively improves issues of low efficiency and frequent misoperation in traditional waste disposal, providing a reference solution with practical application value for the development of smart sanitation equipment.

title: Student-to-job Ranking Framework Based on Knowledge Space Embedding
authors: Aomsap Inkong-ngarm, Jakramate Bootkrajang, Samerkae Somhom, Areerat Thongratsameethong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study proposes a framework for student-to-job ranking that leverages knowledge space embedding to align academic subjects with labor market skill requirements using O*NET knowledge as a shared semantic space. The approach incorporates contextualized embeddings, including Word2Vec, BERT, and SBERT, and examines the effect of LLM-based augmentation on skill and subject representation. Experimental results on computer-related student transcript and job posting data reveal that SBERT without augmentation consistently achieves the highest ranking accuracy (nDCG = 0.98, Top-l Accuracy = 0.84), outperforming other models in both alignment and downstream matching tasks. We also find that LLM augmentation can introduce semantic drift that negatively impacts ranking performance when paired with some embedding algorithms. These finding highlights the importance of selecting embedding strategies and controlling the level of LLM augmentation.

title: Emotional Prediction of Tourism Reviews Based on Knowledge Graph and Multimodal Feature Fusion
authors: Xin Li, Yuyu Zhou, Aiming Xian, Fujia Huang, Ke Wang, Shengying Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This The tourism industry is of great significance to regional economy and cultural heritage, and mining based on massive comment data plays an important role in intelligent decision-making for smart tourism. This article explores the complementary lexical features of LDA and TF-IDF, as well as the construction and fusion methods of multimodal features such as content features and structured features enhanced by knowledge graph context. After feature screening using ANOVA F-value, the geometric weighted average ensemble model RF-XGBLGBMLDA proposed in this article is used for sentiment prediction of tourism reviews. At the same time, integrating tourists' emotional tendencies and semantic associations with scenic spots into a knowledge graph, combined with Dijkstra's algorithm to achieve personalized path optimization, and realizing a complete closed-loop from text feature fusion to intelligent recommendation. The experiment shows that the model has an accuracy rate of 99.15%, a negative review recall rate of 96.43%, path optimization reduces the total mileage by 43.8%,and increases the sentiment index by 16.5%. This study provides a multidimensional technical framework for analyzing tourist satisfaction in tourism scenarios, which has important reference value for optimizing sustainable services in tourism destinations.

title: Deep Learning-Based Image Deblurring for Graphite Ore Images
authors: Liang Hu, Jionghui Wang, Xueyu Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address motion blur in graphite ore images caused by equipment motion and environmental interference during industrial acquisition, this paper proposes a lightweight deep learning-based image deblurring method. MobileNetV2 is employed as the feature extraction backbone to capture stable representations with reduced computational complexity. On this basis, a multi-scale feature fusion Transformer module is introduced to model global dependencies across different scales, enhancing robustness to complex blur patterns. In addition, an Efficient Channel Attention Residual Network (ECA-ResNet) is integrated into local feature modeling to strengthen critical channel representations and improve detail restoration. To alleviate training instability under limited sample conditions, transfer learning is adopted for parameter initialization, and a joint loss function combining content loss and frequency loss is designed to jointly constrain structural consistency and high-frequency details. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior performance in graphite ore image deblurring, outperforming comparative methods in terms of PSNR, SSIM, and visual quality.

title: An Interpretable Image Classification Approach Using Prototype-Based Deep Belief Rules
authors: Jiawei Wu, Lianmeng Jiao, Quan Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Belief rule-based systems, despite their interpretability in classification tasks, face two critical limitations in image analysis: the curse of dimensionality when handling highdimension data and incompatibility with non-tabular formats, which restrict their applicability to interpretable image classification applications. To address these challenges, we propose a prototype-based deep belief rule reasoning methodology for interpretable image classification. The core methodology involves replacing decision layer in deep neural networks with a deep belief rule base, enabling joint optimization of classification performance and decision transparency. The deep belief rule base construction comprises two critical phases: anchoring initial class boundaries through <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\delta-B e l$</tex> decision graph, and generating comprehensive deep belief rule base, in which the basic principle for constructing the deep belief rule base is to rely on the distance between prototypes, instead of distinguishing the actual categories of prototypes. We conduct extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets, demonstrating that our approach achieves superior interpretability while maintaining high classification performance.

title: The Centralized Control Management Mode of Modern Substation Based on Intelligent Robot
authors: Jin Peng, Luming Huang, Song Zeng, Hailong Hu, Bing Wang, Xixi Zeng, Benhui Mao, Yunhao Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of scattered substations, long inter-station distances, and difficult centralized operation and maintenance management caused by Ganzhou's large land area (39,380 km<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, nearly a quarter of Jiangxi Province and the largest among prefecture-level cities in six central provinces), this paper proposes an intelligent robot-based modern centralized substation control management model. Integrating technologies like neural network image recognition, 3D SLAM positioning and navigation, and precise contour-matching infrared thermal imaging, the model takes Yanfeng Centralized Control Station as the core and seven operation and maintenance teams as sub-control stations to manage 17 key 220kV substations in Ganzhou. It enables top-down information transmission and communication, realizes timely equipment defect detection, significantly reduces the workload of operation and maintenance personnel, and improves work efficiency. Research and practical results demonstrate that this model can effectively manage scattered substations, accurately dispatch operation and maintenance staff, and promptly identify and handle defects. By integrating cutting-edge technologies into power grid operation, it builds a highly intelligent and efficient modern centralized substation management system, which better guarantees the reliable and stable operation of Ganzhou's power system.

title: Infrared UAV Target Tracking with Dynamic Feature Refinement and Global Contextual Attention Knowledge Distillation_supp1-3668517.pdf
authors: Houzhang Fang
abstract: Unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) target tracking based on thermal infrared imaging has been one of the most important sensing technologies in anti-UAV applications. However, the infrared UAV targets often exhibit weak features and complex backgrounds, posing significant challenges to accurate tracking. To address these problems, we introduce SiamDFF, a novel dynamic feature fusion Siamese network that integrates feature enhancement and global contextual attention knowledge distillation for infrared UAV target (IRUT) tracking. The SiamDFF incorporates a selective target enhancement network (STEN), a dynamic spatial feature aggregation module (DSFAM), and a dynamic channel feature aggregation module (DCFAM). The STEN employs intensity-aware multi-head cross-attention to adaptively enhance important regions for both template and search branches. The DSFAM enhances multi-scale UAV target features by integrating local details with global features, utilizing spatial attention guidance within the search frame. The DCFAM effectively integrates the mixed template generated from STEN in the template branch and original template, avoiding excessive background interference with the template and thereby enhancing the emphasis on UAV target region features within the search frame. Furthermore, to enhance the feature extraction capabilities of the network for IRUT without adding extra computational burden, we propose a novel tracking-specific target-aware contextual attention knowledge distiller (TCAKD). It transfers the target prior from the teacher network to the student model, significantly improving the student network's focus on informative regions at each hierarchical level of the backbone network. Extensive experiments on real infrared UAV datasets demonstrate that the proposed approach outperforms state-of-the-art target trackers under complex backgrounds while achieving a real-time tracking speed.

title: Reliability Risk Assessment and Standardization Strategy for Single-phase Immersion-cooled Data center
authors: Jungeon Lee, Daeil Kwon
abstract: Recently, immersion cooling has been widely adopted in hyperscale data centers. Despite the rapid adoption, its reliability challenges remain insufficiently addressed due to a lack of standardization. This study identifies the potential reliability risks of data center under immersion cooling, and proposes a standardization strategy to bridge the gap between current protocols and immersion-specific environments. First, utilizing a FMMEA, this study systematically identifies and prioritizes potential failure mechanisms of data center server under immersion cooling. The results highlight immersion specific failure mechanisms such as absorption, environmental stress cracking, corrosion, and oxidation that can introduce reliability risks distinct from those in air cooled data center operation. Based on the FMMEA results, a gap analysis of existing reliability standards is comprehensively conducted across methodology, domain, and practice levels. This analysis reveals that current practice-level standards, predominantly designed for air cooling, fail to capture the identified unique failure mechanism. Consequently, the direct application of current standards can lead to inaccurate reliability assessments. Finally, this study suggests specific modifications to current reliability standards to address these identified gaps. By aligning failure mechanisms with test methods, this work provides a strategic direction for developing reliability standards tailored to immersion-cooled environments.

title: A Dynamic Framework for Causal User Profiling and Treatment Segmentation via Uplift Modeling in Internet Lending
authors: Jianqing Jiang, Madya Nor Asilah Wati Abdul Hamid, Ng Keng Yap, Madya Choo Wei Chong
abstract: The growth of internet lending has created a need for decision frameworks based on models that are both personalized and causally interpretable. Conventional uplift models detect treatment responsiveness without recognizing user heterogeneity, the temporal consistency of user behavior, or the upstream design choices that carry important causal implications. This paper proposes an integrated and reproducible Causal User Profiling (CUP) framework that combines causal inference, uplift modeling, and response-based segmentation within a single pipeline. CUP realizes treatment-effect heterogeneity through a four-type response taxonomy (Persuadable, Sure Thing, Lost Cause, Do-Not-Disturb) and embeds it in a multi-stage pipeline involving hybrid feature selection (Information Value (IV), Causal Forest importance, Population Stability Index (PSI) stability, and Stepwise refinement), stratified clustering with a “C2 replacement strategy,” and meta-learning via both the X-Learner and the Doubly Robust (DR) Learner using Logistic Regression (LR). A component-wise ablation analysis finds that feature selection increases AUUC by 25–30%, C2 clustering by 10–12%, and the DR-Learner + LR by another 5–8%. Overall, the integrated CUP framework yields 45–50% higher AUUC than the baseline (“all features + no clustering + standard learner”) while retaining behaviorally coherent and temporally stable insights. Methodologically, we provide: (i) an end-to-end causal user profiling framework that interoperates profiling, causal estimation, clustering, and uplift evaluation; (ii) a behaviorally and causally consistent response segmentation mechanism grounded in the potential-outcomes model; and (iii) a reproducible experimental design that quantifies pipeline-level uplift gains through systematic ablation. Applied to large-scale internet-lending data, CUP reveals opportunities for treatment-aware personalization, enabling financial institutions to target Persuadables, support Sure Things, and avoid disturbing Do-Not-Disturbs based on causal evidence.
