authors: Jim McBride
abstract: Provides society information that may include news, reviews or technical notes that should be of interest to practitioners and researchers.

title: IEEE Fellows: Congratulations to the 2026 IEEE PES Fellows Class! [Awards]
authors: N/A
abstract: Presents the recipients of IEEE Power & Energy Society (PES) awards for 2026.

title: Improving Neural Architecture Search by Minimizing Worst-Case Validation Loss
authors: Haochen Zhang, Xuefeng Du, Ruisi Zhang, Pengtao Xie
abstract: Neural architecture search (NAS) aims at automatically searching for high-performance architectures and has achieved considerable progress. Existing NAS methods learn architectures by minimizing average-case validation losses. As a result, the searched architectures are less capable of making correct predictions under worst-case scenarios. To address this problem, we propose a framework which leverages a deep generative model to generate adversarial validation examples to measure the worst-case validation performance of an architecture and improves the architecture by minimizing the loss on the generated adversarial validation data. Our framework is based on multi-level optimization, which performs multiple learning stages end-to-end. Experiments on a variety of datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our method.

title: A Differentially Private Quadrature Amplitude Modulation Mechanism for Federated Analytics
authors: Yujie Gu, Richeng Jin, Chongwen Huang, Xiaofan He, Zhaoyang Zhang, Huaiyu Dai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Wireless federated analytics face two critical challenges: data privacy and communication efficiency, since the local data may contain sensitive information and the users may be equipped with limited communication capability. Existing methods often adopt a direct combination of privacy-preservation schemes and compression mechanisms but overlook the privacy amplification effect from errors introduced in compression and wireless communication. With such consideration, a Differentially Private Quadrature Amplitude Modulation (DP-QAM) scheme, which leverages privacy amplification from both compression and noisy wireless channels, is proposed. The privacy guarantee is established in terms of the emerging f-DP, and the trade-off between privacy, communication cost, and accuracy in terms of mean square error (MSE) is characterized in the fundamental use cases of distributed mean estimation and frequency estimation, which outperforms the state-of-the-art methods. Moreover, the advantage of the proposed method over the classic Gaussian mechanism is further demonstrated from a rate-distortion perspective. Finally, extensive simulation results validate the effectiveness of the proposed mechanism.

title: Title Page III
authors: N/A
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)

title: Enveloped Generalized Voronoi Graph and Multihomotopy Path Planning for Navigation in Unknown Environments
authors: Hengbo Qi, Yu Zhang, Chenxi Pan, Mengting Li, Fei Meng, Zhangguo Yu, Peng Lu, Xuechao Chen
abstract: Efficient navigation in unknown environments relies on a compact yet geometrically expressive map that enables optimal path generation. Among various representations, Voronoi diagram offers advantages in structural compactness and global connectivity. However, in partially explored environments, its limited spatial expressiveness degrades planning performance. To address this, we propose the enveloped generalized Voronoi graph (EGVG) and a multihomotopy path-planning algorithm, integrated within a unified navigation framework. EGVG enhances the original Voronoi skeleton by incorporating Euclidean signed distance field (ESDF) contours to envelop obstacles, enriching spatial representation and bridging the gap between the Voronoi skeleton and the optimal solution space. Next, a multihomotopy search is performed on the EGVG, where the planner deactivates key vertices after each iteration, forcing the exploration of multiple homotopy classes and increasing the likelihood of finding globally shortest paths (i.e., solution optimality). Finally, a motion-consistency criterion is applied to select the most efficient path for execution. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves up to an 88% improvement in solution optimality over the original generalized Voronoi graph. Compared to roadmap and visibility-graph representations, it reduces planning time and graph size by over an order of magnitude. In unknown navigation tasks, our planner outperforms state-of-the-art methods, achieving shorter travel distances and execution times.

title: Dual Imbalance Suppression for Source-free Object Detection in Aerial Images
authors: You Ma, Lin Chai, Shihan Mao, Yucheng Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Source-free object detection (SFOD) aims to adapt pre-trained source models to the target domain without accessing the source domain. In this paper, we focus on two challenges that are unexplored in existing SFOD methods but prevalent in aerial scenes: Ⅰ) class imbalance, and Ⅱ) scale representation imbalance, i.e., the feature representations of objects at different scales vary significantly, even within classes. To address these problems, we propose a novel SFOD method in aerial images known as dual imbalance suppression. Specifically, for challenge Ⅰ, we introduce a class-aware weighting strategy, which adjusts the weights of each class according to the model learning state to realize dynamic balance among classes. For challenge Ⅱ, we design a scale-aware contrastive learning (SACL), which combines fine constraints on intra-scale features and coarse constraints on inter-scale features to improve the compactness of intra-class features, while eliminating the negative impact of forcibly aligning different scale features with significant representation differences. In addition, we propose a multi-scale interaction transformer module that not only enhances feature representation but also constructs high-quality positive/negative sample pairs for SACL. Extensive experiments on 5 aerial scenes demonstrate that our method outperforms existing state-of-the-art SFOD methods.

title: A 5–230-GHz 20-dB-Gain InP Matrix Amplifier Using Wideband Tapered Capacitors
authors: Phat T. Nguyen, Anthony S. Pham, Viet-Anh Ngo, Natalie S. Wagner, Alexander Stameroff, Anh-Vu Pham
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents the design of a wideband high-gain matrix amplifier (MA) comprising 12 amplifier cells in a two-tier six-stage (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$2\times 6$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) configuration. In the designed MA, wideband input signals are initially amplified by first-tier amplifier cells, generating forward and reverse waves that are then further amplified by second-tier cells, resulting in high gain and good output power over a broad frequency range. To further enhance the gain–bandwidth product, tapered capacitors are used to counteract line loss, therefore balancing the base voltages entering amplifier cells of the designed MA. The enhanced signal amplitudes along the MA’s middle line enable flexible designs and optimizations of tapered capacitors for gain, ripples, and power across a frequency range from a few GHz to sub-THz. The fabricated indium phosphide MA exhibits an average gain of 20 dB with ±2.5-dB ripple across a 5–230-GHz bandwidth. The measured input and output return losses are below −10 dB up to 190 GHz. The saturated power is averaged at 15.7 dBm over a 5–170-GHz bandwidth and is maintained above 14 dBm up to 180 GHz and 7.8 dBm up to 220 GHz. The output 1-dB compression point and the third-order intercept point peak at 13.8 and 24.8 dBm, respectively. The MA occupies a compact core area of 0.32 mm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> and consumes 767 mW of DC power.

title: A Low-Loss Silicon-Micromachined Waveguide Crossover Switch for Spaceborne Radiometer Calibration Circuits at 163.6–182.55GHz
authors: Suxian Yu, Umer Shah, Armin Karimi, Michael Brandt, Sebastian Legel, Petri Viljo Piironen, Joachim Oberhammer
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a low-insertion-loss, high-isolation, and highly integrated silicon-micromachined waveguide crossover switch for an internal calibration subsystem in spaceborne radiometers operating over seven subbands in the 163.6–182.55-GHz range, located on the lower frequency wing of the 183.31-GHz water-vapor absorption line. The device consists of four microelectromechanical switching surfaces integrated with two waveguide couplers and is implemented as a seven-chip bonded stack, providing a compact form factor of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$16 {\,}\times {\,}11.4 {\,}\times {\,}2.1~\mathrm {mm}^{3}$</tex-math> </inline-formula> with four WR-5.1 waveguide interfaces. The micromachined prototype was characterized using a custom CNC-milled waveguide module that routes the on-chip ports to standard waveguide flanges, together with a two-tier calibration employing a multiple-offset-short calibration standard fabricated in the same process run. In the crossover state, the device exhibits an insertion loss of 0.5–0.7dB, return loss better than 24dB, and isolation exceeding 29dB across 163.6–182.55GHz. In the straight state, it achieves an insertion loss of 0.5–0.75dB, return loss better than 15dB, and isolation above 33dB. Both states meet the requirements of the intended application, and the measured results show good agreement with electromagnetic simulations. In addition, the actuator enables isolation tuning—particularly in the straight state—providing a tunable attenuation capability.

title: Flexible Superpixel-Based Graph Neural Network for Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Haojie Hu, Jiaxin Li, Shuanghao Fan, Fang He, Dong Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Recently, Graph Neural Network (GNN) has garnered increasing attention in hyperspectral image classification (HSIC) due to their ability to model complex spatial-spectral relationships. Most GNN-based approaches for HSIC typically rely on manually constructed graphs according to predefined superpixels after segmentation. However, such predefined graph nodes is fixed, and may not optimally capture the discriminative features required for classification, particularly given the high variability in spectral characteristics across different landcover classes. To address this limitation, we propose a flexible superpixel-based graph neural network that generates adaptive superpixels. The entire framework is designed to be end-toend trainable. Experiments on three widely used hyperspectral datasets validate the effectiveness of our method, demonstrating superior performance compared to existing approaches.

title: Demand response potential of green hydrogen production in the green energy transition in Finland
authors: Ilkka Jokinen, Matti Lehtonen
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Hydrogen can be produced with low emissions by water electrolysis utilizing low emission electricity. This green hydrogen may be used in industrial processes which are otherwise hard to decarbonize. This study considered the green hydrogen demand in Finland in the future, including current hydrogen use by industry and presuming steel production to convert producing steel with green hydrogen. It was examined how the electrolyzer capacity and hydrogen storage size affected their capability in demand response operation to limit the required dispatchable generation capacity, generated energy, and carbon emissions in the energy system. Results presented that, compared to constant hydrogen production, the demand response was able to reduce the dispatchable capacity by 1.54 GW and the generated energy significantly, up to $4.05 \mathrm{TWh} / \mathrm{a}(49 \%)$, by increasing the utilization of other generation sources, mainly wind and solar power. In an electrified energy system, the demand response was able to reduce carbon dioxide (CO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) emissions up to $1.2 \mathrm{Mt} \mathrm{CO}_{2} / \mathrm{a}$ ($18 \%$). Moreover, the electrolyzer capacity was more important than the hydrogen storage size, and a larger storage resulted in greater reductions only with a greater electrolyzer capacity. Compared to today, a wider electrification of the energy system reduced the total emission up to 72%.

title: Design of Debris Sorting Robot System on Coal Mine Belt Conveyor
authors: Lei Wu, Ming Lu, Minghui Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: During the transportation process of coal mine belt conveyors, issues such as foreign objects like gangue, wood blocks, and ironware often arise. To address these challenges, a new - type debris sorting robot has been designed to replace manual sorting operations. Based on its unique structural composition, implementation methods, and functional characteristics, this robot can efficiently and accurately sort out various foreign objects moving at high speed on the belt conveyor, enabling unmanned operation. It provides a strong guarantee for the construction of digital mines and the intelligentization of production systems.

title: Multi-Broker Workflow Simulation for Simulating Task Load Changes in Cloud Computing
authors: Lixian Zhao, Shuhan Dong, Zhiqiang Fan, Weicheng Lu, Hao Li, Mengyuan Zou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: WorkflowSim is a simulation software that extends from CloudSim. Based on cloud computing simulation, it provides workflow simulation capabilities, implements multiple task scheduling algorithms, enhances the simulation capabilities for faults and other aspects, and is closer to a real distributed environment. However, in the case of multiple brokers, WorkflowSim currently exhibits the limitation of assigning all tasks to the first broker. Furthermore, all tasks are submitted at the start of the simulation, becoming schedulable as soon as their parent tasks finish. Each task executes only once. This static submission approach makes it challenging to accurately simulate variations in task execution time under dynamic workload conditions. Therefore, this article presents a method(TLCMS), using an improved multi-broker mode to reduce simulation completion time and supporting simulating the variation of task execution time with changing task loads.

title: Diplomatic Discourse and National Image Construction Based on Proximization Theory: A Corpus-Assisted Study of China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs Regular Press Conference Speeches
authors: Yu Liu, Jinsong Yang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: As a new method of critical discourse analysis, Proximization Theory has been widely used in political discourse, especially in the analysis of strong discourse, but the research on positive political discourse is still lacking. Based on Proximization Theory, this research builds a corpus and uses AntConc4.0 to retrieve keywords and high-frequency words to explore the distribution characteristics of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs' regular press conference speeches in terms of temporal, spatial, and axiological proximization strategies and the national image they reflect. The results show that in temporal proximization, diplomatic discourse highlights China's image as a responsible country; in spatial proximization, it reflects the image of China as a fair, friendly and inclusive country; in axiological proximization, China is a cooperative and win-win image. This study aims to provide a new path for the analysis of positive political discourse and national image construction by using Proximization Theory.

title: Research on Compiler Fuzzing Based on Syntax-semantics Dual-Dimensional Classification
authors: Shiyu Wang, Lin Han, Mengyao Chen, Haoran Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Fuzz testing is an effective method for compiler testing, but existing tools generate test programs with low syntactic correctness due to a lack of program semantic awareness. To address this, this paper proposes a syntax-semantic aware gray-box fuzz testing method and designs the improved tool VarFuzz based on AFL: replacing AFL's character-level mutations with semantic mutations, and integrating a semantic distance matrix with Thompson sampling for dynamic operator probability allocation. Experiments show that, compared to AFL and GrayC, VarFuzz increases code coverage on GCC and LLVM compilers by approximately 10.46% and 6.2%, respectively.

title: Multidimensional Sampling for Land Use and Land Cover Classification
authors: Glauber José Vaz, Alexandre Camargo Coutinho, Júlio César Dalla Mora Esquerdo, João Francisco Gonçalves Antunes, Anderson Rocha
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: This study presents a methodology for generating high-quality datasets of samples to train machine learning models for land use and land cover (LULC) classification, representing an initial step toward large-scale LULC mapping. The proposed approach generates a robust set of multi-pixel, multi-temporal, and multi-band samples by integrating label generation with remote sensing data processing across two phases. In the first phase, multidimensional data are collected from remote sensing sources, and label information is derived by comparing harmonized LULC maps, supplemented with additional processing to improve data quality. In the second phase, samples are extracted to produce a diverse, representative, and balanced dataset of reliably labeled multidimensional data for LULC classification. In this study, reference maps were sourced from the TerraClass and MapBiomas projects, and satellite imagery was obtained from the Brazil Data Cube platform.

title: Identification of Factors behind High-Risk Pregnancies in Rural Punjab, India using Machine Learning
authors: Gurnoor Shergill, Gaganpreet Kaur
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: High Risk Pregnancies (HRPs) are still a medical challenge and contribute to 75% of the perinatal morbidity and mortality in India. In this work study has been conducted to identify the dominant factors responsible for HRPs. The factors of medical facilities, community awareness on maternal healthcare, medical advancements, rural urban divide are subject to demographics. This study focuses on identifying the dominant factors contributing to high-risk pregnancies (HRPs) in rural Patiala, Punjab, which are responsible for a significant portion of perinatal morbidity and mortality in India. A dataset of 1364 pregnant women from a government hospital was analyzed over six months (Sept 2023 - Feb 2024), with 287 cases identified as HRPs. The study uses machine learning algorithms (Random Forest, SVM, and KNN) to classify HRPs and highlight key contributing factors. Age has been key factor with maximum of HRPs in age bracket of 46-55 years followed by minor age pregnancies. Amongst the maternal factors previous still births is dominant with $\mathbf{6 0. 6 8 \%}$ of these cases as HRPs.

title: Automated VTE Risk Stratification System Incorporating KNN Imputation and Cluster Stratified Sampling
authors: Zhonghe Fan, Peng Qiao, Dandan Sun, Shufang Zhang, Xuehong He
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This study developed an interpretable venous thromboembolism (VTE) risk prediction model for discharged patients, aiming to unify recurrent VTE assessment. A real-world dataset of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,340 \text{VTE}$</tex> patients from a tertiary hospital was analyzed. A BERT model pre-trained on Chinese medical corpora extracted features from clinical text. After handling missing data with KNN imputation and structuring the dataset via cluster stratified sampling, separate CatBoost models were built using numerical variables alone and a fusion of text and numerical features. The fusion model significantly outperformed the numeric-only model, achieving 76 % accuracy and a 77 % weighted F1-score, compared to 60 % and 64 %, respectively. Data preprocessing analysis demonstrated that KNN imputation notably improved medium-risk prediction (recall increased from 0.18 to 0.38), while SMOTENC oversampling further enhanced low-risk recall but slightly reduced overall accuracy. The results confirm the model's potential for early identification of high-risk cases and data-driven anticoagulation therapy planning.

title: UAV-RIS-Aided Intelligent Physical-Layer Secure Communications in Urban Networks
authors: Ying Ju, Jing Yu, Haoyu Wang, Zhenghao Li, Lei Liu, Jianbo Du, Mian Ahmad Jan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Complex urban communication scenarios confront severe challenges, including weak signal penetration capability, high susceptibility to blockage effects, and potential eavesdropping threats. Nevertheless, when combined with the regulation signal ability of reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) and the mobility of unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV), they offer flexible, secure, and highly adaptive services for urban wireless communications. To enhance the uplink communication security between users (UEs) and a base station (BS), a novel UAV-RIS-aided urban wireless communications network based on deep reinforcement learning (DRL) is established. This network aims to maximize the sum secrecy rate of the system by jointly optimizing the trajectory of the UAV and the phase of the passive RIS, while taking into account the quality of service (QoS) constraints of all UEs. Specifically, a dual proximal policy optimization (D-PPO) algorithm is proposed to enhance the system performance, where the first PPO and second PPO are dedicated to optimizing the trajectory of UAV and phase control of RIS. Numerical results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm can learn effective strategies, thereby promoting an overall improvement in the secrecy capacity while ensuring the QoS requirement of each UE throughout the entire communication process.

title: An Integrated Approach for Pulse Detection and Classification of Partial Discharge (PD) Signals
authors: Maninder Choudhary, Ivar Kiitam, Ivo Palu
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) measurement is an efficient technique used for fault detection, condition monitoring and aging study of insulation. While phase resolved partial discharge (PRPD) patterns and IEC 60270 features have been widely used in the past. Energy transition, particularly the inclusion of power electronics converters (high frequency and slew rates), and high voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission, demands more advanced PD diagnostic tools. PD signals or pulses are key measurable quantities that characterize PD activity. This article presents an integrated approach used for pulse detection and classification of PD signals. It utilizes wavelet denoising and the local maxima algorithm for PD data denoising, pulse detection, and multi-dimensional feature extraction. Principal component analysis (PCA) for dimensionality reduction and hierarchical clustering for classification of PD signals. At the final stage, it utilizes a rulebased single input and single output (SISO) fuzzy inference system (FIS) for the identification of clusters. The developed approach has been implemented on various PD data sets obtained from surface erosion-based accelerated aging of Nomex insulation to validate the four-stage aging phenomenon. The key goal of this approach is to develop an unsupervised system for asset managers and researchers that performs efficient PD data analysis.

title: The Impact of AI Tools on Cross-Border E-Commerce English Learning Effectiveness
authors: Meiling Liang, Loo Yew Liang, Zhiying Tan, Li Xing
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study investigates the impact of AI tools on cross-border e-commerce English learning effectiveness among vocational students. Drawing on UTAUT2, cognitive load theory, and foreign language anxiety theory, we surveyed 197 students from Guangdong Polytechnic of Science and Trade and analyzed the data using PLS-SEM. The model explained 66 percent of the variance in learning effectiveness. Results showed that cognitive load reduction and performance expectancy significantly enhanced learning outcomes, while learning anxiety had no significant effect. These findings suggest that AI tools enhance specialized English learning primarily through cognitive support rather than anxiety reduction. The research provides practical guidance for implementing AI-assisted domain-specific English education in vocational settings.

title: Analysis of Learning Conditions and Teaching Strategies in College English at Open Universities Under Big Data
authors: Haibing Hou, Jiesheng Huang, Jiachang Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Educational digitalization provides strong support for the high-quality development of open education. Conducting an analysis of college English learning conditions and teaching strategies based on big data is an important guarantee for promoting the connotative development and digital upgrading of college English. As one of the core courses in open education, college English generally lacks refined approaches to accurately grasp students' learning conditions and proactive intervention measures to provide personalized teaching strategies. Using the learning situation of 20,605 undergraduate students enrolled in the autumn semester of 2024 on the Guangdong Open University course platform as the research sample, this study analyzes learning performance and monitoring data, explores the relationship between learning engagement and academic achievement, and proposes optimization schemes for teaching strategies based on data analysis. The aim is to improve the quality of college English teaching, promote the implementation of personalized education, and provide both theoretical foundations and practical guidance for realizing the intelligent transformation of education.

title: A Reusable Software Framework for Satellite Attitude and Orbit Control System
authors: Wenting Niu, Shang Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the rapid development of the aerospace industry, satellites are increasingly used in fields such as meteorological monitoring and remote sensing identification. Therefore, low cost, short development cycle, high reliability, and high integration have become the main directions in current satellite development. The Satellite Attitude and Orbit Control System (AOCS), as an extremely important part of the satellite platform, is mainly responsible for completing the tasks of satellite attitude control and orbit control. As the functional core of the AOCS, the rapid development of AOCS software technology is a top priority. This paper takes the satellite AOCS software as the research object, proposes a reusable software framework, separates the interaction between the system application software and the underlying hardware, improves the reusability of the system software, and addresses the current problems of long satellite development cycle, high cost, and low integration.

title: Research on Online Learning Achievement Prediction Algorithm Based on Student Behavior Profile
authors: Shuang Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Traditional online learning performance prediction methods are often based on simple features such as historical grades and learning duration, and the results are often limited by a single data dimension, making it difficult to fully reflect students' learning behavior and characteristics, and the prediction accuracy is limited. Therefore, a research on online learning performance prediction algorithm combining student behavior profiling has been proposed. Firstly, by crawling student behavior data through multiple channels, covering multiple dimensions such as learning interaction, resource utilization, and time management. Based on crawled data, analyze students' online learning behavior, construct student behavior profiles through clustering analysis, and obtain students' learning situation and characteristics. Design and implement an online learning performance prediction algorithm based on student behavior portraits, and use support vector machine models to learn and predict features. The experimental results show that after applying this algorithm, students' learning behaviors and characteristics can be more comprehensively captured, and the online learning performance prediction results better fit the changing trend of real grades. The prediction mean square error is small, showing extremely high prediction accuracy.

title: Wide-Field Effect Correction for Low-Frequency SKA Imaging Using a Multi-Stage Network
authors: Xinglong Wang, Li Zhang, Mei Lu, Zujuan Xu, Yuxin Xia
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: The wide-field effect observed in low-frequency Square Kilometre Array (SKA) observations is one of the key factors leading to image quality degradation, and represents a critical challenge in SKA data processing that urgently needs to be addressed. The target sources in this paper are selected from the Galaxy Zoo survey data. By simulating wide-field effects, the corresponding dirty images are generated to construct the dataset. The MPRNet deep learning model is then exploratively applied to the task of correcting wide-field effects in astronomical images. Experimental results demonstrate that the method achieves a PSNR of 45.31 dB and an SSIM of 0.992 on the test set, effectively recovering the source structures with superior restoration quality. This paper presents an initial exploration of deep learning methods for astronomical image processing, and future work will focus on advancing the network to address more complex coupled degradation effects.

title: Hierarchical Task Decomposition for Long-Horizon Robotic Autonomy via Multimodal Language Model Synergy
authors: Genjian Yang, Liuhua Zhang, Zhiwei Gao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Robots are increasingly being applied in fields such as industrial manufacturing and logistics sorting, where task planning has become a critical component for enabling autonomous and efficient operations. Unlike short-term tasks such as grasping or moving, long-horizon and complex tasks—such as electronic device assembly and sanitation services—span extended time periods and involve multiple consecutive and interrelated actions. These tasks often take place in complex and dynamic environments, where obstacles and task requirements may change unpredictably. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a task planning framework based on the collaboration between large language models (LLMs) and vision-language models (VLMs), incorporating an active question-answering mechanism for continuous task updates. The framework decomposes complex tasks into executable action sequences based on scene descriptions, and employs a dynamic, asynchronous hierarchical executor to adaptively regulate the frequency of task planning and action execution. Additionally, we introduce a training strategy based on a real-time learning method that leverages both positive and negative experiences. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed task planning framework effectively leverages pretrained policies and dynamically updates action sequences, thereby improving the success rate of complex task execution.

title: Optimization of Control Strategy and Over-Temperature Protection of a Turbocharger Wastegate Valve Based on Thermal Management Model Predictive Control (MPC)
authors: Chenqi Hu, Zhongyuan Li
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This paper proposes an exhaust bypass valve control strategy based on thermal management model predictive control (MPC) to address the problem of a certain type of turbocharger being prone to overheating in front of the turbine under high load and transient conditions, and traditional PID and extreme value search control (ESC) being difficult to balance power performance and thermal protection. Firstly, a first-order discrete state space model including turbine efficiency, compressor characteristics, exhaust gas flow rate and thermal inertia is constructed, and the accurate prediction of pre-turbine temperature and boost pressure is realized by bench data identification and online correction of Kalman filter. Then, a rolling time domain multi-objective optimization function is designed, which quantifies the temperature hard constraint, pressure tracking error and smoothness of valve action into a quadratic programming problem, and introduces fuzzy logic to dynamically adjust the weight according to the load and temperature margin, so as to realize the adaptive control of “giving priority to over-temperature protection and giving consideration to dynamic response”. The comparative experiment on the bench of a 2.0 L gasoline engine shows that the proposed MPC strategy can control the peak temperature in front of the turbine within 948° C and 953° C, respectively, and the zero overtemperature will continue under the two extreme working conditions of “high load climbing” and “rapid acceleration”. The steady-state error of boost pressure is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq-\mathbf{5 ~ k P a}$</tex>, the response time is better than ESC, and the valve action is smooth without jitter. The results show that this method has obvious advantages over traditional PID and ESC in terms of constraint satisfaction, multi-objective tradeoff and robustness, and can provide an engineering solution for synergistic control of thermal safety and performance of turbocharger.

title: Some Features of Using the RES
authors: Josifs Survilo
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Climate change makes us more aware of it every year. In recent centuries, people have lived at a level of culture that requires increased energy consumption. Now, this energy needs to be derived from renewables rather than from fossil fuels. This, of course, must be done at the lowest cost avoiding unnecessary installed capacity of electricity devices. Due to the volatility of renewable energy, it is necessary to store electricity. Given the nature of the volatility, the power of the generators depends on the capacity of the electricity storage devices. In order to fully rely on renewable energy sources, it is necessary to transmit energy over a distance. Using equations for transmission line active power loss, voltage loss and observing conductor admissible current makes it possible to better choose line parameters.

title: Optimized QCA Implementation of Reversible Feynman, Toffoli, Peres, and Double Feynman Gates
authors: Saakshi P Aayasya, V Ramesh Kumar
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: This work presents an optimized design of Feynman, Toffoli, Peres, and Double Feynman gates using Quantum-dot Cellular Automata (QCA) with a focus on improving area efficiency and minimizing energy dissipation. By refining circuit layouts and eliminating redundant components, significant reductions in cell count have been achieved: the Feynman Gate from 32 to 16 cells, the Toffoli Gate from 40 to 24 cells, and the Peres Gate from 79 to 43 cells, the Double Feynman Gate from 51 to 45 cells. These enhancements lead to reduced quantum cost, lower power consumption, and improved functional density. A comparative study has been conducted to evaluate the proposed designs against existing implementations, demonstrating their superiority in terms of gate count optimization, circuit depth reduction, and energy efficiency. These advancements establish QCA as a promising approach for next-generation computing architectures, with potential applications in low-power CMOS design, nanotechnology, DNA computing, and quantum computing.

title: A Study on LLM-Agent-Driven Methods for Generating Short English Speaking Scenarios
authors: Sha Li, Hua Li, Yunxiao Ma, Yufeng Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This paper presents an LLM-powered multi-agent framework for generating personalized English-speaking scenarios across different educational stages, with a special focus on high school learners. Grounded in China's national curriculum standards, the system leverages intent recognition and generative AI to construct micro-learning dialogues tailored to learners' goals, interests, and developmental levels. By integrating science-related content into oral practice, the high school agent bridges general language learning with domainspecific exploration, offering a customized path toward future professional English. The agent-based architecture, enhanced by prompt engineering and speech interaction, supports dynamic, adaptive learning and enables students to practice spoken English efficiently in fragmented time. This work demonstrates how generative AI can drive personalized learning pathways, enhance learner profiles, and empower intelligent task design within next-generation smart education ecosystems.

title: Construction and Application of Knowledge Systems for University Practical Courses
authors: Shu Fan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Knowledge systems can facilitate students' comprehension of subject matter and enhance their ability to build and update personalized knowledge frameworks by organizing, sharing, and disseminating content in a structured and coherent manner. The development and application of knowledge systems that align with university course content can optimize teaching outcomes. This paper investigates the methodology for constructing knowledge systems for university practical courses. Recognizing that practical problems are complex, multi-faceted issues composed of various sub-problems, the paper models cases as structured event chains. These case collections are subsequently transformed into eventuality knowledge graphs. The case event chains are then integrated with both the knowledge graph of course content and the eventuality knowledge graph to create a comprehensive knowledge system that reflects the collaborative dynamics of practical behaviors and supports intelligent teaching. Analysis of test scores reveals that the knowledge system significantly improves students' performance on practical operation questions and overall scores. The significance of this study lies in the structured analysis of practical cases and the construction of eventuality knowledge graph, which strengthen the logical relationships between case knowledge content and optimize the organization of knowledge in practical courses. The proposed knowledge system design can be promoted and applied across various university practical courses.

title: Predicting Influenza Trends in Urban Communities Using Heterogeneous Spatio-Temporal Graph Models
authors: Xiaoyu Chen, Junbo Wang, Qiuying Lv, Honglin Wang, Kang Liu, Zhigao Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Predicting community-level influenza trends in densely populated cities is a challenge in tropical or subtropical regions with irregular seasonality. Given the high noise in community-level data and complex multi-factor interactions, machine learning models that integrate multi-source data (such as meteorological indicators, geographical correlations, and population mobility) outperform traditional methods in prediction accuracy but suffer from unstable feature learning logic and insufficient generalization ability. Based on Shenzhen’s 2023-2025 influenza surveillance data and meteorological data, we propose a heterogeneous spatiotemporal graph model to predict urban community influenza trends. This model effectively integrates multi-source epidemic data and explicitly models the potential correlations between internal and external transmission factors. Compared with advanced time-series prediction methods, our approach achieves higher accuracy, more stable feature learning logic, and better interpretability.

title: RFAN: Region-aware Facial Attention Network
authors: Feifei Zhang, Boxue Hou, Yufei Ma, Xun Liu

title: Hardware-Aware Offline Training of CTT-Based Neuromorphic Hardware
authors: Rezvan Mohammadrezaee, Ataollah Saeed Monir, Okyanus T. Gumus, Nakisa Shams, Boris Vaisband
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: Neuromorphic hardware combined with spiking neural networks (SNNs) supports low-power, highly parallel AI inference. Inference accuracy can drop sharply when models trained under ideal software assumptions are deployed on inherently noisy hardware. This noise is especially evident in the current of subthreshold operated charge-trap transistors (CTTs) representing network weights. Although CTTs are CMOS-compatible and attractive for large-scale neuromorphic systems due to their energy and cost efficiency, programming imprecision and process, voltage, and temperature (PVT) variations can significantly alter their current, degrading network accuracy. In this article, CTT weight and neuron sources of variability are first identified. Multiparameter noise models are then derived from the hardware components, and sampled noise is injected into both weights and neuron parameters during training, improving robustness across a wide range of noise levels without increasing model size or significantly increasing training time. A novel training algorithm is introduced in which each mini-batch is evaluated multiple times under different noise samples, with the loss optimized to enforce consistency across various noise values. The software behavioral model is verified using Cadence simulations across 45 binary classifiers. The accuracy of ideal-trained classifiers degrades by up to 44.2% under hardware-induced noise, while the proposed training algorithm improves accuracy by up to 23.34%. The validated framework and training algorithm are then used for larger networks performing image classification on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets, improving accuracy from a random state under noise to competitive levels relative to state-of-the-art, with the main overhead being additional epochs determined by noise range rather than network size.

title: Performance Improvement of an HF Matching and Decoupling Network Using Physics Informed Neural Networks
authors: Saeed Bilaki, Arman Afsari, Barry D. Van Veen, Nader Behdad
publish: IEEE Transactions on Antennas and Propagation
abstract: This paper presents a machine learning-based framework for modeling and utilizing an electronically reconfigurable matching and decoupling network (MDN) for two-element arrays operating across the high-frequency (HF) band (3-30 MHz). The proposed neural network model incorporates prior physical knowledge of the system and serves as a reliable digital replica of the physical MDN, accurately capturing the non-idealities and imperfections that arise in practice and are too complex to model using purely theoretical approaches. The dataset used for model development was collected through an automated measurement setup consisting of the MDN, a network analyzer and computer control software. Leveraging this digital replica, we propose a solution-generation methodology that uncovers a vast number of valid MDN configurations for any given load and frequency—a capability that theoretical analysis predicted but conventional methods failed to achieve in practice. In addition, as an optional step, a few hardware fine-tuning adjustments may be used to further increase port-to-port isolation to levels exceeding 40 dB, which were not previously attainable in practice. Moreover, the method allows designers to incorporate additional performance criteria besides impedance matching and decoupling such as low insertion loss, wide matching and decoupling bandwidths, and out-of-band interference suppression, significantly expanding the practical utility of reconfigurable MDNs.

title: Enhancing Few-Shot Medical Image Segmentation with Med-CAP: A Step Towards Personalized Healthcare in the Digital Twin Era
authors: Tao Zhang, Mengya Mo
abstract: The advancement of Healthcare 5.0 and medical Digital Twin systems increasingly relies on accurate anatomical modeling, real-time physiological monitoring, and personalized treatment simulation. These capabilities rely on effective medical image segmentation. However, the lack of annotated medical data and the diversity of clinical imaging severely restrict the applicability of conventional fully supervised models. To address these practical limitations, we propose Med-CAP, a Medical Cross-image Adaptive Prior Segmentation Network designed for real-world low-annotation settings. Med-CAP integrates self-attention–based context encoding, cosine-similarity prior generation, a lightweight prior-guided fusion decoder, and cross-image adaptive multi-head attention to improve structural correspondence between support and query images with minimal supervision. By using just a single annotated example for reliable segmentation, Med-CAP offers a scalable and privacy-friendly solution. It is well-suited for deployment in smart hospitals, remote healthcare systems, and medical Digital Twin platforms. Experiments on abdominal CT and MRI datasets demonstrate that Med-CAP not only achieves superior accuracy compared with state-of-the-art few-shot methods but also exhibits strong robustness across modalities, highlighting its value for next-generation intelligent healthcare applications.

title: RustSFQ: A Rust-based Domain-Specific Language for Efficient SFQ Circuit Design
authors: Mebuki Oishi, Sun Tanaka, Shinya Takamaeda-Yamazaki
abstract: Single-flux-quantum (SFQ) circuits have a strict fan-out constraint, called I/O consistency. It requires every output to be connected to exactly one input. Despite this distinctive constraint, no dedicated tools for describing SFQ circuits exist, and SPICE netlists for analog circuits are still used, leading to reduced productivity. We propose RustSFQ, a domain-specific language embedded in Rust that statically verifies the I/O consistency by leveraging Rust’s ownership system. While gates are represented as functions, wires are represented as variables and managed under the ownership system. For example, if a wire is connected to multiple inputs, a corresponding variable is used multiple times, which is detected as an ownership error. RustSFQ enables immediate detection of design errors and supports abstraction over cell libraries, improving the efficiency of SFQ circuit design. As a case study, a 4-bit Reed–Solomon encoder was implemented using RustSFQ, and simulation confirmed correct operation at 10 GHz.

title: Laser Direct Writing Assisted Fabrication and Characterization of High- T c Superconducting Quantum Magnetometers
authors: Wenzhi Zhang, Yicong Huang, Jianxin Lin
abstract: This paper presents a comprehensive systematic approach methodology for the development of high-temperature superconducting (HTS) magnetometers based on YBa₂Cu₃O₇₋δ (YBCO), encompassing material synthesis, device design, nanofabrication, characterization and their mutual interdependencies and closed-loop feedback dynamics. The approach integrates the optimization of YBCO thin-film growth, the design of low-noise superconducting quantum interference device (SQUID) magnetometer circuits, and advanced micro/nanofabrication processes respectively. Besides that, we introduce and implement a novel nano-laser direct writing (NLDW) technique for precise alignment of grain-boundary on bi-crystal substrates, achieving high-yield, well-defined Josephson junctions and circuit interconnects. Through iterative optimization across all stages – from the initial material properties through circuit layout to the final fabrication steps – we demonstrate integrated YBCO magnetometers exhibiting low magnetic field white noise levels of approximately 100 fT/Hz<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1/2</sup> at 77 K. The established systematic approach, assisted by the NLDW alignment capability, paves the way for practical high-T<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub> magnetometers application.

title: AlScN-based Ferroelectric Memcapacitor on Silicon Platform
authors: Wenxin Sun, Xihui Yuan, Ruiqing Wang, Hengrui Zhang, Siying Zheng, Haiwen Xu, Zhen Yuan, Jiuren Zhou, Yan Liu, Yue Hao, Genquan Han
abstract: We report an experimentally realized silicon-integrated AlScN-based ferroelectric memcapacitor (FMC) with a metal-ferroelectric-semiconductor (MFS) structure, whose underlying physical mechanism is elucidated through TCAD simulations. The FMC exhibits a distinct capacitance memory window (CMW) at zero bias with a stable high/low capacitance state (HCS/LCS) ratio of ~ 124%, enabling eight uniform and tightly spaced capacitance states for 3-bit storage. Furthermore, by utilizing non-destructive read operation (NDRO) to decouple read from write operations, the FMC achieves read-endurance surpassing write-endurance by > 4 orders of magnitude, with ultra-low read energy consumption of sub-fJ/μm2. This work presents a low-power wurtzite ferroelectric memory with promising potential for silicon-based large-scale memory and read-intensive applications.

title: Fractional-Order Dynamics Learning and Control via Data-Driven Approaches: Taking Soft Manipulator as an Example_supp1-3665811.pdf
authors: Guanghui Sun
abstract: The intrinsic memory and nonlocality that allow fractional-order calculus to capture complex dynamical behaviors also pose significant challenges for accurate modeling and stable control. This article presents a unified data-driven framework that simultaneously addresses these challenges through three key innovations. First, we propose a fractional-order deep Lagrangian network (DeLaN) with a Transformer-like structure, fPLCS-DeLaN, to learn system’s inherent fractional-order behaviors directly from uniformly sampled data. It not only enforces fractional-order Lagrangian structure by integrating key physical priors, but also enhances capturing ability of memory effects by incorporating long-short-term convolutional self-attention mechanism. Second, we develop a hybrid network-based disturbance observer, T2F-CRNN, which synergizes CNN’s temporal feature extraction, hierarchical recurrence, and interval-based fuzzy inference to robustly estimate uncertainties with unknown nonuniform bounds and capture temporal dependencies. Third, we establish a fully fractional-order controller with practical finite-time convergence. It incorporates input saturation compensation and sliding mode constraints to ensure robustness and high performance. Simulations show that fPLCS-DeLaN achieves modeling errors at least one order of magnitude lower with less than a 15% increase in computational time. The proposed fractional-order controller reduces transient and steady-state tracking errors by 23.1% and 87.6% compared to state-of-the-art controllers, respectively. Experiments on a soft manipulator platform further demonstrate consistent superiority in model learning and tracking performance.

title: Spatiotemporal Monitoring of Land Cover Using Machine Learning and GIS
authors: Natalia V. Revollo Sarmiento, Federico Javier Beron De La Puente, Carlos Berger, Edinéia Aparecida Dos Santos Galvanin
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Monitoring land use and land cover is critically important for the development of environmental, social, and economic policies. These analyses not only provide information about ongoing changes but also allow us to relate them to anthropogenic factors or climate change. In recent years, access to large volumes of freely available satellite imagery, along with platforms designed for processing this type of data, has enabled a better understanding of land cover changes and the factors that drive them. In this study, we propose the use of a machine learning model to classify land cover in preservation areas during the period from 2019 to 2025. We then analyze the results of accumulated precipitation data and in situ water quality measurements. The results show that it is possible to achieve land cover classification with an accuracy of 97%. In addition, we analyzed the relationship between precipitation levels and the extent of surface water within the reservoir. The results indicate that fluctuations in surface water are consistent with the classifications derived from the machine learning model and correspond to the accumulated rainfall during the classified periods. Furthermore, we examined the relationship between areas identified as urban and non-urban and the in-situ water quality measurements. The integration of satellite imagery, meteorological data, and in situ measurements provides a robust framework for the interdisciplinary analysis of environmental dynamics and interactions.

title: Energy Management System for Islanded Microgrids Considering Droop Control Settings and Uncertainty in Renewable Energy and Load
authors: Mohamed Elgamal, Amir Abdel Menaem, Vladislav Oboskalov, Anatolijs Mahnitko, Roman Petrichenko
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Effective operational management of islanded microgrids (MGs) is essential for achieving both economic efficiency and environmental sustainability. This paper presents a day-ahead energy management system aimed at minimizing the total operational costs of distributed generators (DGs) and reducing power losses in an islanded MG, while accounting for droop control settings and uncertainties in renewable energy generation and load demand. The MG comprises solar generation units, wind turbines, microturbines, a fuel cell unit, and an energy storage system. The proposed optimization framework determines the optimal active and reactive power setpoints for dispatchable DGs, alongside the charge/discharge scheduling of energy storage systems, while maintaining voltage quality and system reliability. In addition to these state variables, the active and reactive power droop coefficients and the reference voltage settings of droopcontrolled DGs are treated as tunable control variables. Uncertainties associated with renewable generation and load profiles are modeled using Monte Carlo Simulation. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is demonstrated using a modified 33-bus islanded MG test system.

title: Collaborative Anomaly Detection Using Agent-Based Modeling in Smart Manufacturing
authors: Shuai Yuan, Haoxu Nong, Yunbo Rao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Industrial anomaly detection is critical for ensuring product quality, preventing equipment failure, and maintaining efficient manufacturing processes. Traditional methods often struggle with the diversity and complexity of potential anomalies and require extensive labeled data for specific tasks. Recent advancements in large pre-trained vision models and large language models, such as Grounding DINO for flexible object detection and Segment Anything Model V2 (SAM V2) for precise segmentation and Llama 3 for large corpus, offer new possibilities for more adaptable anomaly detection systems. This paper proposes an agent-based framework that leverages the power of Grounding DINO, SAM V2, and Llama 3 for zero-shot industrial anomaly detection. Our method employs an intelligent agent to first localize potential anomalies using text prompts with Grounding DINO. These prompts are carefully designed to highlight common industrial anomalies to guide the model in detecting specific deviations from the norm. The identified regions are then segmented precisely using SAM V2, which generates pixel-level masks based on the bounding boxes detected in the localization step. Once the anomaly regions are segmented, the agent generates an anomaly mask that highlights the anomalous regions. Finally, the agent visualizes the anomaly detection results by overlaying the mask onto the original image, allowing human inspectors to identify and address potential defects. We evaluate our approach on the MVTec and VisA anomaly detection dataset. Compared with the mainstream methods, our method has at least increased 1.9% on accuracy, demonstrating its capability to effectively localize and segment various types of industrial anomalies across different object categories without requiring task-specific training data, thus significantly reducing costs.

title: How Digital Competence Facilitates Critical Thinking Skills in Project-Based Learning: Mechanisms and Pathways
authors: Xing Chen, Zhijie Jin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Over the past years, project-based learning plays a crucial role in stimulating students' initiative and enthusiasm for thinking. Project-based learning integrates knowledge, skills, information literacy, and attitude in the learning process, and combines problem- solving, self-directed learning, and motivation within a cycle system. This opens possibilities and opportunities for students' learning through digital tools and resources. Digital practice facilitates communication, cooperation, and accessing information, transmuting it into digital competence, and further fueling the practice of critical thinking skills. This study proposes a series of hypotheses based on the relationships among project-based learning, digital competence and critical thinking skills, and examines them using the technique of structural equation modeling (SEM) with partial least squares (PLS) applied to a sample of 817 college students. The results show that project-based learning has a positive impact on digital competence and indirectly influences students' critical thinking skills. This research represents an exploration in the process of digital transformation in higher education, providing a referential pathway for curriculum design and student development.

title: A LiDAR-Based Kolmogorov-Arnold Point Transformer Algorithm for Semantic Segmentation of Urban 3D Point Clouds
authors: Haodong Huang, Ying Guo, Maoyi Tian, Xinya Niu, Rusong Sun
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate semantic segmentation of 3D point clouds is essential for autonomous driving, HD map updating, and road asset management. Although Point Transformer models local-global geometry with self-attention, its MLP-based feature transformations may limit representational flexibility. We propose KAN-Point Transformer by integrating a Jacobi-polynomial Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (KAN) into the Transformer Block to replace the key MLP-based transformations. On ShapeNet Part, our method improves Cat.mIoU from 76.0% to 78.1% and Ins.mIoU from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 2. 7 \%}$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{8 4. 2 \%}$</tex>, with the best setting at <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\boldsymbol{\alpha}=\boldsymbol{\beta}=\mathbf{1. 0}$</tex> and degree <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{D}=4$</tex>. On Toronto-3D with identical xyz+RGB inputs, it raises mIoU from 65.63% to 72.67%, and boosts road marking IoU from 9.69% to 59.24%. These results show that KAN-Point Transformer better exploits RGB cues and improves multimodal feature utilization in real-world segmentation.

title: Exploring Gender and Education Level as Moderators of Students' Intentions to Continue Learning in Blended Environment
authors: Aleksandra Kobicheva, Dmitrii Mokhorov, Tatiana Baranova
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The purpose of the current study is to identify the main factors influencing the intention of students to study in a blended learning environment and reveal whether gender and level of education moderate the students' blended learning continuance intention. For the research model we took as the basis an expectation-confirmation model and theory of planned behavior. A total of five key factors were identified: satisfaction, perceived utility, attitude, subjective norms, and perceived behavioral control. The study involved 487 undergraduate and postgraduate students studying in the blended learning format. According to the results, there is no significant difference between men and women regarding the five contributing factors. The intention of students to study in a blended environment among groups of different levels of education varies significantly. It turned out that the average scores of postgraduate students are higher than the scores of undergraduates in almost all factors. Multigroup confirmatory factor analysis showed that the effect of perceived utility was very significant for graduate students. A high influence of attitude on the intention of undergraduate students to study in a blended environment was also revealed, that is not in consistency with the previous studies which propose the satisfaction as the strongest predictors of continuance intention. Thus, university managers and teachers should pay more attention on building the positive attitude of students towards the blended learning format to enhance the efficiency of the educational process.

title: Study on the Influence of Search Types and Metacognitive Prompts on Learning Performance in Virtual Science Inquiry
authors: Yiqing Huang, Jingjing Xu, Qiao Han, Shanjiang Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Using search engines to resolve doubts when students encounter difficult is a particularly important approach in scientific inquiry tasks. However, during the process of scientific inquiry, relying solely on search engines without external support from instructors often fails to promote deep thinking. Thus, the provision of metacognitive prompts is necessary to assist learners in monitoring their understanding. This study aims to investigate how different search types and metacognitive prompts jointly influence students' learning performance during scientific inquiry tasks. A total of 95 students <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(\text{MD}_{\text{age}}=20.83,\ \text{SD}_{\text{age}}=2.23)$</tex> were told that they would be engaged in science inquiry tasks. The results showed that the GenAI group had higher transfer scores with metacognitive prompts. Search types has a significant effect on the improvement of four sub-dimensions (PK, P, M, DS) in metacognitive awareness. These findings suggest that educators should provide metacognitive prompts to support learners when they use search tools to learn, and that structured prompt are more effective when students use GenAI.

title: An Empirical Study on how Blended Learning Promotes the Enhancement of Digital Application Competence Among Pre-Service Teachers
authors: Yimin Wang, Yaoyao Li, Minghao Zhang, Yachun Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Against the backdrop of educational digital transformation, technologies such as artificial intelligence and big data are flourishing, creating an urgent need for teachers to enhance their digital sensitivity, utilize digital technology in teaching, and drive pedagogical innovation. Pre-service teachers, being at the initial stage of teacher training, must rapidly adapt to the competency demands of digital transformation and explore new cultivation pathways to lay a solid foundation for advancing high-quality teacher development. By analyzing the connotation of digital literacy among pre-service teachers, this study clarifies the components of digital application competence. It constructs a blended teaching model aimed at enhancing the digital application competence of pre-service teachers. Empirical research was conducted using the public course “Modern Educational Technology” for pre-service teachers in higher education as an example. The results validate the effectiveness of the model, demonstrating that it promotes the digital application competence of pre-service teachers, strengthens their ability to utilize digital resources for instructional design, teaching innovation, and reflection, and supports their comprehensive development.

title: Performance Analysis of Voltage-Controlled Ring, Current-Starved, and LC-Tank Oscillators in CMOS Phase-Locked Loop Design
authors: Abhishek Kumar, Kashish Thakur, Rajeevan Chandel
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: A Phase-Locked Loop (PLL) is an important negative feedback closed loop electronics circuit which facilitates locking the phase of its output to an input reference signal. The functional components of PLL consist of Phase Frequency Detector (PFD), Charge Pump (CP), Loop Filter (LF) and Voltage Controlled Oscillator (VCO). This work presents a comparative analysis of different VCO architectures for PLL systems. Three VCO topologies, namely, Ring oscillator, Current- Starved Ring oscillator (CSRO), and LCTank oscillator are considered in the present study. The designs are implemented using a 180 nm CMOS technology node. The key performance metrics includes lock time, settling time, jitter and power efficiency are assessed. The results reveal that the LC Tank oscillator exhibits fast lock and settling times but suffers from power consumption. The CSRO design offers a balanced trade-off between power and frequency stability, making it a more versatile choice for modern communication systems. The implemented circuits are significantly advantageous for various communication and signal processing applications.

title: Determining the Parameters of AC Transmission Line According to Its Losses
authors: Josifs Survilo
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: There are various methods of determining the parameters of transmission lines. Some of them use the geometric dimensions of the line, others are based on modern synchronization measurements, and still others use recalculation. The more accurate and get-at-able methods are necessary in some cases, especially in tropical regions with warm humid climate and increased leakage power losses. Those losses, directly recalculated to line conductance parameter, fail to yield the adequate results compared to those determined by ABCD coefficients. The proposed method of transmission line parameter determination takes into account the losses measured in the sending ends of the idle line.

title: DFECformer: An iTransformer-Based Model with Variable Differential and Frequency Enhanced Channel Attention for Multivariate Time Series Prediction
authors: Desong Chen, Xi Yu, Mengji Yang, Xiaodong Yu, Shichun He
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Time series forecasting is of crucial importance in optimising resource allocation and decision-making across various domains, including finance, healthcare, and industrial automation. However, extant transformer-based models frequently encounter two significant challenges: interference from irrelevant variables, which results in ineffective attention allocation, and insufficient modelling of frequency dependencies, limiting their ability to capture complex patterns in multivariate time series data. These limitations have a substantial impact on the models' performance in real-world forecasting tasks, where variable interactions and frequency characteristics are of critical importance. In order to address these challenges, we propose DFECformer, an enhanced iTransformer-based model that incorporates differential attention in order to suppress noise and emphasise key features, along with frequency-enhanced channel attention to capture the frequency dependencies between channels. A comparison of experimental results with those obtained from other mainstream forecasting models on the publicly available ECL dataset demonstrates that DFECformer achieves significant improvements over the benchmark iTransformer. The mean squared error (MSE) is reduced by 4.7%, and the mean absolute error (MAE) is reduced by 3.8%, the results have been averaged over all four prediction horizons (96, 128, 336, and 720 steps), thus highlighting the model's consistent improvement across different time frames. This finding indicates the substantial efficacy of DFECformer in multivariate forecasting tasks, suggesting that it is a promising approach for general multivariate time series forecasting.

title: Research on Material Life Cycle Management Method Based on Big Data Technology
authors: Zhang Xin, Geng Nan, Feng Jiafeng, Zhang Heng, Luo Yunxin
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: In order to cope with the challenges of process fragmentation, information lag and insufficient decision-making basis in the traditional power material management mode, and to achieve cost reduction, efficiency increase and accurate operation and maintenance, this paper studies a material life cycle management method based on big data technology. A hybrid prediction model combining Transformer deep learning model and HOLT smoothing algorithm is proposed to accurately predict the remaining useful life of the core components of substation equipment. The model gives full play to the advantages of Transformer in capturing the long-term dependence and complex nonlinear characteristics of monitoring data, and combines the HOLT method to smooth the dominant trend, thus significantly improving the prediction accuracy. Through example verification, the model is superior to traditional prediction models such as LSTM and ARMA in key indicators such as RMSE (root mean square error) and MAE (mean absolute error). It provides a highly feasible theoretical model and practical path for power supply enterprises to implement the digital and intelligent life cycle management of substation materials.

title: Research on Short-Term Photovoltaic Power Generation Forecasting Based on TCN-Transformer Model Fused with Cross-Attention Mechanism
authors: Ji Zeng, Xiuqiong Hu, Yunsong Luo, Yuekun Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Short-term accurate forecasting of photovoltaic (PV) power generation holds significant importance for optimizing grid dispatch strategies and enhancing the efficiency of renewable energy consumption. However, due to the dual coupling effects of the dynamic evolution of complex meteorological conditions and the nonlinear characteristics of PV systems, existing methods suffer from insufficient forecasting accuracy. To address this issue, this study proposes a hybrid model (TCN-Transformer-Cross-Attention) that integrates Temporal Convolutional Network (TCN), Transformer, and CrossAttention mechanism. Firstly, raw data undergoes cleaning operations, followed by the selection of features highly correlated with the target variable using the Mean Impact Value (MIV) algorithm. Subsequently, the hybrid model is constructed. TCN is employed to extract spatial features of meteorological factors from PV sequences, and Transformer captures the temporal patterns of historical power generation data. The Cross-Attention mechanism is then utilized to achieve deep fusion of spatiotemporal features, enhancing the model's feature representation capability and ultimately improving forecasting precision.Experimental evaluations are conducted based on real-world data collected during periods of significant fluctuations in 2022 at the Australian Desert Photovoltaic Center. The results demonstrate that, compared to Back Propagation (BP), single TCN, and single Transformer models, the proposed model exhibits significantly improved forecasting accuracy under complex scenarios with frequently changing meteorological conditions. The comprehensive error index is enhanced by 65.37%, 9.55%, and 8.08%, respectively, validating its effectiveness in handling non-stationary time-series forecasting.

title: An Automated Soft Error Evaluation Method for Single Event Effects
authors: Peng Zhang, Xin Chen, Yibo Sheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Space radiation-induced Single Event Effects (SEEs) pose a significant threat to circuit reliability, primarily because soft errors—such as Single Event Upsets (SEUs)—possess low energy thresholds and high probabilities of occurrence. To address this, this paper proposes an automated Register Transfer Level (RTL) SEE evaluation method. This approach facilitates the precise generation of faulty and reference circuits while enhancing the controllability and efficiency of SEU injection. The method is validated using a SHA-256 hash circuit containing 1,617 flip-flops, implemented in a TSMC 28nm process. A total of 1.73 million fault injection experiments were conducted. Experimental results indicate a total SEU error rate of 52.18%, comprising 449,741 Failure cases, 323,415 Silent cases, and 88,705 Latent cases. These findings demonstrate that the platform can efficiently evaluate the SEE sensitivity of large-scale circuits, providing robust support for the optimization of radiation-hardened designs.

title: Design of a V-Band Downconverter Module
authors: Bin Xuan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: A V-band millimeter-wave down-conversion module is designed. The module down-converts V-band signals to L-band signals through two mixings, realizing circuit functions such as signal down-conversion, amplification, filtering, and frequency multiplication. The design of the module mainly includes the analysis of the frequency conversion scheme, circuit layout design, and simulation design. Emphasis is placed on the analysis of links such as the construction of the down-conversion link, the realization method of microwave port connection, and the simulation of the waveguide-microstrip conversion circuit. The test results of the down-conversion module sample meet the design requirements: the test gain is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{2} \text{4 0 d B}$</tex>, the output spurious suppression is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\geq 65 \text{dBc}$</tex>, the input standing wave is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\leq 1.45$</tex>, and the image frequency suppression is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$> \text{2 9 d B}$</tex>, which fully verifies the feasibility of the relevant design.

title: Controlled Electric Vehicle Charging for Optimized Operation of Power Distribution Systems
authors: Illia Diahovchenko, Mykhailo Petrovskyi, Bohdan Nesterenko, Lubov Pertichenko, Anna Mutule
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The integration of electric vehicles into power distribution systems is increasing worldwide, driven by government support and sustainability goals. This study examines the performance of a distribution network with detail-modeled residential loads under varying penetration of electric vehicles and evaluates their impact on peak demand, voltage deviation, phase unbalance, and power losses. The findings show that uncontrolled charging significantly increases evening peak load and causes voltage drops exceeding the $\pm 10 \%$ limit at high penetration levels, while controlled charging effectively mitigates these issues. The results could serve as a basis for distribution system operators to apply controlled charging as a cost-effective measure to manage the growing demand from electric vehicles without extensive grid reinforcements.

title: E- NEXT-TDNN: An Enhanced NEXT-TDNN with Dynamic Weighted Aggregation and Attentive Feature Fusion for Speaker Verification
authors: Bohua Li, Lin Zhang, Ye Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Currently, NEXT -TDNN is one of the state-of-theart deep models for speaker verification (SV) tasks. However, its feature aggregation relies on a static mechanism with simple concatenation or addition, lacking adaptive modeling of framelevel feature importance and global contextual information. To address these limitations, this paper proposes an enhanced NEXT -TDNN (E-NEXT-TDNN) architecture, which incorporates a Dynamic Weighted Aggregation (DWA) mechanism in the main branch and employs an Attentive Feature Fusion (AFF) strategy within the backbone module.
