The Dynamic Weighted Aggregation (DWA) enables the model to dynamically prioritize more informative channel features across different levels through adaptive weighting. The Attentive Feature Fusion leverages channel-wise attention weights to control the fusion ratio between input and output features, enabling the model to dynamically determine which parts of the input should be retained and which parts of the output should be incorporated. The experimental results show that the method we proposed achieves an EER of 0.787% and a minDCF of 0.0788 on the VoxCeleb1-O dataset, which are improved by 5.95% and 14.13% respectively compared with NEXT-TDNN.

title: Algorithmic Optimization and Intelligent Collaboration for End-to-End Warehousing Under Digital-Intelligence Integration
authors: Jia Peng, Rubo Guo, Yu Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: To address the issues of low collaborative efficiency and difficult energy consumption control in warehousing operations, a full-process intelligent optimization framework driven by algorithmic modeling is established. Multi-source perception and semantics are modeled, and Transformer-based task scheduling is proposed. Moreover, by leveraging Support Vector Regression (SVR)-based energy consumption prediction, multi-agent distributed collaboration, and LSTM-attention-based anomaly recovery models, the level of resource sharing is enhanced. The system deployment and algorithm verification were carried out through the cloud-edge collaborative architecture. The results show that this method has significant advantages in terms of scheduling efficiency, energy optimization, and green performance.

title: Finite Element Analysis and Design of Battery Swapping Mechanism for Pure Electric Semi-Trailer Tractors
authors: Cui Fangshuo
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: In view of the problems that the traditional rear-mounted battery box of pure electric semi-trailer tractors will cause load loss and safety hazards, as well as the poor versatility, low battery replacement efficiency, and insufficient durability of the existing bottom-mounted battery swapping mechanism, this paper proposes a set of efficient, universal and reliable bottom-mounted battery swapping mechanism design. The modular main structure design realizes frame adaptation of multiple models, uses an electromagnetically driven multi-point linkage locking device to improve battery replacement efficiency, and optimizes the protective structure under multiple working conditions to enhance durability. Based on CATIA, parametric model is established, static analysis is carried out by ANSYS Workbench, and feasibility of the scheme is verified. With the help of material attribute definition, finite element mesh division and load application under typical working conditions, deformation and stress distribution nephogram of battery box are obtained to verify whether its strength and stiffness meet the design requirements. Two independent locking mechanisms ensure locking reliability; battery box protective housing meets IP6K9K rating.

title: Research on the Impact of Digital Game Experience on Willingness for on-Site Travel
authors: Chen Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This paper takes 506,168 player reviews of Black Myth: Wukong as a sample, and uses the LDA topic clustering algorithm to analyze the influence mechanism of the digital gaming experience on the willingness to travel to real - world destinations. Eight core themes, such as interactive experience, cultural experience, and flow experience, were extracted. It was found that the immersive interactive experience activates tourism imagery, the cultural experience builds cultural identity and tourism willingness, the flow experience strengthens tourism motivation, and the transfer of cross - media experience promotes tourism decision - making. A theoretical model of “digital gaming experience - willingness to travel to real - world destinations” was constructed, revealing the hierarchical influence path. This provides theoretical and practical references for the integration of the digital gaming and cultural tourism industries.

title: A Conflict Detection Method for User Story Acceptance Criteria in Low-Code Development Scenarios
authors: Weili Hu, Zhuoning Zhao, Liang Cao, Kui Liu, Yuanma Du
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In low code development scenarios, user story acceptance criteria are used to characterize verifiable constraints on system behavior in business processes, and their cross-cuttingly coupled nature driven by business processes makes it difficult to identify requirement conflicts in a timely manner. In response to this issue, this article proposes a conflict detection method for user story acceptance criteria for low code development. From the perspective of business processes, this article introduces the concept of cross-cutting requirements, structurally represents acceptance criteria and reconstructs data, and constructs a conflict type system. On this basis, conflict detection is modeled as a multi classification problem between acceptance criteria sentence pairs, and an EA-BERTBC-MC deep semantic reasoning model is designed. The experimental results based on real enterprise business data show that this method achieves better performance than the basic model in multi class conflict recognition tasks, verifying the effectiveness of cross-cutting perspective and structured representation in low code requirement conflict detection.

title: Nano Influencers and Their Impact on Beauty Product Purchase Intentions Among Gen-Z in Indonesia
authors: Levinka Esthaliani, Iston Dwija Utama
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Nano influencers, individuals with a smaller but highly engaged social media following (typically 1,000 to 10,000 followers), have gained significant attention in recent years due to their perceived authenticity and relatability. This research focuses on understanding how nano-influencers impact the decision-making process of Gen-Z consumers, who are known for their digital tension and authentic content preferences. Data is collected through a quantitative method using an online survey distributed to Gen Z individuals in Indonesia who actively follow nano influencers on platforms such as Instagram and TikTok. This study will analyze the relationship between nano influencer credibility, content authenticity, engagement levels, and the purchase intentions of beauty products. The findings are expected to contribute to the literature on influencer marketing, particularly the effectiveness of nano influencers in driving consumer behavior. This study also provides practical insights for beauty brands in engaging Gen-Z consumers in Indonesia through nano influencer strategies.

title: Real-Time Responsiveness & Communication Validation Platform for CubeSats
authors: Abdellah Essetty, Fadwa Belali, Amina Daghouri, Slimane Bah, Zouhair Guennoun
publish: IEEE Journal on Miniaturization for Air and Space Systems
abstract: This paper presents a real-time testing platform designed to evaluate the timing behavior and communication reliability of CubeSat subsystems. At its core, the platform integrates a custom logger, along with mock software modules that simulate key CubeSat subsystems. These modules are implemented using the Zephyr Real-Time Operating System and communicate using the CubeSat Space Protocol over a Controller Area Network bus. The system supports controlled and repeatable experimentation, enabling precise assessment of message latency, deadline adherence, and inter-task communication dynamics. In addition, the platform offers extensible interfaces to support the integration and testing of flight software components under realistic operational constraints. The proposed platform facilitates early-stage design validation and performance characterization within the CubeSat development lifecycle, offering deeper insights into inter-subsystem communication behavior. This enables development teams to deliver more reliable software and produce more accurate data link budgets, with particular emphasis on timing determinism and subsystem interaction fidelity.

title: Fabrication, Characterization, and Optimization of Robust Sensing Materials for MEMS LC Resonators in Harsh Aqueous Environments
authors: Deepak Bansal, Khushbu Singh Raghav, Dheeraj Kumar Kharbanda, Amit Kumar, Deepak Panwar, Dhirendra Kumar, Somak Chatterjee, Mahesh Angira
abstract: Sensing in harsh aqueous environments remains a critical challenge due to the limited lifetime and reliability of conventional electrode-based sensors. While laboratory-based techniques such as mass spectrometry, infrared spectroscopy, Raman spectroscopy, chromatography, filtration, multiple fermentation tube techniques, DNA amplification, capillary electrophoresis, field-flow fractionation, and fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) technique offer high accuracy for heavy metal ion detection, their complexity, cost, and lack of portability restrict their use for on-site monitoring. Resonant sensing, such as MEMS-based LC resonators, offers a promising alternative; however, their long-term performance is often constrained by chemical degradation of electrode materials when exposed to harsh aqueous media. Thus, in the presented work, a hard and chemically inert multilayer electrode stack (Ti/TiN/Al/Ti/TiN) is developed, with material selection optimized using the TOPSIS approach, and deposition is optimized within a single sputtering system. Titanium nitride is employed as the top protective layer due to its excellent chemical and mechanical stability under both acidic and alkaline conditions. The robustness of the proposed materials stack is experimentally validated across a wide pH range representative of industrial runoff, river water, and seawater, demonstrating negligible degradation in resonator performance. As a case study, lead nitrate is introduced to illustrate the sensor response, where changes in the dielectric and conductive properties of the aqueous medium result in measurable shifts in the resonator’s S21 parameter. Both simulated and measured results show good agreement, confirming the viability of the proposed platform. The presented materials stack and resonator architecture provide a robust sensing platform, which can be readily extended to resonant sensing of various analytes in harsh aqueous environments.

title: A Thin Pinhole-Based Tactile Sensor with Rotational Scanning for Field-of-View Expansion
authors: Yuan Gao, Shixin Zhang, Weiwei Wan, Bin Fang, Fuchun Sun, Kensuke Harada
abstract: Vision-based tactile sensors (VBTSs) play a crucial role in robotic manipulation tasks that require high-resolution contact information. However, existing VBTS designs (i.e. the GelSight sensors) often suffer from excessive bulk due to the use of traditional optical lenses. Recently, pinhole-based imaging has been introduced as a means of reducing sensor thickness. Despite its success, the approach significantly restricts the sensor’s field of view (FOV). In this paper, we propose a novel VBTS architecture that overcomes the limitation through the integration of a rotating pinhole mechanism. By rotating the sensing module, the sensor captures a series of overlapping tactile images from distinct viewpoints. The image series is then computationally fused into a single composite one to effectively expand the overall sensing area. The proposed design achieves an approximately twofold increase in FOV compared to conventional configurations. Comparative experiments demonstrate the sensor’s capability for accurate 3D surface reconstruction, highlighting its potential for compact and versatile tactile sensing applications.

title: Aspect-based Sentiment Analysis in Ho Chi Minh city Hotel Reviews using Aspect-Conditioned BiLSTM with Cross-Aspect Attention and Ordinal Regression
authors: Tam Khoi Tran, Phuoc Tran
abstract: The rapid growth of tourism in Ho Chi Minh City has generated a huge volume of Vietnamese hotel reviews, creating an urgent demand for effective aspect-based sentiment analysis (ABSA). This paper introduces a newlarge-scale benchmark dataset of 20,290 manually annotated reviews collected from Agoda, Google Reviews, and Traveloka, covering ten hotel aspects with 5-point ordinal sentiment labels (-2 to +2). The dataset is publicly available at https://github.com/tamkhojj/AC-BiLSTM represents the large and comprehensive Ho Chi Minh city tourism ABSA resource to date.We further propose AC-BiLSTM, a novel model that combines PhoBERT embeddings with aspect-conditioned BiLSTM, multi-head aspect attention, cross-aspect attention, aspect-conditioned gating, and ordinal regression loss. Extensive experiments show that AC-BiLSTM significantly outperforms strong LSTM-based baselines, achieving a macro F1-score of 0.8899, Precision of 0.8921, Recall of 0.8880, and Accuracy of 0.9456. Ablation studies and error analysis confirm the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed components. The dataset are publicly released to facilitate future research on low-resource Vietnamese ABSA.

title: [News Products]
authors: Ken Mays

title: A Novel Approach for Accurate SOC Estimation of Lithium-Ion Electric Vehicle Batteries Using a (Q, S, R)- γ -Based Dissipativity Observer
authors: R. Manivannan, K. Vinothini, Jinde Cao
abstract: For the first time, this article presents a dissipativity-based observer design for accurate state-of-charge (SOC) estimation, essential for improving the safety, performance, and lifespan of lithium-ion batteries (LIBs) in electric vehicle (EV) battery management systems (BMSs). However, model uncertainties and measurement noise significantly affect estimation accuracy. To address this, a novel observer design based on (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mathcal {Q}, \mathcal {S}, \mathcal {R}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>)-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\gamma $</tex-math> </inline-formula>-dissipativity theory is developed, formulated within a linear matrix inequality (LMI) framework, and integrated with the Lyapunov–Krasovskii functional (LKF) approach. The proposed observer ensures robustness and stability in SOC estimation under uncertain and noisy conditions. A one-resistor capacitor (1-RC) equivalent circuit model (ECM) is adopted for battery modeling, with experimental validation performed on a Panasonic 18650PF cell. The proposed method is compared against the adaptive unscented Kalman filter (AUKF) under four drive cycles: the urban dynamometer driving schedule (UDDS), the aggressive US06 supplemental federal test procedure, the Los Angeles 92 (LA92), and the highway fuel economy test (HWFET). Results show that the proposed observer achieves root-mean-square errors (RMSEs) of 0.77%, 0.50%, 0.65%, and 0.48% and mean absolute errors (MAEs) of 0.59%, 0.42%, 0.50%, and 0.40% under UDDS, US06, LA92, and HWFET, respectively. This corresponds to RMSE reductions of 28.38%, 88.93%, 67.25%, and 38.35% compared with AUKF. Notably, the proposed method achieves a maximum accuracy of 99.23%, surpassing the latest reported accuracy of 98.50%.

title: Topology-Aligned Gated Interaction Network for Cross-Domain Hyperspectral Image Classification
authors: Cuiyu Yang, Lin Gao, Yongxin Feng, Junchang Xin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Deep learning algorithms have shown great potential in the field of domain adaptation (DA) in unsupervised learning. However, in the task of cross-scene hyperspectral image classification, current DA methods often focus only on local features or semantic information while ignoring the complementary relationship between the source and target domains. Thus, the cross-domain feature discrepancy limits the adaptation performance. To address these issues, we propose a novel topologically aligned gated interaction network (TAGIN) for cross-scene hyperspectral image classification. First, we introduce the Bidirectional Feature Interaction Network (BFIN), a novel framework in which CNN-extracted local features dynamically interact with Transformer-derived semantic representations through a gating mechanism, forming dual channel feature information. As a core part of BFIN, a cross-domain Spectral Space Cross-Attention (SSCA) mechanism is designed, combined with the bidirectional dynamic feature retention mechanism, to achieve adaptive feature fusion and cross-domain feature selection. Second, to achieve the feature alignment, we design a dynamic adaptive topology alignment loss, which is gradually optimized via memory enhancement and dynamic parameter adjustment mechanisms. Finally, we propose a topology-aware multiview consensus pseudo-label optimization strategy (TA-MVCPO). High-confidence pseudo-labels are screened out through data augmentation samples, and then the credibility of the pseudo-labels is verified through topological structure difference perception. The training model was fine-tuned based on high-confidence pseudo-labels, enhancing the adaptability of the training model to the target domain data. The experimental results conducted on four cross-scenario hyperspectral image datasets demonstrate the superior performance of our proposed method.

title: A Skin-Integrated Force-Electrical Coupling Haptic Interface for Muscle Fatigue Reduction and Tactile Reproduction
authors: Xiangyang Lin, Jianhua Zhang, Hui Li, Peng Zhou, Yufei Hao
abstract: Haptic feedback technology shows great potential in medical rehabilitation and augmented reality, significantly enhancing immersion and feedback accuracy in human–computer interaction. However, most existing haptic feedback devices rely on a single feedback mode and lack flexibility, strong force output, and effective multimodal haptic reproduction. In this article, we propose a thin, soft, dual-mode flexible tactile actuator that delivers high-fidelity haptic feedback through combined force and electrical stimulation. The actuator operates stably within a wide frequency range (0 Hz–150 Hz) and can output a normal force of up to 363 mN with a displacement of 0.96 mm. The current threshold is 1.2 mA, exceeding the tactile perception threshold of human skin. The actuator enhances electrical tactile stimulation by force assistance in the force tactile mode, allowing the generated force to activate deeper skin receptors. This coupling effect reduces the current threshold, alleviates muscle fatigue, and enables accurate texture recognition with an accuracy of 94% for rough textures and 100% for smooth textures, approximating real tactile perception. This tactile technology shows great potential for applications in muscle rehabilitation and force feedback.

title: Research on the Emission Uniformity and Lifetime of Graphite Cathode Improved by In Situ Growth of Silicon Carbide Particles
authors: Ye Hua, Ping Wu, Jun Sun, Yibing Cao, Bei Liu
abstract: Explosive emission cathodes (EECs) are extensively employed in relativistic backward wave oscillators (RBWOs) for the generation of high-power microwave (HPM). The explosive electron emission (EEE) uniformity and service lifetime are typically the focus of cathode research. This article is centered on the foilless diode configuration in a RBWO; the performance of traditional graphite cathode and graphite cathode modified with silicon carbide particles in terms of emission uniformity and lifetime was compared and analyzed. The experimental results indicate that the enhancement with silicon carbide particles markedly improves the angular uniformity of plasma emission from the cathode, while concurrently demonstrating exceptional efficacy in diminishing the material loss at the periphery of the cathode blade. The comprehensive analysis utilizing the particle-in-cell (PIC) simulation methodology has elucidated that the primary reason for this phenomenon is the dielectric characteristic of silicon carbide, which results in a pronounced disparity in the electric field distribution on the surface of protrusion compared to that of the graphite conductor during the electron emission process. The peak electric field strength on the dielectric protrusion progressively migrates from the apex to the surrounding areas, and then the electron emission zone expands from the tip of the protrusion toward its surrounding area, eventually encompassing the entire surface of the dielectric protrusion. Consequently, the effective area of the dielectric protrusion participating in electron emission is larger, and the average current density is smaller. This, in turn, facilitates a more uniform electron emission process and mitigates material degradation.

title: A Regression Model for Enterprise Tax Burden with Empirical Analysis
authors: Xiaoyan Zheng, Mengsi Hu, Xiaotong Wu, Yongsi Wu
publish: Proceedings of the International Conference on Digital Economy and Information Technology
abstract: None

title: Understanding Student Engagement through Technology Acceptance and Academic Emotions
authors: Jing Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The integration of technology into higher education has significantly reshaped how students engage with learning. Grounded in the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and the Control-Value Theory of Achievement Emotions (CVT), this study examines a chain mediation model in which perceived usefulness and learning enjoyment sequentially mediate the relationship between perceived ease of use and student engagement in technology-enhanced learning environments. Empirical data were collected from 205 university students who participated in smart classroom instruction. Structural equation modeling (SEM) was employed to test the hypothesized relationships among the constructs. The results indicate that perceived ease of use significantly predicts perceived usefulness, which in turn enhances learning enjoyment. Learning enjoyment was found to be a strong predictor of student engagement, while the direct effects of perceived ease of use and perceived usefulness on engagement were not significant. Mediation analysis confirmed a significant chain mediation effect from perceived ease of use to engagement via perceived usefulness and learning enjoyment. These findings highlight the importance of integrating both cognitive appraisals and emotional responses in understanding how technology influences student engagement. The study extends existing theoretical models by incorporating positive emotional constructs and offers practical implications for designing emotionally engaging, user-friendly educational technologies in smart classroom contexts.

title: Capacitor Behavior in Rectifier Circuits in EnergyEfficient LED under Harmonic Conditions: Modeling and Experimental Validation
authors: Kamran Daniel, Lauri Kütt, Matti Lehtonen, Noman Shabbir, Muhammad Naveed Iqbal, Rana Muhammad Arslan Qadar
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Rectifier-based LEDs are commonly used in residential and commercial lighting. These nonlinear loads generate current harmonics that affect the power system. Capacitors, which are essential for voltage smoothing, exhibit specific switching behavior when voltage harmonics are present. This paper presents a MatLab-based model to evaluate the load current waveform and harmonic content of a typical rectifierbased LED circuit. The model emphasizes the role of the DCside capacitor in shaping rectifier conduction, which influences both the magnitude and phase of current harmonics. For validation, custom voltage waveforms containing a single harmonic at defined phase angles were applied to the LED load, and the resulting current was compared with simulated results. The comparison shows that the model accurately reproduces the load current waveform and its harmonic spectrum, confirming its suitability for harmonic analysis and evaluation.

title: Invisible Periodic Energy Control of Modular Multilevel Converters
authors: Simon Kappenstein, Lutz Gröll, Veit Hagenmeyer
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: A new approach for controlling the internal energy of modular multilevel converters (MMCs) is being developed. The control of the energy distribution to the six branches is designed via secondary control loop that uses redundant input variables. Its behavior remains invisible for the primary control of the three phase currents. The new control scheme replaces the traditional scheme that controls the mean energy by an instantaneous realization. An essential contribution is the derivation of a linear time-periodic model that is suited for the design of an invisible energy control. After proving the uniform complete controllability of the model, the stabilizing periodic linear quadratic controller is designed. A simulation on a digital twin shows the closed loop performance.

title: Research and Implementation of Path Planning Algorithm for Autonomous Vehicles
authors: Min Yan, Zhangling Yan
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This paper addresses the path planning problem for autonomous vehicles, with the objective of determining the shortest path between two locations using Dijkstra algorithm. Initially, high-definition (HD) maps are modeled as graphs. Subsequently, Dijkstra algorithm is applied to compute the shortest path. The algorithm is implemented in C++ using an adjacency list and a priority queue to enhance computational efficiency. The time complexity of the proposed implementation is analyzed and determined to be <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{O}((\vert \mathrm{V}\vert +\vert \mathrm{E}\vert) \log\vert \mathrm{V}\vert)$</tex>. The correctness of the algorithm is validated through experiments on a sample graph, where it successfully identifies the shortest paths and their corresponding lengths. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in global path planning for autonomous driving systems, offering a general route that can be further refined in response to real-time environmental dynamics.

title: Energy Production and Emission Reduction Potential of VIPV Systems for Urban Trams
authors: Hamid Samadi, Guido Ala, Miguel Centeno Brito, Silvia Licciardi, Pietro Romano, Fabio Viola
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This study evaluates the feasibility of integrating photovoltaic (PV) panels into tram roofs, using Line 1 of the Palermo tram network in Italy as a case study. Solar irradiance was modeled dynamically based on tram motion data and meteorological inputs to estimate the energy yield from two PV technologies: crystalline silicon (c-Si) and copper indium gallium selenide (CIGS). The results indicate that c-Si and CIGS modules can produce up to approximately 256 kWh/year and 177 kWh/year per square meter of installed PV, respectively. Environmental benefits were quantified in terms of avoided greenhouse gas emissions. Economic viability was assessed using a levelized cost of electricity (LCOE) approach, considering grid parity under local electricity rates. Assuming an electricity price of 0.26 €/kWh and a 10-year system lifetime, the maximum system prices to reach grid parity were calculated as 2.608 €/$W_{p}$ for c-Si and 2.503 €/$W_{{p}}$ for CIGS.

title: An Optimization Method for Point Cloud Object Classification Networks Based on PointNet
authors: Yi Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: A systematic investigation is conducted into efficient algorithmic strategies for 3D point cloud processing in deep neural networks, with emphasis on a co-design methodology that jointly optimizes software algorithms and hardware-aware architectural structures. Building upon the PointNet framework, the network undergoes progressive enhancement through structured pruning, multi-level feature fusion, attention-based refinement, and depth reconfiguration. These optimizations substantially reduce computational complexity while explicitly aligning network depth and feature dimensions with hardware-efficient execution, thereby improving both recognition robustness and deployment efficiency on resource-constrained platforms. Experimental results show that the optimized architecture achieves an 80% reduction in total parameters and a $1.65 \times$ improvement in training throughput, alongside an accuracy increase from 88.18% to $\mathbf{9 1. 8 5} \boldsymbol{\%}$. The co-designed solution therefore offers a compact and high-performance model suitable for deployment on resource-constrained platforms.

title: Reform and Application of Teaching in Accordance with Students' Aptitude in New Engineering Specialty
authors: Liang Tang, Xingang Xie, Hong Yu, Hongjian Huang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Teaching students according to their aptitude is an important principle in the educational work of schools in China. However, in the practice of teaching in according with student's aptitude(TASA) in new engineering specialties, the implementation has not achieved satisfactory results. This is mainly because new engineering courses are content-heavy, have fewer class hours, and possess strong foundational elements with significant knowledge differences. In the traditional model of TASA, teachers are often simply classified based on their academic performance, with less consideration given to individual differences. These individual differences refer to students' social attitudes and behaviors, psychological traits, and personal awareness. This paper proposes the use of big data to identify students' individual differences and apply modern science and technology to predict their paths to success. After identifying students' individual differences, the paper discusses several teaching models based on aptitude, including the credit system, stratified teaching, and mentorship systems. The article concludes by stating that only by utilizing modern science and technology in a guidance-based teaching model can the development of personalized talents for students reach a new level.

title: Primary Beam Effect Elimination for Mid-Frequency SKA Imaging Using a Uformer Network
authors: Yuxin Xia, Li Zhang, Xinglong Wang, Zujuan Xu, Ming Zhang, Long Xu, Zhibin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: As the next generation of radio telescopes, the Square Kilometre Array (SKA) has high resolution and sensitivity to support major scientific tasks such as deep space exploration. However, non-ideal responses in the primary beam can introduce imaging distortions, adversely affecting scientific outputs. Traditional elimination methods struggle to effectively handle the high complexity and dynamic variations inherent in the SKA. Therefore, this paper uses the Uformer model to eliminate the primary beam effect of Mid-frequency SKA. This paper is based on 1500 simulated data in the Galaxy Zoo, and after model training and testing, the experimental results show that the average index of PSNR, RMSE and SSIM of the reconstruction image are 37.87 dB, 0.0108,0.9438. Compared with the dirty image of the primary beam effect, the Uformer method improves the PSNR and SSIM by 18 dB and 0.8642, respectively. The results show that the method can effectively improve the image quality, providing algorithm support for high-precision science of SKA.

title: Agent-Based Traffic Monitoring and Regulation System for SDN
authors: Xiaodan Guo, Falong Zhou, Weihao Du, Mengdie Deng, Xun Zhu, Juming Bao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: The management of modern networks is becoming increasingly complex, and while Software-Defined Networking (SDN) offers programmability, existing control mechanisms often lack flexibility and operational intuitiveness due to heavy reliance on manual configurations or opaque machine learning models. To address this limitation, we propose the indirect integration of a Large Language Model (LLM)-powered conversational agent into the SDN control loop, implementing the ReAct (Reasoning + Acting) paradigm as our core innovation to synergize natural language reasoning with concrete network operations. This agent utilizes a specialized toolkit that translates human conversational intents both into SQL queries (via a Text-to-SQL engine) for deep network state analysis and into REST API calls (through OpenFlow rule modifications) for real-time traffic regulation, validated through comprehensive emulation-based evaluation.

title: ROS2-Based Target Tracking and Recording Aircraft Using YOLOv11n
authors: Zixi Chen, Yanqing Li, Yu Cao, Xingyu Liu, Qingbin Huang, Huajin Long
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The advent of drones marks a significant milestone in the advancement of aviation technology and intelligent control systems. Achieving safe autonomous navigation within human-drone coexistence airspace is a core prerequisite for the practical application of drones in civil and industrial scenarios. Given the characteristics of indoor and outdoor low-altitude environments—such as randomly distributed obstacles and complex spatial structures—autonomous drone navigation demands not only the planning of optimal flight paths but also real-time dynamic obstacle avoidance capabilities. Consequently, research into path planning and obstacle avoidance technologies holds critical importance for the development of the drone industry.

title: Third-Party Privacy Data Leak Analysis on Ontario Hospital Websites
authors: Tarun Kalyani, Justin Stewart, Yan Yan, Sampsa Rauti, Ville Leppänen, Zuhaibuddin Bhutto, Wenjun Lin
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This study investigates the privacy concerns associated with third-party data leaks on hospital websites in Ontario, Canada. The study employs both manual and automated analytics to investigate the privacy practices of 135 hospital websites in Ontario. This dual approach entails a thorough evaluation of websites while gathering and evaluating datasharing policies. The findings show substantial variation in datasharing procedures, with some hospital websites disclosing user data to third parties without explicit agreement, posing serious privacy issues. The study uncovers trends and abnormalities in data transfers, emphasizing the need for regulatory measures and enforcement to secure patient information. Furthermore, the findings highlight the critical need for strong privacy safeguards and regulatory frameworks in the healthcare industry. The study also makes practical recommendations for improving data privacy on hospital websites.

title: Aero-Engines' Fault Diagnosis Based on 1DCNN-BiLSTM
authors: Jiaju Wu, Linggang Kong, Ze Xiu, Yao Pan, XiaoDong Wang, Chuan Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Aircraft engines are key components of airplanes. During its lifecycle, an aircraft engine may experience various types of failures due to different flight conditions. By using certain intelligent algorithms to calculate and analyze real-time status monitoring data, real-time diagnosis and prediction of aircraft engine failure can be made, ensuring safe flight of the aircraft and avoiding accidents of aircraft destruction and human casualties. This article proposes a fault mode diagnosis method for aircraft engines based on 1DCNN-BiLSTM. Realtime detection of operational degradation trends during the lifecycle of aircraft engines using 1DCNN-BiLSTM. This method can directly calculate and analyze the degradation of aircraft engines based on QuickAccess Recorder (QAR) and Digital Flight Data Recorder (DFDR) data, without the need to integrate other algorithms to extract fault degradation features. This algorithm fully utilizes the advantages of 1DCNN in extracting local features in the time dimension and BiLSTM in handling nonlinear time series well and utilizing bidirectional contextual information. Finally, fully connected layers are connected to learn the final feature information, and a softmax function is used to diagnose fault categories. The paper categorizes the fault modes into three types: no fault, HPC fault, and HPC&Fan fault. The proposed algorithm has been validated to have higher diagnostic accuracy using the NASA CMAPSS aircraft engine dataset. This fault diagnosis method has practical value in improving the operational reliability of aircraft engines and further predicting the remaining useful life (RUL).

title: Joint Trajectory and Resource Optimization for UAVISAC via Deep Reinforcement Learning with Reward Shaping
authors: Jiaze Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: UAV-Integrated Sensing and Communication (UAV-ISAC) technology, which enables simultaneous communication and environmental sensing on a unified hardware platform, shows vast potential for applications in smart cities and emergency rescue. However, jointly optimizing the UAV's flight trajectory and ISAC resource allocation under stringent energy constraints to achieve the optimal trade-off among communication quality, sensing performance, and energy consumption presents a highly challenging multi-objective optimization problem. This paper models the problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) and an online decision-making framework founded on Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is presented. At the framework's core is the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm, complemented by a novel composite reward shaping design. This design incorporates a “Progress Reward” to overcome reward sparsity by encouraging consistent movement toward the target, along with an “Action Smoothness Penalty” to discourage abrupt maneuvers for better stability and power conservation. In a simulated <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 m} \times \mathbf{1 0 0 m} \mathbf{2 D}$</tex> environment, after 500,000 training steps, the agent's average reward per episode settled near 6000, with a mean survival of 130 out of 200 steps. In comparison to a greedy approach, the agent boosted its mission success rate from 35 % to 78 % and extended its average operational duration by about 49 %. These outcomes show a marked improvement over both random and greedy policies. The DRL framework demonstrates its capability to handle the intricate UAV-ISAC optimization task without needing a precise environmental model, indicating its potential for practical use.

title: Grid Heat-driven Imaging Satellite Multi-type Task Planning Method
authors: Shilong Xu, Yingwu Chen, Yingguo Chen, Yuning Chen
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Earth Observation Satellites (EOS) are critical for acquiring space-based information, supporting diverse applications from environmental monitoring to urban planning. The increasing demand for satellite imaging services necessitates efficient planning to handle complex and heterogeneous observation tasks. Traditional mission planning approaches, often relying on “single objective, single model, single algorithm” paradigms with meta-tasks and time windows, struggle to integrate these diverse requirements and face scalability issues with increasing task complexity. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a novel Grid Heat-driven Imaging Satellite Complex Task Planning Method. We introduce a Grid Heat-based Requirements Observation Model (GHROM) that unifies point, area, and moving targets into a single, time-variant geospatial heatmap, where grid cell “heat” represents observation priority. For this gridbased representation, we design a Satellite Discrete State Task Planning Model that transforms the problem into finding the highest heat gain path within a Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) of discrete satellite states, where attitude transition constraints are embedded as graph edges. Based on this model, we develop a Grid Heat-Driven Dynamic Programming Algorithm (GMDPA). Extensive simulation experiments demonstrate the effectiveness and significant advantages of GMDPA, particularly in largescale scenarios, showcasing its superior performance in terms of solution quality and computational efficiency compared to traditional heuristic, metaheuristic, and genetic algorithms.

title: A DNN-Driven Approach For Cyberattack Classification In IoT Systems Using Edge-IIoTset
authors: Banu Partap Singh, Ashu Taneja
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Due to resource limitations, architectural hetero-geneity, and vulnerability to emerging cyber-attacks, the Internet of Things (IoT) and Industrial IoT (IIoT) devices’ fast expansion has increased security challenges. In such dynamic situations, tra- ditional intrusion detection systems (IDS) that rely on rules and signatures frequently fall short in identifying new or advanced threats. This study suggests a deep learning-based intrusion detection system that uses a specially created Deep Neural Network (DNN) to classify network traffic anomalies into binary and multi-class categories. For training and assessment, the Edge- HoTset dataset—which includes a variety of well-labelled IoT traffic samples representing fifteen attack categories—is used. To guarantee strong model generalization, the suggested approach incorporates a thorough preprocessing pipeline that includes feature selection, scaling, categorical encoding, and stratified data division. The DNN’s two output heads and four hidden layers allow for both fine-grained attack classification and simultaneous binary anomaly detection. With an overall F1-score of 0.87 and a weighted F1-score of 0.97 on the test set, the experimental results show good performance and high reliability in identifying both common and uncommon attack types. The model’s lightweight design allows it to function well on edge and fog devices, which makes it appropriate for real-time intrusion detection in real- world IoT deployments.

title: An Overview of Smart and Essential Features in ACDC Interlinking Converters for DC Buildings
authors: Salamat Ali, Edivan Laercio Carvalho, Dmitri Vinnikov
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The development of dc standardization plays a critical role in the transition to dc-powered buildings. A key requirement in such systems is the isolation between ac and dc parts. A commonly employed solution is the two-stage isolated active front-end (i-AFE) converter, which serves as a grid interface, or interlinking converter between the ac grid and the de distribution inside buildings. This converter is expected to provide functionalities beyond simply interlinking the grids. These functionalities are categorized into three groups: mandatory and important features for (1) ac grid compatibility; (2) dc grid compatibility; and (3) advanced and smart features. However, there is a lack of literature review and standardization specifically related to the interlinking converter. To cover this gap, this paper presents the categorization of these features and identifies the directives for the future design of interlinking converters for the dc building applications.

title: An Investigation of Chinese EFL Pre-Service Teachers' Use of of AI During Teaching Practicum
authors: Qimin Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study examines the integration of artificial intelligence (AI) in teaching practicum among pre-service teachers specializing in English as a Foreign Language (EFL). As AI continues to reshape secondary English language teaching, the knowledge and attitude of AI integration among pre-service teachers have become a critical factor influencing the effectiveness of smart education. This study investigates the current level of technological knowledge of AI integration, their attitude, and ethical awareness of 20 pre-service teachers during a three-month teaching practicum in middle schools. Quantitative data were collected via an 18-item Likert-scale questionnaire covering three dimensions. The results indicate that while pre-service teachers demonstrate moderate capability in integrating AI with pedagogical content knowledge (TPACK) and exhibit positive attitude for technology use, they often fall into the trap of “using technology for technology's sake,” revealing limitations in tool selection and ethical awareness. The findings offer empirical support for developing a gradient model of AI literacy and designing a differentiated training system for pre-service teachers, promoting deeper integration of AI in teacher education.

title: Deep Ensemble-Based Multi-Class Classification of Sports Injuries Using EMG and Kinematic Features
authors: Gurmeet Kaur, Yogesh Kumar
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Accurate classification of sports-related injuries is critical for timely intervention and rehabilitation planning. This paper explores a comprehensive ensemble-based approach using electromyography (EMG) signals and kinematic data for multi-class classification of sports injuries, specifically targeting knee injuries, ankle sprains, and hip injuries. A series of traditional, ensemble, and hybrid models are trained and evaluated using biomechanical dataset. Principal Component Analysis (PCA) is used for feature dimensionality reduction and interpretability of biomechanical variance. Among the tested classifiers, the hybrid Transformer + XGBoost model achieved the highest performance with an accuracy of 96.5%, a log loss of 0.16, and an AUC-ROC of 0.988. Other hybrid configurations, such as CNN + XGBoost and LSTM + Random Forest, demonstrated competitive results, reinforcing the importance of both spatial and temporal feature integration. The paper highlights the efficacy of advanced ensemble techniques in enhancing injury classification accuracy and provides a foundation for real-time, sensor-based diagnostic applications in sports medicine.

title: Research on the Screening of Translation Factors of Shenggong Palace Murals Based on One-Cut Technology
authors: Guo Huiting, Lin Ke, Xie Zhengyang, Cai Yanni, Lian Xiaobo, Zhou Linping
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: To improve the decision-making efficiency and design quality of Shenggong Palace murals in the design of cultural and creative products. Firstly, the improved One-Cut image processing technology is used to perform image segmentation and feature extraction on the mural image. Secondly, the Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) is used to systematically sort out its modeling factors and construct a hierarchical analysis structure model. Finally, the comprehensive weights of each modeling factor are calculated and ranked to screen and determine the design factors. The combined use of One-Cut technology and hierarchical analysis not only improves the efficiency and accuracy of image processing in the design process, but also scientifically and accurately screens design factors for the cultural and creative products of the Shenggong Palace murals.

title: AgriDOE: End-to-End DOE Optimization for Single-Shot Agricultural Spatial Classification
authors: Sebastian Ardila Leal, Pablo Gómez, Hans García, Henry Arguello
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Precision agriculture leverages remote sensing and AI models to monitor crop conditions, but traditional RGB and panchromatic sensors are limited by their fixed optics and reduced spectral selectivity. These limitations hinder the accurate classification of materials in complex agricultural scenes. This work proposes a compact and passive imaging system based on a diffractive optical element (DOE), co-optimized with a spatial classification model via an end-to-end (E2E) learning strategy. The DOE is parameterized through Zernike polynomials and embedded within a differentiable light propagation model based on angular spectrum theory. The classification network is trained using a single timestamp from the CALCROP21 dataset, ensuring realistic supervision while reducing temporal complexity. Two sensor configurations are evaluated, RGB and panchromatic, each compared under a traditional imaging system using a lens and an imaging system based on a DOE. Additionally, this work includes a simulated $\mathbf{1 0}$-band multispectral configuration as an upper-bound baseline without DOE co-optimization; in this regime the optical front end behaves as a fixed bandwise convolution and performance is dominated by the richer spectral input, which allows the results to calibrate the attainable ceiling against which the DOE-augmented 1-band and 3-band systems are compared. The results show consistent gains in spatial classification accuracy for both configurations, with improvements of up to 19.7% in test accuracy when comparing the 1-band model with a trainable DOE against its non-trainable counterpart. Additional ablation studies highlight the impact of DOE initialization and demonstrate robustness to additive noise, confirming the effectiveness of task-specific DOE design for embedded agricultural vision systems. This work relies on a framework validated initially through simulation, wherein a differentiable forward model that integrates a DOE and angularspectrum propagation generates panchromatic, RGB, and 10band observations from CALCROP21.

title: Optimized Inductive Generative Recommendation with Probabilistic Beam Filtering for Dynamic Video Game Personalization
authors: Siyu Lin
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This study addresses the cold-start problem in recommender systems for digital gaming platforms, where frequent new game releases and shifting user interests challenge traditional models. Effective recommendation systems are crucial here to enhance user engagement and adapt to dynamic item pools. This paper proposes an optimized SpecGR framework, an inductive generative recommendation system that integrates a retrieval-based inductive drafter (UniSRec) with a target-aware generative verifier (TIGER). A key feature is probabilistic beam filtering, which balances exploration and utilization to improve recommendations. Evaluated on the Video Games category from the Amazon Reviews 2023 dataset, the preprocessing retained users and items with at least five interactions, followed by a temporal split to simulate real-world scenarios with unseen items. Hyperparameter optimization was conducted using Optuna. The optimized SpecGR framework demonstrates superior recommendation quality in inductive settings. Probabilistic beam filtering yields consistent improvements in Recall@K and NDCG@K metrics over baseline methods. This approach generalizes effectively to unseen items without retraining, thus mitigating the cold-start problem while maintaining competitive performance in familiar environments. These findings highlight the framework's potential for delivering scalable and flexible recommendations in dynamic domains, advancing the field of generative recommendation systems.

title: Improvement of Massive-Data Storage and Retrieval Performance Based on the Hadoop Ecosystem
authors: Shengzhe Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: With the continuous expansion of the big data era, traditional Hadoop architecture faces great challenges in achieving high-performance storage and fast retrieval of massive data. In response to the above problems, this paper proposes a performance-enhancement model based on the Hadoop ecosystem, Hi-LHDFS (Hierarchical-indexed and Load-aware HDFS Enhancement Model). This model preserves the core framework of HDFS while realizing the following three major innovations: (1) A multi-level adaptive indexing mechanism is constructed. The global hash metadata index and the local data node cache structure are constructed based on the Bloom filter technology, which greatly improves the location method and access efficiency of the data block. (2) The cost-aware load balancing scheduler, which predicts the times of the data usage of the future via Deep Recurrent Network (DRN), enables the dynamic adjustment of the data block and the efficient resource scheduling. (3) Query-oriented replica placement strategy. This strategy regards historical job access behavior as a weighted heterogeneous graph for modeling, and optimizes the placing scheme of replicas on the basis of a clustering algorithm based on Graph Neural Network (GNN), which balances the spatial locality and retrieval latency of data in users’ papers. Experimental results show that Hi-LHDFS achieves 32.7% lower average retrieval response latency.

title: A Novel Geolocation-Based APU Performance Prediction Technique
authors: Shanwen Qiao, Wenjie Zhong, Ji Bian, Jinrun Li, Shisheng Zhong, Minghang Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the issue of insufficient consideration of the geographical environment impact of departure airports in the performance prediction of civil aricraft Auxiliary Power Units (APU), this paper proposes a PlaceFormer prediction framework based on geographical location mapping. This framework innovatively designs a location information embedding module that maps discrete airport location information into highdimensional vectors to implicitly encode complex environmental factors such as geography and climate. Simultaneously, the DiffAttention mechanism is introduced to focus on the performance variation differences between consecutive flight missions, thereby enabling more accurate modeling of the cumulative degradation process of the APU. Experiments conducted on a dataset constructed from real Flight Data Recorder (QAR) data show that compared to traditional Transformer, GRU, and other state-of-the-art prediction models, PlaceFormer reduces the Root Mean Square Error (RMSE) in predicting multiple APU units. These results validate its application potential in leveraging geographical location information for more accurate APU performance state prediction.

title: QLPMR: Q-Learning-Based Path Dynamics-Driven Multipath Flow Routing in Software-Defined Vehicular Networking
authors: Patikiri Arachchige Don Shehan Nilmantha Wijesekara, Kalupahana Liyanage Kushan Sudheera, Harsha S. Gardiyawasam Pussewalage, Geeth Priyankara Wijesiri, Peter Han Joo Chong
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: Reliable multipath flow routing in highly dynamic vehicular networks, while adhering to stringent quality of service requirements, presents a formidable challenge. Existing research notably lacks a comprehensive framework for Software-Defined Vehicular Networks (SDVNs) that addresses multipath flow routing by leveraging underlying path dynamics to achieve intelligent flow splitting. This paper introduces a novel Q-learning-based approach specifically tailored for multipath flow routing within SDVNs. Our methodology adeptly captures and utilizes critical real-time network characteristics, including node mobility and traffic requirements, to derive path dynamics-associated parameters in order to inform and guide the Q-learning process. Specifically, at the SDVN controller, when routing a flow, each sub-flow’s path lifetime is derived using node mobility status data and link lifetime. By integrating path lifetime and latency considerations into the Q-learning process, our method intelligently adapts flow splitting by learning optimum flow splitting ratios to improve flow delivery and reduce overall packet latency and jitter among the sub-flows. Performance evaluations confirm that our proposed approach consistently achieves superior performance in terms of packet loss and jitter, coupled with highly competitive latency, thus significantly advancing the state-of-the-art in vehicular network routing.

title: Scale-Up Interconnect Technology in AI Clusters: Architecture Evolution, Performance Analysis, and Development Trends
authors: Yanling Chen, Xinxin Wang, Yang Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the explosive growth of trillion-parameter large language models, GPU cluster scale-up interconnect technology has emerged as a critical bottleneck limiting the efficiency of distributed training. Its performance optimization and systemlevel innovation directly determine the effective utilization of large-scale computing resources in modern AI workloads. This paper systematically reviews the evolutionary trajectory and industry landscape of scale-up interconnect technologies for AI clusters. Starting from the fundamental technological driving forces, we first analyze the extreme requirements for ultra-low latency and ultra-high bandwidth imposed by the high-frequency, large-volume communication patterns inherent in tensor parallelism (TP). Building on this analysis, we conduct an in-depth comparison of architectural designs and performance trade-offs across different technological routes, including NVIDIA's proprietary solutions and open alternatives such as RDMAintegrated interconnects, memory-semantic Ethernet, and PCIebased fabrics. In addition, the role of scenario-specific innovations is examined through representative examples such as Tesla's System-on-Wafer (SoW) architecture and the TTP/TTPoE protocol stack. Our analysis indicates that AI cluster scale-up interconnect technologies are evolving toward a pattern in which proprietary solutions dominate ultra-large-scale deployments, open standards gain advantages through ecosystem scalability, and optical interconnects progressively overcome physical limitations. Looking forward, performance breakthroughs, convergence toward open standards, and deep integration across heterogeneous technologies are expected to define the core development trends of next-generation interconnect systems. This work provides both theoretical insights and practical guidance for interconnect technology selection in ultra-large-scale AI clusters, as well as for the design of future high-performance interconnect architectures.

title: NSGA-II Algorithm-Based Carrier Aggregation Scheduling Method for 5G Networks
authors: Chi Zhang, Hongmei Wang, Liya Zhang, Junze Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Carrier aggregation (CA) is crucial for enhancing 5G network capacity. However, scheduling resources for both Enhanced Mobile Broadband (eMBB) and Ultra-Reliable Low-Latency Communication (URLLC) users poses a significant challenge. This paper proposes a novel multi-objective scheduling method based on the Non-Dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm II (NSGA-II) to optimize user service ratio, system throughput, and latency simultaneously. Simulation results demonstrate that our method outperforms benchmark approaches in achieving a better balance among these conflicting objectives

title: The TASP Instructional Design Model: Embedding AI Literacy in K-12 STEAM
authors: Shi-Yu Zhang, Xinliang Fu, Peien Fu, Yi-Lin Chang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The rapid advancement of Artificial Intelligence (AI) underscores the necessity of cultivating AI literacy as a foundational competency for 21st-century learners. Yet, standardized and integrable frameworks for embedding AI education into K-12 curricula remain underdeveloped. This paper addresses this gap by proposing a synergistic integration of AI literacy and STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts, and Mathematics) education. We argue that established frameworks such as the Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS) offer a robust foundation for AI education. For example, the NGSS practice of Analyzing and Interpreting Data directly parallels the Data Literacy dimension of AI literacy. Building upon these theoretical linkages, we introduce the TASP (TaskGuided, AI-Supported, Self-Paced, Project-Based) instructional design model as the central contribution of this study. The TASP model provides a structured yet adaptable methodology for designing learning experiences in which students leverage AI tools to solve authentic, project-based problems. By positioning AI literacy not as a standalone subject but as an enhancement to the inquiry-driven ethos of STEAM, the model offers a scalable pathway for curriculum innovation. This conceptual framework contributes to the field of educational technology management by offering a theoretically grounded and practically applicable approach to systematically embedding AI literacy within K-12 education.

title: The Impact of Generative AI in Renewable Energy Applications
authors: Jialie Shen, Haiyan Miao, Fengshou Gu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: With the global emphasis on both sustainable development and environmental protection, countries are more relying on renewable energy, such as wind and solar energy. Although carbon-free and environmentally friendly in terms of power generation process, the output of renewable energy systems is highly volatile and challenging to control because the operation and power generation of those systems depend greatly on environmental conditions. This paper presents a review of the latest advancement, challenges, and future directions driven by Artificial Intelligence (AI), with a special focus on the applications of generative AI in renewable energy. This study also introduces the GAIRE Framework (Generative Artificial Intelligence for Renewable Energy), a four-layer architecture integrating GenAI into energy infrastructures. GAIRE consists of the Data Layer for multimodal synthesis, the Model Layer for physics-informed generation, the Integration Layer linking digital twins and IoT systems, and the Governance Layer ensuring ethical and sustainable AI operation. A case study on wind turbine fault detection demonstrates GAIRE’s ability to combine generative learning with physical realism for predictive maintenance. The framework establishes a foundation for transparent, resilient, and net-zero-aligned renewable energy systems powered by generative intelligence.

title: The Evolution of Distribution Equipment for Power Grid Transformation: Case Studies From North America, Southeast Asia, and Finland
authors: Nenad Uzelac, John Gounaris, Simon Sutton, Tapio Mantysalo, Mikko Heinonen, Tyler McGrath, Alexandra Mutty
abstract:
Over the last couple of decades, the power distribution landscape has undergone a significant transformation, shifting from a reliance on manually operated and not-intelligent equipment to embracing ”smart” connected technologies. Historically, manually operated switchgear, relays, and reclosers formed a system that required human intervention for operation and maintenance. These traditional components offered limited flexibility and responsiveness to the dynamic demands of power distribution. The adoption of distribution automation technologies has been a game changer, introducing intelligent sensors, advanced switchgear, and reclosers that communicate and operate autonomously. This shift not only enhances the efficiency and reliability of the grid but also paves the way for integrating renewable energy sources and adapting to fluctuating energy demands.

This article explores four case studies that illustrate the automation of the grid: Xcel Energy’s innovative distributed energy resource (DER) integration, Parry Sound’s journey to becoming Canada’s first net-zero smart community, the deployment of advanced online monitoring in Southeast Asia, and Vakka-Suomen Voima (VSV), a Finnish utility, using Internet of Things (IoT) traveling wave fault location sensors. These case studies highlight the critical roles of smart reclosers, sensors, and other intelligent distribution equipment in enhancing grid functionality, reliability, and sustainability.

title: Reasoning Acceleration and Multimodal Generation Method of Diffusion Model Based on Deep Learning Optimization
authors: Kaiwen Zhao
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: A method of reasoning acceleration and multimodal generation of diffusion model based on deep learning optimization is proposed to solve the contradiction between generation quality and computational efficiency of diffusion model and improve its semantic alignment ability in multimodal tasks. Aiming at the problems of slow reasoning speed and insufficient modal fusion of traditional diffusion models, this paper designs a three-stage progressive optimization framework, EMDiT (Efficient Multimodal Diffusion Transformer), which includes a multimodal coding and fusion front-end, an efficient denoising diffusion backbone and a device-aware reasoning back-end. Core innovations include: (1) A knowledge distillation method based on noise distribution matching (NDM-KD) is proposed, which combines KL divergence and MSE (Mean Squared Error) loss to realize effective compression of U-Net model while maintaining the probability flow characteristics; (2) Design a Dynamic Gated Attention Module (DGA) to realize the adaptive integration of graphic features, and optimize the cross-modal potential spatial consistency through comparative learning; (3) Develop FP16-INT8 mixed quantization strategy and gradient accumulation lightweight back propagation algorithm to realize high-quality and energy-efficient reasoning deployment on the edge device Jetson AGX Orin. The experimental results show that the FID of the image generated by the proposed method on COCO dataset is 4.51, and the CLIP Score is 0.321. The reasoning speed is 3.8 times higher than that of Stable Difference V2.1, and it shows a stronger semantic alignment ability in multimodal generation tasks. The results of ablation experiment and hardware deployment further verify the effectiveness and practicability of each module.

title: An Interleaved Double Dual Buck-Boost converter with a Low-Loss Modulation Strategy
authors: Dan Hulea, Kaspars Kroičs, Florian-Ioan Baboș, Octavian Cornea, Nicolae Muntean
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This paper introduces a new DC-DC converter topology derived from the Double Dual Boost converter, incorporating buck-boost modules into its structure in order to enable a soft switching modulation strategy. The resulting structure, titled the Interleaved Double Dual Buck-Boost converter topology (IDDBB), combines the benefits of both concepts: lower stress on devices, a wider voltage range, and smaller passive components, all while achieving soft switching modulation.

title: Low-Power Design and Optimization of the S-Box for the PRESENT Cryptographic Algorithm Based on the Nangate 45 NM Process
authors: Xin Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This paper presents an ultra-low-power PRESENT S-box implementation on the Nangate 45 nm process. By employing novel Boolean simplification and logic decomposition, we reduced the gate count to 31, achieving a compact area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$34.047 \mu \mathrm{m}^{2}(42.7 \text{GE})$</tex>. Under typical conditions (1.1 V, 25° C), the design features a static power of 420 nW and an estimated dynamic power of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10.7 \mu \mathrm{W}$</tex> at 20.8 MHz, with a critical path delay of 1.14 ns. Compared to previous 180 nm implementations, this work demonstrates significant improvements in composite metrics, including a 77% reduction in the power-delay product (12.68 fJ) and 92% in the area-delay product, highlighting its suitability for resource-constrained IoT applications.

title: Transformer-Based Dynamic Weight Learning for Compositional Zero-Shot Learning
authors: Wei Zhao, Xianglin Bao, Wenlong Du, Ping Liu, Xiaofeng Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Compositional zero-shot learning (CZSL) aims to recognize novel state-object compositions not seen during training. Existing approaches often struggle with modeling attribute interdependencies and effectively fusing multimodal visual information. To address these challenges, we propose a Transformerbased dynamic weight learning framework for CZSL. Specifically, we design a contextual attribute enhancement module that adaptively enhances discriminative attribute features by exploiting spatial contextual information, improving the model's ability to generalize to unseen compositions. Second, we design a dynamic weight learning module that leverages Transformer self-attention to dynamically assign instance-specific weights to different visual modalities-composition, state, and object features-enabling adaptive fusion and enhancing representation robustness. Experiments conducted on MIT-States and UT-Zappos benchmarks demonstrate that our approach achieves competitive performance, validating the effectiveness of the proposed modules in advancing compositional zero-shot recognition.

title: VI-MGNet: A Visible–Infrared Network for Multimodal Maturity Detection and Grading
authors: Yongjiu Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To mitigate performance degradation in tomato maturity detection and grading under complex field conditions—caused by illumination variations, leaf occlusion, fruit overlap, and low foreground-background contrast—we propose VI-MGNet, a visible-infrared dual-modal network. VI-MGNet leverages crossmodal complementarity through three key designs: (1) a dynamic redundancy suppression mechanism guided by optical flow and feature similarity to adaptively discard redundant regions over time, improving feature diversity and generalization; (2) a cosine-similarity-guided fusion strategy that estimates inter-feature correlations and performs weighted integration for fine-grained multimodal fusion; and (3) a fully gated multi-axis feature enhancement module that adaptively models dependencies across multi-scale features, boosting representation capability in challenging scenes while maintaining computational efficiency. Experiments on a real-world dataset demonstrate that VI-MGNet delivers superior detection and grading performance and exhibits enhanced robustness under occlusion and illumination fluctuations, providing effective technical support for automated tomato harvesting and quality sorting.

title: Classification Method of Language Multimodal Teaching Resources Based on Clustering Algorithm
authors: Shao Gong, Caofang Long
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: When using the current method to classify language multimodal teaching resources, there is no dimension reduction processing for language multimodal teaching resources, resulting in low Jaccard coefficient, Folkes & Mallows coefficient and F-score coefficient and low classification efficiency. Therefore, a classification method for language multimodal teaching resources based on clustering algorithm is proposed. Combined with information entropy and PCA method, the dimension of language multimodal teaching resources is reduced to eliminate the redundant data in high-dimensional data. On this basis, the characteristics of multimodal teaching resources are extracted, and the teaching resources are simplified through semantic network. Combined SOM algorithm with LSI singular value decomposition algorithm, a new clustering algorithm is proposed to make sure the effective classification of language multimodal teaching resources. The findings of the experiment indicate that the suggested approach exhibits excellent classification performance and efficiency.

title: Fault Detection-Based Optimal Performance Recovery Control for Nonlinear Systems via an Actuator Replacing Method
authors: Wencheng Wang, Sai Huang, Ning Xu, Ning Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: This paper concentrates on a fault detection-based performance recovery control scheme using an actuator replacing method. The main procedure includes: 1) an integral sliding-mode (ISM) based event-triggered nominal controller; 2) a shifting-function-assisted fault detection design; and 3) an event-triggered reconfigurable controller. Unlike existing FTC schemes, no prior fault model is required. Minor actuator faults are directly attenuated via ISM, whereas significant faults are handled by switching to a backup actuator. For both nominal and reconfigurable phases, a modified Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) equation is solved with a single critic neural network, and an experience replay mechanism mitigates the persistence of excitation requirement while reducing computational complexity. A shifting function guarantees that post-replacement reconstructed states re-enter the prescribed performance bound within a finite window, and an actuator-oriented event-triggered strategy lowers update frequency while ensuring single-actuator operation. The effectiveness of the proposed scheme is validated by two simulation examples.

title: Resilient Load Frequency Control for Multi-Region Power Systems Against False Data Injection and Asynchronous Leakage Delays
authors: Hailing Sun, Jun Wang, Kaibo Shi, Lanfeng Hua, Yanbin Sun, Hui Lu, Xiao Cai
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: The deceptive nature of false data injection (FDI) attacks and the hysteresis effect of asynchronous leakage delay (ALD) can weaken the resilient load frequency control (RLFC) system’s dynamic response and stability. Therefore, this paper investigates the system dynamic fluctuations caused by the coupling of FDI and ALD. First, a dynamic attack model is established. An attack modulator (AM) is introduced to dynamically adjust the attack process to accurately simulate attacks on critical control paths. Second, we systematically characterize the asynchronous leakage delay caused by multi-source communication link switching. This delay behavior is embedded into the control system. This enables the system to more comprehensively reflect feedback delay characteristics under complex communication environments. Next, a robust control method is designed to enhance the system’s response to FDI and ALD under these complex situations, which can effectively ensure the stability of the system. Then, the improved Lyapunov-Krasovskii functional is used to analyze the system performance. Finally, simulations of a dual-region power system are conducted. The simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed method in terms of disturbance immunity, control stability, and engineering adaptability.

title: A Fully Integrated N-P Source Follower Domino Buffer FVF LDO With Fast Transient Response
authors: Fengyuan Zuo, Xu Han, Shi Tao, Yuyang Chen, Chi Zhang, Chao Luo, Guoping Guo
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems II: Express Briefs
abstract: This brief presents a fully integrated low-dropout regulator (LDO) capable of handling rapid load current transients, and a novel Domino buffer that consists of an N-type source follower and a P-type super source follower (SSF) is proposed herein. While ensuring low output impedance, this buffer also achieves sufficient voltage swing under both light and heavy load conditions. Compared to other Domino buffers reported in the brief, the proposed LDO reduces quiescent power consumption by an order of magnitude under heavy loads, enabling its application in scenarios demanding low quiescent current and fast transient response. Fabricated in TSMC 65 nm CMOS process, the LDO delivers a maximum load current of 10 mA and a dropout voltage of 200 mV, and measurement results show that the voltage undershoot and overshoot are 63.2 mV and 61.5 mV, respectively, when the load current steps from 200 μA to 10 mA with a 1 ns edge time, and the active chip area is 0.00417 mm².

title: Human-centric Evaluation of Semantic Resources: A Systematic Mapping Study
authors: Marta Sabou, Stefani Tsaneva, Miriam Fernandez, Maria Poveda, Mari Carmen Suárez-Figueroa
abstract: None

title: Time-Varying Aerodynamic Model and Adaptive Control of the High Angle-of-Attack Maneuvering Flight for Fixed-Wing AAVs
authors: Yufan Peng, Su Cao, Xiangke Wang, Huangchao Yu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: To enhance the autonomous maneuverability of aircraft, it is urgent to develop high angle-of-attack (AOA) maneuvering flight methods suitable for fixed-wing autonomous aerial vehicles (AAVs). Addressing the issue that most existing methods neglect the nonlinearity of the flow field under high AOA conditions, this article considers the hysteresis loop phenomenon that represents the unsteady aerodynamics due to stall. Inspired by the correlation between aerodynamic coefficients and the change rate of the AOA, a time-varying aerodynamic model is established. It utilizes time-varying parameter equations to describe the aerodynamic coefficients and combines them with the fixed-wing aircraft’s longitudinal dynamics model. To address the uncertainties arising from the established model, a Lyapunov-based adaptive maneuvering controller is developed for the nonlinear time-varying system.
