With the prior knowledge that aerodynamic coefficients are bounded, a projection operator based on convex set theory is designed to achieve real-time bounded estimation of the unknown time-varying parameters. Additionally, the designed switching cascaded control strategy enables the aircraft to restore steady flight autonomously. The comparing simulations under parameter perturbation demonstrate that the proposed controller can effectively enhance the AAV’s tracking performance for high AOA maneuver commands.

title: High-Accuracy Dynamic North-Finding Method based on Dual-Polarization Fiber-Optic Angular Accelerometer
authors: Xiangdong Ma, Xiaoyue Qiu, Chengcai Zheng, Yuzhuo Li, Hao Zhang, Mingming Peng, Weiping Fu, Xiaojia Wang, Xisheng Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: North-finding technology is an autonomous orientation technique used to determine the true north direction of carrier platforms, and it holds significant application value in fields such as seismic exploration and aerospace. In this work, we propose, for the first time to the best of our knowledge, a high-accuracy dynamic north-finding method based on a dual-polarization fiber-optic angular accelerometer (DP-FOAA). The angular accelerometer exhibits high sensitivity and a flat response across a broad frequency band. With an enclosed fiber coil area of 12.4 m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>, it achieves a sensitivity of 2.7 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">-</sup>⁷ rad/s²/(Hz)<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">1/2</sup> within the frequency range of 0.01 - 100 Hz. A north-finding system was stablished using this angular accelerometer integrated with a high-precision turntable. The novel north-finding method based on the DP-FOAA, together with its effective suppression of turntable vibration noise, has significantly enhanced the accuracy of dynamic north finding. The experimental results show that the north-finding accuracy of this system is better than 52 arcsec, with a total north-finding time of 18 s. Compared to a fiber-optic gyroscope with the same fiber coil, its measurement accuracy is improved by 3.2 times. This research provides a feasible approach for the development of novel high-speed, high-accuracy north finders, thereby advancing their technological progress.

title: A Precision Capacitive Rotary Encoder with Multi-Stage Offset Calibration
authors: Manhyuck Choi, Mookyoung Yoo, Gibae Nam, Minhyeok Son, Inju Yoo, Dahyun Jin, Euiyoung Lee, Hajun Bin, Junghyun Park, Sanghoon Lee, Chulseoung Choi, Joonho Shin, Dong-II Cho, Hyoungho Ko
abstract: This paper presents a precision capacitive rotary encoder that integrates a shared charge amplifier, and a multi-stage offset calibration scheme. Recently, capacitive encoders have gained significant attention due to their intrinsic advantages over optical and magnetic counterparts, including simple structure, low production cost, potential for miniaturization, and robustness in harsh environ- ments. However, improving their resolution, precision, and linearity to minimize signal distortion caused by inter-channel interference or mismatch remains a highly challenging issue. To address these limitations, a 6-bit programmable capacitor array and a 12-bit R-2R digital-to-analog converter (DAC) were implemented at the analog front-end, achieving effective DC offset cancellation. Additionally, a quadrature modulation/demodulation scheme was adopted to enhance channel isolation. The entire encoder IC was fabricated using a standard 180-nm CMOS process. Measurement results demonstrate a low angular nonlinearity of 0.189%, an SNR of up to 74.1dB, and stable operation across a wide speed range up to 4000 RPM. The system consumes 20 mA in total, of which 7.12 mA is drawn by the chip, offering a favorable balance between performance and power efficiency. The fabricated encoder prototype achieves a highly compact form factor with an outer diameter of just 21.6mm, making it one of the most miniaturized implementations among comparable designs.

title: Sculpting Margin Penalty: Intra-Task Adapter Merging and Classifier Calibration for Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning
authors: Liang Bai, Hong Song, Jinfu Li, Yucong Lin, Jingfan Fan, Tianyu Fu, Danni Ai, Deqiang Xiao, Jian Yang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Real-world applications often face data privacy constraints and high acquisition costs, making the assumption of sufficient training data in incremental tasks unrealistic and leading to significant performance degradation in class-incremental learning. Forward-compatible learning, which prospectively prepares for future tasks during base task training, has emerged as a promising solution for Few-Shot Class-Incremental Learning (FSCIL). However, existing methods still struggle to balance base-class discriminability and new-class generalization. Moreover, limited access to original data during incremental tasks often results in ambiguous inter-class decision boundaries. To address these challenges, we propose SMP (Sculpting Margin Penalty), a novel FSCIL method that strategically integrates margin penalties at different stages within the parameter-efficient fine-tuning paradigm. Specifically, we introduce the Margin-aware Intra-task Adapter Merging (MIAM) mechanism for base task learning. MIAM trains two sets of low-rank adapters with distinct classification losses: one with a margin penalty to enhance base-class discriminability, and the other without margin constraints to promote generalization to future new classes. These adapters are then adaptively merged to improve forward compatibility. Furthermore, we propose a Margin Penalty-based Classifier Calibration (MPCC) strategy to alleviate decision boundary ambiguity during incremental tasks. Extensive experiments on CIFAR100, ImageNet-R, and CUB200 demonstrate that SMP achieves state-of-the-art performance in FSCIL while maintaining a better balance between base and new classes. Code will be publicly available at https://github.com/beiyan1911/SMP.

title: Predicting and Optimizing Gamma Voltage in OLED Display Manufacturing through Sub-pixel Crosstalk Modeling
authors: Yoongyu Lee, Nam Ik Cho
abstract: The accuracy of low-luminance optical characteristics in OLED displays is a critical performance metric. This is especially important because the human eye is more sensitive to differences in color and brightness at low luminance levels than at high levels. However, achieving precise adjustments in this area is challenging due to crosstalk phenomena within OLED structures, where unintended current flows between sub-pixels, disrupting the desired optical outputs. Additionally, varying degrees of crosstalk across different panels further complicate predictions and adjustments. To address this issue, we introduce a novel methodology for quantifying the degree of sub-pixel current crosstalk and incorporating this quantification into gamma voltage prediction models. This approach involves calculating optical characteristics and voltage differences before and after gamma tuning. It breaks down the white luminance into theoretical red, green, and blue components and derives a crosstalk parameter based on the discrepancy between theoretical and actual voltage differences. This crosstalk parameter, along with measured optical differences, is used as input for a predictive model that estimates the gamma voltage required to achieve the desired optical characteristics. The proposed methodology has been validated using data from production lines and laboratory measurements, demonstrating superior performance in enhancing low-luminance optical accuracy. By effectively mitigating the effects of crosstalk, this research significantly contributes to improving the quality of low-gray optical characteristics in OLED displays, addressing both practical manufacturing challenges and the increasing demand for high-quality display performance.

title: A Multiple Spectral Criteria Active Fire Detection Algorithm Supported by Hyperspectral Dataset_supp1-3670276.docx

title: Research on the Impact Mechanism of Green Finance on Carbon Emissions in Guangdong Province, China
authors: Yuyang Xiao, Tianzi Guo, Shuyi Wang
publish: Proceedings of the International Conference on Digital Economy and Information Technology

title: Modernizing Distribution Networks Through Microgrid Innovation: Bronzeville Community Microgrid
authors: Roshan Sharma, Sri Raghavan Kothandaraman, Brooks E. Glisson, Christian T. Mukania, Katy Katona
abstract: The proliferation of distributed energy resources (DERs) and the increasing frequency of high-impact events have introduced challenges for the reliability, resilience, and operational flexibility of modern electric power systems. Microgrids have emerged as a promising solution, enabling localized control, seamless transition between grid-connected and islanded modes, and enhanced integration of DERs. This article presents the design, implementation, and operational validation of the Bronzeville Community Microgrid (BCM), a pioneering utility-scale microgrid commissioned by Commonwealth Edison (ComEd) in Chicago, Illinois. The BCM integrates a diverse mix of customer loads and DERs, advanced automation, and a microgrid master controller (MMC) to manage dynamic operating modes and adaptive protection. The article details the technical architecture, control strategies, and functionality validation through hardware-in-the-loop simulations and field tests, demonstrating the BCM’s capabilities for planned islanding, grid reconnection, and clustering with the Illinois Institute of Technology campus microgrid. Lessons learned and strategic opportunities for future microgrid deployments are discussed, highlighting the role of microgrids in advancing resilient, distributed, and intelligent power systems.

title: GGIW-PMBM Filter for Spawning Object Base on Sample Detection
authors: Youpeng Sun, Peng Li, Wenhui Wang, Wenqi Geng, Jiajun Ding, Bingyang Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The Poisson multi-Bernoulli mixture(PMBM) filter performs well in multi-target tracking in complex scenarios, but its performance significantly decreases when the object undergoes spawning, as it only distinguishes between new-born and surviving objects, fails to model spawning object, and ignores the dynamics of surviving objects. This article proposes a PMBM filter that can recognize spawning targets. The filter uses Bernoulli processes to independently model derived targets, improving tracking accuracy. At the same time, a sample detection method is designed to assess the credibility of spawning events through measurement and theoretical sample evaluation, effectively alleviating the misjudgment problem under multi-target proximity and high clutter. The simulation results show that the proposed method has improved tracking accuracy and spawning recognition speed compared to the original PMBM filter, and exhibits stronger robustness in complex scenarios.

title: Operational Amplifier Design for High-Speed, High-Resolution Two-Residue Pipeline ADCs
authors: Rongxin Wu, Haijun Lin, Xiao Yang, Tao Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: As one of the core circuits in analog-to-digital converters, the operational amplifier faces increasingly stringent requirements as ADCs evolve toward higher speeds and greater precision. The amplifier architecture adopted in this work is based on the conventional folded-cascode operational amplifier, with enhancements made to the input stage to increase the effective transconductance, thereby improving the gain-bandwidth product (GBW) of the amplifier. A coupling stage is employed to perform level-shifting at the output stage, which enhances the output voltage swing while increasing the output resistance, leading to higher overall gain. Simulation results show that the amplifier achieves DC gain of 54.2 dB, a GBW of 4.62 GHz, and a slew rate of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2517 ~\mathrm{V} / \mu \mathrm{s}$</tex>. Process corner simulations confirm the circuit with good robustness, making it suitable for pipelined ADCs based on a two-residue architecture.

title: Solar Trigeneration System Optimisation of Residential Use in Latvia
authors: Laila Zemite, Davids Kronkalns, Leo Jansons, Sanda Lapuke, Andris Backurs
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This study evaluates the technical and economic performance of a solar trigeneration system designed for residential applications in temperate European climates, with a specific case scenario modelled for a $200 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$ dwelling in Latvia. The proposed system integrates photovoltaic (PV) electricity generation, solar thermal heating, and absorption cooling into a unified configuration to optimise household energy consumption. The study focuses on assessing the feasibility of delivering heating, cooling, and electrical power simultaneously, thus reducing reliance on grid electricity and fossil fuels. The methodology includes dynamic simulation, incorporating climate data, building thermal profiles, and real-world component efficiencies. The modelled configuration includes a six kWp PV system, a $6 \mathrm{~m}^{2}$ solar thermal collector with a thermal storage tank, and a $7-\mathrm{kW}$ absorption chiller. Results indicate the system can supply approximately $6000 \mathrm{kWh} /$ year in electricity and $1900 \mathrm{kWh} /$ year in thermal energy, with the absorption chiller providing around $8000 \mathrm{kWh} /$ year in cooling. The trigeneration setup meets about $70 \%$ of the domestic hot water demand, $40 \%$ of heating needs, and $50 \%$ of the building’s cooling requirements. The economic analysis projects a Capital expenditure (CAPEX) of EUR 43,600 and an operating expense (OPEX) of EUR 1612/year. Annual energy savings of EUR 2000 yield a simple payback period of 21.8 years and a return on investment of $22.25 \%$ over 25 years. Despite long amortisation, the system offers energy cost stability, greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions reduction, and increased energy autonomy. The findings underscore the value of trigeneration in achieving climate targets and energy resilience, particularly when combined with passive building strategies and smart energy controls. The study concludes that optimised residential solar trigeneration systems present a viable, decentralised energy solution with significant long-term environmental and economic benefits.

title: Design of Reflectionless Bandstop Filter with Flat Passband Group Delay by a Four Port Network
authors: Yuan He, Gongda Hu, Dong Wang, Gang Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: An X-band planar reflectionless bandstop filter (RBPF) with flat passband group delay is designed by multiobjective optimization. The RBSF is realized on the basis of a symmetric four-port network. To configure the network on single layer PCB, fragment-type structures (FTS) including particles of mixed sizes are used, and then multiobjective optimization algorithm is used to optimize the FTS to obtain the desired four-port network. For demonstration, an X-band fourport network is first designed on a PCB with an area of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.43 \lambda ~\mathrm{g} \times 0.74 \lambda ~\mathrm{g}$</tex>. Then X-band RBSF are realized by appropriately terminating the ports of the FPN. The X-band RBSF can acquire 3-dB stopband bandwidth of 34.6%, reflectionless bandwidth of 98.7%, minimum insertion loss of 0.6 dB, and very flat in-band GD of variation less than 0.09 ns.

title: The Impact of a Situated Learning-Gamified Virtual Exploration System on Chinese Traditional Architectural Education
authors: Fei Liang, Shaoshuai Liu, Zipeng Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Traditional Chinese architectural education has long faced the challenge of a disconnect between theoretical cognition and spatial experience. To address this issue, this article proposes a paradigm shift in teaching, based on situational learning theory and constructive teaching philosophy, and designs and implements a gamified virtual exploration system based on Unreal Engine 5 (UE5). The system takes the east hall of Foguang Temple in the Tang Dynasty as a case study, constructs a high fidelity historical environment, and designs core game tasks such as “virtual tour guide” and “structural deconstruction”. A key innovation in the research is the integration of an intelligent learning partner based on the RAG architecture, which provides learners with timely and accurate knowledge clues rather than direct answers. To verify the effectiveness of the system, we conducted a quasi experimental study by dividing students into an experimental group (using a gaming system) and a control group (using traditional review methods). The post test results showed that the experimental group was significantly better than the control group in terms of knowledge mastery and application ability. This study confirms that placing gamified learning within a solid theoretical framework of teaching, supported by intelligent technology, can effectively break through the temporal and spatial limitations of traditional architectural education, enhance students' learning outcomes and spatial cultural cognition.

title: Extraction of Astronomical Disaster Information from Texts Using Large Language Models
authors: Chenhan Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: With the continued growth of modern astronomical observation, the demand for extracting disaster information from reports of astronomical observations is increasing. Such information is often presented in an unstructured format in academic papers and observation logs. It is also rich in specialized terminology, and has diverse linguistic structures depending on different contexts. Traditional information extraction methods often fail to achieve effective extraction results. This paper combines large language models with their potential for disaster extraction from astronomical text to propose a modular and freely composable prompt design framework. This framework, based on methods such as CoT reasoning and few-shot prompting, significantly enhances the large model's ability to discover implicit semantics and special terms in astronomical observation texts. An evaluation process is also designed to guide the dynamic modular improvement of prompts in practical applications. This comprehensive framework allows astronomical researchers to clearly observe the specific impact of each prompt module on astronomical disaster entity extraction and modify it accordingly. Furthermore, a set of basic label sets for astronomical texts is designed to ensure the professionalism and usability of the framework. The experimental results show that this method has better generalization ability and extraction accuracy than direct extraction, ensuring its practicality in astronomy (Code and demo are available at:https://github.com/eneraing/AstroDisasterLLM).

title: A Novel Demodulation Method of Phase-Modulated Signals for Plasma Sheath Channel
authors: Chao Qin, Nan Xie, Peng Wang, Xianhua Shi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: During the reentry of high-speed vehicles, the communication blackout caused by plasma sheaths has attracted widespread attention. Due to the parasitic modulation effects of time-varying plasma, the constellation of phase-modulated signals exhibits unique rotational distortions, rendering traditional decision method based on Euclidean distance entirely ineffective. Classification algorithms in machine learning (ML) can handle constellation with irregular shapes and achieve satisfactory signal classification under appropriate signal-to-noise ratios (SNR). However, misclassification still occurs when processing isolated constellation points or overlapping clusters. This study improves the misclassification of specific constellation points by introducing error-correcting codes (ECC) into a decision model trained by support vector machines (SVM). Simulation results demonstrate that, the integration of ECC significantly enhances the bit error rate (BER) performance of the algorithm under high SNR.

title: Online Blended Teaching Resource Sharing Method for Computer Network Courses in MOOC Environment
authors: Zijia Yu, Yunxiao Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Aiming at the problems of slow update speed, low resource sharing efficiency, and high transmission packet loss rate in the current teaching resource sharing method mainly achieved through centralized storage and distribution, a hybrid teaching resource sharing method for computer network courses online in MOOC environment is proposed. Evaluate the collected teaching resources through a resource quality rating model and establish a distributed resource repository to store resources. By combining cloud computing and intelligent technology, a basic shared MOOC environment is set up. Based on the geographical distribution characteristics of teaching resources and user access needs, multi-level teaching resource collection nodes are deployed. The node layout is completed through three stages: sharing range recognition, node mapping and connection, resource acquisition chain construction, and node control, and node control indicators and parameters are set. In the MOOC environment, blockchain technology and adaptive resource processing programs are introduced to design a bidirectional cross resource sharing model. The resource sharing work is divided into four levels, and secure storage and access control of resources are achieved through smart contracts and FSCOBCOS blockchain platform to improve the effectiveness of educational resource sharing. The experimental results show that this method can effectively reduce the packet loss rate during resource sharing transmission, thereby improving learning efficiency and quality.

title: Research on the Sharing of Online Teaching Resources for Preschool Game Music Based on Artificial Immunity
authors: Zhaohui Ge, Jie Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Traditional methods mainly achieve resource sharing through keyword matching, but ignore the deep correlation and personalized needs of resources, resulting in inaccurate resource matching and poor user experience. Therefore, a method for sharing online teaching resources of preschool game music based on artificial immunity has been proposed. Firstly, classify and organize the online resources of preschool game music, standardize them, and integrate and process heterogeneous data from multiple sources of teaching resources through horizontal and vertical fusion; Semantic recognition algorithms are used to explore the semantic information of resources and improve the accuracy of resource matching; Based on the principle of artificial immunity, a resource sharing model is constructed, and knowledge graph technology is used to design the priority order of resource sharing. By simulating the self-learning and optimization ability of the immune system, efficient resource sharing is achieved. The experimental results show that the sharing efficiency of preschool game music resources has been improved, reducing energy consumption during resource sharing, better meeting user needs, and improving resource utilization.

title: Construction of a Badminton Training Data Acquisition and Intelligent Analysis Platform Integrating Multiple Sensors
authors: Zongsheng Liu, Qihang Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: With the development of intelligent sensing technology, in the process of sports training, integrating multiple sensors to collect and analyze sports training data has become an important means to improve athletes' athletic performance. This paper establishes a badminton training data collection and intelligent analysis platform based on multi-sensor fusion. By using various sensors such as acceleration sensors, gyroscopes, heart rate sensors, and GPS positioning systems, real-time collection of athletes' behavior data, physical index data, and location data is achieved. Through diversified data fusion and intelligent analysis methods, Real-time assessment and analysis of athletes' training results are conducted, and personalized training suggestions such as hitting timing, Angle analysis, and heart rate monitoring are provided. Practice has shown that this platform has great potential to enhance training effectiveness and athletes' technical abilities, providing significant assistance for future intelligent sports training.

title: A Novel Hybrid Machine Learning Strategy for Early Oral Cancer Detection
authors: Princy Tyagi, Santosh Rani, Shivani, Abhishek Saini
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Oral cancer is a common and dangerous tumor that needs to be identified early in order to be treated effectively and provide better results for patients. Oral cancer can now be automatically and effectively classified thanks to machine learning (ML) techniques, which have become extremely effective tools for the analysis of medical pictures. In order to identify oral cancer using medical images, including radiography scans, intraoral photos, and histopathology slides, this work presents a reliable image classification method that makes use of cutting-edge machine learning techniques. Pre-processing images to improve their quality, feature extraction, and categorization into benign, pre-malignant, and malignant lesions are all part of the methodology. For the suggested method to guarantee good accuracy and predictability, a large dataset of annotated oral cancer images is required. The efficacy of the model is evaluated by metrics such as F1-score, recall, specificity, and accuracy. This study emphasizes how machine learning can revolutionize medical imaging and how crucial it is to improving the diagnosis of oral cancer.

title: Wavelet-Guided Progressive and Dynamic YOLO for SAR Ship Detection in Complex Scenarios
authors: Xipeng Chen, Ming Hao, Jie Guo, Long Zhuang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: To address the challenges of multi-scale targets, arbitrary orientations, and complex background interference in ship detection from synthetic aperture radar (SAR) imagery, this paper proposes a wavelet-guided dynamic multi-scale fusion ship detection algorithm based on YOLO11n architecture, WPDYOLO. First, we develop a Progressive Residual Multi-Scale Fusion (PRMSF) module that enhances multi-scale feature representation via residual reconfiguration and channel compression. Next, we replace traditional downsampling with WaveletPooling, which suppresses background noise and preserves high-frequency texture through frequency-domain decomposition. Finally, we propose a Dynamic Task-Aligned Oriented Object Detection Head (DTAO-Head) that achieves deep collaboration between classification and regression tasks through shared convolutions and an adaptive angle prediction mechanism. Experiments on the RSDD-SAR dataset show that the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP}_{50-95}$</tex> of WPD-YOLO reaches 76.27 % with a parameter count of only 1.85 M, which is a 30.4 % reduction in the parameter count relative to the original YOLO11-obb, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P}_{50-95}$</tex> is improved by 2.53 % in the complex scenarios. The model provides an effective solution for real-time detection of ships by satellite SAR.

title: Adaptive Huber Based Federated Learning for Sparse Optimization
authors: Ji Cheng, Hongxin Zhao, Xin Wang, Wanping Zhang, Zhaoqilin Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Federated learning (FL) is a machine learning method that supports collaborative model training across distributed devices without exposing local data. However, it is still vulnerable to adversarial or corrupted updates. In this paper, we propose an adaptive Huber based aggregation (AHA) method to enhance the robustness of the federated learning for solving sparse optimization. It can also be used for differentiable optimization. This method dynamically adjusts the robustness Huber-parameter, improving the tolerance to outliers and malicious behaviors. The experimental results confirm that the AHA significantly outperforms classical methods like FedAvg, Trimmed Mean, static Huber aggregation, and has superior robustness and stable convergence, even at high corruption levels.

title: An Efficient Simulation Method of Non-Uniform Carrier Frequency Offset for LEO Broadband OFDM Communication Systems
authors: Jingqi Zhang, Yingnan Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Orthogonal Frequency Division Multiplexing (OFDM) technology has become the core modulation technology for satellite communication systems due to its prominent advantages such as high spectral efficiency and resistance to multipath, hence it is adopted by 5G-A protocols for links between users and base stations. Due to the high dynamic characteristics of Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite-ground links, non-uniform Carrier Frequency Offset (CFO) caused by the Doppler effect leads to inter-carrier interference (ICI) and signal distortion. So far, there are no clear modeling and simulation methods for the non-uniform CFO. Hence, to solve this problem, this paper proposes an efficient simulation method of nonuniform CFO for LEO broadband OFDM communication systems. First, we analyze the causes of non-uniform CFO and establish a comprehensive mathematical model for it. Then, we propose a matrix-based simulation method, which clearly reveals the behind mechanisms. Finally, numerical results verify that the proposed method can effectively simulate the non-uniform CFO that the broadband OFDM signals suffered in the LEO scenarios.

title: Foundations of a Decentralized IoT Greenhouse Control System: Problem Statement and Preliminary Results
authors: Andrejs Potapovs, Mikhail Gorobetz, Jurijs Timofejevs, Gustavs Bruno Grodzs, Kristaps Usins
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainable and efficient food production calls for advanced monitoring and control technologies in greenhouse environments. This paper introduces a decentralized open-source IoT-based greenhouse control and monitoring system designed to improve transparency, flexibility, and reproducibility in agricultural research and practice. The system architecture integrates modular hardware nodes equipped with environmental sensors, soil moisture probes, and computer-vision-based imaging units, coordinated through distributed communication protocols. Methods for data acquisition, processing, and decision support are described, with emphasis on the adaptability of the platform to heterogeneous greenhouse settings. Preliminary results demonstrate sensor structure, integration, stable data transmission and initial deployment greenhouse monitoring system.

title: Constructing Academic Collaboration Graphs for Teacher Performance Analysis with GCN
authors: Shifeng Li, Guolong Li, Jinyu Wei, Shuai Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to teacher performance evaluation by constructing an Academic Collaboration Graph (ACG) that captures relational patterns among teachers. Unlike traditional methods that treat teachers as independent and isolated instances, we model their collaborative interactions using a graph structure and employ a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) to learn relational representations. The proposed method achieves strong performance, with an Accuracy of 0.925, Weighted F1-Score of 0.924, and AUC of 0.966. Experimental results demonstrate that modeling teacher relationships significantly enhances evaluation accuracy compared to conventional feature-based approaches, highlighting the value of graph-based modeling and the potential of graph learning techniques in educational data analysis. The source code and dataset are publicly available at github.com/SVIL2024/GCN-Threshold-graph-teacherEval. git.

title: A Literature Review of E-Textbook Research in the Past Two Decades
authors: Chang Chen, Qing Lei
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study focuses on e-textbook research published from 2004 to 2024, and applies bibliometric and content analysis methods to systematically examine global research progress in the field. By analyzing publication volume, country of origin, contributing authors, keyword co-occurrence, and co-citation networks, the study constructs a comprehensive knowledge map of e-textbook research. The findings reveal four main research streams: the creation of e-textbooks, user engagement and perception, educational applications and learning effects, and the impact on related industries. Results show that the number of publications has exhibited a steady growth trend over the period, with significant contributions from multiple countries and collaborative networks among prolific authors and institutions. This approach provides substantial data support for understanding the global development of e-textbook research.

title: Etutor: An End-to-End Multimodal Framework for AI-Driven Educational Video Generation
authors: Henghui Meng, Kainan Liu, Chengxiang Li, Yifan Li, Da Shang, Zihao Jia
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Online education platforms often lack personalized learning experiences and effective emotional interaction, partly because existing technologies struggle to generate audio and video synchronously, leading to inconsistencies in semantics and emotional expression. To address this challenge, this paper introduces Etutor, an innovative, end-to-end multimodal AI framework for educational video generation that can rapidly create high-quality, personalized instructional videos from a single text input. At the core of Etutor is a three-layer architecture—encompassing semantics, expression, and technology—coordinated by a Large Language Model (LLM). This architecture uniformly interprets user intent, generates instructional content, and orchestrates all modal parameters to ensure a high degree of semantic and emotional consistency. The framework integrates two key technical modules: NMCTTS, a speech synthesis system optimized for Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) content that accurately handles professional terminology and formulas; and CF-RTVideo, a video generation system that significantly enhances the clarity of facial details, the vividness of expressions, and the preservation of identity. To comprehensively evaluate system performance, we introduced the Teaching Cognitive Load Synergy Index (TCLSI) as a new evaluation metric. Experimental results show that Etutor achieved a high score of 0.78 on the TCLSI, outperforming all baseline systems that use separate modular components. This result demonstrates Etutor's comprehensive advantage in balancing instructional content delivery, multimodal expressiveness, and learner cognitive load, offering an effective automated solution to the challenges in modern online education.

title: A Human Micro-Scale Activities Recognition Method Based on Range-Doppler Matrix of FMCW Radar
authors: Qi Wang, Yong Bo, Zhenheng Fang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Human activity recognition technology plays a key role in healthcare, elderly care, security monitoring, and human-computer interaction. Non-intrusive continuous monitoring enabled by precise mmWave sensor data makes this technology more universal and accessible. The Range-Doppler Matrix (RDM) captures the accurate distance and radial velocity of all scattering points in the field of view at one time. To better recognize human micro-scale activities, this paper proposes a multi-scale parallel residual network based on the RDM heatmap of FMCW radar. It replaces serial branches with parallel convolution-pooling branches, fuses features into residual modules, and realizes multi-scale detailed signal extraction of human micro-scale activities. Compared with classical network models, the proposed scheme achieves a balance between accuracy and performance.

title: Olympic Project Based on Support Vector Machine (SVM) Gender Equality Index Prediction Model Construction and Application
authors: Chengyu Zuo, Zhuolang Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: This study, according to the theory of the Olympic project gender equality index prediction data isomerism, small sample learning and more challenges such as the modal characteristics of fusion, build the prediction model based on support vector machine (SVM). Based on the nearly three Olympic Games women participating scale data, integration project type, historical trends, multidimensional characteristics, such as national policy, by optimizing the kernel function parameters and the regularization parameter (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{C}$</tex> value), processing high-dimensional nonlinear relationship. Model group with 500 valid samples training, using industrial-grade F1 - score (0.85 or higher) for evaluation criteria. Test results show that the model of F1 - score of 0.85, compared with 2023 up <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18.1 \%(0.72)$</tex> and the basic value of hybrid project forecast accuracy (92.0%), the highest niche project due to lack of data rate 20% performance is relatively low <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(83.7 \%)$</tex>. The time consumption of a single prediction is controlled within 0.5 seconds, which meets the needs of real-time decision-making. The study proves that the SVM model can effectively capture the nonlinear dynamics of gender equality indicators and provide a scientific prediction tool for Olympic organizers. The future need to further optimize niche project data processing strategy and improve model interpretability, to reduce the decision making rejection rate of 78% sports managers, accurate implementation to promote gender equality policy.

title: Blockchain Based Spectrum Leasing for 5G and Beyond
authors: Dilshara Niromali, Sampavi Sivakumaran, Harsha S. Gardiyawasam Pussewalage, Sachith Piumantha, Geeth P. Wijesiri, Indika A. M. Balapuwaduge
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: In this technically advanced era, the Internet of Things and Machine-Type Communication are becoming more important as the requirement for the wireless spectrum is increasing to unseen levels. Current spectrum management tactics are not providing the desired push to such demand, leading to inefficient utilization of spectrum resources. It is important to note that, licensed spectrum bands have certain unused spectrum bands known as “spectrum holes” or “white holes” which could be varied in duration from milliseconds to weeks. To address this inefficiency, this paper describes a blockchain-based solution to develop a decentralized, efficient, and trustworthy platform for leasing spectrum resources, specifically using the Ethereum platform. This paper also presents the development of a user-friendly Decentralized Application that can facilitate easy access and interaction with the marketplace. Thus this unique trading platform assists in developing a seamless, secure, and transparent spectrum leasing framework. Unlike generic blockchain systems, our architecture incorporates the use of a dynamic spectruma-ware auction and micropayment channel that is optimized for 5G spectrum leasing.

title: Rethinking Big Data Scale: Balancing Accuracy, Collaboration, and Storage
authors: Panos K. Chrysanthis, Constantinos Costa
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: The scale of modern big data is increasingly shaped by collaboration as a result of the constant exchange and sharing of information between organizations, users, and systems. As data collaboration grows, the cost of moving, storing and synchronizing shared datasets has become a major challenge. Existing systems focus on capacity and compression within isolated environments, overlooking the broader need to reduce redundant data movement while keeping results accurate and consistent.In this vision paper, we argue that balancing accuracy, collaboration, and storage should be at the core of rethinking the big data scale. We propose an approach where systems learn when and what to share, reducing unnecessary transfers through collaborative compression, shared summaries, and intelligent reconstruction. Rather than sending entire datasets, collaborating sites can exchange compact representations that preserve essential information, while AI methods fill in or refine details when needed. By aligning accuracy with collaboration efficiency, future data systems can scale not by storing or moving more data, but by sharing smarter. We outline key design ideas for such collaboration-aware systems and discuss open challenges in building intelligent, efficient, and sustainable data infrastructures.

title: Predicting Employment Timeline for Computing Studies Graduates: A Decision Tree Analysis of Job Search Duration
authors: Felix L. Huerte, Jeruz E. Claudel, Alice M. Lacorte, Joselle A. Banocnoc, Kurt Robin A. San Jose, Reymark R. Natividad
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study developed a predictive model using the Decision Tree algorithm to forecast the job-waiting duration of alumni from the College of Computing Studies, Laguna University. The model used demographic and academic variables—age, gender, general weighted average (GWA), and internship experience—to determine factors influencing employability. The study utilized a Decision Tree algorithm to identify patterns and predict the time it takes for graduates to secure employment. 488 alumni in all took part in the structured online survey, which was examined using the scikit-learn framework in Python. With strong and balanced precision, recall, and F1-score across validation folds, the model's overall accuracy was 81%. A macro-average ROC-AUC score of 0.88 validated the Decision Tree's superior interpretability and robustness over Logistic Regression, Random Forest, and SVM, according to comparative analysis. The findings showed that the most significant predictors of shorter job-waiting times were internship experience and GWA. In order to improve graduate employability outcomes, the findings offer data-driven insights to help educational institutions improve internship programs, curriculum alignment, and career readiness initiatives.

title: Towards Collaboration-Aware Resource Sharing in Research Computing Infrastructures
authors: Souradip Nath, Ananta Soneji, Jaejong Baek, Carlos Rubio-Medrano, Gail-Joon Ahn
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: As scientific research grows increasingly collaborative, data-driven, and computation-intensive, Research Computing Infrastructures (RCIs) have emerged as critical platforms for enabling scientific collaboration across diverse disciplines. These infrastructures offer advanced computational and storage solutions essential for collaborative research, yet securing shared access to such resources remains a significant challenge. Consequently, there has been limited investigation into how collaboration context is conceptualized and leveraged during access provisioning, especially within RCIs. In this paper, we investigate existing resource sharing practices within RCIs, revealing key gaps in their ability to support secure authorization within dynamic, project-driven collaborative workflows. Building on these insights, we introduce a framework called CLEARS (Collaboration-Aware Authorization for Resource Sharing) that formally represents collaboration contexts and operationalizes them to guide secure, context-aware, and dynamically evolving resource sharing authorization throughout the collaboration lifecycle. Through experiments, we demonstrate that CLEARS delivers precise access enforcement under evolving collaborative scenarios while maintaining minimal execution overhead.

title: Predicting Air Quality Index at Dome Argus, Antarctica through the Integration of Meteorological Data and Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Tejal N R, Dakshayani R N R
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: This work demonstrates that the machine learning application can be used in the prediction of the Air Quality Index (AQI) at Dome Argus, East Antarctica. The daily meteorological parameters for March 2025, including rainfall, temperatures, wind direction, wind speed, humidity, pressure and solar irradiance data were obtained from the NASA POWER database. K-means clustering was applied in segmentation according to similar meteorological patterns. Using an empirical AQI estimation equation based on weather variables, initial AQI values were simulated. A Random Forest regression model was trained on this estimated AQI and the meteorological input. The model was evaluated with five-fold cross-validation, yielding good results with a root mean square error (RMSE) of about 2.4 AQI units averaged across folds, and an RMSE of about 1.3 and the coefficient of determination ($R^{2}$) was high near 0.81 on separated test data. As shown in Fig. 3, that the predicted AQI values follows the simulated AQI values within the “Good” air quality category. The precipitation and solar irradiance came out as key factors (refer Fig. 5). These results underline that even in remote regions such as Antarctica, machine learning can effectively simulate AQI using meteorological data alone.

title: Severity Analysis of Depression Based on Text Convolutional Neural Network
authors: Xianlong Tan, Shuhua Mao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Depression, a prevalent mental disorder, affects over 280 million people globally, necessitating objective and efficient identification methods. Traditional diagnosis relies on subjective assessments, suffering from limitations like misdiagnosis and delayed intervention. This study proposes a text-based severity analysis of depression using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). Comments from social platforms (e.g., Weibo, Baidu Post Bar) were collected, preprocessed (removing noise, stop words), and labeled into three categories: normal, mild, and severe depression. Word2vec was employed to generate word vectors, and a TextCNN model was trained to classify severity. The results show that the model achieves the best performance with a convolutional kernel size of 3, yielding precision rates of 65.7% for normal, 92.7% for mild, and 74.5% for severe depression. However, challenges remain, including dataset size limitations and classification ambiguities. This approach provides an automated alternative for depression severity analysis, highlighting the potential of NLP techniques in mental health assessment.

title: A Distributionally Robust Optimization-Based Stochastic Self-Triggered Model Predictive Control
authors: Bin Li, Wenhao Zhang, Mingming Shi
publish: IEEE Transactions on Systems, Man, and Cybernetics: Systems
abstract: In this article, we propose a self-triggered distributionally robust model predictive control algorithm for linear discrete systems with state chance constraints and unbounded stochastic disturbances. Assuming that only the first and second moments of the disturbance are accessible, we transform the objective function into a compact quadratic form and reformulate the state chance constraints into linear inequalities, which is more tractable when solving. In order to reduce communication and sampling times of the system, we propose a self-triggered update scheme, in which the state sampling and the control input sequence are updated when the control performance predicted based on the current sampling exceeds that of the periodic sampling scheme. We demonstrate that the optimization problem in the proposed self-triggered model predictive control (MPC) method is recursively feasible and stable. Numerical simulation results verify the effectiveness of the proposed algorithm.

title: A Regional Benchmark for Deep Learning–Based Hourly Precipitation Nowcasting in Latin America
authors: Adriano P. Almeida, Henrique M. J. Barbosa, Sâmia R. Garcia, David J. Gagne, Kanghui Zhou, Takuji Kubota, Tomoo Ushio, Shigenori Otsuka, Simon Pfreundschuh, Alan J. P. Calheiros
abstract: Accurate short-term precipitation forecasting is critical for Latin America, but the region lacks a standardized framework to evaluate data-driven approaches due to the sparse coverage in the ground. This study introduces the Artificial Intelligence for Nowcasting Pilot Project (AINPP) Precipitation Benchmark (AINPP-PB-LATAM), providing curated datasets and a scalable, optimized training pipeline designed to accelerate deep learning development in high-performance computing environments. Beyond establishing a baseline using seven years of satellite-based data (2018–2024), the framework reduces engineering barriers, enabling researchers to focus on fine-tuning strategies to extract the full predictive capacity of models for regional specificities. As a demonstration use case, we trained and evaluated five deep learning architectures, AFNO, Inception-V4, ResNet-50, U-Net, and Xception, comparing them against Lagrangian Persistence and the operational AI Nowcast. The results reveal critical trade-offs: spectral methods like AFNO excel in continuous skill by capturing large-scale dependencies, while convolutional architectures offer robust categorical performance. However, pixel-wise optimization challenges persist, with systematic under-prediction of heavy rainfall. By providing open-access code and optimized baseline implementations for distributed computing, AINPP-PB-LATAM establishes a scalable foundation for collaborative research, facilitating the advancement of operational AI-based nowcasting and transferability assessments in data-scarce regions.

title: Federated Learning with Stage-wise Trajectory Matching for UAV Networks on Non-IID Data_supp1-3670272.pdf
authors: Mincheng Wu
abstract: Federated learning enables multiple unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) to collaboratively train a global model while preserving data privacy. However, data heterogeneity across UAV swarms hinders model convergence and generalization. While existing trajectory matching methods address this problem by synthesizing distilled data from early-stage training trajectory, this trajectory are non-convergent and fail to capture the complete global distribution. To address this issue, we propose a novel federated learning framework based on stage-wise trajectory matching (FedSTM). This framework performs trajectory matching during both the early and late stages of training to synthesize distilled data that comprehensively represent the global distribution, and subsequently utilizes these distilled data to fine-tune the global model, effectively enhancing its generalization capability and stability. Additionally, we introduce a decorrelation regularization term during local training to mitigate dimensional collapse in the representation space, thereby improving the quality of the global model’s trajectory. Extensive experimental results on multiple datasets demonstrate that the proposed method surpasses baseline approaches in model performance and convergence. Code is available at https: //github.com/zhangweikk/FedSTM.

title: Impact of Firefighting Water Jets on Public Safety Long-Term Evolution Communication Reliability
authors: Insu Yeom, Tien Dat Nguyen, Hong Sik Kim, Chang Won Jung
abstract: In fire environments, firefighting water jets can cause significantly attenuated electromagnetic signals, potentially impairing wireless communication reliability. In this study, the impact of water droplets generated by water jets on electromagnetic wave propagation in the PS-LTE band (718–783 MHz), designated for Korea’s disaster safety communication network, was systematically investigated. Experiments were conducted under heavy (100 mm·h-1) and light (2.5 mm·h-1) rainfall conditions, considering two scenarios: when a wireless terminal is positioned outside the rain (Antenna Placed Outside Rain : AoR) and when it is in direct contact with raindrops (Antenna placed Inside Rain : AiR). Electromagnetic wave transmission was simulated using linearly polarized (Dipole) and circularly polarized (Bi-Quad) antennas, with the results validated experimentally. The radiation patterns and efficiency of each receiving antenna were also analyzed. In the AoR scenario, the measured efficiency of the linearly polarized antenna was 89%, while that of the circularly polarized antenna was 79%. By contrast, under the AiR scenario, the efficiency of the linearly and circularly polarized antennas decreased to 36% and 32%, respectively. The results confirmed that in the AiR scenario, direct contact with raindrops led to electromagnetic absorption and scattering, reducing the receiving antenna’s efficiency by nearly 50% compared to the AoR scenario. Additionally, the communication characteristics of a wireless device exposed to water were analyzed, revealing that droplet exposure reduced receiver sensitivity by over 3 dBm. This represents one of the first systematic experimental analyses of PS-LTE signal degradation under emulated firefighting water-jet conditions. These findings highlight the potential impact of water jets on signal attenuation in the PS-LTE band, emphasizing the need for mitigation strategies to ensure reliable communication in fire environments.
