title: CA-KMTNet: CUDA-Accelerated Gaussian Radial Basis KAN Multi-Frequency Transform Network for Loess Sinkhole Identification
authors: Zhaojun Pang, Wu Zhu, Ruixuan Zhang, Xiaoyu Liu, Qiangbing Huang, Jing Wu, Tong Yang, Haixing Shang, Zhanxi Wei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: Automatic identification of sinkhole hazards is crucial for ensuring the safety and stability of human lives, infrastructure, and ecosystems. Deep learning has demonstrated significant potential in unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) low-altitude remote sensing monitoring of sinkholes due to its high scene fine-grained comprehension. However, constrained by the micro-relief characteristics and spatial heterogeneity of loess sinkholes, existing models suffer from the edge confusion and missegmentation of homogeneous internal regions. This fundamentally stems from shallow convolutional kernels failing to effectively decouple nonlinear coupling mechanisms in high-dimensional feature spaces. In this context, we propose a multi-level frequency-domain transformation network named CA-KMTNet. The network employs compute unified device architecture (CUDA)-accelerated Kolmogorov-Arnold networks (KANs) to enhance nonlinear learning capabilities for loess sinkholes. To prevent overfitting, wavelet transforms are integrated into each encoder level, while KANs are embedded in the shallow layers to provide high-dimensional multi-frequency spatial features. Subsequently, inverted residual convolutional structures are introduced during deep encoding to optimize edge pixel identification for small loess sinkholes. Experimental results demonstrate the promising performance of the proposed method. On the Baijia gully dataset in the Loess Plateau, the proposed model achieves 15.90%, 19.19%, and 15.90% improvements over UNet in F1-score, intersection over union (IoU), and Kappa metrics, respectively. To further verify the generalization ability of CA-KMTNet, this study tests the previous Baijia gully dataset and the Laozi gully dataset. CA-KMTNet shows stronger spatiotemporal transferability and performance advantages. These findings highlight the identification potential of the proposed model for loess sinkhole hazards and provide new insights for integrating the KAN structure into semantic segmentation tasks.

title: A Hardware-Aware Methodology for Distributed In-Network Intelligence on FPGA-Based SmartNICs
authors: Fanny Spagnolo, Mattia Giovanni Spina, Stefania Perri, Antonio Iera, Pasquale Corsonello
abstract: Future network infrastructures will increasingly leverage artificial intelligence (AI) models for automation of internal management procedures and for service offering. To this aim, distributing learning capabilities within Programmable Data Planes (PDPs) can offer clear advantages, but at the cost of complex design challenges. In this paper, we introduce an effective methodology for distributing AI capabilities across PDP devices, considering hardware resource utilization and energy consumption. Experimental evaluations conducted on FPGA-based programmable platforms demonstrate that the proposed design, when applied to anomalous network traffic detection, attains very high precision, high throughput, and improved power efficiency compared to state-of-the-art solutions in the literature.

title: Simulating Muscle-Level Energetic Cost When Humans Walk With a Passive Biarticular Thigh Exosuit_supp2-3668912.pdf
authors: Vahid Firouzi

title: Haptic coupling to negotiate motion plans
authors: C. De Vicariis, E. Ivanova, V. Sanguineti, E. Burdet
publish: IEEE Transactions on Neural Systems and Rehabilitation Engineering
abstract: Haptic coupling is integral to everyday life, enabling humans to collaborate on tasks without the need for verbal agreement of every detail. However, collaborative partners often have differing plans and use haptic coupling to negotiate a common strategy. While previous studies have focused on tasks where partners share the same plan, little is known about haptic negotiation when plans diverge. This study investigates how the motion plans of two mechanically connected partners evolve during repeating a via-points arm-reaching task when they start from differing initial plans. In one group, partners had plans requiring similar effort, while in another group, plans were unbalanced, requiring different levels of effort. The analysis of shape and movement metrics shows that all dyads coordinated their plans through practice, influencing subsequent movements even when disconnected. For symmetric plans, partners exhibited slight mutual adaptation toward each other. In contrast, for asymmetric plans, the partner with the simpler plan tended to lead the movement, while the partner with the more complex plan complied and relaxed their plan. These findings suggest that, during collaboration, partners leverage mechanical interaction to simplify tasks and minimize effort.

title: TFSCL: A Novel Time-Frequency Similarity Contrast Learning Method with Hybrid Augmentation for Robust and Accurate Specific Emitter Identification
authors: Kaixuan Huang, Yongtao Ma, Jialu Zhu, Yuxiang Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: As the Internet of Things (IoT) and Sixth Generation (6G) technologies advance rapidly, the cryptographic identification of electronic devices has become a critical issue in information security. Radio frequency fingerprint (RFF)-based specific emitter identification (SEI) has emerged as a prominent physical-layer authentication technique. To enhance the stability and accuracy of multi-target recognition in complex electromagnetic environments, a novel technique for individual specific emitter identification based on time-frequency similarity contrastive learning (TFSCL) is proposed. In this study, we present a novel pre-training method utilizing a deep complex-valued pyramid network (DCPN) to enhance the extraction and reconstruction of time series and frequency domain sequences. The DCPN enables contrastive learning of signal features in both the temporal and frequency domains, significantly reducing computational complexity and improving pretraining performance. Additionally, we first introduce the Time-Frequency Synchronization Data Added (TFS-DA), a Time-Frequency Hybrid Data Added Technique that employs Gray code to generate random sequences, effectively improving feature representation in both domains. Empirical results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an accuracy rate of 97.12% on an automatic-dependent surveillance-broadcast (ADS-B) dataset that contains 10 categories with only data labeled 10%. On a LoRa dataset containing 30 categories with only data labeled 10%, the accuracy rate reaches 77.06%.

title: A Fully Differential D-Band Low-Noise Receiver With High-Efficiency Dielectric Resonator and Asymmetric Transformer in 40-nm CMOS
authors: Yun Qian, Yizhu Shen, Yifan Ding, Jiapeng Wan, Sanming Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article presents a high-gain, low-noise I/Q receiver operating at the D band, implemented using a 40-nm bulk CMOS process. To achieve high integration and low noise of the system, a high-efficiency dielectric resonator (DR) slot-line antenna is employed. The use of DR suppresses the surface wave leakage in the silicon substrate, minimizing the undesired coupling and ensuring stable radiation after integration with the receiver circuits. To enhance gain performance, the low-noise amplifier (LNA) incorporates the differential asymmetric transformer, which increases the effective transconductance (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$G_{\text {m}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of the transistor while preserving the wideband characteristics of the common-gate (CG) topology. By leveraging the virtual ground of the differential structure, the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$g_{\text {m}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-boosting technique bypasses the parasitic impedance of decoupling capacitors and bonding wires, thereby achieving the superior performance and interference immunity compared with single-ended topology. The receiver employs a differential architecture and achieves simultaneous noise and impedance matching through an asymmetric transformer in conjunction with the co-design of the antenna. Measurement results demonstrate that the receiver achieves a peak gain of 39.9 dB with a low power consumption of 48.1 mW. The receiver circuit noise figure (NF) is 6.5 dB, while the overall system NF is 7.5 dB. To the best of the authors’ knowledge, this represents the lowest system NF for D-band receivers using a bulk CMOS process.

title: Performance analysis of multi-user task offloading in mobile edge computing networks: A hierarchical modeling approach_supp1-3670701.docx
authors: Lili Jiang
abstract: Recently, the explosive growth of connected intelligent user devices has generated massive amounts of latency sensitive and computationally intensive applications and services, which leads to a sharp increase in network traffic. Therefore, UAV-assisted mobile edge computing networks (UMECN) are proposed to alleviate network congestion and reduce response time. This paper focuses on quantitatively evaluating UMECN service performance under different parameters, which helps to identify performance bottlenecks, and provide a basis for optimizing services, thereby improving service quality, enhancing user experience. Firstly, we propose the hierarchical model for capturing the dynamical behaviors of UMECN system, supporting interruption/resume uploading and returning process of delay sensitive tasks due to UAV movement. And then, we derive the performance metric formulas of UAV movement and UMECN system, and propose a multi-factor selection strategy base the task historical waiting time, queue length, and the number of remaining resources (WQRSS). Finally, we verify the approximate accuracy of the proposed formulas and model by comparing numerical and simulation results, demonstrate the effectiveness of the model through comparative experiments, and evaluate the impact of different parameters on UMECN service performance, which can help service providers effectively manage UMECN system.

title: Probabilistic Evaluation of Solar and Wind Power Plants Joint Operation for AES on Data of Weather Conditions Forecast
authors: Anatolijs Mahnitko, Boris Papkov, Tatjana Lomane, Inga Zicmane
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: A probabilistic approach to the assessment of solar power plants (SPP) and wind power plants (WPP) joint operation for an autonomous electrical system (AES) with taking into account the random nature of the electricity produced by them is proposed. The realization of this approach is illustrated on a concrete conditional example of a sample of retrospective weather conditions on solar activity and wind strength. The modeling results make it possible to determine the most probable conditions of power shortage in AES for specific possible forecasts of the states of the external environment for the variables “Sun” and “Wind”.

title: Towards a radar-only satellite-based methodology for subsurface characterization of salt pans
authors: Matías Ernesto Barber, Verónica Rocío Martínez, José Manuel Lattus
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Retrieval of the soil parameters beneath a salt crust using Synthetic Aperture Radars (SARs) relies on the knowledge of the surface roughness configuration. It involves costly ground surveys or the use of informed guesses, whose accuracy is challenging to assess due to the lack of available information. We present here a fully remotely-sensed methodology that uses a combination of $\mathbf{C}$ - and L-band: (1) co-polarized channels at C - and L -bands for a roughness estimate and (2) dual polarized channels at L-band for the retrieval of the subsurface configuration. This SAR-based methodology is tested on an Andean salt flat where a dedicated ground survey was made. Results are promising since the use of roughness estimated for a synergy between C- and L-bands performed equally well (in terms of root mean squared error, RMSE) regarding the methodology using fieldwork estimates. The brine salinity and the water table depth both retrieved from the SAR-based methodology were overestimated with an RMSE error of 2-3 % and 4-6 % o respectively.

title: Multi-Source Heterogeneous Data Based Joint Optimization Model for IoT Device Identification and Intrusion Detection
authors: Qingchan Liu, Yunxu Tao, Yujun Yin, Cong Lin, Chengxi Yin, Haolin Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This paper presents MH-JOID, a novel time-series neural network approach for simultaneous Industrial Internet of Things (IIoT) device identification and intrusion detection. Our framework employs a dual-branch architecture combining temporal convolution with adaptive gating mechanisms to capture complex behavioral patterns in network traffic. We evaluate MH-JOID against LSTM, CNN1D, and Transformer baselines on two benchmark datasets: ICS-Flow for industrial control system intrusion detection and CIC-IoT-22 for IoT device identification. Experimental results demonstrate that MH-JOID achieves 99.92 % F1-score on ICS-Flow with <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{5 8 k}$</tex> parameters, outperforming alternatives while maintaining the lowest inference latency (0.26 ms p50). On CIC-IoT-22, MH-JOID attains 97.54% F1-score with superior balance across all metrics. The proposed solution provides real-time monitoring capabilities essential for intelligent cybersecurity in industrial IoT environments.

title: Key Technologies of Intelligent Monitoring and Control System for Smart Construction-Oriented Large-Section Bridge-Building Machine
authors: Yuquan Hu, Zhiwen Ding
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Facing the urgent needs of national strategies such as “traffic power” and “new infrastructure” for the industrialization, intelligence and greening of bridges, this paper systematically puts forward the key technologies of intelligent monitoring and control system integrating “perception-decision-execution” around the goals of high precision, high efficiency and high safety in the construction of large-section bridge-building machines. Firstly, a monitoring framework for multi-sourccoordinate system and construction coordinate system, microsecond clock synchronization of PTP (Precision Time Protocol) and Adaptive Kalman Filter (AKF), high-precision alignment and optimal fusion of multi-source data such as lidar, fiber grating, inertial measurement unit (IMU) and GNSS in space and time dimensions are realized, and the comprehensive positioning accuracy after fusion is <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{\pm 1. 1 ~ m m}$</tex>; Secondly, based on model predictive control (MPC), a dynamic closed-loop control mechanism is established, aiming at the optimization of segmental beam line shape, embedding physical safety constraints such as hydraulic speed and pressure, and adopting rolling optimization and real-time feedback correction, the response time of calibration displacement is shortened by 74.4%, the hydraulic synchronization error is reduced by 74.3%, and the energy consumption is reduced by 28.3%. Thirdly, the LSTM-GRU hybrid deep learning and dynamic threshold strategy are combined to build an intelligent early warning and decision support system, so as to realize the high reliable early warning of structural stress overrun, hydraulic leakage and typhoon risks with an average advance of 52 minutes, an accuracy rate of 95.7% and a false negative rate of 0.7%. Finally, based on the 1666 m main span suspension bridge project, the system-level verification was carried out, and the results showed that the linear deviation of segmental beam was reduced from <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 5$</tex> mm to less than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2 ~\text{mm}$</tex>, which significantly improved the engineering quality and construction safety. The research results provide a replicable and popularized technical paradigm for upgrading the large-section bridge-building machine from “mechanization” to “intelligence”, which is of great significance for promoting the high-quality development of smart construction and bridge engineering in China.

title: Integration of MODIS imagery and HYSPLIT simulations for seasonal identification of airspace affected by volcanic ash from Popocatépetl
authors: Carolona Juárez-Pérez, José Carlos Jiménez-Escalona, José Luis Poom-Medina, Alejandro Monsiváis-Huertero, Rodrigo Florencio Da Silva
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: The Popocatepetl volcano $\left(19.02^{\circ} \mathrm{N}, 98.62^{\circ} \mathrm{W}, 5425\right.$ masl) began its current eruptive phase in late 1994 and has since exhibited low- to moderate-intensity eruptive events (VEI 1-3). Due to the crater’s elevation, volcanic ash is frequently injected into flight levels FL180 to FL260 ($6-8 \mathrm{~km}$ altitude), where it is rapidly dispersed by upper-level winds. Between 1999 and 2023, the Washington VAAC reported 2381 days with ash presence in the Popocatepetl region, with $\sim 90 \%$ of cases reaching these critical flight levels, posing a persistent hazard to commercial aviation. This study proposes a methodology for developing volcanic ash risk mitigation maps using integrated tools from remote sensing and atmospheric modeling. The goal is to identify regions with higher probability of ash cloud impact around the volcano, accounting for seasonal wind variability. A climatological analysis of upper-level wind patterns was conducted using NCEP/NCAR Reanalysis 1 data via NOAA’s READY platform, covering the years $2000-2023$. Wind profiles revealed seasonal dispersion tendencies, critical for predicting ash transport directions. MODIS satellite imagery (Terra and Aqua) was used to detect ash signatures using the Brightness Temperature Difference (BTD) technique between bands $31(11 \mu \mathrm{~m})$ and $32(12 \mu \mathrm{~m})$. Approximately 60% of eruptions reported by VAAC were identifiable in MODIS imagery To normalize the spatial data across events, the HYSPLIT dispersion model was employed to simulate 8 -hour ash cloud trajectories. This standardization allowed reconstruction of full dispersion patterns even when direct connection between ash clouds and the crater was missing. The combined analysis identified two main dispersion regimes: NNW-ESE (November-May) and SSW-WNW (July-September). These patterns support the generation of probabilistic hazard maps, offering a preventive tool for aviation safety planning in the Popocatepetl region.

title: OpenWAMS: Brazil’s Open Source Leap in Wide Area Grid Monitoring
authors: Ricardo Lira, Janio Los, Arthur Mouco
abstract:
The reliable and secure operation of large-scale, complex power systems increasingly relies on high-fidelity, synchronized data. Unlike traditional supervisory control and data acquisition (SCADA) systems, phasor measurement units (PMUs) provide real-time, high-sampling-rate data, which are crucial for the observability of dynamic phenomena. They offer precise, synchronized, and real-time measurements of fundamental electrical parameters, including three-phase voltage and current magnitudes, and phase angles, with much higher resolution than conventional systems. This enables immediate disturbance detection, grid stability monitoring, and accurate postevent analysis.

Within this context, this article introduces the reader to the openWAMS project, an innovative open source wide area monitoring system (WAMS) implemented by Brazil’s independent system operator (ONS), operational in the control room since mid-2024. Its open source nature facilitates customization, adaptability, and future proofing, allowing engineers to customize the platform and integrate algorithms tailored to the country’s grid challenges.

To provide context, the article first outlines the role of ONS and the current challenges in operating the National Interconnected Power System (NIPS). It then discusses the project’s implementation and operational impact, highlighting the migration experience of a multidisciplinary team and the rapid deployment of a minimum viable product (MVP).

Furthermore, the article discusses its architecture, which integrates open source components for data acquisition and storage, along with an open source web-based user interface (UI) for visualization. Examples of dashboards currently used in control rooms are presented. Finally, the article provides a glimpse into real-time advanced applications (e.g., continuous assessment of PMU data quality and disturbance detection), highlighting the ongoing development of the WAMS platform.

title: Multi-Band Zero-Order Resonance Antenna for WLAN and WiMAX Applications
authors: Bei Yang, Gang Liu, Aihua Zhang, Lingwei Zeng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: As the performance requirements for antennas in mobile communication devices continue to rise, single-band antennas are no longer sufficient to meet practical application needs. Additionally, as electronic devices continue to evolve toward miniaturization, the space available for antennas is increasingly limited. In this context, this paper proposes a compact zero-order resonant tri-band antenna designed for WLAN and WiMAX applications. Based on a 5.6 GHz monopole antenna structure, the antenna incorporates a zero-order resonant unit with a virtual ground structure, successfully introducing a new resonant frequency at the 2.45 GHz band. Additionally, a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\lambda / 4$</tex> radiation branch corresponding to the 3.5 GHz band is added, enabling stable operation across all three frequency bands. The designed tri-band antenna demonstrates excellent impedance matching characteristics across the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2.45 \text{GHz} / 3.5 \text{GHz} / 5.6 \text{GHz}$</tex> frequency bands, providing robust support for multi-band operation in 5 G communication systems.

title: A Digital Twin-Based Approach with a System-Agnostic Integration Method to Enable Intelligence Capabilities and What-If Scenario Orchestration
authors: Abdelhadi Belfadel, Stephen Creff, Jean-Patrick Brunet, Sin-Seok Seo, Guillaume Doquet, Kevin Mantissa, Christophe Duhil, Yann Bouju, Fikri Hafid, Amira Ben Hamida
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: In Industry 4.0, digital twins are transforming decision-making, operations optimization, and outcome prediction. However, implementing digital twins remains challenging, particularly in managing diverse intelligence capabilities defined by the Digital Twin Consortium’s Periodic Table of Capabilities, including orchestration, alerts, analytics, simulation, and AI. Current technology landscape lacks open-source solutions for designing, orchestrating, and executing “what-if” scenarios, as well as real-time monitoring, decision support, and interoperability.We propose an open-source framework addressing diverse intelligence capabilities, with a focus on openness, interoperability, and integration. Building on prior work in core data management using the Asset Administration Shell standard, we detail its approach, architecture, implementation, and a generic integration method applied to three industrial use cases to validate the framework’s feasibility and potential.

title: Analysis of Climbing Characteristics and Short-Term Prediction of Peak Summer (Winter) Temperature Load Based on Multivariable Coupling Model
authors: Tao Weilong, Kong Dejun, Chen Shuo, Zhang Chuanhai, Fang Huimin
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Aiming at the challenge of “double peak” climbing fluctuation of power load to the safe operation of power grid under frequent extreme temperature events, this paper proposes a multivariable coupling short-term forecasting model that integrates physical mechanism and deep learning. By introducing the apparent temperature to quantify the coupling effect of “temperature-humidity-wind speed”, the physical characteristic engine of building thermal inertia is constructed, the equivalent heat capacity and thermal resistance parameters are estimated reversely, and the nonlinear root of load climbing is revealed. Design a spatiotemporal Transformer architecture to capture long-term temporal dependencies with temporal attention, dynamically quantify meteorological coupling strength with variable attention, and achieve adaptive fusion of multiple variables. Propose the Load Climbing Event Warning Index (LCEI) and output the load forecast and climbing probability for the next 24 hours. Based on the data experiment of mega cities in East China from 2018 to 2023, the Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of the Phys-TST model decreased to 2.21 %, which is 20 % lower than the standard Transformer. The F1 Score of the climbing event reached 0.83, significantly better than traditional and deep learning benchmarks. This can explain the clear attention weight and reveal the dominant mechanism of “temperature humidity”, providing reliable decision support for peak summer (winter) scheduling of the power grid.

title: Construction of a Virtual Simulation Practical Teaching System for Equipment Management Course
authors: Qingsong Zhao, Yajie Dou, Renpeng Yuan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Equipment management courses are an essential component of the military curriculum system. Practical skills in equipment management represent a higher-level requirement built upon the foundational knowledge of equipment management. Virtual simulation practical teaching serves as a crucial method for enhancing these practical skills. This paper constructs a five-tier practical teaching system for equipment management courses, encompassing “practical background, practical objectives, practical content, practical platform, and practical support environment.” Additionally, a virtual simulation practical teaching platform is established, consisting of “foundation layer, resource layer, service layer, and business layer.” The paper also designs a virtual simulation practical teaching process for equipment management, which includes three stages—“pre-class preparation, in-class practice, and postclass improvement”—and involves two roles: teachers and students.

title: A Consistency-Oriented Verification Framework for Reliable Power Industry LLMs
authors: Binbin Wang, Lei Zhang, Youran Li, Naiwang Guo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The deployment of Large Language Models (LLMs) in the power industry shows promise for intelligent grid operations and cybersecurity analysis, but practical adoption is hindered by hallucinations and factual inconsistencies in critical tasks. To enhance reliability, this paper proposes a consistency-oriented framework for power-industry LLMs. We apply a Factored Chain-of-Verification (CoVe) mechanism to a Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct model fine-tuned with LoRA on heterogeneous power-system data, enabling inference-time self-verification of generated claims. Experiments on a proprietary Power Network Security Vulnerability Analysis dataset show that our approach significantly improves performance: the CoVe-enhanced model achieves 75.65% accuracy in security summarization, outperforming the LoRA baseline by 3.02 % and the foundation model by 12.63 %. Parameter sensitivity analysis further indicates that a moderate sampling temperature (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{T}=\text{0. 4}$</tex>) combined with skeptical prompting is key to maximizing verification diversity and correction effectiveness.

title: Deep Learning Based Adaptive Underwater Optical Wireless Communication System
authors: Lin Zhao, Zixian Yang, Ming Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Underwater optical wireless communication (UOWC) is a promising solution for high-rate data services in underwater environment. However, optical signals suffer from severe corruption caused by absorption, scattering and turbulence. To overcome the impacts from these environmental effects, we propose a deep learning based adaptive UOWC (DLAUOWC) scheme. By employing multiple light-emitting diodes (LEDs) to transmit signals of different wavelengths, the proposed deep learning (DL) based solution can effectively learn the channel characteristics and then adaptively optimizes modulation constellations in a three-dimensional space. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed scheme performs better than its conventional counterparts.

title: Design of a Thermally Stable Narrowband Absorber Using Multi-Physics Field Coupling Technology
authors: Zhi Lian, Jiahao Ren, Zemin Tian, Mian Peng, Feiran Wang, Wan Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the performance degradation of narrowband absorbers under high-temperature conditions caused by thermal deformation, this paper proposes a design method based on multi-physics coupling analysis to achieve thermal stability in the absorption performance of the absorber in hightemperature environments. By establishing a bidirectional electro-thermal-structural coupled numerical model, the temperature distribution, structural deformation, and their impact on absorption performance under combined external heating and internal electromagnetic loss were analyzed. The calculated results indicate that, without constraints, the absorber exhibits significant thermal warping with a maximum deformation of 0.023 m under 200° C external heating, leading to resonant frequency shift, reduced absorption rate, and the appearance of spurious peaks in the S11 curve. To suppress this performance degradation, a constraint fixture was designed based on engineering experience. Subsequent multi-physics coupling simulations verified that this fixture effectively controls the deformation, maintaining the electromagnetic performance of the deformed absorber comparable to its initial state. The multi-physics coupling framework developed in this study, which incorporates structural deformation feedback, provides an effective analytical tool and corresponding structural optimization strategy for designing thermally stable absorbers, offering practical reference value for solving the problem of electromagnetic performance degradation in complex thermal environments.

title: A Novel Model for Cyanobacterial Chlorophyll-a Estimation: Fusing Satellite Remote Sensing and In Situ Data
authors: Rocío Guido, Sofía Gutiérrez, Mariana Correa, Francisco Nemiña, Marina Compagnucci, Matias Bonansea, Oscar Oviedo, Anabella Ferral
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Harmful Cyanobacterial Blooms (HABs), detrimental to both environmental health and human wellbeing, are a growing global concern. Addressing this challenge, the present study adopts a dual approach, combining in situ measurements with satellite remote sensing imagery for effective monitoring. The primary objective of this research is to initiate the development of a semi-empirical algorithm specifically designed to quantify cyanobacteria chlorophyll-a concentration in San Roque Reservoir, Córdoba, Argentina. Notably, this represents the first algorithm of its kind to be developed for this specific water body, which frequently experiences severe blooms. The methodology involved leveraging satellite images from Landsat 8 and 9, acquired concurrently with comprehensive in situ field campaigns. During these campaigns, chlorophylla concentration was directly measured, radiometric data were collected, and microscopic algal abundance counts were performed to validate cyanobacteria presence. Among the relationships investigated, the most promising predictive model was a multiple linear regression incorporating the B5/B7 and B6/B7 band ratios as explanatory variables, which achieved a high coefficient of determination $\left(\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{0. 9 5}\right)$. This developed model was subsequently applied to a satellite image captured during a significant algal bloom event on February 20, 2025. The results demonstrated a clear consistency between the model’s chlorophyll predictions and the visual representation of the bloom in the RGB satellite composite. For future work, it is essential to further calibrate and refine this model using a more extensive and diverse dataset, ensuring its robustness and broader applicability for routine monitoring and management of San Roque Reservoir.

title: Research on AI-Driver Computer Professional Teaching Reform and Innovation
authors: Yaping Jiang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence technology, the traditional teaching system has been difficult to meet the demand for computer professionals in the era of artificial intelligence. How to empower computer professional teaching through artificial intelligence has become an important issue to be solved at present. This article puts forward key measures for artificial intelligence to empower computer professional teaching, focusing on how to use artificial intelligence technology to build an intelligent teaching platform, improve teachers' digital literacy, and promote the innovation of textbook construction, ideological and political education, and teaching evaluation systems. Research shows that this teaching reform not only significantly improves the teaching efficiency of teachers but also effectively improves students' practical ability, innovative thinking, and ideological awareness, thus greatly improving the overall quality of computer professional teaching. This article aims to provide valuable reference and references for front-line educators.

title: An Academic Status Prediction Approach by Ensemble Learning
authors: Jianbo Zhao, Xiaoyun Qin, Zhiheng Li, Bo Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Academic status is of the most importance in education, as it provides very useful information for improving education strategies and learning ways for teachers and students at school. The development and popularity of information and communication technology (ICT) bring both opportunities and challenges to academic status management. To capitalize on the opportunities and address the challenges, an academic status management system is presented, collecting and analyzing education data generated from offline classes, online learning platforms, and various smart sensors, and provides various functions for students and teachers to improve their teaching and learning strategies. In addition, an academic status prediction algorithm based on ensemble learning technology was proposed to help teachers and students proactively make their improvement decisions. In this study, the proposed algorithm is evaluated using real-world data, and the results demonstrate its effectiveness and superiority of the proposed algorithm.

title: Fine-Grained Asynchronous CPU-GPU Optimization for Efficient Large-Model Training
authors: Decheng Tang, Wanfeng Zhang, Shan Zhou, Jinbo Wang, Min Zhu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: We propose a fine-grained asynchronous CPU-GPU optimization framework for large-scale model training. Unlike existing offloading methods such as ZeRO-Offload and ZenFlow, which suffer from GPU stalls and partial overlap, our approach performs per-parameter Adam updates that fully overlap CPU computation, PCIe transfers, and GPU backward execution. Parameter scheduling is dynamically determined via backward hooks, forming a continuous three-stage pipeline of to-host transfer, CPU Adam computation, and parameter upload, with a GPU-tail update mechanism for load balancing. Experiments on an NVIDIA A800 (80 GB) show up to 80% shorter optimize step time, 45% lower GPU memory usage, and consistently higher GPU utilization compared with existing systems. This shows that fine-grained asynchronous optimization enables efficient and scalable large-model training on heterogeneous CPU-GPU platforms.

title: Lightweight YUV Low-Light Image Enhancement Network Based on Improved Attention Mechanism
authors: Yuqi Zhang, Feng Ye, Shuosen Ding, Mingxin Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Existing deep learning-based low-light image enhancement methods often involve substantial computational demands and complex model structures. To address this issue, this paper proposes a lightweight YUV low-light image enhancement network based on an improved attention mechanism. This approach processes images in the YUV color space, separating luminance from chrominance to enhance brightness while avoiding color distortion. The proposed method integrates three key innovations: First, the Multi-branch Discrete Convolutional Transposed Attention (MDTA) mechanism is employed to enhance feature extraction by capturing both local details and global correlations. Second, PixelShuffle replaces traditional upsampling operations to achieve smoother and clearer image detail reconstruction. Finally, a Convolutional Multi-stage Compressed Excitation Fusion (CSEF) module is designed to effectively preserve local texture information during feature fusion. Comparative experiments on the LOLv1 dataset demonstrate that this method outperforms existing counterparts while maintaining extremely low computational complexity and model size.

title: Performance Analysis and Adaptive Behavior of the Least Linex Function-Based Filter
authors: Hui Zhong, Shiwei Yun, Yihui Yang, Liwei Wang, Chuanwu Zhang, Sihai Guan
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a novel least Linex adaptive filtering (LLAF) algorithm to address the limitations of conventional methods in handling asymmetric measurement noise distributions. The proposed approach leverages the Linex loss function to develop a robust adaptive filtering framework. The algorithm's performance is evaluated through theoretical analysis, focusing on the mean and steady-state mean square errors (MSE). Furthermore, the computational complexity of the proposed algorithm is systematically analyzed. Extensive simulations in system identification scenarios demonstrate the algorithm's effectiveness, with experimental results aligning well with theoretical predictions. Comparative studies show that the proposed LLAF algorithm outperforms existing robust techniques regarding steady-state performance and robustness against diverse measurement noise conditions.

title: Development of a Portable Drilling Simulator Training System Based on Unity3D
authors: Jian Yang, Fugui Fang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: The portable drilling simulator features comprehensive functionality, compact equipment size, and excellent portability, making it highly favored and recognized by users. It plays a significant role in the field of drilling simulation training. This paper proposes a solution for a portable drilling simulator training system developed with Unity3D. Leveraging the powerful capabilities of the Unity3D engine, the system realistically constructs a 3D environment of drilling sites and displays real-time 3D animations synchronized with operations. It accurately simulates drilling workflows, enabling trainees to familiarize themselves with operational procedures and standards during simulation training. By observing parameter variations, trainees learn to detect, diagnose, and resolve faults, thereby enhancing operational proficiency and ultimately ensuring the safety of drilling operations.

title: A Study on Fetal Arrhythmia Detection in Single-Channel Abdominal ECG Based on Machine Learning and SHAP-based Interpretability Analysis
authors: Xiaojian Xu, Yifan Zhang, Yunze Zhu, Yinru Xu, Zhijun Mao, Huating Tu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate detection of fetal arrhythmia is crucial for improving pregnancy outcomes. Traditional Doppler ultra-sound, limited to fetal heart rate monitoring and susceptible to interference, contrasts with single-channel abdominal electrocardiography (ECG), which enables non-invasive continuous acquisition of detailed fetal ECG data but is hindered in clinical adoption by poor model interpretability. This study evaluated five machine learning models using 258 preprocessed samples from the Non-Invasive Fetal ECG Arrhythmic Database (NIFEADB), with preprocessing involving 50 Hz notch filtering, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.5-100 ~\text{Hz}$</tex> bandpass filtering (optimized for fetal ECG frequency to avoid noise), db4 wavelet denoising (preserving arrhythmia features), and Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique combined with Tomek Links (SMOTE-Tomek) for class imbalance. The optimal model achieved 90.38 % accuracy, 90.42 % precision, 90.38 % recall, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$90.37 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex>-score, and 94.57 % Area Under the Curve (AUC). RR interval features integrated temporal pattern analysis to capture sustained arrhythmias, while the SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) framework clarified the model's decision logic, addressing the “black-box” issue and completing the interpretability analysis to enhance clinical trust for precise diagnosis.

title: Sustainability-Oriented Multi-Objective Design of an LLC Onboard Charger Considering Semiconductor Carbon Footprint
authors: Meltem Lordoglu, Abdulsamed Lordoglu, Mehmet Onur Gulbahce
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This paper presents a multi-objective optimization framework for the design of an LLC resonant onboard charger that explicitly incorporates environmental considerations into semiconductor selection. While traditional design approaches primarily focus on electrical efficiency, volume, and cost, the proposed method introduces carbon footprint-both cradle-to-gate and cradle-to-grave-as a key design metric. Using chip-area-based global warming potential (GWP) estimation adapted from industrial disclosures and LCA literature, MOSFETs are evaluated for their embodied and usephase emissions. The optimization algorithm explores a wide design space of magnetic and semiconductor configurations, balancing efficiency, cost, volume, and GWP in a unified fitness function. Application to a $3.7 \mathrm{~kW}, 400 / 48 \mathrm{~V}$ onboard charger demonstrates the trade-offs between high-efficiency SiC devices and low-carbon Si alternatives, and reveals how carbon-aware design decisions can influence not only switch selection but also magnetics. The proposed approach provides a scalable, sustainability-oriented design methodology for modern power electronics.

title: LaMAGIC: Advanced Circuit Formulations for Language-Model-based Topology Generation for Analog Integrated Circuits
authors: Chen-Chia Chang, Wan-Hsuan Lin, Yikang Shen, Guanglei Zhou, Yiran Chen, Xin Zhang
publish: ACM Transactions on Design Automation of Electronic Systems
abstract: None

title: Adaptive Coarse-to-Fine Parameter Optimization for Incremental Hyperspectral Target Detection
authors: Jiahui Qu, Yonghui Chen, Wenqian Dong, Yunsong Li, Qian Du
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Hyperspectral target detection effectively identifies fixed targets using specific spectral signatures but suffers from catastrophic forgetting when detecting multiple targets of interest within the same scene. Traditional data replay strategies may further exacerbate training instability due to mislabeled samples. To address these limitations, we propose an Adaptive Coarse-to-Fine Parameter Optimization framework (ACFPO) for incremental hyperspectral target detection, which enables stable continual learning via structural adaptation and parameter sensitivity–aware refinement. ACFPO formulates the task as a dual-stage process: coarse-grained matching and fine-grained detection. Specifically, an Adaptive Spectral Prior-Guided Coarse Matching (AS-PCM) module is designed to hierarchically organize detection tasks into semantic domains and construct intra- and inter-class spectral pairs for coarse-level alignment to adaptively select optimal submodels. Subsequently, a Distance-Aware Localized Fine-Grained Parameter Optimization (DA-LFPO) module is proposed to identify layer-wise sensitive parameters of the selected submodels by measuring spectral–spatial discrepancy, enabling selective retraining to preserve model stability on previously learned classes. By dynamically freezing non-sensitive parameters and optimizing critical modules, our approach mitigates inherent model drift and gradient conflicts in replay-based methods. Extensive experiments on three benchmark datasets demonstrate the superior performance of ACFPO, achieving a balanced trade-off between stability of the existing target and the adaptability of incremental targets. The code is available at https://github.com/Jiahuiqu/ACFPO.

title: Table of Contents
authors: N/A

title: Constant Modulus Waveforms for IoT-Centric Integrated Sensing and Communications
authors: Tian Han, Shalanika Dayarathna, Rajitha Senanayake, Peter Smith, Aryan Kaushik, Alain Mourad, Richard A. Stirling-Gallacher, Jamie Evans
abstract: Integrated sensing and communications (ISAC) is considered a key enabler to support application scenarios such as the Internet-of-Things (IoT) in which both communications and sensing play significant roles. Multi-carrier waveforms, such as orthogonal frequency division multiplexing (OFDM), have been considered as good candidates for ISAC due to their high communications data rate and good time bandwidth property for sensing. Nevertheless, their high peak-to-average-power-ratio (PAPR) values lead to either performance degradation or an increase in system complexity. This can make OFDM unsuitable for IoT applications with insufficient resources in terms of power, system complexity, hardware size or cost. This article provides IoT-centric constant modulus signalling scheme designs that leverage the advantage of unit PAPR and thus are more suitable in resource-limited scenarios. More specifically, several single-carrier frequency and/or phase signalling schemes are considered. A comprehensive discussion on their radar sensing and communications performance is conducted based on performance metrics including the radar ambiguity function, the bandwidth property, the data rate, and the communications receiver complexity. Results demonstrate that under the constraint of unit PAPR, the sensing-communications tardeoff can be achieved by selecting among the discussed signalling schemes. Recommendations for linking particular low-rate IoT scenarios with those signalling schemes are also provided based on the their performance.

title: Comparative Analysis of Contemporary Quantum Computer Processors: Architectures, Performance and Perspectives
authors: Marko Vukšić, Jasmin Ćelić, Aleksandar Cuculić
abstract: Quantum computing promises to revolutionize several scientific and industrial domains by tackling problems that are intractable for classical supercomputers. At the centre of this effort is the quantum processor unit (QPU), implemented today across multiple physical qubit platforms. This review provides a comparative analysis of the state of quantum processors as of late 2024/early 2025, focusing on superconducting qubits, trapped ions, photonic systems, neutral atoms, silicon spin qubits, and emerging topological approaches. We compile and compare recent processor generations such as IBM’s Heron family, Google’s updated Sycamore-class devices, Quantinuum’s H-series, IonQ’s Aria and Forte, neutral-atom platforms with more than 1,000 trapped atoms, and silicon spin prototypes like Tunnel Falls. Key performance metrics, including qubit count, gate and readout fidelity, coherence time, operation speed, and holistic benchmarks such as Quantum Volume (QV) and Algorithmic Qubits (#AQ) are examined with reference to concrete experimental milestones. We discuss algorithmic capabilities demonstrated on current hardware, early realizations of logical qubits and fault-tolerant primitives, and the distinction between feasibility-oriented “quantum advantage” experiments and emerging signs of practical “quantum utility”. Finally, we outline the main technical bottlenecks for each platform on the path from today’s NISQ devices toward universal fault-tolerant quantum computers.

title: A Hierarchical Shared Control Framework for Distributed Drive Electric Vehicles Considering Driving Ability and Energy Efficiency
authors: Guangliang Liao, Zhenwu Fang, Jinxiang Wang, Guodong Yin, Marcelo H. Ang
abstract: To address the problem of personalized and ability-aware shared control for distributed drive electric vehicles, this paper proposes a hierarchical shared control framework that simultaneously accounts for driving ability and energy efficiency. First, a driving-ability metric is constructed by jointly considering path-tracking accuracy and handling stability, and a long short-term memory network (LSTM)-based short-horizon predictor is developed to capture its rapid variations under dynamic conditions. Then, an adaptive model predictive control (MPC) scheme is designed to coordinate the driver, active front-wheel steering (AFS), and direct yaw moment control (DYC) within a multi-agent formulation, dynamically adjusting multi-objective weights and authority allocation according to the predicted ability. At the lower layer, an energy-aware Nash-game torque vectoring allocation (TVA) strategy is introduced, where each in-wheel motor minimizes its own energy loss while satisfying global longitudinal and yaw-moment demands. Driver-in-the-loop experiments demonstrate that the proposed strategy significantly improves the level of human-machine cooperation, achieving approximately a 14.5% improvement in tracking accuracy and a 4.6% improvement in energy efficiency, while maintaining vehicle stability.

title: Uncertainty-Aware Jamming Mitigation with Active RIS: A Robust Stackelberg Game Approach
authors: Xiao Tang, Zhen Ma, Limeng Dong, Yichen Wang, Qinghe Du, Dusit Niyato, Zhu Han
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Malicious jamming presents a pervasive threat to the secure communications, where the challenge becomes increasingly severe due to the growing capability of the jammer allowing the adaptation to legitimate transmissions. This paper investigates the jamming mitigation by leveraging an active reconfigurable intelligent surface (ARIS), where the channel uncertainties are particularly addressed for robust anti-jamming design. Towards this issue, we adopt the Stackelberg game formulation to model the strategic interaction between the legitimate side and the adversary, acting as the leader and follower, respectively. We prove the existence of the game equilibrium and adopt the backward induction method for equilibrium analysis. We first derive the optimal jamming policy as the follower’s best response, which is then incorporated into the legitimate-side optimization for robust anti-jamming design. We address the uncertainty issue and reformulate the legitimate-side problem by exploiting the error bounds to combat the worst-case jamming attacks. The problem is decomposed within a block successive upper bound minimization (BSUM) framework to tackle the power allocation, transceiving beamforming, and active reflection, respectively, which are iterated towards the robust jamming mitigation scheme. Simulation results are provided to demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed scheme in protecting the legitimate transmissions under uncertainties, and the superior performance in terms of jamming mitigation as compared with the baselines.
