authors: N/A
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)

title: Fuzzy Decoupled Adaptive Impedance Control for a Five-Wheel Wall-Climbing Robot on Discontinuous Wall Surfaces_supp1-3670440.mp4
authors: Lei Chen
abstract: Aiming at the problems such as insufficient driving force, body oscillation, and contact instability that easily occur when wheel-based wall-climbing robots operate on discontinuous wall surfaces (such as corners and obstacles), this paper proposes an adaptive control strategy integrating joint impedance control and fuzzy decoupling compensation for a Five-Wheel Wall-Climbing robot(FWWC-robot). This strategy optimizes impedance parameters (mass, damping, and stiffness) online through an adaptive parameter update mechanism based on force error to minimize magnetic gap fluctuations and ensure stable adsorption. Meanwhile, a Mamdani fuzzy controller is used to dynamically compensate for the coupling interference of the front and rear swing arms, realizing real-time adjustment of joint compliance and improving the robot’s motion stability and dynamic adaptability when encountering abrupt changes in wall structures. Simulation and experimental results show that, compared with the traditional impedance control, this method reduces the attitude error by 60%, controls the magnetic gap fluctuation within 0.3 mm, achieves a 100% success rate of obstacle crossing under the working conditions of 63° corner and 30 mm obstacle, and keeps the attitude stability within ±1.2°. It provides an efficient and robust solution for the compliant motion of robots in complex wall environments, and can directly support the automated applications in high-risk operation scenarios such as ship derusting and large oil tank inspection.

title: A Few-Shot Multi-Energy Demand Forecasting Method Based on Meta-Learning Graph Neural Network
authors: Ruixia Zhang, Xuguang Hu, Qiuye Sun, Dongyue Chen
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Forecasting user-level multi-energy demand under data scarcity is challenging due to the strong spatial interdependencies among users and the complex coupling across heterogeneous energy carriers. To address the challenges, this paper proposes a few-shot forecasting approach for integrated electricity-gas-heat systems, enabling reliable prediction with limited historical data. First, a meta-learning graph neural network is developed to extract generalizable energy conversion features adaptively across user patterns, effectively alleviating data insufficiency. Second, to accurately capture system dynamics, a multi-relational spatiotemporal graph module is designed to hierarchically model intra-energy dynamics and inter-energy coupling, achieving adaptive fusion of heterogeneous spatiotemporal features. Furthermore, a knowledge-enhanced training mechanism is introduced by embedding physical energy conversion laws into meta-training via pseudo-tasks, enhancing physical consistency and preventing overfitting in few-shot adaptation. Extensive experiments on multi-energy datasets ultimately demonstrate that compared to state-of-the-art forecasting models, the proposed approach achieves significant improvements in average metrics 3.80% for MAE, 7.89% for RMSE, and 13.12% for MAPE).

title: Front Cover
authors: N/A

title: Modeling Ancient Shu Road Relics of China Through Fisheye-Adaptive 3-D Gaussian Splatting Reconstruction Framework
authors: Anbang Liang, Jingrong Wang, Yirong Pan, Han Wang, Yongrong Li, Zhipeng Chen, Qingquan Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Geoscience and Remote Sensing
abstract: The Ancient Shu Roads constitute a significant historical and cultural heritage attesting to the economic and humanistic interactions between China’s ancient central plains and the Ba-Shu regions. To achieve the digital preservation and management of the Ancient Shu Road relics, it is necessary to conduct 3-D reconstruction to obtain their photorealistic models. However, these relics are typically located in dense forests and cliffs, under such occluded environments, conventional visual 3-D reconstruction methods fail to meet the high-fidelity requirements of cultural heritage digitization. The recently emerged 3-D Gaussian splatting (3DGS) approach shows potential for real-scene reconstruction in complex environments, but its original pipeline follows pinhole camera model, making it unsuitable for fisheye images with severe distortion. To address these challenges, we propose a fisheye-adaptive 3DGS framework for modeling realistic scenes: First, a visual-inertial structure-from-motion (SfM) method is employed to recover real-scale sparse geometry initialization; Next, we introduce a hybrid distortion field and an octahedron projection model into 3DGS, which enables joint optimization of lens distortion and scene representation parameters, thereby remarkably suppressing the rendering artifacts caused by fisheye distortion and building high-fidelity 3-D models. We collected a close-range dataset with handheld devices across several representative Ancient Shu Road heritages, upon which we conducted comprehensive evaluation of our method, validating its superiority in 3-D reconstruction tasks under occluded environments, and offering a viable new solution for cultural heritage digital preservation.

title: Decadal Assessment of Environmental Changes in the Cabrobó’s Desertification Nucleus Using Orbital Multidata and GIS
authors: José Galdino De Oliveira Júnior, Pabrício Marcos Oliveira Lopes, Cristina Rodrigues Nascimento, Geber Barbosa De Albuquerque Moura, José Francisco De Oliveira Júnior
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: The effective discrimination and combat of land degradation, principally in arid, semi-arid, and dry sub-humid environments, relies on the efficient application of detection and monitoring Land Use and Land Cover Changes (LULCC) techniques. In this way, this study evaluated the decadal trends of environmental changes in Cabrobó’s Desertification Nucleus (CDN), Pernambuco, Brazilian semiarid region, between 2001 and 2021. For this, we manipulated CHIRPS data, MODIS products (MOD13A1, MCD43A3, and MOD16A2), and MapBiomas data in the Google Earth Engine (GEE) digital platform and the QGIS software (version 3.10.9). We performed a space-temporal trend assessment for all biophysical parameters via non-parametric tests Mann-Kendall (MK) and Sen’s Slope Estimator (SSE). The results obtained showed the effectiveness of using these orbital products for long-term environmental analysis, as well as highlighting the municipalities of Belém do São Francisco, Itacuruba, and Floresta with significant trends at the 1% probability level of the biophysical indices (from decrease to potential evapotranspiration and NDVI, and addition to albedo surface) due to a more change in land use and cover. This methodology proved useful for studies on the discrimination of spacetemporal trends in other Caatinga areas after the appropriate methodological adjustments.

title: A Study of the Effects of Intelligent Learning Assistants on Learning Behaviour and Performance in Open Education Programming Learning
authors: Bing Wu, Jun Xiao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Currently, AI technology is extensively integrated and applied within educational environments. In this new cognitive synergy framework, it is essential to investigate how and to what extent AI interventions influence learners' behaviors and performance. This study first employs AI and knowledge graph technology to establish a transformed learning environment that leverages intelligent learning systems to assist open education learners in programming courses. For the intelligent assistant, three supportive learning strategies are developed with the goal of altering learning pathways to encourage deep learning. Students are more likely to adhere to the learning routes that the intelligent teaching assistant (ILA) suggests during interventions, according to an analysis of learner behavior logs. Notably, students continue to show high levels of engagement even after several rounds of intervention. The impact of AI-assisted learning on academic achievement under intervention settings is also compared and examined in this study. Performance analysis reveals that AIassisted learning positively impacts academic achievement. The findings suggest that designing effective learning intervention strategies for intelligent assistants fosters a positive and supportive learning environment, ultimately contributing to students' ongoing exploration and deep learning.

title: A Novel Educational Paradigm for the AI Era: From Problem-Oriented Thinking to Human-AI Collaborative Innovation
authors: Yun Guo, Xiaoyu Yan, Hong Zhao, Jian Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Confronting the dual challenges of “educational involution” and AI's disruption of traditional pedagogy, this paper proposes a systematic and innovative educational paradigm. Grounded in the “Tao of Teaching” and first principles, it introduces the Problem-Oriented Thinking (POT) Cognitive Model to replace the conventional Knowledge-Oriented Thinking (KOT) Cognitive Model, establishing POT Outcome-Based Education (POT-OBE). To implement this philosophy, the paper elaborates on the 5E pedagogy (Excitation, Exploration, Enhancement, Execution, Evaluation), which aims to elevate student outcomes from “knowledge + ability” to “ability + cognition”. In response to the AI era, this paradigm evolves into the “Nankai Model”, which recasts the roles of educators and learners as “the teacher as screenwriter and director, the student as lead actor, and AI as partner”. By restructuring curriculum logic, redefining roles, and developing a human-AI collaborative innovation competency map, this model systematically addresses the fundamental questions of “what to teach”, “how to teach”, and “how to learn” in the context of AI. Empirical results from multiple course implementations demonstrate that this paradigm significantly enhances students' complex problem-solving skills, innovative consciousness, and higher-order cognition, offering a viable theoretical and practical solution to overcome educational involution and navigate the challenges posed by AI.

title: Computer Vision-Based Driver Behavior Analysis and Fatigue Detection System for Advanced Driver Assistance
authors: Litao Liu, Tao He, Chao Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Driver fatigue constitutes a predominant contributor to traffic accidents worldwide, necessitating the development of reliable real-time detection systems for intelligent transportation infrastructure. This research presents a multi-modal deep learning architecture for driver drowsiness detection that integrates facial feature analysis with adaptive temporal fusion mechanisms. The proposed framework extracts eye state dynamics, mouth behavior patterns, and head pose trajectories through lightweight convolutional neural networks, with subsequent processing of these heterogeneous feature streams via bidirectional Long Short-Term Memory networks enhanced by cross-modal attention mechanisms. A personalized baseline calibration strategy accommodates inter-subject behavioral variability through dynamic threshold adjustment. Experimental validation on three benchmark datasets comprising 141 subjects and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1,260,000$</tex> frames demonstrates 96.8 % classification accuracy, 0.954 macro-averaged F1-score, and 0.994 area under the receiver operating characteristic curve, representing 4.3 % improvement over state-of-the-art methods. The system achieves real-time inference at 41 frames per second with end-to-end latency below 25 milliseconds on embedded platforms, while maintaining accuracy above 93.5 % under environmental perturbations spanning illumination variations from 5 to 50,000 lux. These characteristics collectively establish practical viability for deployment within advanced driver assistance systems.

title: A Fourth-Order Low-Power Filter With Improved Figure of Merit for Wearable Biomedical Applications
authors: Naman Garg, Diksha Thakur, Aryan Choudhary, Deepika Bansal
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: The global demand for wearable biomedical systems is growing, as it is used in identifying brain disorders using IC technology, mainly in capturing electroencephalogram signals. For achieving a long battery life, architectures used in the wearable biomedical systems must be capable of operating at lowest power possible and at low frequencies. This article analyses a $4^{\text {th }}$-order LPF designed by cascading two $2^{\text {nd }}$-order biquads through class-AB, folded, pseudo-resistive biasing and flipped source follower techniques for low-power operation. The proposed LPF demonstrates a gain of −0.28 dB, a cut-off frequency of 100 Hz, dynamic range of 49.28 dB, input referred noise of μV<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">rms</inf> and a total harmonic distortion of −57.68 dB. Additionally, it demonstrates low power consumption of 0.6 nW and minimal figure-of-merit of 5.93 × 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">−15</sup> J. According to these findings, LPF architecture is extremely helpful for long-term EEG acquisition systems with low figure-of-merit and low power usage, paving the way for environmentally friendly wearable biomedical systems.

title: Database-Oriented Driver Loss Evaluation of PWM and PAM Methods for BLDC Motor Applications
authors: Yusuf Baran Subasi, Ege Uzun, Batı Eren Ergun, Mehmet Onur Gulbahce
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This paper presents a database-oriented driver loss evaluation of two widely adopted modulation strategies for Brushless DC (BLDC) drives: pulse-width modulation (PWM) and pulse-amplitude modulation (PAM). A comprehensive MOSFET loss model, including conduction and switching losses was implemented in MATLAB/Simulink. A simulation framework was developed in which motor parameters, switching behavior, and semiconductor device characteristics were incorporated. Simulation is completed with a real BLDC motor which has 62 W rated power, 24 V DC rated voltage and 3000 rpm rated speed. Additionally, a MOSFET database was created to allow comparison of multiple device technologies based on their conduction and dynamic parameters. The findings highlight the fundamental trade-off between efficiency and torque quality in BLDC drive design and provide a systematic framework for selecting both modulation strategy and power devices.

title: BPRET: A Blockchain-Based Framework for Decentralized, Secure, and Privacy-Preserving Real-Time Electricity Trading
authors: Youshui Lu, Haitao Xu, Yaohui Li, Rui Li, Jie Feng, Jianbo Du
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: The global low-carbon transition makes real-time electricity markets crucial for balancing supply and demand. Blockchain-based smart contracts enable automated, tamper-proof, decentralized trading for such markets but suffer from inherent transparency-causing critical vulnerabilities like Residual Bids Extractable Value (RBEV). We propose BPRET system, a blockchain-based privacy-preserving real-time electricity trading system integrating smart contracts and MPC. It enables lightweight, privacy-aware, fair trading without trusted third parties, with extensive simulations confirming its practicality.

title: A Joint Optimization Approach for Low-ISL Binary Sequence Design
authors: Jiali Chen, Jiahui Cao, Yuxi Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: The design of binary sequences with low aperiodic autocorrelation sidelobes is essential for radar, sonar, and wireless communications. In this paper, we propose an enhanced algorithm for the design of aperiodic binary sequences with low integrated sidelobe level (ISL), building on the Cyclic Algorithm New (CAN) algorithm. The proposed algorithm, named CDLS-CAN (Continuous-Discrete Local Search CAN), consists of two main stages. First, a converging function is introduced to relax the constant modulus constraint and enable joint optimization of both amplitude and phase, progressively driving the solution toward binary values. Second, the single-bit flip local search (SBFLS) algorithm is incorporated to further optimize the resulting sequences. Numerical results demonstrate that the CDLS-CAN algorithm achieves superior merit factors (MF) compared to existing methods, confirming its effectiveness in binary sequence design.

title: Hybrid Neighborhood Attention Network for 3D Indoor Semantic Segmentation
authors: Gengchen Shi, Hongtao Wei, Yong Hu, Chaoyan Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: 3D indoor point cloud semantic segmentation requires joint modeling of fine-grained geometric details and large-scale contextual information, which is challenging due to the irregular structure of point clouds. We propose Hybrid Neighborhood Attention Network (HyNex), an efficient encoder—decoder framework that integrates multi-scale geometric modeling with localized attention. The encoder employs a Multi-Scale Neighborhood Feature Enhancement (MSNFE) module and an Attention-Guided Sampling (AGS) strategy to construct a task-adaptive point hierarchy. The decoder uses stacked Regional Cross Attention Blocks (RCAB) to propagate contextual information from coarse to fine resolutions. Experiments on the S3DIS dataset demonstrate that HyNex achieves competitive performance compared with representative state-of-the-art methods, and ablation studies validate the effectiveness of the proposed components.

title: A Novel Gate-Level Parity Checker Design for Sequential Data Streams
authors: Yuxin Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: A parity checker is a commonly used circuit component for detecting data transmission errors, which logic gates and flip-flops can easily construct. Parity checkers are crucial for error detection in data transmission and are typically constructed from logic gates and flip-flops. Therefore, this paper presented a newly designed gate-level parity checker based on basic circuit elements, aiming to reduce power consumption while balancing circuit complexity and flexibility. The novel digital circuit structure integrates a 4-bit parallel exclusive-OR (XOR) tree with a T flip-flop, combining both combinational and sequential circuit techniques. A key innovation of this design is an All-Zero Detector, which controls the flip-flop to enter a “rest” state under specific circumstances, thereby lowering power consumption. The proposed design achieves lower power consumption at 55 mW, as confirmed through postimplementation simulation, which also verified its full functionality. Compared to several conventional gate-level parity checker designs, this novel design offers a high level of flexibility, primarily due to the introduction of a reusable XOR tree and a multiplexer (MUX).

title: Privacy-Preserving Computing Architecture for Secure Big Data Processing in Distributed Environments
authors: Haiqing Zhang, Wenxuan Hou, Yue Guan, Zihao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: This paper proposes a privacy-preserving computing architecture based on the fusion of homomorphic encryption, secure multi-party computation and differential privacy. During the design process, the system uses Paillier homomorphic encryption to ensure the security of data transmission and storage, introduces secure multi-party computation to achieve distributed collaborative processing in an encrypted state, and uses differential privacy mechanisms to improve the output results' ability to resist inference attacks. Experimental verification based on the UNSW-B20 IoT big data set shows that this architecture can effectively reduce the attack success rate and improve the level of privacy protection when processing ultra-large-scale data tasks, while maintaining low communication overhead and good system throughput. The research results provide a feasible path for the engineering application of privacy protection and secure computing technologies in distributed big data environments, and lay the foundation for the future technological evolution of related fields.

title: Teacher Support and Pre-service Teachers' Behavioral Intention to Adopt AI Tools: The Mediating Roles of Perceived Effectiveness and Learning Gains
authors: Ning Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study used the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) to examine how teacher support (TS) influenced preservice early childhood education teachers' behavioral intention (BI) to adopt AI-assisted learning tools. Data were collected from 178 students at a private university in Shanghai, and mediation analysis was performed to evaluate the theoretical model. Results showed that TS strongly predicted both perceived effectiveness (PE) and perceived learning gains (PLG), which subsequently influenced BI positively. Interestingly, no direct relationship was found between TS and BI, indicating a full mediation effect. The findings indicated the important role of both PE and PLG in shaping technology acceptance and emphasized that teacher support indirectly fostered students' intentions to use AI tools. This research provides evidence for enhancing ECE teacher training programs and creating supportive educational environments that promoted meaningful AI integration.

title: Intelligent Generation Method of Physical Education Teaching Training Program Based on Machine Learning
authors: Xin Feng, Fengyang Fu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Traditional physical education teaching and training often rely on coach experience and subjective judgment to develop plans, which suffer from insufficient scientificity and individualization. Therefore, research on intelligent generation of sports teaching and training programs based on machine learning is carried out: sensors are deployed in key parts and equipment of athletes to comprehensively collect and asynchronously process multimodal sports data to align their time; Extracting motion feature sequences based on 3D human body models and constructing athlete ability portraits; Design personalized training plan generation methods using machine learning algorithms to achieve intelligent customization. The experiment shows that this method can develop targeted plans based on the actual situation of athletes, and the average strength improvement rate of students exceeds 30%, with significant advantages.

title: Conjunctive Keyword Search with Fine-Grained Access Control from Lattice
authors: Peng Wang, Hui Wang, Jun Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of encrypted data outsourcing storage in the Internet of Things, how to achieve secure and efficient multi-keyword retrieval on encrypted data has become a key challenge. As a core technology for achieving ciphertext data retrieval, the existing schemes of conjunctive keyword search mostly based on traditional number-theoretic problems, which are susceptible to quantum computing attacks and have deficiencies in fine-grained access control. To mitigate this, we proposed a conjunctive keyword search with fine-grained access control from lattice which can resist chosen keyword attacks, named SPECK. Its security is relied on the hardness of LWE problem, and thus it can resist quantum attacks. Our scheme adopts subset predicate encryption technology, which can achieve highly fine-grained access control in multi-user scenarios. We conduct a performance benchmarking of major algorithms to compare the efficiency of our scheme with other searchable encryption approaches.

title: A Bibliometric Analysis of Trends of Machine Learning Research in Children’s Mental Health and Online Gaming Disorder
authors: Swati Singh, Dharmendra Kumar, Vijaylakshmi Sajwan
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: The mental health of children has become a growing concern in the digital age. Numerous studies indicate that excessive internet use and online gaming addiction contribute significantly to mental health problems among adolescents. To better understand research progress in this area, this study conducts a bibliometric analysis of 3,204 publications from the Scopus and PubMed databases. The analysis explores key research themes and trends through network analysis of cooccurring keywords, citation patterns of documents and authors, and the application of artificial intelligence in this domain. In addition, bibliographic coupling of documents, authors, and organizations was performed to examine collaborative research patterns. The clustering results reveal four major research streams: mental health disorders, machine learning techniques, neurodevelopmental conditions, gaming disorders, and digital well-being. The findings highlight the United States as the leading contributor (1423 documents, 72855 citations, total link strength: 712). Among authors, David Shaffer received the highest number of citations. In terms of journals, the International Journal of Environmental Research and Public Health published the largest number of documents (231, with 3991 citations), while the Journal of Child Psychology and Psychiatry achieved the highest citation count (13461 across 159 publications). Overall, the results suggest that machine learning approaches can be effectively used to predict gaming disorders in children, while deep learning models may support assessment of sleep quality, particularly for children experiencing gaming-related mental health challenges.

title: Improving Task Performance of the Teachers by Blended Learning as Their Realization of the Organizational Commitment (Systematic Literature Review Approach)
authors: Arcadius Benawa, Raja Oloan Tumanggor
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Blended learning has developed into a significant pedagogical approach in the world of education, especially in improving teacher performance. Blended learning combines elements of face-to-face learning with digital technology, enabling a more flexible and personalized learning experience. In addition, this approach not only aims to improve the quality of teaching, but also to strengthen the commitment of teacher organizations as part of the educational ecosystem. Teachers' organizational commitment reflects their level of involvement, loyalty and participation in achieving the vision and mission of educational institutions. This research uses a Systematic Literature Review (SLR) approach to analyze how the implementation of blended learning can influence teacher performance and reflect their organizational commitment. This study identified, evaluated, and synthesized 30 relevant scientific articles, published between 2015 and 2023. The research process began with filtering articles based on main keywords such as “blended learning,” “teacher performance,” and “organizational commitment,” followed by with analysis of emerging themes. The research results show that blended learning has a significant contribution to improving teacher's task performance in various aspects, such as time efficiency, teaching innovation, and mastery of technology. Teachers who implement blended learning are better able to meet the learning needs of individual students, and show improvements in adaptation to technology. Apart from that, blended learning also strengthens teachers' organizational commitment by encouraging their involvement in professional training organized by institutions, increasing their sense of belonging to the institution, and increasing work motivation. This research also reveals challenges in implementing blended learning, such as limited technological infrastructure, the digital divide between urban and rural areas, and resistance to change among teachers. To overcome this obstacle, policies are needed that support infrastructure development, continuous training for teachers, and effective change management strategies. Through systematic analysis, this research provides in-depth insight into the relationship between blended learning, teacher performance, and organizational commitment. It is hoped that this article can become a basis for policy makers and educational institutions to design more effective strategies for integrating technology in the learning process and developing teacher capacity.

title: Enhancing Academic Integrity, Motivation, and Soft Skills in Electrical Engineering Education in the AI Era
authors: Antons Patlins
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This paper proposes a future-oriented framework for integrating artificial intelligence (AI) into electrical engineering education while preserving academic integrity, enhancing student motivation, and strengthening soft skills. Through a review of current literature, institutional practices, and practical case studies conducted in European educational institutions, the study examines how AI technologies reshape learning environments and student behavior. The results demonstrate that, when strategically embedded in digital learning environments, AI can significantly support the development of essential soft skills such as communication, collaboration, and adaptability, while also fostering intrinsic motivation and ethical awareness. The paper introduces a fourpillar framework - Ethical Foundations, Cognitive Engagement, Collaborative Learning, and Reflective Practice, that provides educators and policymakers with practical guidance for responsible $A I$ adoption. This framework ensures that future engineers remain technically proficient, critically minded, and ethically grounded in the rapidly evolving digital landscape. The findings contribute to the ongoing discourse on aligning AI use with human-centric educational values and offer actionable strategies for higher education institutions seeking to prepare students for complex, interdisciplinary engineering challenges in the AI era.

title: Constructing a Practical Teaching Model for Digital Media Arts Major in the Context of AIGC
authors: Qingqing Wang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This paper addresses the application of AIGC (Artificial Intelligence Generated Content) technology in the practical teaching of the Digital Media Arts major and explores the construction of a practical teaching model. Firstly, it analyzes the current state of practical teaching in Digital Media Arts, identifying existing problems. It then examines the application status of AIGC technology in this context and its impact on practical instruction. Secondly, principles for constructing a practical teaching model oriented towards students' personalized developmental needs are proposed. A “multidimensional, modular, and collaborative” practical teaching model is constructed, encompassing precise alignment with student needs, cultivation of problem-solving abilities, emphasis on innovative thinking, and integration of professional literacy training. Subsequently, a specific practical teaching model framework is introduced, including project-based teaching, team collaboration, interdisciplinary integration, and comprehensive evaluation. This research provides valuable insights and exploration for constructing practical teaching models for Digital Media Arts in the AIGC context.

title: Multiport Converter Based Hybrid Storage Integration for Improved DC Power Delivery Resilience in DC Microgrids
authors: Neelesh Yadav, Andrii Chub, Dmitri Vinnikov
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Renewable energy sources like solar and wind are growing quickly, leading to more use of microgrids for local power supply. However, their variable nature makes reliable power delivery challenging. Hybrid energy storage systems (HESS), which combine batteries and supercapacitors, help by storing energy long-term and responding quickly to sudden changes. This paper proposes a highly efficient multiport power converter designed for HESS in dc microgrids. The converter improves power delivery resilience by managing energy flow between solar panels, batteries, and supercapacitors efficiently. The new design reduces size and cost while increasing performance. The effectiveness of the system is verified through simulations in PSIM software, showing stable and reliable dc power delivery even with rapid disturbances.

title: A Hybrid AHP-Entropy-TOPSIS Method for Evaluating “Dual-Qualified” Foreign Language Teachers in the VETS Training Base
authors: Qingwei Meng, Changyu Xu, Ran Chen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Based on the Vocational English Test System (VETS) training base, this study constructs a competency assessment model for “dual-qualified” foreign language teachers based on a newly-built hybrid AHP-Entropy-TOPSIS method after comparing the limitation of AHP and Entropy-TOPSIS method respectively. By combining two methods, this study not only retains the weight's authority in the AHP method but also corrects the objective biases caused by the Entropy-TOPSIS method, making the evaluation results more scientific and reasonable. Through the assessment, it was found that foreign language teachers scored relatively low in the indicators of “enterprise practice” and “social service”. Based on the construction of the VETS training base, reasonable suggestions are given to the teachers for improving their “dual-teacher” qualities.

title: Research on Real Pedestrian Recognition Based on YOLOv10 and Deep Information Fusion
authors: Peng Yuan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the popularization of intelligent monitoring and autonomous driving technologies, accurate pedestrian identification and authenticity discrimination are key challenges. To overcome the vulnerability of traditional methods to lighting variations and complex background interference, this paper proposes a pedestrian recognition method fusing YOLOv10 and Depth Anything. It first employs YOLOv10s for object detection, then generates high-precision depth maps via Depth Anything to construct 3D scene information. Meanwhile, it modifies LeNet5 into LeNet5-DP (LeNet5 Depth Processing), a classification model incorporating batch normalization, adaptive pooling, and Dropout to classify depth maps and accurately distinguish real pedestrians from dummies. Experiments on the KITTI dataset demonstrate 96.43 % recognition accuracy, outperforming 2 state-of-the-art models in accuracy and generalization. Robustness tests confirm its strong anti-interference capability under extreme lighting and complex occlusion, while ablation studies validate the indispensable role of each core module. This work provides a reliable reference for intelligent monitoring and autonomous driving applications.

title: Public Health, the U.S. Federal Communications Commission, and the Wireless Industry Association [Health Matters]
authors: James C. Lin

title: Fusiontrack: an Adaptive Hybrid Tracking Framework Overcoming Rapid Object Motion, Frequent Occlusions, Dynamic Scene Transitions, and Appearance-Scale Variations
authors: Akhil Penta, Vaibhav Adwani, Ankush Chopra
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Visual tracking in dynamic winter sports presents unique challenges, including fast athlete movements, intricate multi-camera setups, and various occlusions. We introduce FusionTrack, a novel single-object tracking (SOT) framework. It provides reliable skier tracking in demanding broadcast sports videos and similar datasets. Our approach strategically adapts multi-object tracking (MOT) principles, such as appearancebased re-identification and intelligent detection fusion, into a specialized Tracking-by-Detection (TbD) pipeline for single skier. Moving beyond traditional Kalman filter-based methods, our main contributions include: 1) A deep LSTM sequence model for improved bounding box predictions, enhancing temporal motion modeling; 2) A novel adaptive association strategy that thoughtfully switches from a harmonic mean blend of motion and appearance cues to solely an appearance-based metric during sudden camera changes, detected via simple pixel-wise scene change; and 3) A filtered Exponential Moving Average (EMA) for appearance embeddings, which maintains identity consistency despite noise or occlusions. Tested extensively on the challenging SkiTB [1] dataset, FusionTrack achieved an average F1-score of 88.5%, outperforming the previous best of 87.7%. For the Freestyle Skiing (FS) discipline, our F1-score reached 84.9%, against an existing best of 78.9%. This represents an overall absolute improvement of 8% compared to our team's earlier transformer-based baseline, which had an average F1-score of 80.5%.

title: Lightweight FastCNN-Based Apple Maturity Classification for Real-Time Orchard Deployment
authors: Yixuan He, Guanyu Chen, Minyao Xu, Jinpeng Ma
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate and real-time assessment of apple maturity is essential for intelligent harvesting and grading. This paper proposes FastCNN, a lightweight convolutional network tailored to subtle colour-texture maturity cues. On our orchard image dataset captured under natural illumination, FastCNN achieves a macro F1-score of 0.9936 with only 0.34 M parameters and 18.2 M FLOPs. Throughput reaches 372.55 FPS measured on a desktop NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 GPU (batch size 1). Since the current dataset comes from a single orchard environment, future work will validate generalization across varieties, orchards, and seasons, and benchmark FastCNN on concrete edge devices.

title: The Effect of Gamification Features in Increasing User Interaction on Online Shopping Applications
authors: Fauzan Mohammad, Yogi Pratama Hutagalung, Adele Mailangkay
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: This study examines the impact of gamification features on user interaction in online shopping applications within the Indonesian e-commerce context. With Indonesia’s rapid e-commerce growth and high mobile user population, understanding effective user engagement strategies becomes crucial. The research integrates gamification elements with the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) to investigate how game mechanics influence continued use intention through various mediating variables. Using Structural Equation Modeling (SEM), the study tests eleven hypotheses examining relationships between gamification of mobile apps, flow experience, perceived ease of use, perceived enjoyment, perceived usefulness, attitudes towards mobile use, and continued use intention. The research addresses a significant gap in the literature by proposing an enhanced theoretical framework that combines gamification elements with technology acceptance factors specifically for online shopping applications. Findings from this study provide valuable insights for e-commerce platform developers and marketers on implementing effective gamification strategies to enhance user experience, encourage interaction, and build long-term loyalty. This research contributes to advancing theories related to gamification and technology acceptance while offering practical guidance for improving mobile shopping application design in the competitive digital marketplace.

title: Does Font Size and Illustration Size Affect Your Study?
authors: Lujie Zhao, Weizhao Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Teaching courseware has always been the main teaching tool in modern classroom teaching. How to design and make teaching courseware better has become an important issue for teachers and researchers. In this study, 48 students in G high school were selected as the research objects, and eye movement experiments were carried out in two different images and texts presentation. The tobbi Pro Spectrum Desktop eye tracker was used to collect the eye movement behavior of the objects when learning English words. Combined with their retention test scores, transfer test scores, and cognitive load, the study found that the English presentation in the teaching courseware had an impact on learners' cognitive processing and learning performance. When using word+static picture (5cm*7.5cm size), the effect of transfer learning and memory is significant. When using word in 32 font size+static picture, the average fixation duration is longer, the fixation times are more frequent, and learners need to deal with more cognitive resources and cognitive load. When using word+static picture <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(5 ~\text{cm} * 7.5 ~\text{cm}$</tex> size), the average fixation duration is longer and the cognitive load is heavier.

title: Robust IDA-PBC Control Design for Grid-Forming Converter
authors: Shafquat Hussain, Massimiliano Passalacqua, Luis Vaccaro, Mohammed Ali Khan, Navid Bayati
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The dynamic performance of the controllers is crucial in obtaining stability for the power system. This paper proposes a robust interconnection and damping assignment-based passivity controller design using a port-controlled Hamiltonian model with effective dynamic performance, tracking, and power quality compared to a classical controller. Classical control is combined with modern control to compute the required damping resistances of the interconnection and the damping assignmentbased passivity controller to enhance the system stability and robustness. The controller is implemented on a grid-forming converter with load variations, and the system is tested in MATLAB Simulink to validate the dynamic performance of the controller.

title: Deep learning-based damage recognition techniques for aero-engine surface images: current status, challenges and research advances
authors: Zhou Fang, Kai Zhao
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
abstract: None

title: Client-Cooperative Split Learning
authors: Haiyu Deng, Yanna Jiang, Guangsheng Yu, Qin Wang, Xu Wang, Wei Ni, Shiping Chen, Ren Ping Liu
abstract: Model training is increasingly offered as a service for resource-constrained data owners to build customized models. Split Learning (SL) enables such services by offloading training computation under privacy constraints, and evolves toward serverless and multi-client settings where model segments are distributed across training clients. This cooperative mode assumes partial trust: data owners hide labels and data from trainer clients, while trainer clients produce verifiable training artifacts and ownership proofs. We present CliCooper, a multi-client cooperative SL framework tailored for cooperative model training services in heterogeneous and partially trusted environments, where one client contributes data, while others collectively act as SL trainers. CliCooper bridges the privacy and trust gaps through two new designs. First, Differential Privacy–based activation protection and secret label obfuscation safeguard data owners' privacy without degrading model performance. Second, a dynamic chained watermarking scheme cryptographically links training stages on model segments across trainers, ensuring verifiable training integrity, robust model provenance, and copyright protection. Experiments show that CliCooper preserves model accuracy while enhancing resilience to privacy and ownership attacks. It reduces the success rate of clustering attacks (which infer label groups from intermediate activation) to 0%, decreases inversion-reconstruction (which recovers training data) similarity from 0.50 to 0.03, and limits model-extraction–based surrogates to about 1% accuracy, comparable to random guessing.

title: Are Large Vision-Language Models Robust to Adversarial Visual Transformations?
authors: Daizong Liu, Xiaowen Cai, Pan Zhou, Xiaoye Qu, Lichao Sun, Wei Hu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Large vision-language models (LVLMs) have demonstrated remarkable capabilities across a wide range of multimodal understanding and reasoning tasks. However, recent research shows that LVLMs are susceptible to adversarial examples. Existing attackers either optimize the perturbations on the visual input or manipulate prompts to fool the LVLM models, requiring extensive design and engineering on these adversarial manipulations. While straightforward visual transformation can boast training generalization-ability, its potential risks to LVLMs in terms of safety and trustworthiness have been largely neglected. In this paper, we ask an intriguing question: can simple yet easy-to-implement adversarial visual transformations be utilized to attack the LVLM models? Motivated by this research gap and new attack setting, we propose the first comprehensive assessment of LVLMs’ adversarial robustness to visual transformations by testing LVLMs’ resilience to all possible transformation operations. Our empirical observations suggest that with the appropriate combination of the most harmful transformations, we can build transformation-based attacks more adversarial to the LVLM models. Moreover, adversarial learning of visual transformations is further introduced to adaptively apply the malicious impacts of all potentially harmful transformations to the raw images via gradient approximation for improving the attack effectiveness and imperceptibility. We hope that this study can provide deeper insights into the potential vulnerability of LVLMs to adversarial visual transformations.

title: An Entropy-Optimised Wavelet Synchrosqueezing and Gaussian Clustering Approach for Separation of Partial Discharge Signals
authors: Raj Kumar Mandal, Harimurugan Devarajan, Arvind Sharma
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper proposes an enhanced source separation technique for multiple overlapping partial discharge (PD) acoustic emission signals in a power transformer. The proposed method utilises third-order Rényi entropy-based optimisation to achieve highly concentrated time-frequency (TF) representations. Four statistical TF features, namely, temporal centroid, temporal spread, frequency spread, and temporal duration, are extracted and clustered using a Gaussian mixture model for probabilistic source assignment. Experimental results on mixed PD signals demonstrate improved TF concentration, accurate source separation, and robust reconstruction performance, confirming the effectiveness of the proposed approach for high-resolution PD signal measurement and analysis. The performance of the proposed method is evaluated with laboratory-acquired and onsite field-acquired PD signals. The proposed method is validated by comparison with other established source separation techniques.

title: Multi-Frequency Coded ARFI Imaging of Porcine Eye Using Polarization-Inversion Piezocomposite Transducer
authors: Gyu Li Ra, Hak Hyun Moon, Jong Seob Jeong
abstract: For accurate diagnosis and management of anterior and posterior segment lesions, visualizing the elasticity of the entire eye is essential for comprehensive biomechanical assessment. Acoustic Radiation Force Impulse (ARFI) imaging provides a promising ultrasound-based approach, however, conventional pulse-based ARFI imaging suffers from low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and limited penetration, often requiring high input voltage, which may compromise safety in energy-sensitive ocular applications. In this study, we propose a whole-eye ARFI imaging technique that integrates multi-frequency coded excitation with a polarization-inversion piezocomposite transducer (PIPT), enabling whole-eye elastography at relatively low voltage. The PIPT, fabricated by stacking bulk and composite piezoelectric layers of identical thickness, simultaneously generates broadband fundamental (f0) and second-harmonic (2f0) components. Using chirp-based multi-frequency signal excitation, the mean displacement SNR in the far field increases by up to 12.2 dB compared with conventional pulse excitation. Furthermore, frequency compounding effectively combined complementary information from f0 and 2f0, yielding a more detailed representation of both anterior and posterior intraocular structures. Experimental validation with ex vivo porcine eye confirmed that the proposed method overcomes the limitation of conventional pulsed ARFI, achieving stable elastography across the entire eye. These results demonstrate that the proposed approach provides a promising pathway toward higher-resolution whole-eye ARFI imaging at increased operating frequencies under low-voltage operation.

title: Coded Computing Meets Differential Privacy: Privacy-Preserving and Straggler-Resilient Distributed Machine Learning_supp1-3669529.pdf
authors: Jun Wu
abstract: Coded distributed machine learning mitigates straggler effects and provides privacy protection by introducing redundancy through coded computing. However, the system remains vulnerable to privacy breaches when the number of honest-but-curious nodes surpasses the designed threshold, or when outsider adversaries eavesdrop on sensitive data. To address these limitations, we propose a privacy-preserving and straggler-resilient distributed learning framework, namely, differential privacy-based Lagrange coded computing (DP-LCC). First, we design a three-layer protection strategy against privacy threats and stragglers by retaining labels at the master, obfuscating features via Lagrange interpolation, and injecting calibrated noise into local computations. Second, we theoretically prove that the aggregated gradient is an unbiased estimator with bounded variance, and derive convergence bounds under both Gaussian and Laplace mechanisms, revealing the trade-off between privacy budgets and model utility. Third, we provide a comprehensive analysis of the system's computational complexity, privacy composition, and heterogeneity to verify the framework's efficiency and adaptability in realistic distributed environments. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets validate the theoretical results, demonstrating the robustness of DP-LCC against varying system parameters and heterogeneous environments.
