title: Integrating Digital Pedagogy into Sports Curriculum Design:A Theoretical and Practical Approach
authors: Luo Lan, Fawei Huang, Shorouk Mohamed Farag Mohamed Aboudahr
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Integrating digital pedagogy into physical education curriculum design represents a significant frontier in educational innovation. Despite the growing prevalence of digital tools, a comprehensive theoretical framework guiding their systematic application in physical education instruction is lacking. This study aims to bridge this gap by constructing a comprehensive theoretical model that elucidates the mechanisms through which digital pedagogy enhances teaching effectiveness and student engagement. We conducted a systematic review of 40 peer-reviewed articles (2019-2024) from major databases, using thematic analysis and theoretical synthesis to identify core concepts. The proposed Input-Process-Output (IPO) model treats key digital tools (e.g., motion analysis software, VR/AR technologies) as input elements. These undergo mediated transformation through instructional processes-such as teaching strategies (e.g., flipped classrooms) and student engagement mechanisms (e.g.,gamification instruction)-ultimately facilitating optimized outputs: enhanced teaching effectiveness, increased student engagement, and maximized learning outcomes. The theoretical contribution of this model lies in integrating constructivism, connectivism, and the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) principles into a practical framework for physical education. Additionally, we propose implementation pathways including modular digital curriculum design, teacher competency development, and continuous iterative evaluation mechanisms. This model provides educators and researchers with a structured approach to leverage digital pedagogy in enhancing the quality and effectiveness of physical education in the digital era. To enhance originality, this model is compared with prior frameworks such as TPACK, which focuses on teacher knowledge integration but lacks emphasis on student engagement dynamics in physical contexts; our IPO approach extends this by incorporating connectivism for networked learning and empirical pathways for measurable outputs.

title: Exploring the Application of Improved GA in Multi-objective Path Planning for Engineering Robots
authors: Zejun Gong, Hui Zhao, Wei He, Yaoguang Zhao
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Path planning is the core task for the engineering robots. Given the NP-hard nature of path planning problems, GA has been widely used by taking advantage of its strong optimization ability. However, the conventional GA may face the early convenience problem and cause high computation cost. To address the drawbacks and to improve the scalability, flexibility, adaptability, and performance of the robot path planning systems, a hybrid algorithm for the Multi-objective Path Planning is proposed, which combines the Case-based reasoning and improved grey wolf optimization (GWO) for the GA. Firstly, a CBR-based initialization strategy is employed to enrich the diversity and quality of initial population. then, GWO-GA is proposed to improve the planning performance. The simulation experiments showed that the proposed algorithm could minimize the path length and produce a smooth path without collision. And compared with the traditional algorithm. The mean runtime and iteration times are significantly decreased, demonstrate the effectiveness and superiority of the proposed algorithm.

title: Epsilon-Greedy Applied to LODCO for Dynamic Computation Offloading in Multi-Server Mobile Edge Computing Systems
authors: Xuanye Zeng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Mobile Edge Computing (MEC) addresses computational limitations of mobile devices by offloading tasks to nearby edge servers. However, existing offloading algorithms struggle to balance exploration and exploitation in dynamic multiserver environments. This paper used epsilon-greedy LODCO, integrating controlled exploration with Lyapunov optimization to improve multi-server offloading decisions while maintaining theoretical queue stability guarantees. Unlike naive random exploration, this approach applies epsilon-greedy selection specifically at the server allocation phase, preserving optimal local/drop decisions. Comprehensive experiments under moderate overload conditions (~ 150% expected load) demonstrate that epsilon-greedy LODCO (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon=0.8$</tex>) achieves 2.3-2.5% execution delay reduction under severe resource pressure, with loaddependent optimal values: moderate load favors higher exploration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon \approx$</tex> 1.0), while severe overload benefits from structured exploration (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\varepsilon=0.7-0.8$</tex>). These findings provide practical guidance for MEC system design where intelligent exploration becomes increasingly valuable as resource contention intensifies.

title: Research on CAD Front-End and Back-End Interaction Methods and Systems Based on ServiceOriented Message Passing Mechanisms
authors: Zhining Wang, Zhen Xu, Liguo Yang, Kangcheng Luo, Xin Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: This paper proposes a CAD frontend-backend interaction method and system based on a service message-passing mechanism, aiming to address the highly coupled logic between frontend and backend in existing CAD platforms, which makes it difficult to adapt to multiple frontend types. By considering the coexistence of CAD platforms in both desktop and cloud environments, the frontend-backend separation is achieved. A unified framework supports the operation of multiple frontends through a single set of code, enabling interactive free modeling and parametric modeling in multi-frontend environments. The proposed solution has been successfully applied in practice, yielding favorable results.

title: Multi-Scale Feature Fusion UNetFormer: Improving the Accuracy of Remote Sensing Urban Scene Image Segmentation
authors: Shichun He, Xiaodong Yu, Desong Chen, Xi Kuang, Maolin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: In view of the semantic segmentation challenges caused by the diversity and complexity of targets in remote sensing images, this paper improves the UNetFormer network structure and proposes an enhanced remote sensing image semantic segmentation model. By analyzing the limitations of UNetFormer in the feature fusion stage, a parallel hybrid feature fusion module (PMFB) is designed, and the global-local enhancement and multiscale convolution fusion (GLEMSCF) mechanism is introduced to significantly improve the model's feature expression ability for multi-scale complex targets. Experiments on the ISPRS Vaihingen dataset show that the model achieves 83.6% mIoU, which is 0.9% higher than the original UNetFormer and is better than existing lightweight models. While maintaining efficient reasoning, the improved model significantly improves the segmentation accuracy of complex urban scenes (such as small-scale roads and building boundaries), verifying its effectiveness in practical applications.

title: Self-Supervised Pretraining for Laryngoscopic Image Classification: Leveraging MAE-Style Masked Image Modeling
authors: Jie Kong, Houwei Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Due to the limited availability of annotated data and the time-consuming and costly nature of manual labeling, the field of medical imaging faces significant challenges. This issue is particularly pronounced in the case of laryngoscopic images, where the scarcity of labeled data contrasts sharply with the abundance of unlabeled medical images. This study aims to address the problem of limited annotated data in laryngoscopic imaging and improve the accuracy of laryngeal disease classification. To this end, we propose a self-supervised learning (SSL) approach that employs a masked image modeling strategy to train a model capable of extracting meaningful representations from unlabeled data. Specifically, the proposed method adopts the MAE (Masked Autoencoders Are Scalable Vision Learners) framework for pretraining, enabling the model to learn robust features from diverse unlabeled laryngoscopic images. The pretrained model is then applied to a downstream task focused on laryngoscopic image classification, where labeled data are limited. To balance accuracy and computational efficiency, we employ ViT-Wee as the backbone network, containing only 13.4 million parameters. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach, achieving an accuracy of 94.67% in a three-class classification task.

title: A Noval CU-Shaped Antenna for Sub-6 GHz 5G NR n77 Applications
authors: Jeevan Jyoti, Nitin Kumar Saluja, Shivani Malhotra, Nitika Dhingra
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: The study presents the simulated and fabricated design of a microstrip antenna (MSA) for sub-6 GHz 5G applications, to meet the needs of next-generation wireless technology. The proposed antenna comprises a novel CUshaped radiating patch and a partial ground plane, both etched on an FR4-epoxy substrate with a permittivity of 4.4 and a thickness of $\mathbf{1. 6 ~ m m}$. The antenna achieves resonance at $\mathbf{3. 7 0} \mathbf{G H z}$, with a return loss ($\mathbf{S}_{\mathbf{1 1}}$ parameter) of $\mathbf{- 4 2 ~ d B}$ and a peak gain of $\mathbf{1. 2 5 ~ d B i}$, making it suitable for the n77 band. The result confirms the antenna’s reliability and cost-effectiveness for compact sub-6 GHz 5G new radio (NR) applications operating in the n77 band.

title: Case Study on College Teaching Innovation Empowered by Digital Intelligence Technology
authors: Fang Lu, Yuanpeng Zhu, Bo Li, Hongji Zhan, Xiaolin Ma
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Teaching innovation serves as both a requirement and a pathway for talent cultivation in the digital-intelligent era. Framed by the evaluation criteria of the National Teaching Innovation Competition for College teachers, this paper conducts a text analysis of 100 courses that won the highest school-level awards in the teaching innovation competition and were recommended to participate in the provincial-level competition. Combining with the evaluation experience of thousands of teaching innovation courses, it analyzes the main pathways for teaching innovation in colleges and the implementation pathways for teaching innovation empowered by digital-intelligent technologies. Taking the public basic calculus course for non-mathematics majors as an example, it introduces the practice and outcomes of teaching innovation empowered by digital-intelligent technologies.

title: Review on Anti-Interference Principles and Tests of Infrared Target Detectors
authors: Zifu Wang, Shuxin Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Infrared detectors are capable of characterizing the geometric feature information of targets. Boasting high measurement accuracy, they hold distinct advantages in object recognition; endowed with robust anti-interference performance, they are widely deployed in industrial control, environmental monitoring, biological control, and other fields, as well as in a broad range of visual detection, target recognition and tracking applications.However, engineering application environments are inherently complex, where a wide array of interferences arise constantly. This imposes stringent demands on the antiinterference capability of such detectors. Consequently, achieving effective anti-interference is of paramount importance in the operation of infrared imaging detectors. This paper focuses on investigating the anti-interference principles and current development status of infrared imaging detectors, aiming to provide valuable insights for future research directions in this field.

title: Overview of Fuel Cell Technologies for Combined Heat and Power Generation
authors: Ilya A. Galkin, Alexander Bubovich, Igors Ušakovs
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Recent tendencies in power supply includes increasingly widespread use of the renewable energy sources. The uneven energy generation profile of such sources, however, requires their combining with energy storages. Hydrogen fuel cells in conjunction with electrolyzers can perform this function having at the same time good compatibility with DC supply approach. On the other hand, fuel cells produce lots of losses and are suitable for parallel dedicated heat production. This paper evaluates the suitability of several fuel cell types for combined heat and electric power generation in the area of small households with DC power supply.

title: Lightweight QoS-Aware Workflow Scheduling for Efficient Task Execution in Cloud Environments
authors: Vishalika
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Scheduling in workflows plays a crucial role in optimizing task execution and resource utilization in cloud computing systems, particularly when Quality of Service (QoS) requirements, such as deadlines and task priorities, have to be fulfilled. This paper presents a novel, lightweight heuristic scheduling algorithm that incorporates QoS metrics into the heuristic scheduling process using the WorkflowSim simulation framework. The algorithm filters tasks based on the feasibility of deadlines, computes dynamic priority scores based on a blend of urgency and task weight, and employs an earliest-finish-time (EFT) policy for task to VM allocation. To test the proposed approach, simulation experiments were performed with synthetic Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) workflows in a heterogeneous VM (Virtual Machine) cloud environment. The performance of the algorithm was evaluated relative to traditional baseline approaches, including First-Come-FirstServe (FCFS), Round Robin (RR), and Min-Min, using the metrics makespan, deadline miss rate, workflow success rate, and VM utilization. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method in reducing makespan by up to 31.7% and improving workflow success rate to $\mathbf{7 2 \%}$ while achieving $\mathbf{7 9 \%}$ VM utilization, outperforming traditional schedulers. These findings suggest that lightweight heuristics can graciously meet the objectives of QoS without suffering the computational cost of metaheuristics. Enhancements in the future may include energy- and cost-conscious goals, re- scheduling, and predictive models to make it applicable across the various dynamics of a production field.

title: Forecasting Household Demand-Side Flexibility Using XGBoost for Smart Energy Management
authors: Rana Muhammad Arslan Qadar, Noman Shabbir, Lauri Kutt, Kamran Daniel, Kristjan Peterson, Argo Rosin
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The increasing penetration of renewable energy sources introduces variability and uncertainty into power systems, creating a growing need for demand-side flexibility (DSF). Forecasting the availability of demand-side flexibility, while maintaining consumer comfort, remains a central challenge in supporting aggregators with efficient energy management. This study presents an assessment of DSF using one year of household load consumption data, analysed through statistical methods and machine learning. Hourly appliancelevel consumption patterns were examined to identify shiftable loads and quantify the flexibility potential. An XGBoost (Extreme Gradient Boosting) regression model was developed to predict flexible demand, including Root Mean Square Error (RMSE), Determination Coefficient ($\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ Score) & total shiftable load. The demonstrated proposed model delivers high forecasting accuracy and outperforms several conventional prediction methods. A comparative analysis of pre-shifting and post-shifting load profiles demonstrated the potential to flatten evening peaks and redistribute demand to off-peak hours, improving system efficiency without reducing overall energy consumption. This work highlights the role of data-driven approaches in quantifying household flexibility and provides a foundation for demand-response strategies and smart energy management systems.

title: Cost-Effective and Non-Invasive Integration of Power-to-Heat Systems for Domestic Hot Water Using Renewable Electricity in Multi-Apartment Buildings
authors: Jevgenijs Kozadajevs, Antanas Sauhats
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: with the anticipated growth in renewable electricity generation, there is a pressing need to develop effective strategies for its utilization, in order to meet climate targets. A key challenge lies in transforming large numbers of multi-apartment buildings into active energy consumers capable of supporting renewable integration, while ensuring that technology adoption is non-invasive and financially viable. One possible solution is to convert excess renewable electricity into domestic hot water through power-to-heat systems. This research evaluates the economic performance of a hybrid thermal energy supply system that combines power to heat conversion with conventional district heating. The control strategy prioritizes power to heat operation during periods of low electricity prices, typically associated with renewable overgeneration, while reverting to district heating during higher-price intervals if it is more cost-effective. This approach minimizes operational costs and avoids the capital and maintenance expenses of fully replacing district heating infrastructure. A representative nine-storey multi-apartment building in Riga, Latvia, is used as a case study to model system performance. Results demonstrate that the hybrid approach enhances economic efficiency and supports renewable energy integration without compromising heating reliability.

title: Design and FPGA Implementation of a Sound Source Localization System Based on an Improved MUSIC Algorithm
authors: Yi Wan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In Industry 4.0, precise localization of fault sound sources is critical for predictive maintenance, but traditional methods struggle in low-SNR and high-reverberation environments. This paper proposes an improved Robust Weighted Spatial Covariance Multiple Signal Classification (RWSC-MUSIC) algorithm to enhance robustness in complex industrial settings. The algorithm integrates forward spatial smoothing to mitigate coherent interference and employs SNRbased frequency-domain covariance weighting to suppress noise. Furthermore, the complete system is designed on an FPGA, where computation-intensive modules like covariance calculation and eigenvalue decomposition are optimized using systolic arrays and the CORDIC algorithm. Simulation results based on the MIMII dataset validate that the improved algorithm achieves higher localization accuracy than conventional methods. The hardware implementation demonstrates its feasibility and efficiency for real-time embedded applications.

title: Power-Domain NOMA-Based Capacity Expansion Scheme for Low-Altitude UAV ADS-B System
authors: Tao Bin, Anwen Liao, Jinjing Bi, Chaoshuo Hui, Xinke Chen, Shidong Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the development of the low-altitude economy, the number of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) has grown exponentially.Existing broadcast Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast (ADS-B) systems employ the Aloha protocol based on random access, which is highly prone to signal collisions in high-density scenarios. This leads to system capacity saturation and surveillance blind spots.To address this issue, this paper proposes an ADS-B enhancement scheme based on power-domain non-orthogonal multiple access (PD-NOMA) for low-altitude airspace management. Leveraging channel gain differences between UAVs and ground stations, the scheme enables multiple users to transmit signals within the same time-frequency resource block. Receiving-end serial interference cancellation (SIC) technology separates the superimposed signals.Simulation results demonstrate that compared to traditional ADS-B systems, the proposed scheme significantly reduces packet collision rates while maintaining low latency characteristics, increasing system access capacity by approximately 40 %. This effectively supports regulatory demands in densely populated low-altitude airspace.

title: Impact of Renewable Energy Integration on the Reliability of Protection System Operation
authors: Sergejs Kovalenko, Inga Zicmane, Tatjana Lomane, Aleksandrs Sahnovskis
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The increasing integration of renewable energy sources (RES), particularly inverter-based resources (IBRs), is transforming the behavior of power systems and exposing the limitations of conventional protection schemes. Traditional relays, such as overcurrent, distance, and directional relays, often fail to operate correctly due to low fault current levels, distorted waveforms, and bidirectional power flows typical of high-RES networks. This paper reviews the major challenges of protection under high RES penetration, presents real-world case studies of misoperation, and analyzes the impact of IBRs on relay performance. The study also surveys recent advances in artificial intelligence (AI)-based approaches for fault classification, adaptive autoreclosing, and protection setting coordination. A hybrid protection framework is proposed, combining deterministic logic with AI models and systemawareness mechanisms. Key implementation challenges, including generalization, explainability, data quality, cybersecurity, and regulatory gaps, are discussed. The paper concludes by outlining research directions to enable reliable and adaptive AI-assisted protection for next-generation grids.

title: Experimental Verification of Quantum Key Distribution with on-Chip Encoding—Decoding and Detector Defects
authors: Ying Zhang, Bao Feng, Xin Sun, Yuanqiang Wang, Chao Lv
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Quantum key distribution (QKD) enables information-theoretically secure key establishment, yet its practical performance strongly depends on the behavior of singlephoton detectors. In high-speed systems, afterpulsing effect and dead time can distort detection statistics and cause substantial overestimation of the secure key rate if not properly modeled. To address this issue, we construct a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$625-\text{MHz}$</tex> BB84-QKD system based on a silica planar-lightwave-circuit asymmetric MachZehnder interferometer chips, incorporate a dependency-modelbased description of detector afterpulse and dead time into the theoretical analysis. This model provides corrected estimations of gain and error rates that more accurately reflect the real detection process. Experimental results show that the system achieves secure key rate of 30.47 kbps at 18 dB channel loss. The strong agreement between the corrected model and measured data confirms that it effectively avoids key-rate overestimation and reliably predicts system performance under practical conditions. The demonstrated on-chip encoding-decoding architecture and refined detector modeling together highlight a promising route toward compact, stable, and high-speed QKD systems, offering valuable guidance for future metropolitan and large-scale quantum communication networks.

title: Vibration Acceleration Testing System Based on the Dual-Core Architecture of ARM and FPGA
authors: Long Zhang, Shiqian Song, Yongxiang Shi, Xuejie Jiao, Zequn Ba
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: A vibration acceleration testing system based on the dual-core architecture of ARM and FPGA was designed in this paper. And the system adopted a cooperative control method between the dual-core architecture. Firstly, FPGA completed the ADC sampling control, SDRAM data buffering, and FIFO readwrite control according to the set sampling parameters. Secondly, by configuring the FSMC bus, the high-speed transmission of sampling data and control instruction be-tween dual-core architecture was achieved. In addition, Ethernet real-time communication and Micro SD card offline storage were used as two different data storage methods, which improved the system flexibility and data security. Finally, various performance parameters of the system were tested. And the results showed that the system could achieve simultaneous sampling with 8 channels, 18 -bit resolution and 200 kHz sampling rate. Moreover, the power consumption was 1.2 W approximately, the channel isolation was greater than 85 dB, the dynamic range was greater than 90 dB, and the data transfer rate of Ethernet interface was greater than <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10.0 \text{MB} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>. The vibration table experiment results showed that the testing system could obtain the vibration frequency accurately, with the maximum relative error of vibration amplitude being 0.5%.

title: Construction of an Artificial Intelligence Experimental Teaching Platform for Computer Science Based on STEM Education
authors: Xing Liu, Fangfang Xu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the rapid development of digital intelligence technology represented by artificial intelligence, artificial intelligence has not only become an important driving force for knowledge updates, but also an important tool for promoting the development of higher education. In order to create a computer professional artificial intelligence laboratory that is functionally intensive, resource sharing, fully open, and operates efficiently, this article analyzes the demand for computer professional teaching reform in the AI era. Based on this, STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics) education is combined with artificial intelligence education to design an experimental teaching platform that integrates teaching, research, experimentation, and project creation. This platform achieves an organic connection from virtual verification to real-life scenarios and provides teachers and students with a full process basic service for AI teaching.

title: Assessment of Power-to-X Technologies for the Estonian Residential Sector
authors: Ievgen Verbytskyi, Anna Kyselova, Jarek Kurnitski, Dmitri Vinnikov, Ilya Galkin
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The increase in the share of renewable energy sources (RES) in energy systems forces additional measures to balance power grids, a number of these measures are usually classified as Power-to-X technologies (PtX). The problem of balancing power from renewable energy sources (RES) is crucial for the residential sector to achieve nearly zero energy consumption of households throughout the year. To fully meet this goal, it is necessary to provide energy storage systems capable of balancing not only daily but also seasonal energy imbalances, which significantly increase the investment costs when conventional batteries are considered. In this paper, the energy consumption of electric and thermal energy for the Estonian region is analyzed. A hydrogen energy storage system, as part of Power-to-X (PtX) technology for residential households, is evaluated. Its efficiency, weight, and cost are assessed and compared with those of battery-based energy storage.

title: Exploration and Implementation of a Competition-Project Integrated Teaching Model for Progressive Engineering Competency Cultivation in Additive Manufacturing
authors: Yanmei Li, Yebin Yang, Weiliang Chen, Zhibiao Yang, Dingren Hu, Wenwu Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: To address the prevalent challenges of the disconnect between theory and practice, and insufficient innovation capability in undergraduate engineering training under the new engineering context, this study innovatively proposes a dual-technology integrated and three-stage progressive teaching model. By organically integrating the lowcost rapid prototyping advantages of Fused Deposition Modeling (FDM) technology and the high-precision manufacturing characteristics of Selective Laser Melting (SLM) technology, a “competition-project dual-driven synergy” system for the cultivation of three-dimensional (3D) engineering capabilities is constructed. The instructional implementation follows a progressive path: foundational capability building → projectdriven advancement → dual-driven innovation, enabling a stepwise enhancement of students' engineering competencies. Finally, the dual-wheel drive of academic competitions and industry-university-research projects is used to cultivate systematic innovation capabilities. After 36 months of pedagogical practice, the model has yielded significant outcomes: (1) Breakthrough progress has been achieved in talent cultivation. Undergraduate students have obtained 6 authorized invention patents, published 10 core journal papers, won 32 provinciallevel or above scientific and technological competition awards, incubated 7 national/provincial innovation projects, and completed 22 industry-academia-research collaboration projects -an 80 % increase compared to the pre-implementation period; (2) The research capacity of the teaching team has been notably enhanced; (3) Establishment of a replicable “technology integration-project drive-competition feedback” 3D engineering practice capability cultivation paradigm. The research demonstrates that this model effectively achieves multidimensional improvement of students' engineering practice capabilities, providing an implementable reference path for talent cultivation in application-oriented undergraduate institutions.

title: Operator Learning for Delay Differential and PDE Systems through Structured Temporal Memory Kernels
authors: Dongni Jia, Shuai Li, Haibo Shi, Xiaofeng Zhou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Time delays and history-dependent effects are ubiquitous in industrial and scientific dynamical systems, yet most deep learning and neural operator models remain fundamentally Markovian and unable to represent delayed dynamics explicitly. This work proposes a neural operator framework for delay differential and PDE systems by incorporating structured Volterra temporal memory kernels into the time-stepping operator. The model separates instantaneous dynamics from delayed responses, while sparsity and smoothness priors enforce physically meaningful memory structures. Experiments on delayed FitzHugh-Nagumo equations and a recycle-loop CSTR demonstrate that the proposed method achieves superior shortand long-horizon prediction accuracy and accurately identifies dominant delay times. The results show that memory-structured neural operators provide an interpretable and effective approach for modeling non-Markovian systems with complex delay mechanisms.

title: Research on the Deep Integration Mechanism of Interlinked Learning Environment and Social Learning Platform Based on the Perspective of Technology-Society Synergy and Ecological Evolution
authors: Liya Yu, Hanina Halimatusaadiah Hamsan, Zhengmao Ye, Longyue Cheng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The rapid evolution of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies has brought about transformative changes across various industries, with the financial sector standing as a prominent beneficiary. This study focuses on exploring the integration and application of AI in the financial industry, aiming to uncover the underlying mechanisms, current status, and future trends of this technological fusion. This paper delves into the deep integration mechanism of interlinked learning environments and social learning platforms, along with their ecological evolution path, from the perspective of socio-technical-evolution. By analyzing the transformative role of big data, artificial intelligence, and other technologies in reshaping the learning environment, and incorporating the co-evolutionary mechanism of natural technology and social technology from socio-technical theory, a dual-wheel driving integration model of “technological empowerment-social fitness” is constructed. It is revealed that deep integration necessitates overcoming challenges such as data silos and scene fragmentation. Through the application of key technologies like multi-modal perception, cross-domain learning scene modeling, and human-machine collaborative community construction, combined with the synergistic innovation of socio-technical elements such as policy and regulations, industry models, and social culture, personalized learning support and sustainable ecological development can be achieved. This research proposes a triad ecological evolution path of “environment-platform-community,” offering theoretical references and practical guidance for the construction of smart education ecosystems.

title: Research on Environmental Adaptive Intelligent Control Technology of Large-Segment Bridge-Building Machine Suitable for Complex Terrain of Plateau
authors: Zhiwen Ding, Hao Qiu, Youliang Yu, Zhibin Yuan, Huali Yu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: The bridge construction in plateau complex terrain faces severe challenges such as strong wind, sudden temperature change and frozen soil settlement. The traditional bridge builder control technology is difficult to ensure the safety and accuracy of construction due to the limitations of passive response, single factor consideration and manual intervention. Therefore, a set of environment adaptive intelligent control system is constructed in this study, aiming at breaking through the limitation of plateau environment on the construction of large-segment bridge-building machine. The system achieves comprehensive monitoring of complex environments through multimodal environmental perception: three-dimensional ultrasonic anemometers are deployed within a range of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 20 \mathrm{m}$</tex> from the bridge deck height, distributed fiber optic temperature sensors are arranged longitudinally along the main beam, micro inertial measurement units (MIMUs) are configured on the main support legs, and ground radar interferometers are used to monitor frozen soil settlement. At the same time, the adaptive Kalman filter (AKF) algorithm is used to fuse multi-source data to improve perception reliability. Based on this, a multi field coupled dynamic model of wind temperature geology is established, and a hybrid intelligent controller architecture Reinforcement Learning (RL)-Terminal Sliding Mode Control (RL-TSMC) is designed. The upper layer uses the Soft Actor Critic Algorithm (SAC) to optimize the tension setting value, and the lower layer achieves disturbance resistant control through Robust Sliding Mode Control (TSMC). At the same time, a three-level warning and human-machine collaborative safety mechanism (integrated AR assisted decision-making platform) are introduced. The experimental results show that the performance of the system is significantly improved compared with that of the traditional PID control under simulated plateau conditions: the lateral displacement amplitude is reduced by about 63% under the gust of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$18 \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$</tex>, the leg elevation deviation is reduced to -1.4 mm under the coupling condition of sudden temperature rise and foundation settlement, the hydraulic overshoot is reduced to 6.3%, the compensation delay is shortened to 0.8 s, and the average value of segment docking dislocation is reduced to 1.2 mm and the overrun rate is 0%. The research results have effectively improved the safety and efficiency of the construction of large-segment bridge-building machine under the complex terrain of plateau, and provided technical support for similar projects.

title: From Sampling to Generation: Hybrid Distillation for Data-Free Semantic Segmentation
authors: Hongxuan Sun, Bei Dou, Tao Wu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Data-Free Knowledge Distillation (DFKD) for semantic segmentation represents an emerging task aimed at transferring knowledge from a pre-trained teacher network to a student model without access to the original training data. While conventional DFKD methods leverage generators to produce proxy data, they are often plagued by distributional collapse and poor visual fidelity. Alternative strategies involving open-world sampling show promise but are stymied by the logistical overhead of processing vast data repositories. The necessity for large-scale queries to ensure sample quality leads to unsustainable storage and computational demands, necessitating a more efficient integration of synthetic and real-world data. To address these limitations, we propose a Generation-Sampling Mixed (GSMix) method that strategically integrates synthetic data with carefully sampled real-world data, thereby enhancing both representational relevance and diversity, and ultimately advancing the performance of DFKD for semantic segmentation. Specifically, during the data acquisition phase, our framework employs the pre-trained teacher network as a filter to select informative samples from an open-world dataset, while concurrently caching the corresponding dense semantic predictions. These predictions are subsequently refined into structured segmentation masks and mapped to class-conditioned textual prompts. To augment the training distribution, a high-fidelity latent diffusion model is utilized to synthesize auxiliary samples that mirror the empirical distribution of the retrieved real-world data. In the distillation stage, we introduce an authenticity-aware weighting scheme to modulate the loss contribution of each sample based on its perceptual fidelity. This strategy ensures that the student model prioritizes high-quality representations, thereby achieving a more robust and generalized knowledge acquisition.

title: High-Power Excitation System for Electromagnetic Acoustic Transducers Based on Direct Digital Synthesis
authors: Dezhi Zheng, Zhiyi Yang, Chun Hu, Zonglin Li, Peng Peng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Electromagnetic Acoustic Transducer (EMAT) technology enables non-contact and high-temperature-tolerant inspection of oil pipelines, but conventional excitation circuits often suffer from waveform distortion and insufficient output power, which limit the engineering application effect of this technology. To address these issues, this paper presents a high-power EMAT excitation system based on Direct Digital Synthesis (DDS) with a field-programmable gate array (FPGA) as the core controller. The system generates programmable waveforms and drives a two-stage power amplifier to achieve high-voltage, wide-band excitation. Circuit simulation and experimental results demonstrate an output of over 1200 Vpp, an output power of over 5000 W, and an excitation frequency of 4 MHz. Thickness measurements on steel samples <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(4.9-13.7 ~\text{mm})$</tex> achieve <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$<1 ~\text{mm}$</tex> error, and volumetric defects <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(6 ~\text{mm}$</tex> depth) are accurately identified. The system stably outputs clear echo waveforms, meeting engineering requirements for pipeline inspection. This research provides strong support and technical reference for the engineering application of high-performance EMAT pipeline detection systems.

title: Enhancing Node Access of Semantic Communication in Energy Harvesting Networks
authors: Yuyi Sun, Shunyu Lu, Tingting Xiao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Industrial monitoring increasingly relies on dense IoT deployments; however, many outdoor nodes remain energy-constrained, limiting their ability to sustain high-frequency and long-duration data transmission. Semantic communication (SemCom), which conveys semantics instead of raw data, can significantly reduce the transmission load and energy overhead of energy-harvesting (EH) networks. Nevertheless, SemCom still experience severe access contention in multi-node environments, resulting in throughput degradation and additional energy cost. To overcome these limits, we propose a semantic-aware access framework that updates the access probability of the nodes based on their semantic importance and energy level. Then, we formulate a semantic-energy efficiency to optimize the transmitted semantics with less energy. The experimental results show that the proposed framework maintains <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 10^{5}$</tex> (suts/s/J) when the scale of nodes is large. By tuning the 0dBm transmit power of the nodes in EH networks, we can maintain the semantic-energy efficiency of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$2 \times 10^{8}$</tex> (suts/s/J).

title: Extracting Training Dialogue Data from Large Language Model based Task Bots
authors: Shuo Zhang, Junzhou Zhao, Junji Hou, Pinghui Wang, Chenxu Wang, Jing Tao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have been widely adopted to enhance Task-Oriented Dialogue Systems (TODS) by modeling complex language patterns and delivering contextually appropriate responses. However, this integration introduces significant privacy risks, as LLMs, functioning as soft knowledge bases that compress extensive training data into rich knowledge representations, can inadvertently memorize training dialogue data containing not only identifiable information such as phone numbers but also entire dialogue-level events like complete travel schedules. Despite the critical nature of this privacy concern, how LLM memorization is inherited in developing task bots remains unexplored. In this work, we address this gap through a systematic quantitative study that involves evaluating existing training data extraction attacks, analyzing key characteristics of task-oriented dialogue modeling that render existing methods ineffective, and proposing novel attack techniques tailored for LLM-based TODS that enhance both response sampling and membership inference. Experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness of our proposed data extraction attack. Our method can extract thousands of training labels of dialogue states with best-case precision exceeding 70%. Furthermore, we provide an in-depth analysis of training data memorization in LLM-based TODS by identifying and quantifying key influencing factors and discussing targeted mitigation strategies.

title: Standardized Digital Twin Framework for Modeling, Monitoring and Detection of Cyber-Physical Threats in Photovoltaic Plant Sensors
authors: Pablo José Hueros-Barrios, Francisco Javier Rodríguez-Sánchez, Pedro Martín-Sánchez, Miguel Tradacete-Ágreda, Carlos Santos-Pérez, José Antonio Jiménez-Calvo, Frede Blaabjerg
publish: IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement
abstract: This paper presents a cost-effective and real-time Digital Twin (DT) framework for cyber-physical anomaly detection, monitoring, and modeling in photovoltaic (PV) plants. The proposed approach employs open-source tools, such as OpenModelica, and integrates the Functional Mockup Interface (FMI) standard to create portable, scalable, and interoperable Functional Mock-up Units (FMUs). These FMUs serve as dynamic virtual representations of the physical system, enabling continuous estimation, tracking, and recognition of abnormal behavior in real time. The framework is validated using a Hardware-in-the-Loop (HIL) platform that emulates realistic PV plant operations, providing a controlled environment for testing and verification. Additionally, the DT runs on a low-cost Single Board Computer (SBC), comparing real-time sensor measurements with model-based estimations to accurately detect anomalies without requiring extensive computational resources. For effective data analysis and visualization, the system uses open-source time-series database solutions, facilitating early detection and traceability of cyber-physical threats. By integrating measurement and monitoring functions with behavioral modeling and anomaly detection into a unified, standardized framework, this work supports the detection and analysis of cyber-physical security threats in PV plant sensors, contributing to more reliable, efficient, and accessible system monitoring.

title: Design of Filtering Nolen Matrix With Uniform and Nonuniform Power Distribution
authors: Qiang Shao, Yi Lu, Rui-Sen Chen, Guan-Long Huang, Fu-Chang Chen, Yi Wang, Mustafa K. Taher Al-Nuaimi, Chow-Yen-Desmond Sim, Yiannis Vardaxoglou
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: This article introduces an innovative design method for a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times 3$</tex-math> </inline-formula> Nolen matrix with inherent filter function. The matrix, composed of a broadband 90° coupler and two 180° filtering couplers as well as the compensation resonators, provides output phase differences of −90°, 150°, and 30° and exhibits fourth-order bandpass filtering characteristics. Based on the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times 3$</tex-math> </inline-formula> filtering Nolen matrix, a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times 5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> matrix with nonuniform power distribution is realized by adding two power dividers at the output ports with a division ratio of <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$K$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. The design procedures and synthesis methods for both filtering matrices are described in detail. To validate the approach, the prototypes of the <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times 3$</tex-math> </inline-formula> and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$3\times 5$</tex-math> </inline-formula> matrices, designed for a center frequency of 2.4 GHz with bandpass characteristics, were fabricated and experimentally tested. Measurement results show good agreement with simulations, demonstrating the feasibility and effectiveness of the proposed design.

title:
X
                    <sup>2</sup>
                    O: Cross Parallel Optimization of the CROSS Post-Quantum Scheme on GPU
authors: Yijing Ning, Jiankuo Dong, Yajie Zhao, Jingqiang Lin, Tian Zhou, Jiachen Wang, Fu Xiao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: The CROSS Digital Signature Algorithm (DSA), currently a second-round candidate in the NIST standardization process for additional post-quantum digital signatures, offers compact public keys and strong security guarantees rooted in the code-based Restricted Syndrome Decoding Problem (R-SDP) and its variant R-SDP(G). Despite its strong theoretical foundation and practical significance, existing CPU-based implementations of CROSS exhibit evident performance limitations, while its potential for high-throughput acceleration on GPU architectures remains insufficiently investigated. In this work, we present X<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>O, the first systematically optimized GPU implementation framework for CROSS on NVIDIA GPUs. X<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup>O introduces a novel cross-parallel architecture that integrates both horizontal and vertical parallelism to fully exploit the massive concurrency of modern GPU platforms. The framework incorporates a series of targeted optimizations, including fine-grained thread scheduling, optimized memory access patterns, hash function tuning, and GPU-efficient tree construction. Experimental results on a NVIDIA RTX 4090 demonstrate the efficiency of our design, achieving up to 1,082,904 signature generations and 1,589,595 verifications per second at NIST security level 1. Compared to the official AVX-optimized CPU implementation, our GPU-based approach achieves up to 120× speedup, establishing a new performance benchmark for CROSS and demonstrating the viability of high-throughput, post-quantum digital signatures on parallel computing platforms.

title: Toward Intelligent and Secure Satellite Communications: Agentic AI for Covert Semantics
authors: Zhaoxin Feng, Ruichen Zhang, Xiaoqi Qin, Chengwen Xing, Nan Zhao, Dusit Niyato, Naofal Al-Dhahir
abstract: With the rapid growth of satellite service, efficient and secure transmission in satellite networks has become a central challenge. To address it, semantic communication can improve the efficiency by conveying only the essential meaning of information, while covert communication can ensure the security by concealing the transmission behavior. However, existing solutions often treat these two objectives in isolation and lack intelligence for the joint optimization in a dynamic environment. In this article, we first provide a comprehensive overview of the integration of semantic communication and covert transmission in satellite networks, highlighting its representative applications and fundamental challenges. Then, we introduce the concept of large language model (LLM) enabled agentic AI with its capabilities and enabling technologies. Specifically, we propose an LLM-enabled agentic AI framework for covert semantic satellite communication to unify the semantic understanding, reinforcement learning (RL) based covert transmission and knowledge-driven reconstruction for intelligent and secure information delivery. We present a case study to demonstrate the effectiveness of this approach. Finally, open issues and future directions are discussed.

title: RESOLVE: Reasoning in Hyperdimensional Spaces With Relational and Object Level Features Using Vector Symbolic Processing
authors: Mohamed Mejri, Chandramouli Amarnath, Abhijit Chatterjee
abstract: Modern transformer-based encoder-decoder architectures struggle with reasoning tasks due to their inability to effectively extract relational information between input objects (data/tokens) without interference from object-level information. To address this, we propose RESOLVE, a neuro-vector symbolic architecture that first combines object-level features with relational information in high-dimensional spaces, using fast, efficient operations such as bundling (summation) and binding (Hadamard product). This allows both object-level features and relational representations to coexist within the same symbolic structure with minimal interference with one another while maintaining a high level of abstraction. RESOLVE is driven by a novel attention mechanism that operates in a bipolar high-dimensional space, allowing fast attention score computation compared to the state-of-the-art. By leveraging this design, the model achieves both low compute latency and memory efficiency. RESOLVE also offers better generalizability while achieving higher accuracy in partially relational reasoning tasks compared to state-of-the-art methods.
