title: Learning to Localize in Indoor Environments: Adaptive Multimodal Fusion and Graph-Structured Modeling for Camera Pose Estimation
authors: Xinrong Huang, Zhiliang Zhu, Xiaogang Zhang, Wei Cao, Shiwen Yu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate camera pose estimation in complex indoor environments is essential for visual localization, robotic navigation, and AR/VR applications, yet purely RGB-based methods remain vulnerable to illumination changes, texture-poor regions, and motion blur. This paper presents a learning-based RGB-D camera pose estimation framework that combines adaptive multimodal fusion with graph-structured modeling. A lightweight channel-spatial attention module is used to adaptively fuse RGB and depth features, while a graph neural network (GNN) captures the spatial relationships among fused features for pose regression. The framework is implemented and evaluated on three representative indoor sequences from the TUM RGB-D dataset under a unified training setting. Experiments show that the proposed method achieves stable, centimeter- to decimeter-level trajectory reconstruction on both long indoor paths and desk-scale motions, and that the fused RGB-depth representation improves robustness in challenging views. Ablation studies further indicate that attention-based fusion and graph modeling generally enhance trajectory smoothness and robustness, while a simpler MLP-based regressor without explicit graph structure remains a strong baseline. Overall, the results suggest that multimodal fusion and graph-based feature aggregation are promising directions for improving the reliability of learning-based camera pose estimation in indoor environments.

title: Why Students Adopt Educational Technology: The Role of Usability and Psychological Needs Among Francophone African International Students
authors: Hsin-l Lee, Jie Li
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: As higher education becomes increasingly digital, African French-speaking students studying in China encounter persistent obstacles to adopting digital technologies, arising from linguistic, cultural, and technical barriers. To investigate the factors shaping their engagement with these tools, this study draws on the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM) and Self-Determination Theory (SDT). A survey of 329 students from 36 African countries was conducted, and the data were analyzed to explore connections among key variables. Results indicate that perceived usefulness plays a central role in shaping students' intention to adopt digital tools and in strengthening their sense of competence. Perceived ease of use, by contrast, appeared to support feelings of autonomy but showed little direct influence on adoption intentions. Students' intention to engage with technology was also tied to a stronger sense of connection and belonging within the learning environment. These findings suggest that technology adoption is shaped not only by usability but also by the extent to which digital platforms meet learners' psychological needs. This study advances cross-cultural research by offering guidance for designing inclusive, culturally responsive strategies and policies that promote educational technology adoption among international students.

title: Routing and Resource Allocation Algorithm for Minimizing Spectrum Fragmentation in “IP+Optical” Networks
authors: Bo Xu, Yaping Li, Huan Li, Yiyang Chen, Jingjing Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This paper proposes a routing and resource allocation algorithm optimized for spectrum fragmentation in the “IP+Optical” architecture of power communication networks. Focusing on the dynamic scheduling requirements of power services, the algorithm precisely suppresses spectrum fragmentation by synergistically optimizing routing selection and resource allocation logic. Simulation results demonstrate its superior performance in service blocking probability.

title: ShadowScan: LLM-Based Device Fingerprinting in IoT Networks
authors: Duwarahavidyan Jeganathan, Tharuka Harshajith Bandara, Harsha S. Gardiyawasam Pussewalage, Thilina Deshan Dissanayake, Dinithi Sachinthana Fernando, Kushan Sudheera Kalupahana Liyanage, Thilini Dahanayaka
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: The rapid expansion of Internet of Things (IoT) devices has increased the importance of accurate device identification from network traffic to ensure system security and user privacy. While conventional machine learning (ML) models are effective for fingerprinting known devices they fail at generalizing to previously unseen ones unavailable in their training datasets. This paper presents a novel application of Large Language Models (LLMs) for IoT device identification from encrypted network traffic features, focusing on zero-shot recognition of unseen devices. We introduce a LLM approach combined with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture that enables accurate identification without prior exposure to target devices, a common limitation in traditional ML approaches. Using a combination of five publicly available datasets, a large-scale LLM LLaMA and two medium-sized transformer models (DistilBERT, RoBERTa) are evaluated. Evaluations were done for base LLMs as well as fine-tuned LLMs against conventional ML baselines. Experimental results show that fine-tuned LLMs achieve approximately 10% higher accuracy than traditional ML models, while the proposed zero-shot LLM + RAG method attains 81% accuracy in detecting previously unseen IoT devices while achieving a 94% accuracy in identifying seen ones in mixed IoT and non-IoT environments. The findings establish LLM and RAG-based architectures as promising tools for adaptive and scalable IoT security in dynamic network settings.

title: Reconfigure the Classroom: An Activity-Theoretic Analysis of an AI Conversational Agent in Vocational Technical Communication
authors: Weiqi Liu, Xuxin Huang, Yilin Zou, Kejia Zhu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The rapid expansion of the global digital economy has exposed a critical skills gap in vocational education, particularly in technical communication. This study reports on the integration of “TradeTalker,” an AI conversational agent, into a vocational English course. Guided by Activity Theory, we conceptualized AI as a transformative mediating artifact that fundamentally reconfigured the classroom's learning system. A 12-week, mixed-methods investigation using pre-test/post-test design and systematic observation found a statistically significant improvement in students' technical communication performance. Qualitative analysis revealed a profound shift from passive reception to active, collaborative problem-solving and the emergence of peer coaching. The study introduces and validates a generalizable “One System, Four Stages, Five Criteria” pedagogical framework. This research offers a theory-informed pathway for leveraging generative AI to cultivate the dynamic, future-ready communication skills essential for global economic competitiveness.

title: Synchronous Condenser Inertial Response-Based Load Shedding: Design of Automation Terminal and Laboratory Testing
authors: Antans Sauhats, Aleksandrs Dolgicers, Andrejs Utans, Ivars Zalitis, Diana Zalostiba, Edgars Eisons
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This work addresses the problem of excessive declines in system voltage frequency caused by significant power imbalances. It builds upon a previous proposal, where a novel rapid load shedding (LS) method is suggested, by developing a dedicated automation terminal prototype and its control algorithms required for implementation of the new method. Unlike traditional under-frequency LS (UFLS) approaches relying on frequency measurements, the rapid LS (RLS) method is based on real-time measurements of the active power injected into a power system (PS) by synchronous condensers (SCs) following a contingency. In contrast to conventional UFLS methods, the RLS approach enables execution of LS without waiting for the system frequency to fall below a predefined threshold. This significantly improves frequency profile under the same conditions of power imbalance and LS. The RLS method is foreseen as particularly effective in PSs with a high penetration of non-inertial renewable energy sources (RESs) and a significant contribution by SCs to an inertia of a whole PS. Results of laboratory testing are presented demonstrating correct operation and blocking of the developed automation terminal in different situations.

title: Multi-QoS Constrained Hierarchical Routing Algorithm for LEO Satellite Communication Networks
authors: Xiaohong Zhu, Xinrong Zheng, Wei Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Routing in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite constellations must react to topology changes occurring every few seconds while respecting multi-service QoS commitments. We present HOM-QoS, a hierarchical optimizer that (i) performs rectangular hop-domain pruning with utilization-aware filtering to retain only 12% of links, (ii) recalibrates delay/jitter/loss weights through Dynamic Intra-layer Weight Adjustment (DIWA), and (iii) bounds enumeration through Gini-guided adaptive K-shortest search. Implemented on a 66-satellite constellation, HOM-QoS cuts voice delay by $\mathbf{1 1} \boldsymbol{-} \mathbf{1 8} \boldsymbol{\%}$, lowers data packet loss by $\mathbf{1 5}-\mathbf{2 8 \%}$, and boosts throughput by $\mathbf{6 - 1 0 \%}$ over baselines while keeping per-request computation below 12 ms.

title: Research on CUDA Acceleration for two-dimensional DGTD on Hybrid Meshes
authors: Heping Liu, Jiarong Dong, Linqian Li, Bing Wei
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: The two-dimensional Discontinuous Galerkin Time Domain (DGTD) method is an efficient full-wave numerical technique for analyzing complex electromagnetic structures. Its unstructured hybrid meshes, such as triangular and quadrilateral elements, can flexibly conform to complex geometric boundaries. However, conventional serial computing modes encounter significant efficiency bottlenecks when dealing with massive mesh elements and prolonged simulation time steps. The primary constraint lies in the excessive time consumption required for traversing vast elements to perform local matrix operations and data interaction within each time step. To overcome this limitation, this paper proposes and implements an acceleration scheme based on the NVIDIA Compute Unified Device Architecture (CUDA). The core strategy involves the parallel reconstruction of two computationally intensive processes within the two-dimensional hybrid mesh DGTD algorithm: element-wise local matrix solving and element loop updating during time-step iterations. These processes are efficiently mapped onto the many-core computing units of the Graphics Processing Unit (GPU). By employing a meticulously designed thread block allocation strategy and optimizing memory access, the parallel computing capability of the GPU is effectively utilized, significantly enhancing the overall execution efficiency of the algorithm. Numerical results from typical two-dimensional electromagnetic scattering examples demonstrate that, compared with traditional Central Processing Unit (CPU) serial computing, the proposed CUDA parallel acceleration method achieves significant speedup for hybrid mesh two-dimensional DGTD simulations while maintaining computational accuracy and stability.

title: Outdoor digital screens’ luminance determination using illuminance measurements
authors: Martin Parker, Aiko Liisa Olek, Toivo Varjas, Argo Rosin
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The use of digital outdoor screens for advertising and information purposes is currently in a noticeable growth trend. The increase is fueled by ease of use and is encouraged by the falling price of outdoor digital screens’ technology. Moreover, the technology used is becoming more energy efficient. However, the proliferation of outdoor digital screens has increased the perceived light pollution. Therefore, the measurements of outdoor digital screens are needed to confirm that these will adhere applied constraints, rules and provide the desired functionality: informing, advertising. This paper presents a proof of concept for determining the luminance of outdoor displays using illuminance measurement and reflector surface. The paper also provides an analysis based on scientific articles and brings out the importance of the methodology used.

title: Semi-Supervised Stochastic Configuration Networks with Explainable Nodes
authors: Pengxin Tian, Dianhui Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Semi-supervised learning provides an effective paradigm for industrial regression tasks where labeled data are scarce but unlabeled data are abundant. This paper proposes a semi-supervised regression framework based on stochastic configuration networks with explainable nodes, termed ESCN, in which multiple base learners operate in parallel to generate reliable pseudo-labels through a consensus mechanism. By iteratively selecting pseudo-labeled samples with high inter-model agreement and incrementally updating the learner models, the proposed framework effectively exploits unlabeled data while mitigating error accumulation during self-training. To enhance robustness under imperfect supervision, ESCN incorporates an explainable evaluation metric into the training process to guide hidden-node selection. This mechanism improves training stability and reduces sensitivity to noisy or inaccurate targets, which is particularly important in semi-supervised learning scenarios. The effectiveness of the proposed approach is validated on a realworld substation load forecasting task under limited labeled data conditions. Comparative experiments are carried out to demonstrate the effectiveness and merits of the proposed modelling techniques in terms of the model's accuracy and reliability.

title: Evaluation of the Application Effectiveness of Blended Learning Strategies in Higher Education Institutions Under Multimedia Technology Environment
authors: Shuang Zhao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: While multimedia technology brings multimodal interaction and immersive experience to blended learning in universities, traditional evaluation methods are no longer suitable for its complex scenarios. Traditional quantitative evaluation methods have limitations such as single data and reliance on subjective feedback, resulting in significant deviations in evaluation results. This study introduces an innovative approach to assess the effectiveness of blended learning strategies in higher education within a multimedia-enhanced setting. The methodology begins by designing a structured data acquisition framework to gather relevant information, ensuring a robust and well-organized dataset for analysis. Key performance metrics for teaching outcomes are carefully selected and standardized across dimensions, forming a multi-tiered assessment system. By employing fuzzy analytic hierarchy process (AHP), a fuzzy relational matrix is generated and integrated with weighted evaluation criteria to derive a consolidated performance score. Empirical findings demonstrate that the proposed evaluation model yields results closely aligned with real-world observations, achieving a confidence level exceeding 96%. The high precision of the assessment outcomes underscores the notable advantages of this blended learning approach.

title: Research and Implementation of a University Intelligent Digital Interviewer Model System Based on ChatGLM
authors: Dongxiang Song, Minghong Yang, Tao Chen, Shoujian Duan, Yiran Wang, Yongxian Pu, Yongbin Wu, Cuilan Yu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Artificial Intelligence (AI) has deeply integrated into numerous fields, especially in the recruitment and interview process. Traditional recruitment and interview procedures are prone to being influenced by the subjective factors of interviewers, leading to assessment biases and affecting the selection of talents and the employment decisions of enterprises. To enhance the efficiency of interviews, this study, based on a frontier border university, has developed an intelligent digital human interviewer model system for universities, which is based on ChatGLM. The system builds a high-performance backend service with the SpringBoot3 framework, uses Vue to construct the backend management system, and develops a mobile APP client with UniAPP. It takes the ChatGLM large language model as the core to drive intelligent interview assessment and integrates advanced digital human technology to create a highly realistic interview interface. By leveraging the customized output of the large model, it effectively addresses the pain points of traditional interviews, establishing a quantifiable, low-bias standardized assessment system. This system helps to upgrade the digitalization of recruitment in enterprises and employment guidance in universities, providing an innovative solution for the reconstruction of intelligent interview scenarios. Experiments show that: 1. The system realizes the function of an intelligent digital human interviewer for universities and provides scientific and objective interview assessment results; 2. The accuracy of voice-to-text conversion is on average 99.2%; 3. The similarity between intelligent assessment (ChatGLM large model and human assessment results) is 97%; 4. The current model efficiency can support 20 students to complete interviews simultaneously online. Future research will delve into the integration and application mechanisms of multimodal AI technologies such as speech recognition, natural language processing, and computer vision in interview scenarios, providing a replicable technical model for the digital transformation of employment services in universities and expanding the innovative application models of AI in the human resources field.

title: Hardware Accelerator for 3D Convolutional Neural Networks Based on Dynamic Reconfiguration of FPGAs
authors: Qirui Chen, Xiyan Dong, Meini Tang, Chengying Chen, Zhixian Hou, Xinghua Wang, Xiaoran Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Three-dimensional convolutional neural networks (3D CNNs) are increasingly being used in many computer vision tasks. Deploying 3D CNNs on hardware is a challenge due to their high computational complexity and storage requirements. This brief presents an FPGA hardware accelerator for 3D CNNs that implements the well-known C3D architecture on the Zynq7035 platform through a dynamic reconfiguration scheme, with 10 acceleration IP cores designed for high performance based on the characteristics of the different layers of the C3D architecture. Compared to a high performance 4.2 GHz AMD CPU, the compute speed is 54.3 times faster and the power efficiency is 738 times better. The operating power consumption of the proposed accelerator is only 4 W.

title: A Hybrid Multiband-Structured Subband Adaptive Filtering Algorithm with Fixed Filter for Active Noise Control
authors: Cheng Liang, Yu Zhao, Yin Cao, Yi Zhou, Shuxuan Yang, Zhenghua Cheng
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Fixed-filter-based active noise control (ANC) are widely adopted in commercial headphones due to their inherent stability and immediate response. However, their noise reduction performance is highly sensitive to the incident direction and spectral characteristics of the noise. Adaptive filters adjust weights in real-time but suffer from slow convergence and poor frequency band adaptability. To address these issues, this paper proposes a hybrid multiband-structured adaptive filtering algorithm integrated with a fixed filter for ANC. It can achieve rapid initial noise suppression through fixed filters, while adopting a multiband decomposition architecture to map residual noise signals into subband domains and accelerate convergence speed.

title: Solar Mobility for Urban Transit: Performance and Feasibility of VIPV in Bus Fleets
authors: Hamid Samadi, Guido Ala, Miguel Centeno Brito, Marcello Marchese, Silvia Licciardi, Pietro Romano, Fabio Viola
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This study assesses the potential of vehicleintegrated photovoltaics (VIPV) for public transport buses through a case study in Palermo, Italy. Using high-resolution operational data and a dedicated optical-thermal-electrical modeling chain, irradiance, module temperature, and energy yields were estimated for different bus surfaces and sizes. Results show that VIPV can supply a meaningful share of energy demand, reduce emissions, and achieve competitive electricity costs compared to grid supply and charging tariffs, highlighting its value for sustainable urban mobility and its relevance for planning strategies in dense urban areas.

title: Methods for Predicting and Controlling Traffic Carbon Emissions in Urban Sustainable Development Driven by Big Data
authors: Nan Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: To address the issue of traffic carbon emissions in the context of urban sustainable development, this study proposes a data-driven prediction and control method. By constructing a prediction model combining Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) network and an attention mechanism, the dynamic characteristics of traffic flow, speed distribution, and road network structure can be accurately captured, achieving high-precision carbon emission prediction. Model robustness is further enhanced through parameter optimization and data preprocessing. In the control strategy, reinforcement learning is used to optimize traffic signals, and dynamic route planning is integrated to effectively reduce carbon emissions during peak hours by approximately 25%, with an average reduction rate of 22.5%, while also reducing vehicle delay time by 15%. Simulation results demonstrate that this method significantly improves traffic efficiency and reduces carbon emissions, providing a scientific basis and practical tools for urban low-carbon transportation management, thus contributing to the achievement of sustainable development goals.

title: Next-Generation Industrial Safety Challenges in Education of Industrial Engineering and Management Master Students
authors: Ojars Krumins, Nadezhda Kunicina, Gokul Manju Santhoshkumar, Herath Mudiyanselage Hansika Devindi Wijekoon
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Industrial processes are developing at a rapid pace, creating a trade-off in the education of management students between engineering and economics. This research analyses a case study of Industrial Engineering and Management Master’s students and considers the future development of courses for specialization in this field. Specifically, the paper examines the practical application and integration of industrial safety as a subject for master’s students, which focuses on equipping students with skills to evaluate reasonability and economic value to develop secure, energy-efficient autonomous industrial technologies. Key aspects include the implementation of modern production systems, including autonomous networks, AI-driven threat detection, and sustainable smart systems in the study course. The paper details the educational methodologies, challenges, and long-term impact of this initiative on the next generation of industrial experts.

title: Technical And Economic Analysis Of A Solar-Hydrogen System For Energy Supply Of Remote Settlements
authors: Jumayev Aganiyaz Yagshivevich, Kakabayev Atageldi Sapargeldiyevich
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: “Green” hydrogen is a promising solution for a decarbonized energy system. The article proposes a pilot project of a solar-hydrogen system for power supply of remote settlements. The work develops an energy supply system consisting of an electricity source - a photovoltaic solar station with an installed capacity of 1.5 MW, a hydrogen production system - an electrolyzer with a capacity of 1 MW and a 2 G generator with a capacity of 115 kW, and also provides an assessment of the economic feasibility of the production of “green” hydrogen. The article presents an economic analysis of the pilot project of solar-hydrogen energy supply system developed at the State Energy Institute of Turkmenistan. With the growing popularity of renewable energy, more and more countries are using renewable energy sources in the production of “green” hydrogen. However, hydrogen production systems from renewable energy often have high equipment capacity, in addition, it requires rational design, as well as analysis of the system performance to meet technical requirements while ensuring cost-effectiveness.

title: Application of Model Order Reduction in Evaluation of Small-Signal Stability of Power Systems with Virtual Power Plants
authors: M. V. Avendano-Caiza, Javier Roldan-Perez, Njegos Jankovic, J. L. Rodriguez-Amenedo, J. D. Rios-Penaloza, Milan Prodanovic
abstract: Virtual Power Plants (VPPs) represent an aggregation of distributed generators and loads that are capable of following power references sent by grid operators. To guarantee system stability, grid operators need to verify the impact of VPPs on the small-signal stability of electrical transmission systems. Since dynamic models of VPPs have a large number of states, the application of model order reduction (MOR) techniques should be considered for stability analyses. In this paper, the influence of VPPs on the small-signal stability of electrical transmission systems is evaluated. For that purpose, VPPs are modelled in detail, including the dynamics of renewable sources such as wind and solar. Then, the models are reduced by using two MOR techniques, namely state relevance and balanced truncation. The VPPs are connected to the benchmark two-area power system model and their impact on the small-signal stability assessed. The results show that renewable energy-based VPPs have a significant impact on the system eigenvalues and that they provide additional damping to the system. It is shown that the low-frequency dynamic properties can be preserved using low-order reduced models, while higher-order ones are needed for the medium-frequency dynamics. Eigenvalue analysis is used to validate the theoretical developments.

title: Parallel Dual-Path Network for Clarity Restoration of Scanned Posters via Frequency Decomposition
authors: Peng Chao
abstract: Scanned poster materials frequently exhibit blur degradation arising from scanner hardware constraints, document manipulation movements, and optical apparatus imperfections. This research introduces PDPNet, a parallel dual-path network framework designed for clarity recovery in digitized graphic documents, with particular emphasis on poster and print media scenarios. The architecture operates via two autonomous processing channels: a spatial pathway preserving layout organization and textual content integrity, alongside a frequency pathway recovering fine printing details through wavelet-based decomposition mechanisms. An adaptive integration strategy dynamically merges outputs from both channels according to regional content characteristics. Experimental assessments on poster scanning collections demonstrate that our methodology attains PSNR of 31.82 dB (mean over 3 runs) and SSIM of 0.943, constituting statistically significant improvements (p<0.01) of 0.11 dB and 0.015 respectively compared to state-of-the-art approaches. Critically, document-specific evaluations reveal substantial enhancements in text recognition: Character Error Rate reduced to 4.23% (12.8% relative improvement) andWord Error Rate to 10.12% (10.8% relative improvement), directly validating practical usability for document digitization applications. The framework demonstrates superior performance in maintaining text legibility and graphical sharpness within scanned documents while requiring only 5.2M parameters.

title: Genetic Algorithm based Camera Calibration in Digital Twin
authors: Kwanho Kim, Dokyung Kim, Minseung Kim, Jaehyeon Sung, Kanghyun Jo
abstract: Cyber-physical system (CPS) supports the analysis, simulation, and automation based on the exchange of information between real and digital twin (DT) world. Among various sensing modalities, cameras are widely employed, and camera calibration plays a crucial role in achieving geometric consistency between cyber and physical domains. Genetic algorithm (GA) based camera calibration has been introduced with its global search capability, compared to gradient descent based approach. However, several challenges such as the need for predefined lower and upper bounds of each parameter, limited fitness function and slow convergence lead to a lack of open-source implementations. To overcome these bottlenecks, this paper proposes a novel GA based camera calibration framework that leverages DT. Unlike conventional approaches relying solely on reprojection error, the proposed fitness function better reflects the parameter errors by leveraging the geometric advantages of DT. Utilizing GA enables the use of the proposed non-differentiable fitness function. Besides, a hyperparameter scheduling strategy is integrated to accelerate the convergence. Experiments with five distortion parameters demonstrate that the proposed method consistently converges to near-optimal solution, even when keypoints are nearly planar and widely distributed. The proposed fitness functions reduce the camera position error by approximately 2.1 meters, and hyperparameter scheduling reduces the convergence time by half compared to fixed-parameter GA. Furthermore, a comparative analysis with and without tangential distortion validates the robustness under increased non-linear complexity. The source code is available at https://github.com/Kwan-Ho-Kim/GADT.

title: Modeling and Analysis of Permanent Magnet Synchronous Machines With Axial Magnetization Rotor by Air Gap Field Modulation Theory
authors: Peixin Wang, Heyang Liang, Jikai Si, Shuai Xu, Rui Nie, Shaofeng Jia, Wei Hua
abstract: The permanent magnet synchronous machine with axial magnetization rotor (AMR-PMSM), which is capable of low noise, low vibration, and high reliability, has a wide application prospect in wind power generation, industrial automation, robotics, etc. In this article, the novel analytical models (AMs) originating from the air gap field modulation theory (AFMT) are proposed and constructed to quickly predict and analyze the performance of the AMR-PMSM. First, the radial and tangential modulation functions of the stator and rotor cores are deduced. Second, combined with the primitive magnetomotive force (MMF) of the permanent magnets (PMs) and armature windings (AWs), the air gap radial PM and AW magnetic fields are modeled, as well as the tangential PM and AW magnetic fields. Furthermore, the AMs for the back-EMF, cogging torque, and electromagnetic torque of the AMR-PMSM are derived based on the radial and tangential magnetic fields. Then, taking a 12-slot/50-pole(12s/50p) AMR-PMSM, for example, its performance predicted by AMs is obtained and compared with that calculated by the 3-D finite element model (3D-FEM). Finally, experiments concerning a 12s/50p AMR-PMSM prototype are conducted to verify the effectiveness of the proposed AMs and 3D-FEM.

title: Structure-Preserved Conditional Diffusion for Hyperspectral LiDAR Fusion via Cross-Modal Alignment
authors: Zhengyu Zhou, Erning Feng
abstract: Hyperspectral imagery (HSI) and LiDAR provide highly complementary cues for remote sensing: HSI offers rich spectral signatures for material discrimination, while LiDAR delivers reliable geometric and elevation structure. However, their heterogeneity and the inherent spectral-structure trade-off make it challenging to generate fused products that are simultaneously spectrally faithful and spatially sharp. In this paper, we propose a structure-aware, diffusion-driven fusion framework for high-resolution HSI reconstruction conditioned on co-registered LiDAR. The framework first couples a Transformer-style Cross-modal Feature Alignment Module (CFAM) that performs bidirectional cross-attention between HSI and LiDAR tokens and further enforces co-located correspondence using an InfoNCE-based contrastive objective. Second, a Conditional Diffusion Fusion Network (CDFN) that models the conditional distribution of high-resolution HSI through iterative denoising guided by aligned multimodal features. Third, a Structure-Preserving Diffusion Module (SPDM) that injects LiDAR-derived edge/gradient priors into the denoiser and constrains boundary and gradient consistency to mitigate geometric drift and boundary blur. To balance spectral fidelity, structural consistency, and perceptual quality, we optimize the whole model end-to-end with a cooperative multi-objective loss. Experiments on two benchmark datasets (MUUFL Gulfport and Trento) demonstrate consistent improvements over representative fusion baselines, achieving 35.24 dB/0.963/4.02° (PSNR/SSIM/SAM) on MUUFL and 38.52 dB/0.975/3.02° on Trento, validating the effectiveness of coupling explicit cross-modal alignment with structure-preserved conditional diffusion generation.

title: Finite-Time Leader–Follower Tracking Control of Multiple Euler–Lagrange Systems via Sliding Mode under Switching Topologies
authors: Yu Wang, Xiaoyu Zhang
abstract: The problem of finite-time cooperative tracking is investigated for a class of heterogeneous Euler–Lagrange (EL) agents interconnected through time-varying switching directed networks and subject to exogenous disturbances. A distributed sliding-mode control framework is developed to ensure that the tracking errors between each follower and the leader converge to a neighborhood of zero within a finite time. The control design explicitly accounts for communication constraints and time-varying disturbances by introducing topology switching-resilient sliding manifolds and a finite-time convergence mechanism, whose stability properties are rigorously established via Lyapunov analysis. Unlike existing asymptotic approaches, the proposed method guarantees finite-time convergence and disturbance suppression under switching directed topologies, provided that each interaction graph contains a leader-rooted spanning tree. The finite-time stability of the closed-loop system is analytically proven, and numerical simulations are presented to validate the effectiveness of the proposed control protocol.

title: A new method for fabrication of high quality single domain GdBCO ring superconductor by RE+011 TSIG method
authors: Wanmin Yang, Kun liu
abstract: In this paper, we provided a modified Gd+011 TSIG method to prepare single domain GdBCO bulk and ring superconductors. A single domain GdBCO bulk of 32 mm in diameter and a single domain GdBCO ring with inner and outer diameter of 13 and 32 mm have been fabricated. The physical properties of samples indicate that: (1) The levitation force are of 112.5 N(77 K, 0.5 T)and 111.9 N(77 K, 0.5 T)for the single domain GdBCO bulk and the single domain GdBCO ring respectively. (2) The trapped field are of 0.401 T (77 K) and 0.386 T (77 K) for the single domain GdBCO bulk and single domain GdBCO ring respectively. (4) The trapped magnetic flux of the single domain GdBCO ring is 1.72×10-4 Wb (77 K), which is higher than that of 1.55×10-4 Wb (77 K) for the single domain GdBCO bulk. These results provided a new way to prepare high quality single domain REBCO rings for practical application.

title: Simultaneous and Independent Evaluation of Single-element Inductive and Resistive Sensors Using a k -Insensitive Wireless Readout
authors: Anil Kumar Appukuttan Nair Syamala Amma, Jeshma Thalapil Vaheeda, Pavel Ripka, Sreenath Vijayakumar
abstract: This article presents a compact wireless readout circuit capable of simultaneous and independent measurement of inductance and resistance in a series-RLC configuration. The sensing elements, a resistor and an inductor, are connected in series with a known capacitor in the secondary circuit, while the measurements are wirelessly obtained from the primary (readout) coil by analyzing the reflected impedance. The key advantage of the proposed method is its insensitivity to variations in the magnetic coupling coefficient (k) over a wide range, making it robust to misalignment and distance changes between the coils over a wide range of k. Unlike conventional methods, the proposed system enables simultaneous and independent measurement of both inductance and resistance, with a minimum error propagation and thereby enhancing overall measurement accuracy. The proposed method was first validated through numerical simulations and subsequently tested experimentally using different values of standard resistors (10 − 100Ω) and variable inductors (110 − 180μH). A laboratory-scale experimental setup was then implemented, with an inductive displacement sensor and an NTC thermistor connected on the sensing side. The experimental results demonstrate that the worst-case error remains within 2.5% for displacement (inductance) measurements and 3% for temperature (resistive) measurements over a range of 0 − 30mm and 28 − 73 °C.

title: An Attentive Hybrid Network for Myocardial Infarction Severity and Risk Assessment
authors: Akriti Jaiswal, Samarendra Dandapat, Prabin Kumar Bora
abstract: Myocardial infarction (MI) is a life-threatening cardiac condition that necessitates early diagnosis and accurate severity assessment for effective clinical management. Existing automated techniques mostly focus on differentiating MI from healthy controls (HC), limiting their clinical utility by overlooking severity staging and quantitative risk evaluation. To address this gap, we propose a novel Attentive Hybrid Multiscale Fusion Network (AHMFN) for simultaneously classifying MI severity stages and estimating quantitative risk score (RS) levels. The network integrates dual-stream CNN with both fixed and variable receptive fields for multiscale spatial feature extraction, utilises multi-head attention for improved feature weighting, and a BiLSTM module to capture temporal dependencies in ECG data. The proposed RS quantitatively measures diagnostic certainty based on cardiac axis deviation (CAD). Comprehensive evaluations of the PTB-XL dataset exhibit an accuracy of 99.82% for MI severity stages and 95.2% for RS levels, while external validation on the PTBDB and STAFF III datasets attained an accuracy of 99.5%, hence affirming generalisability. RS further exhibits robust clinical interpretability, with a Pearson correlation of 0.85 to CAD angles. Despite its advanced design, AHMFN ensures deployment feasibility with ≤ 89 ms/step inference latency and a compact ∼ 2.15 MB model size, making it suitable for real-time wearable applications. This balance of efficiency and accuracy enables interpretable early-stage diagnosis and timely intervention, while outperforming existing methods.

title: Simulating Muscle-Level Energetic Cost When Humans Walk With a Passive Biarticular Thigh Exosuit_supp3-3668912.pdf
authors: Vahid Firouzi

title: Fuzzy Decoupled Adaptive Impedance Control for a Five-Wheel Wall-Climbing Robot on Discontinuous Wall Surfaces_supp5-3670440.mp4
authors: Lei Chen
abstract: Aiming at the problems such as insufficient driving force, body oscillation, and contact instability that easily occur when wheel-based wall-climbing robots operate on discontinuous wall surfaces (such as corners and obstacles), this paper proposes an adaptive control strategy integrating joint impedance control and fuzzy decoupling compensation for a Five-Wheel Wall-Climbing robot(FWWC-robot). This strategy optimizes impedance parameters (mass, damping, and stiffness) online through an adaptive parameter update mechanism based on force error to minimize magnetic gap fluctuations and ensure stable adsorption. Meanwhile, a Mamdani fuzzy controller is used to dynamically compensate for the coupling interference of the front and rear swing arms, realizing real-time adjustment of joint compliance and improving the robot’s motion stability and dynamic adaptability when encountering abrupt changes in wall structures. Simulation and experimental results show that, compared with the traditional impedance control, this method reduces the attitude error by 60%, controls the magnetic gap fluctuation within 0.3 mm, achieves a 100% success rate of obstacle crossing under the working conditions of 63° corner and 30 mm obstacle, and keeps the attitude stability within ±1.2°. It provides an efficient and robust solution for the compliant motion of robots in complex wall environments, and can directly support the automated applications in high-risk operation scenarios such as ship derusting and large oil tank inspection.

title: Descriptor: Drone Nadir-view Annotated Images of Vehicles Detection Dataset for India with Heading-angle Oriented Bounding Box (DRASHTI-HaOBB)
authors: Yagnik M. Bhavsar, Mazad S. Zaveri, Mehul S. Raval, Krishna R. Patel, Shaheriar B. Zaveri
abstract: Road accidents remain one of the leading causes of fatalities and injuries worldwide. In developing and densely populated countries such as India, the number of vehicles has increased exponentially; however, road infrastructure has not kept pace, leading to more traffic problems and road accidents. Hence, an effective Road Traffic Monitoring (RTM) system is essential for studying traffic problems and mitigating the risk of road accidents. In recent years, Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) have been used for RTM by capturing aerial imagery. The first step in computer vision-based RTM is to effectively detect vehicles using deep learning-based vehicle detection models. Researchers have recently developed Oriented Object Detection (OOD) models that detect vehicles and their orientations using Oriented Bounding Boxes (OBBs). These detected vehicles can be used for subsequent RTM tasks (e.g., driving behaviour, traffic violations). For training OOD models, we need a country-specific vehicle detection OBB dataset, especially in India, due to the diverse vehicle classes. Hence, this paper introduces the DRASHTI-HaOBB dataset (for vehicle detection), made up of 27,577 annotated images (containing nearly 1.3 million OBB vehicle samples), comprising 14 different vehicle classes prevalent in India: Auto3WCargo, AutoRicksaw, Bus, Container, Mixer, MotorCycle, PickUp, SUV, Sedan, Tanker, Tipper, Trailer, Truck, Van. In addition to the OBB, the annotation also provides the heading angle (indicating the front of the vehicle) and the UAV flight height of the image. Finally, this paper benchmarks the performance of various OOD models on the DRASHTI-HaOBB dataset and establishes a performance baseline. Results show that the YOLOv11n and YOLOv11s models achieved 85% and 92% detection accuracy at 104 and 84 FPS, respectively, making them suitable (in terms of accuracy-speed trade-off) for real-time deployment.

title: Toward Robust Radio Frequency Fingerprint Identification: A Federated Learning Framework with Feature Alignment
authors: Yuteng Wang, Zhenxin Cai, Tiantian Tang, Tomoaki Ohtsuki, Guan Gui
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: With the growing adoption of Internet of Things (IoT) devices, ensuring the security of wireless communications has become increasingly critical. Radio frequency fingerprint identification (RFFI) has shown promise in this regard due to its capability of uniquely identifying devices. Although deep learning (DL) approaches have significantly improved RFFI performance, they typically rely on large-scale centralized data. This poses challenges in terms of privacy preservation and heterogeneous data distributions. To address the performance degradation caused by non-independent and identically distributed (non-IID) data in cross-receiver scenarios, this paper proposes a feature alignment strategy based on federated learning (FL) for RFFI. In such scenarios, due to differences in receiver hardware characteristics, deployment locations, and channel conditions, the signals captured by different receivers often exhibit distribution shifts, resulting in misaligned feature spaces across clients. The proposed method guides each client to learn aligned intermediate feature representations during local training, effectively mitigating the resulting adverse impact on model generalization. Experiments conducted on a real-world RF dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves higher identification accuracy and improved stability compared with representative federated baselines, including FedAvg and FedProx. The highest identification accuracy reaches 90.83%, and the performance gains are accompanied by generally reduced variance across different client configurations, highlighting the robustness and generalization capability of the proposed approach in heterogeneous wireless environments.

title: Direct Matrix Converter-Based Modular High-Power Wireless Charging Systems for Heavy-Duty Electric Vehicles
authors: Zichen Deng, Shibo Zhang, Gangwei Zhu, Jianning Dong, Pavol Bauer
publish: IEEE Journal on Wireless Power Technologies
abstract: Due to the increasing demand for high-power charging in heavy-duty electric vehicles (HDEVs) and the inherent limitations of conductive charging, the development of a modular wireless power transfer (WPT) charging system has become indispensable. However, most existing studies rely on conventional two-stage AC-DC-AC converters at the primary side, which limit power density and reliability because of the bulky DC-link electrolytic capacitors. This paper proposes a novel modular medium-voltage AC (MVAC) grid-connected WPT system based on direct matrix converters (DMCs). A full-bridge DMC (FBDMC)-based WPT system is proposed with a simple fixed 50 % duty-cycle modulation strategy. Analytical and simulation results verify that the AC-input FBDMC-based WPT system can be equivalently represented by a DC-input fullbridge inverter-based WPT system with the same seriesseries compensation, RMS input voltage, and modulation. To achieve higher power transfer capability, scalability, and efficiency, input-series output-parallel (ISOP) and inputparallel output-series (IPOS) modular topologies are investigated. The simulation results demonstrate that, when connected to the MVAC grid using the ISOP configuration, the system is capable of delivering power at the hundred-kW to MW level. The proposed topologies are verified by the downscaled experimental platform. The FBDMC-based prototype achieves an output power of 2.51 kW with a grid-to-load efficiency of 91.6 % at 230 VAC. The IPOS and ISOP configurations are experimentally validated using two modular half-bridge DMC (HBDMC) modules derived from the FBDMC design.

title: MithrilRB: Resource-Efficient Redactable Blockchain with Single-Use Authorization
authors: Tianming Hou, Hui Ma, Jinchao Zhang, Yang Li, Bo Li, Weiping Wang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Redactable blockchains preserve the integrity of hash links while enabling authorized redactions to comply with regulatory requirements. However, existing permissioned solutions suffer from three severe issues. First, fine-grained privilege control incurs significant storage overhead, especially in the case of single-use authorization. Second, the reliance on bilinear pairings in chameleon hash leads to significant performance degradation when handling large-scale redaction requests. Finally, multiple incorporated components often unconsciously introduce centralized entities, undermining the decentralized nature. In this paper, we propose MithrilRB, a resource-efficient and decentralized redactable blockchain with single-use authorization. Specifically, we introduce a privilege control mechanism with our proposed multi-authority attribute-based signature (MA-ABS) and the threshold BLS signature, achieving fine-grained single-use authorization and direct user revocation without extra ciphertext storage. We also design a pairing-free non-interactive threshold chameleon hash (PNITCH), which enhances efficiency and is better suited for large-scale redaction requests. Moreover, MithrilRB eliminates centralized trust points that hold secret information, ensuring fully decentralization-compatible functional integration in redactable blockchains. Finally, we implement MithrilRB, and the experimental results demonstrate that MithrilRB significantly outperforms existing solutions in both computational efficiency and storage requirements.

title: FedPalm: A General Federated Learning Framework for Closed- and Open-Set Palmprint Verification
authors: Ziyuan Yang, Yingyu Chen, Chengrui Gao, Andrew Beng Jin Teoh, Bob Zhang, Yi Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Current deep learning (DL)-based palmprint verification models rely on centralized training with large datasets, which raises significant privacy concerns due to the sensitive and immutable nature of biometric data. Federated learning (FL), a privacy-preserving distributed learning paradigm, offers a compelling alternative by enabling collaborative model training without the need for data sharing. However, FL-based palmprint verification faces critical challenges, including data heterogeneity from diverse identities and the absence of standardized evaluation benchmarks. This paper addresses these gaps by establishing a comprehensive benchmark for FL-based palmprint verification, which explicitly defines and evaluates two practical scenarios: closed-set and open-set verification. We propose FedPalm, a unified FL framework that balances local adaptability with global generalization. Each client trains a personalized textural expert tailored to local data and collaboratively contributes to a shared global textural expert for extracting generalized features. To further enhance verification performance, we introduce a Textural Expert Interaction Module that dynamically routes textural features among experts to generate refined side textural features. Learnable parameters are employed to model relationships between original and side features, fostering cross-texture-expert interaction and improving feature discrimination. Extensive experiments validate the effectiveness of FedPalm, demonstrating robust performance across both scenarios and providing a promising foundation for advancing FL-based palm-print verification research. The related code has been publicly available at https://github.com/Zi-YuanYang/FedPalm.

title: A Study on the Impact and Marginal Effects of Digital Transformation on Outward Foreign Direct Investment

title: An Ecological Footprint Image Dataset for Automatic Identification of Paleontology Discovery
authors: Yinghao Cai, Shaoning Zeng
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Paleontological relics are precious and rare, and non-specialist paleontological explorers can bring a wealth of information to the field. However, there are quite a few of shortcomings, including misdiagnosis and corruption of information. Until now, no image dataset is collected to assist the recognition of ecological footprints, especially using the machine learning techniques. We initially collect an ecological dataset, containing two kinds of images with 2385 samples from the Internet, as well as the scientific publications of paleontology for computer vision. Furthermore, we utilize the Frequency-tuned Salient Region Detection (FT) and Local Contrast based Salient Region Detection (LC) algorithms to preprocess the data. These methodologies facilitate the identification of the striking parts of an image to help the recognition algorithms. Subsequently, we deploy six classical deep neural networks for the purpose of classification, which have garnered extensive application across a spectrum of image recognition and processing tasks. As a result, the dataset can be effectively used scientifically for automated identification.

title: Integration of satellite and field data for the detection of a harmful algal bloom in a reservoir
authors: Sofia Rosario Gutierrez, Mariana Lucia Correa, Rocío Eluney Guido, Lucio Pinotti, Anabella Ferral, Matias Bonansea
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Lakes and reservoirs are aquatic systems that play a crucial role in both environmental balance and human well-being. However, these systems are exposed to eutrophication and deterioration of water quality. Therefore, comprehensive monitoring programs are essential to establish effective strategies for its improvement. The main objective of this study was to characterize an algal and cyanobacteria bloom (HAB) recorded in the Río Tercero Reservoir (Córdoba, Argentina) by integrating In-situ monitoring techniques with satellite-based remote sensing data. Water quality variables and phytoplankton composition were measured in the reservoir and related with a Landsat 9 image. Results revealed the presence of a HAB occurring in the central-western region of the reservoir. This founding was also demonstrated by Landsat data which was used to predict the total chlorophyll-a concentration (Chl-a ${ }_{\text {Total }}$), and cyanobacterial chlorophyll-a (Chl- $\mathbf{a}_{\text {Cyano }}$) across the entire reservoir surface.

title: Deep Learning-Based Nonlinear Time Series Statistical Modeling and Trend Forecasting
authors: Yaning Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Nonlinear time series are prevalent in finance, meteorology, and industrial processes. Their strong nonstationarity, nonlinearity, and long-range dependencies challenge traditional statistical models such as ARIMA and GARCH, leading to poor long-term prediction accuracy. This paper proposes a deep learning-based nonlinear statistical modeling and trend forecasting method. Statistical features including autocorrelation, partial autocorrelation, and volatility clustering are first extracted and fused with raw sequences. An enhanced gated recurrent unit (GRU) network incorporating multi-head attention and residual connections is designed to capture complex temporal dependencies and alleviate gradient vanishing. A sliding-window scheme is used for multi-step forecasting. Experiments on the NASDAQ composite index and global temperature anomaly series show that the proposed model achieves an $\mathbf{R}^{\mathbf{2}}$ of 0.962, improving by 18.5% over ARIMA, and reduces MAE to 0.038, a 22.4% reduction compared to a standard GRU. The long-term error increase remains below 5%, proving its robustness. The model provides an efficient framework for nonlinear time-series prediction, applicable to financial risk warnings and climate forecasting.

title: Voice-Based Parkinson’s Disease Stage Classification
authors: Gottumukala Gayathri, Ganesh B Regulwar, Sreeja Vanga, Arukala Pradeep, V.Muni Shekhar, Nikhila Kathirisetty
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: The progressive neurodegenerative condition known as Parkinson’s disease (PD) impairs both cognitive and motor abilities. Predicting illness phases accurately is essential for early intervention and individualized care. This work suggests a machine learning method based on clinical and biological data to categorize and forecast the stages of Parkinson’s disease using the XGBoost algorithm. The patient records in the dataset include characteristics including voice abnormalities, motor function scores, and other pertinent biomarkers. The XGBoost model is assessed against conventional classification models like SVM. Test findings show that XGBoost outperforms other machine learning methods in terms of accuracy, sensitivity, and specificity. This strategy improves patient care and disease management by offering a dependable and effective way to forecast Parkinson’s stage. According to comprehensive comparative results, XGBoost performs better than traditional classification models like SVM in tests, with higher accuracy, sensitivity, and F1-scores throughout all Parkinson’s stage.The proposed XGBoost model achieved an accuracy of 93.02%, precision of 0.93, recall of 0.92, and an F1-score of 0.91, outperforming traditional classifiers.

title: MTT-S Technical Committee TC-20 on HF/VHF/UHF Technology: 2024–2025 Activities Report

title: Mapping of wetlands in the Pantanal with SWOT
authors: Luana Sales, Daniel Beltrão, Thiago Lappicy, Larisse Jesus, Tati Almeida, Rejane Cicerelli
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: The Pantanal, a biome characterized by extensive wetlands, presents significant challenges for accurate mapping. The Surface Water and Ocean Topography (SWOT) mission, designed to monitor water surfaces through two-dimensional imaging, offers a promising tool for this purpose. This study analyzes the capability of SWOT to map flooded wetlands in the Pantanal Mato Grosso National Park relative to Sentinel-1 (radar) and Sentinel-2 (optical) imagery. Flooded wetlands were analyzed both spatially and temporally. SWOT pixels classified as open water frequently overlapped with Sentinel-derived water masks, showing an overall correspondence of 85%. Moreover, in comparison of the entire inundated area, considering the SWOT methods relative to Sentinel, higher and more concentrated relative errors were observed, especially for Sentinel-1, and lower errors for Sentinel-2. Relative errors decreased for both Sentinel missions when focusing on a lake, while for the Paraguay River, greater variability and higher values were observed with Sentinel-1. The composite and simple methods showed similar performance, whereas the water fraction method produced the poorest results overall across all scenarios.

title: Adaptive Multi-Diversity Techniques for Evaporation Duct Channels
authors: Mi Lin, Mingliang Chen, Xiaoyu Li, Zhilong Chen, Wenbin Cai
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: In evaporation duct channels, multipath propagation causes the received signal to be the vector sum of components that have travelled along multiple paths with different delays and phases. These components can either reinforce the signal or lead to severe attenuation. Consequently, the received signal power can fluctuate rapidly and dramatically, and at certain times may fall $20-30 \mathrm{~dB}$ below the mean level, resulting in deep fading. If such deep fading persists for an extended period, the link may be interrupted and communication can fail completely. To mitigate the multipath fading effects of the sea-surface evaporation duct, this paper proposes a method that combines multiple diversity techniques. Multi-diversity techniques are employed to combat multipath fading. Unlike conventional single-diversity schemes, the proposed approach jointly exploits time, spatial and frequency diversity, providing greater stability, higher reliability and improved performance. Relative to no diversity and to time, spatial and frequency diversity alone, multi-diversity yields diversity gains of $2.5 \mathrm{~dB}, 1.9 \mathrm{~dB}, 1.7 \mathrm{~dB}$ and 1.1 dB, respectively.

title: A Tual-Flow Network Based on Attention Mechanism and Global Information for Small Object Detection
authors: Ning Wang, Meijuan Zhou, Zhong Ma
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Small object detection, as an important branch of traditional object detection, has attracted extensive attention and is widely applied in military and civilian fields. Especially in the field of remote sensing, with the development of remote sensing technology, high-resolution satellite data plays a crucial role in ground object observation. Due to the small size of objects in the imaging plane, it brings great challenges to object detection. Aiming at the problem of unsatisfactory small object detection performance, this paper applies DSFNET to small object detection task, utilizing the spatial and temporal information of objects, and extracting global semantic information from the contextual information where objects are located to assist in object detection. In addition, the convolutional block attention module (CBAM) is incorporated into the network to enable different tasks such as classification and regression to adaptively learn effective information, thereby improving the object detection performance. Experimental results also demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Integrating FinTech Innovation and AI-Driven Pedagogy for Curriculum Reform of Object-Oriented Programming
authors: Yali Wu, Xiaojuan Li, Yanxia Zhao, Xiaoran Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the widespread adoption of data-intensive and intelligent technologies such as big data and artificial intelligence in both education and finance sectors, FinTech is increasingly becoming a pivotal direction for curriculum reform in computer science programs within higher education. To address the urgent demand for interdisciplinary talents in the digital economy era, this study systematically explores the integration of FinTech innovation within digitally-intelligent teaching reform. It aims to comprehensively enhance students' practical skills, innovation capabilities, and interdisciplinary competencies through curriculum restructuring, pedagogical innovation, and the design of evaluation mechanisms.

title: NoNitro: A UAV- and AI-Empowered Integrated System for Soil Nitrate Control
authors: Emma Tong, Sophia Chen, Jun Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Earth's growing population requires high crop yields made possible by the addition of nitrogen fertilizer to soil. However, in recent years excess amounts in fields have led to the insurgence of nitrate pollution. Overabundant nitrate in the natural environment disrupts both aquatic and terrestrial ecosystems in various ways, including through eutrophication, in which algae blooms deprive aquatic wildlife of oxygen and sunlight, and pollution of drinking water, leading to health concerns both in wildlife and human populations. Therefore, it is of great importance to monitor and control soil nitrate levels. On the other hand, recent advances in AI have demonstrated its huge potential across a variety of high-impact domains, such as healthcare and agriculture. In this paper, we propose NoNitro, a UAV- and AI-empowered nitrate control system for agricultural fields. The NoNitro system contains three integral modules: the treatment module, the monitoring module, and the feedback module. The treatment module is a disruptive variation of the traditional bioreactor design that utilizes biodegradable pH-sensitive superabsorbent polymer (SAP) cartridges to store incoming water for a slow, controlled release, distributing runoff evenly across time and enabling the bioreactor to treat excess runoff from high flow events during low flow periods. The monitoring module, on the other hand, uses the remote-sensing signals collected by UAVs and leverages cutting-edge AI techniques based on deep neural networks such as hypothesis transfer learning to map soil nitrate across multiple fields prior to the installation of the treatment module. Finally, the feedback module provides guidance to the treatment module in terms of resource allocation, closing the loop within NoNitro. Experiments demonstrate the potential of AI techniques in such a system.

title: Preliminary Exploration of the Application of Time Series Algorithms in Elearning
authors: Kai Zhang, Fenglin Qi, Jun Gao, Jiajie Shen
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: As one of the most commonly used online learning platforms in higher education, eLearning plays a crucial role in supporting teaching activities. Predicting future user access based on platform log data enables administrators to proactively plan resource allocation, thereby enhancing educational service resilience. However, existing methods often fall short in both prediction horizon and accuracy, particularly in capturing the multi-period patterns inherent in educational scenarios. This paper explores a deep learning-based long-term time series prediction approach that explicitly models the relationships among multiple periodic subsequences to better reflect academic rhythms. Evaluated on log data from a university eLearning platform, the proposed method demonstrates consistent advantages over several widely used forecasting baselines across multiple metrics. These results highlight its potential in supporting load anticipation and resource optimization for educational platforms, ultimately improving the teaching and learning experience.

title: A Defect Detection System for Printed Circuit Boards Based on Enhanced HFB-YOLO Architecture
authors: Kunyan Hou
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of the electronics industry, market demand for high-quality printed circuit boards (PCBs) continues to grow. Traditional inspection meth- ods are often limited by low efficiency, high subjectivity, and insufficient detection capabilities. To address these challenges, this paper proposes HFB-YOLO, an automated PCB defect detection system integrating robotics and ad- vanced deep learning. The core of this system is a re- fined YOLOv12n architecture featuring a novel Hierarchi- cal Fusion Block (HFB). The HFB is incorporated into the model's neck network to enhance the fusion of multi-scale features, thereby improving its capability to detect small and varied defects. To accelerate convergence and boost performance on the specific task, the model leverages trans- fer learning. Experimental results demonstrate the system's high accuracy and robustness. On the PKU-Market-PCB dataset, our proposed model achieved a precision of 98.4%, a recall of 98.4%, a mAP@ 50 of 99.0%, and a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{m A P} \boldsymbol{@} \mathbf{5 0: 9 5}$</tex> of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{6 4. 2 \%}$</tex>. Compared to the YOLOv8n benchmark, these metrics represent significant improvements of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$3.6 \%, 8.1 \%, 7.4 \%$</tex>, and 10.9%, respectively. Furthermore, the model achieves a detection speed of 81.5 FPS on an edge CPU, offering an efficient and automated solution for quality con- trol in the PCB manufacturing industry.

title: Research of Project-Based Teaching in Engineering Courses Driven by Gen-AI: Taking Electromagnetic Field Course as Example
authors: Hui Zhong, Xiaolong Wang, Haotian Wei, Chengxian Fan, Li Zhang, Yadi Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The breakthrough development of generative artificial intelligence technology has been continuously reshaping the patterns of educational practice, injecting new impetus into the digital transformation of education. The theoretical research and practical exploration achievements of project-based teaching research in engineering under the support of intelligent technology have also emerged to improve engineering education. Based on the essential attributes of the project-based teaching of Electromagnetic Field course, this paper reveals the key contradictions in the intelligent transformation of project-based teaching in engineering courses by deconstructing the integration mode of generative artificial intelligence (Gen-AI) and blended project-based teaching, and proposes the solution by the practice in project-based teaching. With this research, it is expected to provide a new perspective and practical guidance for the teaching of electromagnetic field course and promote educational innovation and talent cultivation of engineering education.

title: The Effect of a Personalised English Language Teaching Model on Student Motivation and Achievement in Higher Education
authors: Huixin Zong, Shuqing Gao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Personalized English teaching mode is a type of educational approach tailored to diverse students, considering their unique characteristics and needs, aimed at enhancing their learning motivation and achievement. This paper aims to investigate the impact of personalized English teaching mode on college students' motivation and achievement. The research participants were primarily divided into two groups: the experimental group, which implemented the personalized English teaching mode, and the control group, which adhered to the traditional teaching mode. Subsequently, questionnaire surveys and performance statistics were employed to statistically and analytically assess the data regarding students' motivation and performance in college English classrooms. The findings reveal that the personalized English teaching mode exerts a substantial influence on college students' learning motivation and achievement. Notably, students in the experimental group experienced a marked increase in learning motivation and a clearer sense of learning purpose during the teaching process. Furthermore, the English learning achievement of the experimental group significantly surpassed that of the control group. A deeper analysis indicates that the personalized English teaching model is well-suited to students with varying learning levels and needs, thereby facilitating their comprehension and mastery of the English subject. Based on these findings, it is recommended that English education in colleges and universities adopt personalized teaching methodologies that cater to students' individual differences, thereby fostering their learning motivation and achievement.

title: Bluetooth Indoor Fingerprint Positioning for Smart Buildings – Based on TSSA-BPNN Model
authors: Suyi Liu, Jiayang Chen, Tongqing Zhang, Xiaoyu Long
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: With the proliferation of smart terminals and the Internet of Things (IoT), the demand for high-precision indoor positioning has increased significantly. Bluetooth technology has emerged as a popular solution due to its low cost, low power consumption, and potential for high accuracy. However, the heterogeneity in hardware and software across mobile devices leads to substantial variations in Received Signal Strength Indicator (RSSI) values, which severely degrades the performance of fingerprint-based positioning systems. To address this issue, this paper proposes a Bluetooth RSSI calibration method based on a Tent Sparrow Search Algorithm-optimized Back Propagation Neural Network (TSSA-BPNN). The TSSA enhances the BPNN by optimizing its initial weights and thresholds, thereby avoiding local optima and improving calibration accuracy. Experiments conducted in a typical indoor environment demonstrate that the proposed TSSA-BPNN model significantly reduces the average RSSI error compared to uncalibrated and other calibration models (BPNN, WOA-BPNN, SSA-BPNN), with error reductions of approximately 87.5 %, 51.7 %, 32.5 %, and 17.4 %, respectively. Furthermore, tests on multiple mobile devices confirm the model's effectiveness in mitigating RSSI deviations caused by device heterogeneity, underscoring its practical value for enhancing Bluetooth-based indoor positioning systems.

title: Adaptive Modulation with Target-BER Thresholding: BPSK/QPSK/16-QAM Over AWGN
authors: Yue Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Adaptive Modulation Control (AMC) is a key technology for enhancing spectrum efficiency in 5G and future networks, with its performance determined by the switching threshold based on signal-to-noise ratio (SNR). However, a systematic quantitative analysis of how different threshold strategies impact system performance tradeoffs remains lacking. This paper conducts an in-depth investigation into the inherent tradeoff between throughput and reliability for various SNR threshold strategies in AMC systems. This study employs Monte Carlo simulation to design three AMC strategies within an Additive White Gaussian Noise (AWGN) channel model: First, a baseline threshold is calculated based on the target bit error rate. Subsequently, aggressive and conservative strategies are defined by applying <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 2.5 ~\text{dB}$</tex> offsets to this baseline. The results quantitatively reveal this performance trade-off: the aggressive strategy achieves maximum throughput at the expense of reliability, with its error rate even exceeding the quality of service (QoS) target in certain intervals; conversely, the conservative strategy guarantees optimal reliability by sacrificing throughput. This study demonstrates that there is no universal AMC strategy. The selection of threshold strategies must align with the specific QoS requirements of the business scenario, such as enhanced mobile broadband (eMBB) or ultra-reliable and low-latency communications (URLLC). These findings provide important reference for the adaptive design of next-generation wireless links.

title: Topic Identification and Comparative Analysis on LLM Research
authors: Hongwei Fan, Hong Li
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: The rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs) has significantly enhanced performance across NLP, computer vision, and multimodal tasks. Fueled by growing demands for efficient and capable generative AI, LLM research and applications are rapidly evolving, driving an exponential surge in scholarly publications. Understanding LLM research hotspots, evolutionary trends, and interdisciplinary integration is therefore critical both for academic research and industrial applications. However, existing literature reviews predominantly focus on core technical challenges and solutions, lacking quantitative analysis of topic evolution. This study analyzes LLM papers (2021-2025) from the Web of Science and arXiv. Using bibliometrics, LDA topic modeling, and comparative analysis, we identify key research directions, topic distributions over the fiveyear period, and reveal that conference and journal papers showcase partially complementary topic distributions. Furthermore, cross-period analysis of topic evolution demonstrates the inheritance and development of quarterly topics, clarifying their evolutionary trajectories and emerging technical frontiers.

title: Cooperative Optimization of Smart Grids and Urban Transportation Networks: Research on Source-Load Cooperative Dispatch Based on Vehicle-to-Grid (V2G) Interaction
authors: Jiawei Jiang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: In the context of the global energy transition and the rapid development of electric vehicles, smart grids are confronted with challenges such as load fluctuations and the expansion of peak-to-valley discrepancies resulting from the disorganised charging of large-scale electric vehicles. Vehicle-to-grid (V2G) technology is a significant method of coordinating energy interaction between the grid and electric vehicles. It has the capacity to enhance grid stability, promote the consumption of renewable energy, and reduce carbon emissions through source-load coordinated scheduling. The present paper addresses the issue of synergistic optimisation between smart grids and urban transportation networks under the V2G model. In doing so, it proposes a scheduling framework that integrates hierarchical regulation and multi-technology coordination. The proposed framework incorporates a fundamental response layer control mechanism and a load forecasting method based on Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks. The system's bidirectional interaction between electricity prices and load signals enables peak-valley smoothing on the grid side and economic optimisation on the user side. The proposed method demonstrates significant potential in enhancing renewable energy integration capabilities, providing theoretical support and technical pathways for the coordinated development of urban energy-transportation systems.

title: Implementation and Optimization of Multi-Level Decomposition Parallel SAR Echo Simulation Algorithm on FT-M7032 DSP
authors: Shaowei Su, Pu Gu, Yiqian Shen, Qingsong Wang, Tao Lai, Haifeng Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: To extend the Phytium DSP platform’s capability in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) applications, we propose a multi-level parallel optimization method for SAR echo simulation algorithms. By leveraging the FT-M7032 processor’s high-performance architecture, we implement Single Instruction Multiple Data (SIMD) vectorization to enhance DSP core instruction-level parallelism and further exploit the platform’s CPU+DSP heterogeneous multi-core hierarchy through dual-dimensional task partitioning across inter-node CPUs and intra-node GDDSPs, and Ping-pong Buffering mechanism which enables computation-transmission pipeline overlapping.
