Experimental results demonstrate that while maintaining equivalent detection accuracy to the original algorithm, the optimized method achieves approximately $10 \times$ speedup for billion-scale SAR simulation scenarios, significantly narrowing the performance gap with mainstream hardware acceleration solutions.

title: Interactive SQL Tutoring with LLM-Based Data Augmentation
authors: Xinhui He, Haixi Yao, Chunlei Li, Xuxue Sun, Zhiqi Xu, Hao Zeng, Shaowei Chu, Baojun Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study proposes a novel gamified learning approach for database education, integrating contextualized teaching methodology with detective-themed investigations. The approach combines theoretical knowledge of database query techniques with practical case-solving scenarios, enabling students to progressively master fundamental Structured Query Language (SQL) query methods through hands-on application. The virtual learning platform incorporates three key pedagogical strategies: (1) an open graphical interface design, (2) real-time feedback with error diagnostics, and (3) an AI-powered dynamic data generation mechanism, collectively enhancing authenticity and immersion in the learning experience. An exploratory study was conducted with 42 participants using post-intervention questionnaires and performance assessments. The results indicate that more than 90% of the students endorsed the gamified pedagogy. The approach was associated with self-reported improvements in learning motivation, knowledge mastery depth, and practical application capabilities, suggesting its potential as an innovative strategy for database education. Future work will develop customizable templates, fine-tune large language models (LLM) to enhance personalization and system stability, and explore broader applicability of this methodology.

title: Reconstructing Outcome-Based Education Through AI Empowerment: An Empirical Study in Art and Design Disciplines
authors: Fei Qi, Tsen-Shu Tsaur, Yu-Tong Qiu, Yung-Chieh Tsao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This paper takes the art design major as the research object, explores how generative artificial intelligence empowers the outcome-based Education (OBE) model, constructs the theoretical framework of “AI-Enabled OBE”, and conducts empirical analysis through 586 valid questionnaires. Research findings show that the creative assistance effectiveness of AI tools, students' willingness to explore, and their recognition of compound abilities significantly promote the achievement of learning goals. Art design students pay more attention to the matching degree between tools and creative styles rather than the quantity of tools. Privacy concerns are positively correlated with the need for personalized feedback, which is reflected as a rational trade-off of “creative privacy”. Research reveals that AI in art and design education is not only a technical tool but also a creative collaborator and an expander of thinking. Finally, suggestions for teaching reform are put forward, emphasizing that attention should be paid to the cultivation of creative exploration spirit, the integration of aesthetics and technology, the construction of personalized feedback mechanisms, and data ethics education.

title: TFDS Analysis and Resolution Strategy of Image Problem Under Train Speed Change Condition
authors: Sha Wen, Qingmao Ren
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: In order to improve the image acquisition quality of TFDS based on linear array camera and laser light source technology, this paper analyzes the causes of image problems in the actual transportation state of train vehicles such as low speed, variable speed, parking and restart. In order to solve these problems. In this paper, the accuracy of obtaining vehicle speed is improved by special equipment, and the image processing algorithm is optimized. The overall improvement scheme and measures of the system improve the quality of TFDS image acquisition. In the experiment, it is proved that the scheme not only solves the problems of uneven exposure, poor image effect and poor anti-interference performance of the line scan camera by collecting and analyzing the images of the train rail side and rail bottom for many times under the condition of train speed change. It also ensures that no images of the vehicle are lost or misplaced, and finally uses image recognition technology to synthesize a complete image of the entire train of vehicles.

title: Research on the Deepseek Empowered PAD+BOPPPS Teaching Model: A Case Study of Ideological and Political Education at a University
authors: Qin Dong, Jiao Xue
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of Generative Artificial Intelligence (GAI) technologies, the limitations of traditional classrooms in fostering personalized learning and higher-order cognitive skills have become increasingly apparent. This study proposes a hybrid teaching model empowered by DeepSeek, integrating the structured framework of the BOPPPS model with the “Lecture-Assimilation-Discussion” mechanism of the PAD model, all underpinned by the Outcome-Based Education (OBE) philosophy. The model establishes a closed-loop instructional process encompassing pre-class, in-class, and post-class stages. Leveraging the capabilities of the DeepSeek large language model, it facilitates intelligent content generation, dynamic feedback, and learning analytics, thereby promoting a shift toward human-AI collaborative teaching. Empirical implementation in ideological and political education courses at a Chinese university demonstrates that this model significantly enhances student engagement and knowledge assimilation. The results offer a replicable and innovative paradigm for the digital transformation of higher education.

title: Digital Vocational Education Based on AI and Its Effectiveness Evaluation: Taking On-The-Job Internship Course as a Case Study
authors: Jinqiu Shu, Qingke Liao, Qun Gong, Xiaolong Zhang, Yu Zhou, Jiani Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Vocational education has entered a transformative 2.0 era, characterized by a shift towards digitalization in both education and knowledge acquisition. This evolution brings some challenges, such as addressing the diverse needs of students in learning environments. To overcome these challenges, this study advocates for the integration of Artificial Intelligence (AI) into vocational education systems. This paper reports a case study presenting the effects of AI on enhancing taking on-the-job internship courses. Experimental results demonstrate that AI + vocational education can identify and label emotional tendencies with an accuracy rate of approximately 98 %. By analyzing students' emotional changes during critical periods, such as job internships, educators can better understand their cognitive and emotional needs. This understanding enables the pro-vision of tailored ideological and political education, aligning with students' professional and personal development goals. Moreover, this approach not only advances the application of AI in digital management but also offers innovative methods for fostering data-driven talent development, ensuring vocational education remains responsive to industry and societal demands.

title: Model-Free Iterative Learning Control for Multi-UAV Systems
authors: Hengyi Guo
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: In response to the stability issues of trajectory tracking control for multiple unmanned aerial vehicle systems under weakly connected networks, communication delays, data packet loss, and deception attacks, this paper proposes a model free adaptive iterative learning control method using a data-driven compact format dynamic linearization (CFDL) framework and pseudo partial derivative (PPD) estimation technique. The conclusion is drawn that the system can achieve fast and consistent convergence, resist multiple attacks, and balance steady-state/transient performance even in sparse networks; The main contribution of this article is: 1) Pioneering a distributed trajectory tracking architecture based on PPD dynamic estimation, eliminating system modeling dependencies; 2) Design a controller with time-varying structural adaptability, breaking through the constraints of traditional methods on strongly connected graphs; 3) Build a dynamic compensation mechanism to synchronously solve the problems of deception attack defense and data packet loss; 4) Theoretical proof of BIBO stability and error exponential convergence of closed-loop systems under time-varying trajectories.

title: Research on Intelligent Configuration Optimization of Network Devices Based on Deep Reinforcement Learning
authors: Zhixiong Yu, Xin Jiang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: address the core challenges of network device configuration optimization, this paper proposes an intelligent configuration scheme based on Deep Q-Network (DQN) for deep reinforcement learning. It constructs a state space integrating network topology, device status and other information, and designs a discrete action space for multiple devices as well as a multi-objective reward function. Experimental verification based on eNSP and ns-3 simulators shows that the scheme significantly outperforms traditional methods in network throughput, latency, packet loss rate and configuration efficiency, effectively adapting to dynamic network environments and providing a new path for intelligent operation and maintenance of large-scale networks (up to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1 0 0 0}$</tex> devices).

title: Design of Status Monitoring and Anomaly Diagnosis for ECRH Superconducting Magnets
authors: Yuqing Zhang, Weiye Xu, Handong Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To enhance the operational reliability and safety of the Electron Cyclotron Resonance Heating (ECRH) superconducting magnet system within the EAST facility, this paper presents the design of a distributed state monitoring and anomaly diagnosis system. This system integrates various sensors and data acquisition modules (including NI-9221, NI-9230, NI-9226, etc.) to monitor in real-time the voltage, current, vibration, temperature, and continuity status of both the superconducting magnet power supplies and compressors. An event-driven software architecture is employed to achieve multi-task scheduling and fault response. The system demonstrates high-precision data acquisition (current error <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$< \pm 0.5 \%$</tex>, temperature deviation < <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\pm 0.5^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$</tex>), real-time fault diagnosis, and automatic protection functions. It supports remote configuration and unified management of multiple devices. Test results indicate that the system boasts a response latency of less than 200 ms, stable communication, and high control precision, effectively meeting the safety requirements for the superconducting magnet system operation in the ECRH system.

title: Investigating the Impact of AI-Powered Human-Computer Teamwork on Cognitive Learning Performance
authors: Xiuyu Lin, Haiying Cui, Yijing Zhu, Xiaosang Cai
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the rapid development of artificial intelligence technology, the application of intelligent systems in the field of education is becoming more and more widespread, and the human-computer collaborative learning mode is gradually becoming an important way to influence learning performance. The study aims to explore the factors affecting learning performance of human-computer collaboration supported by intelligent systems and analyze its specific mechanism of action through empirical research. Based on the research framework of human-computer intelligent collaboration, the study investigated the relationship between the three dimensions under the variable of human-computer collaboration (human experience, interaction context, and machine technology) and the variable of learning performance, and a total of 239 valid questionnaires were collected. Correlation analysis and path analysis showed that all three dimensions of human-computer collaboration had a significant effect on learning performance, with the facilitating effect of the dimension of human-computer collaboration's interaction context being particularly obvious. It is recommended that the support of learners' personalized learning tasks, environments and learning strategies in different interaction contexts be fully considered when designing human-computer collaborative learning. The study reveals the important role of intelligent systems in human-computer collaborative learning, provides a theoretical basis for educational practice, and points out the issues of technology adaptability and learner acceptance that need to be further explored in future research.

title: MobileNetV3 for Mobile Gaming: A Confidence-Based Analysis of Model Performance on Structured ImageNet Subsets
authors: Zixuan Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: In recent years, China's mobile gaming industry has demonstrated robust growth momentum, becoming an indispensable part of daily life. Against this backdrop, Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) technologies have been increasingly adopted in domestic mobile games since the mid-2010s, driven by enhanced computational capabilities of mobile devices and the widespread adoption of artificial intelligence. Leading companies such as Tencent and NetEase have achieved innovative breakthroughs in areas including NPC intelligence, AI-generated content (AIGC), gesture interaction, and anti-cheat systems. Nevertheless, this field continues to face technical challenges such as excessive model energy consumption and insufficient training data. The application of MobileNetV3 in the gaming industry has emerged as a new development direction. To investigate the performance of MobileNetV3 in relevant tasks, this study employs two 1,000 -sample subsets from ImageNet (Dataset A containing <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{1, 0 0 0}$</tex> classes with one image per class; Dataset B containing 200 classes with five images per class) for experimental analysis. The results indicate that MobileNetV3 exhibits high predictive confidence (average confidence score of 0.65) for datasets characterized by superior image quality and balanced class distribution.

title: Transformer-Based Multimodal Spatio-Temporal Fusion Neural Network for Pedestrian Intention Prediction
authors: Beixuan Lu, Shujuan Huang, Chao Shen, Feng Xiao, Wenjuan Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Accurately predicting pedestrian crossing intentions is crucial for urban traffic planning and management in order to maximize traffic signal control, upgrade pedestrian crossing infrastructure, and improve the pedestrian travel experience. In order to increase the accuracy of pedestrian behavior predictions and safeguard pedestrian crossing safety in the presence of human-vehicle interaction, this work attempts to build a pedestrian crossing intention prediction model based on multimodal data. The Transformer architecture, with its strong parallel processing and long-distance dependent capture capabilities, is fully utilized by the model to interpret complicated traffic scene data efficiently. In order to improve the model's characterization ability under multimodal action patterns and mine deep pedestrian behavioral aspects, the Transformer encoder module is added to encode multimodal data, including pedestrians' trajectory, velocity, acceleration, and so forth. Lastly, to achieve reliable pedestrian crossing intention prediction, the intention prediction module combines pedestrian action features with object interaction features. The automated driving dataset (JAAD dataset) is also used to validate the experimental model, and the findings indicate that the model is effective at simulating the intentions and behaviors of pedestrians.

title: Universal End-to-End Automated Evaluation Framework for Retrieval-Augmented Generation System
authors: Shen Li, Haochen Zhang, Yuejiao Wang, Meijuan Zhou, Zhong Ma, Feng Liang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has become one of the key functionalities in current large language model (LLM) applications. It effectively mitigates hallucination issues in LLMs and provides users with the latest domain-specific information by accessing knowledge bases, serving as a costefficient method for LLM capability migration. However, users primarily interact with LLMs through application systems like OpenWebUI, where RAG functionality is typically embedded as a “black box.” This makes large-scale automated evaluation challenging for existing RAG assessment systems. To address this, this paper proposes an universal end-to-end automated evaluation framework. This framework can evaluate the RAG functionality embedded within various LLM application systems and output quantitative scores. We conducted experimental validation on widely adopted LLM application systems including OpenWebUI, AnythingLLM, and RAGFlow. The results demonstrate that the framework can effectively evaluate RAG performance across different LLM application systems and their configurations.

title: GTWNet: A High-Accuracy and Lightweight Framework for Rice Pest Detection
authors: Shilei Deng, Gaowei Wu, Wensheng Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Rice is one of the most essential staple crops worldwide, with its yield and quality critical to global food security. However, rice is highly susceptible to pest infestations during its growth cycle, which severely impact both yield and quality. To address this, we propose GTWNet, an innovative lightweight framework designed to achieve high-precision pest detection while optimizing computational efficiency. Specifically, GTWNet integrates GhostConv and C3Ghost modules for efficient linear feature extraction, significantly accelerating both training and inference. The architecture incorporates a specialized Tiny-Neck structure to enhance the model's attention to key pest features and improve multi-scale feature fusion efficiency. Furthermore, the Wise-IoU loss function is introduced to optimize label prioritization by adaptively balancing gradients from mediumquality samples while suppressing low-quality outliers, thereby accelerating model convergence and improving detection robustness. Extensive experiments conducted on the self-built dataset IP13 (containing 13 major rice pests) demonstrate GTWNet's superiority over mainstream detectors, achieving state-of-the-art metrics of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 2. 4 \%}$</tex> precision <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(+\mathbf{4. 4 \%})$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 1. 4 \%}$</tex> recall <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(+\mathbf{1 \%})$</tex>, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7 4. 9 \%}$</tex> mAP@ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50(+1.5 \%)$</tex>, and 50.2 % mAP@ <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$50-95(+1.8 \%)$</tex>. Notably, GTWNet reduces parameters to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{7. 9 M}(-\mathbf{2 9. 3 \%})$</tex> and computational costs to 16.5 G FLOPs (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$-42.5 \%$</tex>), establishing an optimal accuracy-efficiency tradeoff for rice pest detection. Consequently, GTWNet delivers an accurate, lightweight solution for real-time rice pest identification, enabling reliable crop health monitoring and yield optimization.

title: Fairness-Aware Resource Allocation in Relay-Aided ISAC Networks via Adaptive Differential Evolution
authors: Ruitong Liang, Jiantao Yuan, Rui Yin, Simin Weng, Jieyu Du, Fambirai Takawira
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) is a key enabler for future wireless networks, where ubiquitous relay devices provide a cost-effective approach to enhance ISAC services. However, such relay-aided ISAC is confronted with challenges in ensuring fairness in quality of service (QoS). To address this issue, this paper first develops a unified sensing metric based on CraméRao lower bound (CRLB). Considering cascaded twohop architecture, we incorporate both the relay’s self-localization error and relative measurement error to holistically evaluate users’ sensing performance. Subsequently, a min-max fairness optimization problem is formulated to jointly allocate power and subcarriers. To solve this complex mixed-integer nonlinear programming (MINLP) problem, we propose an adaptive differential evolution (ADE) algorithm, featuring parameter adaptation and local search. Simulation results demonstrate that the fairness-aware resource allocation framework achieves equitable sensing QoS.

title: Smart Farm Productivity Monitoring Model Based on Task Offloading in Edge-Cloud Architecture
authors: Wenmei Zhang, Wenquan Jin, Ziyuan Lu, Yujing Wu, Yihu Xu, Yingxin Qi
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Smart farm productivity monitoring is pivotal for advancing agricultural intelligence, ensuring food security, and promoting sustainable development. To achieve the efficient operation and precise management of smart farms, traditional monitoring solutions, whether cloud-centric or standalone edgebased, suffer from inherent flaws such as excessive latency, inefficient bandwidth utilization, and unbalanced computing resource allocation, failing to meet the real-time and precise demands of modern smart farms. To address these challenges, this paper proposes a smart farm productivity monitoring model based on edge-cloud architecture and enhanced by task offloading. The proposed model leverages the high-performance computing and storage capabilities for performing intensive tasks including model training and global data analysis. Therefore, edge devices are enabled to provide real-time data collection, lightweight preprocessing and low-latency monitoring tasks through offloading prediction task to the network edges. The proposed edge-cloudbased model supports an efficient smart farm monitoring workflow that achieves accurate identification of farm productivity by delivering information to the cloud instead of large-size image data. Therefore, the monitoring is operated by edge layer to derive the information of farm environment. Furthermore, the delivered farm information is used for presenting the real-environment in the cloud layer.

title: DIGRD: Disguised Inertial Gait Recognition Dataset and Method
authors: Hao Li, Zhaoyang Wu, Wei Wu, Shichao Wu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Inertial gait recognition, renowned for its contactless operation and long-range identification capabilities, enables userfriendly and secure biometric authentication. However, the systematic evaluation of inertial gait recognition under clothing and footwear disguises, a critical factor in real-world security applications, has not been addressed. This paper introduces DIGRD (Disguised Inertial Gait Recognition Dataset), the first benchmark dataset explicitly designed to study disguised inertial gait recognition. Constructed in a controlled indoor building, DIGRD contains 61,346 gait events from 40 subjects across 4 clothing-footwear covariates. Experimental results demonstrate that both clothing and footwear changes significantly degrade recognition performance, with footwear alterations exerting a stronger deceptive effect <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$(84.85 \%$</tex> vs. <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$88.78 \%)$</tex>. This work establishes a benchmark for studying inertial gait recognition under disguise conditions and paves the way for developing more secure biometric authentication systems.

title: Research on Abnormal Behavior Detection and Risk Assessment Method of Big Data based on Graph Neural Network
authors: Haiqing Zhang, Wenxuan Hou, Zihao Yang, Zuyue Yang, Yukun Pan
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Aiming at the problem of difficulty in identifying abnormal behaviors and low risk assessment accuracy in big data environments, a detection and assessment method framework based on graph neural network (GNN) is proposed. This method achieves deep expression of node features and abnormal identification of local patterns by constructing a behavior interaction graph and integrating a multi-layer graph neural structure. In the model design, a graph embedding reconstruction mechanism and a risk scoring function are introduced, and a complete process that can be applied to actual systems is constructed by combining the edge-cloud collaborative deployment architecture. Taking credit card fraud detection data as the experimental object, the model outperforms the isolation forest and traditional rule-based methods in indicators such as AUC and F1 Score, showing higher detection accuracy and stability. The results show that this method has good generalization ability and practical value in processing largescale structured and dynamic behavior data, and provides an efficient and feasible technical path for risk warning in the fields of financial risk control and network security.

title: Channel Modeling for 6G Optical Wireless Terahertz Communication Based on Convolutional Neural Networks
authors: Youpeng Fang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The advent of the sixth-generation (6 G) wireless communication implies the radical methods to address the needs that are never higher in terms of data rates and low latency. Optical wireless terahertz communication based on the 6 G networks is an important technology that has a rich spectral capability and high transmission capacity. Nontheless, channel modeling is still a major problem because of the complicated nature of propagation of terahertz channels. The present paper presents a new channel model enveloped by Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), where the model develops an optical wireless terahertz system of communication and tests the model operation through 16 Quadrature Amplitude Modulation (16QAM). In contrast to classical statistical tools, our data-driven strategy trains channel features based on real-measures of data at a different level of optical power (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$10-14 \text{dBm}$</tex>). It is experimentally shown that the proposed CNN channel modeling algorithm has a minimum Bit Error Ratio (BER) of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1. 7 9} \times \text{1 0 - 2}$</tex> at a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{1 3}-\text{d B m}$</tex> optical power and this is lower than the soft-decision Forward Error Correction (SD-FEC) limit of <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{4. 3} \times \text{1 0 - 2}$</tex>. It is an improvement of about 23 percent over the traditional channel equalization methods that use Deep Neural Networks (DNNs), Long Short-Term Memory networks (LSTMs), and Gated Recurrent Units (GRUs). Also, the average calculation complexity was lessened by 35.9 per cent, which forms a strong basis to smart channel modelling in the coming generation of optical wireless communication.

title: Monitoring coastal processes in a macro-tidal dominated environment in the Pará Amazon over six decades: applications from computational intelligence and remote sensing
authors: Rafael Alexandre Alves Menezes, Kayque Da Silva Dias, Milena Marília N. de Andrade, Eder Mileno Silva de Paula
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Average rates of shoreline change are key indicators for assessing coastal evolution. The study area is located in Salinópolis, on the northeastern coast of Pará, Brazil, covering urban, estuarine, and natural zones, including beaches like Corvina, Maçarico, Farol Velho, and Atalaia. Between 1984 and 2024, despite an overall trend of shoreline accretion, areas with growing human occupation experienced significant coastal erosion, causing building retreat, partial home loss, and damage to beach access roads. Using the Digital Shoreline Analysis System and dense satellite image time series processed in Google Earth Engine, the coastline was analyzed in four sectors (A, B, C and D). The sector B with the greatest erosion showed an average linear retreat of -1076.31 m, while another sector reached a maximum advance of +1079.78 m, indicating a spatially alternating pattern between erosion and accretion. The average linear rate of change showed a slight overall shoreline retreat, accompanied by high morphodynamic variability and low statistical consistency in linear trends. Urbanized sectors exposed to ocean forces were most vulnerable to erosion, whereas estuarine and mangrove areas were more stable. The high sediment supply from estuaries contributed positively to shoreline accretion in several regions. Projections for 2034 and 2044 suggest the shoreline will mostly retain its current shape within uncertainty margins. However, sector C faces a potential pronounced retreat, threatening frontal dunes and coastal infrastructure. These findings emphasize the importance of strategic coastal management considering natural and human influences on shoreline dynamics.

title: Intelligent Evaluation and Implementation Technology of Post-Editing Quality for Machine Translation
authors: Ying Du, Cuicui Zhang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Post-editing quality assessment is based on multi-dimensional corpus features, combined with structured modeling and data-driven algorithms, to achieve a detailed depiction of the differences between the translation and the reference text. The research designs a ternary alignment system to track the translation modification path and semantic shift, and uses deep networks to explore the inherent laws of quality changes. In the process of feature fusion and parameter adjustment, a dynamic judgment mechanism is introduced to enhance the adaptability and stability of the system in complex contexts.

title: Multi and hyperspectral characterization of a RAMSAR water body based on an optical water type approach
authors: Sofίa Paná, Francisco Nemiña, Matίas Bonansea, Claudia Giardino, Mariano Bresciani, Nicola Ghirardi, Inés Del Valle Asίs, Anabella Ferral
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: This study presents an optical water type (OWT) classification approach to characterize the spatial and temporal variability of water quality in Mar Chiquita Lagoon, a large inland RAMSAR site in Argentina. Multispectral (Sentinel-2, Sentinel-3) and hyperspectral (PACE) satellite imagery were used to derive three spectral descriptors: apparent visible wavelength, normalized difference index, and trapezoidal area under the RGB bands. These metrics were used to classify each water pixel into predefined OWTs based on spectral similarity. The results highlight consistent spatial patterns across the sensors, with the central and southern lagoon dominated by moderately turbid, particle-rich waters, and more variable classes concentrated near northern tributary inflows. Temporal analysis using PACE revealed seasonal changes in OWTs, with one class becoming dominant during the spring-summer period. This multi-sensor framework demonstrates the effectiveness of spectral classification for monitoring optically complex inland waters. The findings underscore the value of satellite-based remote sensing for tracking water quality and delineating the spatial influence of tributaries in temporal and spectral dimensions.

title: MelVQGAN: An Enhanced Neural Codec with Fine-Grained Spectrogram Preservation for High-Fidelity Speech Generation
authors: Siyuan Zhou, Hongxia Bie
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Vector quantization-based neural codecs have shown great potential in generating high-quality speech. However, due to the complex distribution of the time-frequency representation of the Mel spectrogram, existing codecs struggle to fully retain the fine-grained spectral representations, resulting in limited speech reconstruction quality. To address this issue, we introduce a novel neural codec, MelVQGAN, specifically designed for the refined representation and reconstruction of Mel spectrogram. Based on the original VQGAN framework, MelVQGAN introduces a hierarchical window attention mechanism to precisely capture the non-uniform representation distribution of the Mel spectrogram in the time-frequency domain. In addition, we propose an innovative multi-resolution and multiperiod discriminator, tailored to effectively capture timefrequency periodic variations of the Mel spectrogram. Moreover, we comprehensively use the L1 loss and the spectral convergence loss to replace perceptual loss for images, achieving a dual optimization of the fitting fidelity of the fine-grained spectral representation of the decoded Mel spectrogram and the naturalness of the generated audio. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our proposed MelVQGAN has better Mel spectrogram reconstruction results and higher generated audio quality at the same bitrate.

title: RideCred: A Decentralized and Incentive-Driven Ride-Sharing System with Trustless Coordination
authors: Shailesh Kumar Sharma, Balaji Palanisamy, Shamik Sural, Sandip Chakraborty
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: Existing ride-sharing platforms are typically centralized, granting service providers full control over pricing, data ownership, and user participation, often resulting in opaque policies, high commission fees, and data privacy concerns. In this paper, we present RideCred, a decentralized, token-based ride-sharing system built on the Hyperledger Fabric (HLF) permissioned blockchain. RideCred promotes fairness and transparency by allowing users to earn non-monetary tokens for offering rides and spend them to request rides, thus incentivizing active and balanced participation. The ride-matching problem is modeled as a constrained optimization task aimed at maximizing the number of rider-driver matches while honoring seat availability and route deviation thresholds. Given its NP-completeness, we propose a scalable greedy heuristic that enables efficient ride allocation in realistic scenarios. Our solution is implemented as verifiable HLF chaincodes and integrated with Google Maps for intuitive, real-time geo-based interactions. Experimental evaluation on real-world road networks demonstrates that RideCred offers a practical, secure, and equitable alternative to traditional ride-sharing services.

title: Wired for Growth: Planning for Data Centers at Entergy
authors: Maryclaire Peterson, Katherine McCulla, Sharma Kolluri, Olumide Aluko
abstract: Maintaining transmission system reliability is becoming increasingly complex as utilities face unprecedented data center interconnection requests. These requests rival the scale of major generation projects, with individual sites exceeding thousands of megawatts and overall demand rising sharply. Proprietary designs, reliance on power electronic equipment, and uncertain dynamic behavior distinguish data centers from traditional industrial customers. This paper highlights the need for advanced modeling, early collaboration with developers, and updated planning practices to ensure reliable integration of these facilities into the grid.

title: Galaxy Image Reconstruction from Sparse Sampling Using a Stripformer-Based Deep Learning Network
authors: Zujuan Xu, Li Zhang, Yuxin Xia, Xinglong Wang, Ming Zhang, Long Xu, Zhibin Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: In radio interferometry imaging, the sparse sampling of the telescope array on the UV-plane results in a significant side lobe structure of the point spread function (PSF). The convolution of the real sky brightness and the PSF will introduce severe artifacts in the final imaging. In order to mitigate such artifacts and improve the quality of galaxy images, this paper explores the application of the Stripformer deep learning model to the reconstruction of galaxy images. The model innovatively uses the Strip Attention mechanism to re-weight the image features in the horizontal and vertical directions, effectively capture the long-distance directional dependence, so as to more accurately mitigate the artifacts caused by sparse sampling, and clearly restore the structure of the galaxy spiral arm and the fine details of the galaxy nucleus. The experiment in this paper uses simulated Galaxy Zoo galaxies observation data. The experimental results show that the average indexes of RMSE, PSNR and SSIM of the reconstructed image are 0.006,45.68 dB and 0.995, respectively, which proves the effectiveness of this method in galaxy structure recovery and artifacts suppression, providing a feasible technique for radio astronomy data processing.

title: An Algorithm for Identifying Main Waterways Based on DBSCAN and Trajectory Cluster Optimization
authors: Zongtang Zhang, Leilei Zhou, Yapin Yin
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: For high-density shipping areas, traditional trajectory clustering algorithms often perform poorly in waterway extraction and identification. To address this issue, we propose a main waterway identification algorithm based on DBSCAN and trajectory cluster optimization. Utilizing AIS data, we preprocess and extract trajectory features to segment and analyze the data. The density-based spatial clustering of application with noise(DBSCAN) algorithm is applied for initial classification of trajectories, followed by trajectory cluster optimization for further refinement, ultimately achieving main waterway identification. Experimental results demonstrate that this algorithm can effectively identify main waterways while removing interference waterways, outperforming traditional clustering algorithms and proving its effectiveness.

title: HeteroAgent: A Confidence-Driven Heterogeneous Multi-Agent Framework for Complexity-Aware Task Coordination
authors: Dailing Hong, Xue Li, Haorun Yang, Jiankun Cao, Yingyuan Huang, Jiaying Li, Xingzhi Zou
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Multi-agent systems (MAS) show strong potential in solving complex tasks, but still face challenges such as insufficient task complexity awareness, mismatched model capabilities, and high resource consumption. To address these issues, this paper proposes HeteroAgent, a confidence-driven heterogeneous multi-agent framework. The framework adopts a hierarchical design in which the BossAgent performs task parsing and feature construction, and models task complexity using a shallow neural network. By integrating an adaptive threshold mechanism based on load awareness and performance feedback, HeteroAgent dynamically routes tasks to ExpertAgents or the SeniorAgent, achieving a balance between accuracy and efficiency. Experiments on real KylinOS operational data show that HeteroAgent achieves 96.35% accuracy, while reducing execution time by 24.75%, API cost by 59.61%, and resource usage by 43.2%. The results demonstrate its advantages in accuracy, efficiency, and resource consumption. Overall, HeteroAgent improves multi-task collaboration and resource scheduling, providing a practical solution for multi-agent systems operating in complex and dynamic environments.

title: Algorithm Design for a Cross-Provincial and Cross-Regional Medium-to-Long-Term Electricity Trading Mechanism Adapted to New Energy Participation Based on a Consortium Blockchain
authors: Zhe Xu, Hao Chen, Yufang Chen, Hui Song, Xiaoting Huang, Wenjin Xie
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: To address issues such as high trust costs among multiple parties, data opacity, and low settlement efficiency in cross-provincial and cross-regional power transactions involving new energy participation, this paper designs a consortium blockchain-based smart contract algorithm. By codifying transaction terms to solidify rules and integrating off-chain data collection interfaces with IoT devices for real-time data acquisition, the algorithm establishes multi-source data consistency verification formulas to ensure authenticity. A dynamic transmission loss calculation model is proposed, supplemented by visualized optimization allocation logic, while incorporating settlement result verification and correction mechanisms to enhance accuracy. Simulation experiments demonstrate that this algorithm can shorten transaction settlement time and eliminate data tampering risks, provide technical support for the efficient and compliant conduct of cross-provincial and cross-regional power transactions involving new energy sources.

title: Global Knowledge Mapping of Artificial Intelligence-Enabled Self-Regulated Learning in the 21st Century
authors: Hanbing Xue, Chen Ni
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Artificial intelligence-enabled self-regulated learning represents an emerging interdisciplinary field that integrates computer science and educational psychology. To systematically examine the research status, hotspot topics, and developmental trends in this domain, this study employs bibliometric methods, utilizing CiteSpace and VOSviewer software to analyze 554 relevant publications from the Web of Science and Scopus databases spanning 2000-2025. The findings reveal: (1) Publication volume exhibits phased growth characteristics, with rapid expansion after 2021, reaching 135 articles in 2024, indicating a transition from marginal exploration to mainstream research focus; (2) Source journals demonstrate diversified characteristics, encompassing both educational technology and computer science journals, with educational journals showing more prominent academic impact; (3) The geographical distribution of research exhibits global characteristics, with China and the United States contributing the highest publication volumes, while countries such as the Netherlands, Germany, and Singapore demonstrate higher average citation rates, reflecting a divergence between publication volume and academic impact; (4) The field has established three major research clusters centered around Dragan Gašević, Roger Azevedo, and Inge Molenaar, focusing respectively on learning analytics, intelligent tutoring, and human-computer collaboration; (5) Research hotspots concentrate on three directions: intelligent learning support, data-driven analysis, and technology integration applications, constructing a comprehensive framework for the deep integration of AI and self-regulated learning; (6) The evolution of research frontiers has experienced three stages: technology foundation construction, theoretical integration development, and intelligent deep application, presenting a developmental logic from “technology adapting to education” to “technology reshaping education.” This study provides a systematic knowledge mapping of the field, offering valuable reference for promoting the deep integration of AI technology and education.

title: Volatility of ambient electromagnetic noise in view of measurement of EMI emissions of RES installations in lower than 30 MHz bandwidth
authors: Lauri Kütt, Taavi Salum, Martin Parker, Juhan Piilberg, Hendrik Keltman, Laur Valgur
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The rapid expansion of renewable energy sources (RES), particularly photovoltaic (PV) and wind power, has intensified the discussions of electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) assessment on electric installations. Power electronic converters (PECs), which form the core of these systems, are efficient but also significant emitters of electromagnetic interference (EMI), especially in the sub-30 MHz frequency range. Unlike conventional equipment that can be evaluated in shielded laboratory environments, large-scale RES installations must be tested in-situ, where the ambient electromagnetic background is remarkably intends but highly variable. Discussed in this paper is a case study of a 3 kW photovoltaic installation in view of reliability of EMI emission assessments below 30 MHz. The loop antenna measurements at 10 m distance capture spectra that are largely shaped by external contributions rather than the equipment itself. Comparison with conducted LISN measurements and time dependent ambient references highlights the risk of false attribution of emission origins. The findings underline the need for caution in interpreting in-situ results and call for refined methodologies to improve the robustness of RES compliance evaluation.

title: Contextual Trust Evaluation for Robust Coordination in Large Language Model Multi-Agent Systems
authors: Kangning Gao, Haotian Zhu, Rui Liu, Jinming Li, Xu Yan, Yi Hu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Large Language Model (LLM)-based multi-agent systems have emerged as a promising paradigm for tackling complex tasks that exceed individual agent capabilities. However, existing approaches often suffer from coordination inefficiencies, a lack of trust mechanisms, and suboptimal role assignment strategies. This paper presents a novel trust-aware coordination framework that enhances multi-agent collaboration through dynamic role assignment and context sharing. Our framework introduces a multi-dimensional trust evaluation mechanism that continuously assesses agent reliability based on performance history, interaction quality, and behavioral consistency. The coordinator leverages these trust scores to dynamically assign roles and orchestrate agent interactions while maintaining a shared context repository for transparent information exchange. We evaluate our framework across eight diverse task scenarios with varying complexity levels, demonstrating significant improvements over baseline approaches. Experimental results show that our trust-aware framework achieves a 87.4% task success rate, reducing execution time by 36.3% compared to non-trust-based methods, while maintaining 43.2% lower communication overhead. The framework's ability to adapt agent roles based on evolving trust scores enables more efficient resource utilization and robust fault tolerance in dynamic multiagent environments.

title: Towards Sustainable Energy Communities: Improving Self-Consumption and Reducing Grid Reliance by Community Battery Storage
authors: Kristjan Peterson, Noman Shabbir, Lauri Kütt, Victor Astapov, Kamran Daniel, Argo Rosin
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The increasing deployment of residential photovoltaic (PV) systems provides environmental and financial benefits but also creates challenges related to grid reliance during peak demand and low-generation periods. Community battery storage (CBS) has emerged as a promising solution to enhance self-consumption, improve self-sufficiency, and reduce dependence on the main grid. This paper evaluates the technical and economic performance of CBS in a suburban distribution feeder with 15 households, each equipped with different PV capacities. One year of measured load and PV data, combined with hourly Nord Pool electricity prices, is used to assess PV utilization, grid reliance, and annual energy costs. Results show that CBS enables households to meet demand locally for the majority of the year, reducing reliance on the grid by $20-35 \%$ and cutting annual energy costs by around $\mathbf{4 0}-\mathbf{5 5 \%}$. These findings confirm that CBS effectively shifts midday PV surpluses to evening peaks, thereby enhancing community selfsufficiency and delivering substantial financial benefits. By linking real operational data with market-based analysis, this study highlights the potential of CBS as a practical and scalable solution for distribution system operators, energy communities, and policymakers seeking to accelerate the transition to sustainable, low-carbon energy systems.

title: Improved Hyperdimensional Computing Strategies for Epileptic Seizure Detection
authors: Zhen Zhang, Zhifa Jiang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Epileptic seizure detection plays a pivotal role in improving the quality of life for epilepsy patients. However, the dearth of practical EEG-based signal detection tools for usual use is a significant challenge, which necessitates the development or enhancement of existing detection methods for epileptic seizures. Hyperdimensional computing, a category of neuromorphic computing methodologies, has recently been employed in various signal processing tasks due to its unique characteristics. Nevertheless, in seizure tasks, the efficacy of standard hyperdimensional computing falls short of other sophisticated algorithms, especially in imbalance datasets. This study replicates the standard hyperdimensional computing method and its subsequent key improvement studies, introducing four enhanced hyperdimensional computing methods following a strategic combination. These methods are refined under identical preconditions and evaluated against the same benchmarks as advanced machine learning methods. The results demonstrate that one of the enhanced iterative compressed Multi-Class hyperdimensional computing methods can achieve a median performance value of 94.3% on an unbalanced CHB-MIT dataset which simulates a real seizure scenario. The above result surpasses the random forest method used as a benchmark. This research underscores the potential of hyperdimensional computing in epileptic seizure detection, setting the stage for future investigations and practical implementations.

title: The Precise Portrayal of Multi-Dimensional Students' Growth Trajectories in the Informatization Construction of Colleges and Universities
authors: Qiuqiong Hu, Wenxuan Hou, Zihao Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: In the process of informatization construction in colleges and universities, technologies such as network security data analysis and mining, big data security and privacy protection, threat intelligence text mining, natural language processing and mobile big data computing are integrated, and machine learning algorithms are introduced to construct a model for characterizing students' growth trajectories. Taking five universities as research samples, 100,000 student data covering dimensions such as academic performance, behavior, and social interaction were collected. After parsing the text information through natural language processing and integrating multi-source data through mobile big data computing, the data was input into the model for training and analysis. The results show that the prediction accuracy of academic trajectory (grade trend) based on LSTM reaches 89.2%, and the recognition accuracy of behavioral trajectory (activity scenarios) based on random forest has increased to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$91.5\%$</tex>. This effectively helps colleges and universities accurately understand the development trend of students, provides data support for the formulation of personalized education strategies, and promotes the in-depth synergy between the informatization construction of colleges and universities and the growth of students.

title: Research on Anti-Fading Mechanism and Communication Capacity Optimisation of Frequency Diversity in Sea Surface Evaporation Duct Channels
authors: Zhufang Yu, Long Chen, Yu Zhang, Long Zhang, Mi Lin
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: This paper investigates the optimization of communication capacity and anti-fading mechanisms in sea surface evaporation duct channels using frequency diversity. While evaporation ducts facilitate over-the-horizon transmission, they introduce severe signal fading and multipath interference due to the non-uniform refractive index profiles. By employing the Parabolic Equation (PE) method, we analyze the path loss characteristics and demonstrate that electromagnetic waves exhibit distinct, frequency-dependent spatial distributions of deep fading. To mitigate this, we propose a frequency diversity scheme. Simulation results at a 100 km range with a 14.2 m antenna height reveal that an $8-10 \mathrm{GHz}$ dual-frequency diversity system increases the 10% outage capacity from 0.0089 Mbps (for a 10 GHz single-frequency system) to 3.12 Mbps. These findings confirm that frequency diversity significantly outperforms single-frequency approaches in enhancing link stability and capacity within complex evaporation duct environments.

title: Parametric Solution of Forward Error Correction Codes Based on Submatrix Elimination
authors: Jiyuan Tan, Xingxuan Liu, Shihong Xing, Jiachen Cui
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: To address the current drawback of poor parameter identification fault tolerance in linear block cipher synchronization scrambling under error conditions in underwater acoustic communication, a parameter determination method for linear block ciphers based on sub-coefficient matrix elimination is proposed. First, based on the characteristics of frame scrambling information, the linear block cipher scrambling undergoes modulo-2 addition operations. The newly constructed sequence eliminates the scrambling effect. Subsequently, based on the sub-coefficient matrix elimination method, the decision threshold for the suspected dual vector is derived. By traversing the code length of the linear block code, the dual vector is solved. Simulation results demonstrate that the method exhibits excellent error tolerance, effectively identifying the dual vector and code length, thereby laying the foundation for subsequent scrambling recognition.

title: Document-Level Cyber Threat Intelligence Relation Extraction via Relation Directed Graph
authors: Rui Wang, Yu Yan, Jiaoli Liu, Po Hu, Jianyi Liu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: How to dig out high-quality entity relationships from the fragmented and massive threat intelligence data is the focus of cyber threat intelligence analysis. Most of the existing research is limited to sentence-level extraction, which is difficult to describe the global threat graph across documents and events in complex attacks. In this paper, we propose a document-level threat intelligence relation extraction model based on relation directed graph. Aiming at the problem of complex coreference relations in document-level extraction, a co-reference feature enhancement method based on contrastive learning was proposed to capture coreference semantic knowledge, and a co-reference enhanced heterogeneous graph was explicitly constructed to aggregate the relations contained in the mention graph. This paper proposed a multi-hop relation reasoning method based on relation directed graph to solve the problem of poor cross-sentence and cross-segment relation reasoning ability. The proposed method achieves 80.78% precision, 61.49% recall and 69.83% F1 score on the CTI document-level entity relation extraction dataset, which are better than the existing models.

title: A Lightweight Network for Intelligent Typhoon Detection and Center Localization
authors: Jingchang Li, Xiuqing Hu, Shaolin Wang, Feng Sang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Real-time monitoring and accurate center localization of tropical cyclones are crucial for forecasting and disaster mitigation, yet many state-of-the-art models are too computationally demanding for satellite-borne embedded processors. We propose TYLNet, a lightweight framework for typhoon detection and center localization on FY-4A AGRI(Advanced Geostationary Radiation Imager) multi-spectral imagery. TYLNet adopts an asymmetric encoder-decoder with Squeeze-and-Excitation (SE) blocks to enhance discriminative features while keeping the model compact. Moreover, we introduce an implicit detection scheme that uses the peak value of the predicted heatmap as a confidence score, enabling detection and localization without an additional classification head. Experiments on an NVIDIA AGX Orin platform show that TYLNet runs at 80.81 FPS (FP32) and achieves a mean absolute error of 25.24 km, offering a favorable accuracy-efficiency trade-off for on-board real-time processing.

title: A Hybrid Deep Reinforcement-Supervised Learning Framework for Energy Management of Fuel Cell-Battery Hybrid Vehicles
authors: Duo Yang, Haoran Lv, Yu Yan, Mince Li, Rui Pan, Jing Liang
abstract: Hydrogen fuel cell vehicles typically integrate energy storage components like lithium batteries to enhance dynamic response and support regenerative braking. The energy management strategy (EMS) plays a crucial role in optimizing power distribution among power components. Although deep reinforcement learning (DRL) offers strong adaptability and optimization capabilities for EMS, its training process often demands extensive data, computational resources, and careful hyperparameter tuning, posing challenges in stability and safety. To address these limitations, this paper proposes a hierarchically structured knowledge-transfer framework that synergistically fuses offline expert guidance with online reinforcement autonomy. First, a supervised behavior cloning procedure extracts optimal power distribution policies from dynamic programming trajectories, achieving hydrogen consumption minimization through direct policy imitation. A deep neural network (DNN) is then trained to replicate these rules, providing a pre-trained model for online EMSs. Subsequently, a twin-delayed deep deterministic policy gradient (TD3)-based EMS is developed for real-time power allocation, incorporating hydrogen consumption, fuel cell degradation, and battery state-of-charge fluctuations into the reward function. Finally, the pre-trained DNN initializes the TD3 actor network, effectively improving training efficiency and strategy security. The results demonstrate that the proposed framework accelerates convergence, improves system performance, and ensures safer control strategies compared to conventional DRL-based methods.

title: IRS-Aided Secure Sensing for Surveillance Area Coverage: Framework and Algorithm Design
authors: Ziheng Zhang, Qingqing Wu, Wen Chen, Yanze Zhu, Ziyuan Zheng, Ying Gao, Qiong Wu
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: This paper proposes a novel IRS-aided framework for secure sensing, which aims to minimize the worst-case Cram´er-Rao Bound (WC-CRB) within an entire surveillance area by optimizing the IRS reflecting beamforming, enabling reliable and secure localization of arbitrary and unknown targets. Specifically, we first establish a general IRS-aided localization coverage model and derive the closed-form expression for the CRB of an arbitrary point, which reveals the relationship between the localization error bound and the Fisher information of the angle of arrival (AOA), angle of departure (AOD) and delay. To solve this challenging min-max optimization problem, we design efficient algorithms for different area types. For sector area, we first represent the Fisher information as trigonometric polynomials, then construct the WC-CRB coverage constraint as a non-negativity problem of these polynomials, and finally approximate it as an efficiently solvable semidefinite program (SDP). For the more challenging case of arbitrarily shaped area, we propose a two-tiered solution comprising a low-complexity heuristic algorithm based on geometric approximation and a high-performance detailed design that accurately solves the problem by decomposing the irregular boundary into multiple continuous segments. Numerical simulations validate the superiority of the proposed framework, demonstrating that our designs significantly outperform various benchmark schemes in terms of robustness and performance uniformity. The results show that the framework not only effectively reduces the WC-CRB but also achieves a highly uniform performance coverage across the entire area, providing a reliable and efficient solution for practical localization security applications.

title: HTCNet: Hierarchical Point-Graph Tooth Point Cloud Completion with Image Assistance
authors: Fucheng Niu, Hui Liu, Mengqi Liang, Jiaqi Chen, Zekuan Yu, Lin Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: With the growing prominence of digital dentistry, high-quality and cost-effective three-dimensional (3D) intra-oral scanned (IOS) tooth data has become essential in various dental applications. However, limited by sensor resolution and occlusion, the acquired tooth point clouds often suffer from sparsity and incompleteness, resulting in missing regions and insufficient 3D detail. In this paper, we propose a Hierarchical Tooth Completion Network(HTCNet), a novel image-assistance framework to integrate geometry and structure learning from 2D images and 3D point clouds for 3D tooth completion. It employs a dual-stream-based hierarchical feature extraction, utilizing a 2D stream for extracting image features and a 3D stream for processing point clouds. Additionally, we introduce 3DTeethSegX for evaluating image-assistance tooth completion to address the lack of consideration for the comprehensive situation in the current dataset. Extensive experiments on two tooth completion datasets demonstrate the superiority and robustness of HTCNet, showcasing its potential to generate high-resolution 3D tooth data from low-resolution, cost-effective sensors with the assistance of 2D images. To the best of our knowledge, it is the first to use image-assistance techniques to significantly improve the accuracy and effectiveness of 3D tooth completion. Source code will be available at: https://github.com/labiip/HTCNet.
