title: Compact Slotline Bandpass Filter With Wide Upper Stopband Without Spoof Surface Plasmon Polariton Gradient Transitions
authors: Weibin Cai, Weiwen Li, Chunhui Zhu, Daquan Yu, Longfang Ye
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: In this work, a compact bandpass filter based on slotline bent spoof surface plasmon polariton (SSPP) units loaded with metal patches is proposed. Through systematic analysis of the unit’s electric field distribution and impedance characteristics, it is found that the metal patch can adjust the equivalent dielectric constant of the entire unit, thereby modifying the unit’s impedance to achieve impedance matching. By loading SSPP units with slow wave effects into the microstrip-to-slotline transition structure, the size of the transition structure is successfully reduced. Combining the microstrip-to-slotline structure with the slotline bent SSPP unit loaded with a metal patch, a compact bandpass filter is designed. The filter eliminates the need for an SSPP gradient transition structure, with upper and lower cutoff frequencies independently controlled by the SSPP structure and the microstrip-to-slotline transition structure during design. To further improve out-of-band suppression, an SSPP low- pass structure is loaded onto the filter, leveraging its stopband effect to enhance the upper stopband suppression. Finally, the filter was fabricated and experimentally measured. The measured results demonstrate that the filter exhibits a passband from 2.4 to 9.0GHz with a fractional bandwidth (FBW) of 115.8%. The <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\vert $</tex-math> </inline-formula>S<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{21}\vert $</tex-math> </inline-formula> is higher than −0.83dB and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\vert $</tex-math> </inline-formula>S<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{11}\vert $</tex-math> </inline-formula> within the passband is lower than −15.0dB, and <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\vert $</tex-math> </inline-formula>S<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{21}\vert $</tex-math> </inline-formula> in the range of 9.8–19.9GHz remains below −30dB. The filter exhibits excellent passband response and out-of-band suppression, with an overall compact size of only <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$0.475~\lambda _{g} \times 0.298~\lambda _{g}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>. It holds significant application potential in microwave circuit systems such as microwave communication base stations, radar systems, and satellite communication terminals.

title: Effects of Termination Impedance and Cell Topology on the Linearity of Gallium Nitride 180° High-Pass/Low-Pass Phase Shifters
authors: Patrick Ettore Longhi, Chiara Ramella, Sergio Colangeli, Walter Ciccognani, Antonio Serino, Marco Pirola, Ernesto Limiti
publish: IEEE Transactions on Microwave Theory and Techniques
abstract: Phase shifters (PSs) are fundamental components of phased-array antennas, but they can also be a major contributor to intermodulation distortion. This article investigates how the internal impedance and cell topology of gallium nitride (GaN) 180° PSs affect their linearity. A theoretical analysis, supported by a simplified, yet accurate electrical model of the series FET in the switch and by measured data, demonstrates that selecting an internal impedance other than <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$50~\boldsymbol {\Omega }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> can significantly improve the input-referred third-order intercept point. In addition, the influence of the PS cell topology (T versus <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\Pi }$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) on linearity is examined. To validate the theoretical results, a comparative experimental analysis is carried out, considering both internal impedance and topology. To this aim, we designed and characterized three demonstrator PS cells. Two demonstrators employ the standard 50-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\Omega }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> impedance but are implemented using a T topology and a <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\Pi }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> topology, respectively. A third demonstrator uses instead a 120-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\Omega }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> internal impedance, together with a matching network (MN), to prove the analyzed relationship between internal impedance and switch-induced distortion. All demonstrator cells were designed for operation at 10 GHz and manufactured in a 0.12-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\mu $</tex-math> </inline-formula>m gate-length GaN HEMT MMIC process. As expected, the best linearity performance is exhibited by the 120-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$\boldsymbol {\Omega }$</tex-math> </inline-formula> PS, achieving an IIP3 of +47 dBm.

title: Reducing Cross-Pod Communication Overhead for MoE Model Training with Hybrid Parallelism in Multi-Tenant Clusters_supp1-3668417.pdf
authors: Shuangwu Chen
abstract: The massive parameter scale of sparsely-activated Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models necessitates distributed training with hybrid parallelism. Placing such training tasks, i.e. mapping the logical partitions of an MoE model to available physical NPUs, is challenging. Due to the bandwidth and latency discrepancies between intra- and inter- Pods, the cross-Pod communication usually becomes a bottleneck. The high dispersion of NPUs in multi-tenant clusters exacerbates this issue further. However, a paucity of studies has considered the cross-Pod model placement problem. To address this challenge, we propose a novel model placement scheme tailored for MoE model training with hybrid parallelism in multi-tenant clusters. By quantifying the cross-Pod communication overhead incurred during MoE model training, the model placement is formulated as a 0-1 integer quadratic problem, which is NP hard. Motivated by the traffic difference between different parallelism, we decompose this problem into two subproblems. To solve the subproblems, we propose a lightweight two-stage algorithm based on Best-Fit strategy and neighborhood search. Experiments under different models and network topologies show that our model placement scheme can reduce cross-Pod traffic by 35.9% and cut communication time by 18.7% compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: CMFC-MOT: Contextual Multi-Modal Fusion with Centroid-Aware Query for 3D Multi-Object Tracking
authors: Xiaoxue Ai, Hui Li, Xiangzhen Kong, Qinghua Lin, Wentao Chen, Tongao Ge
publish: IEEE Transactions on Cognitive and Developmental Systems
abstract: 3D multi-object tracking is a core perception task for autonomous driving, yet it remains challenging in complex scenarios such as occlusion and dense interactions. Existing methods do not fully exploit the complementary cues of LiDAR geometry and image semantics, and cross-modal interactions are often coarse-grained, leading to insufficient object feature representations. Moreover, BEV representations can suffer from detail loss, which hinders stable object perception. In the data association stage, cost modeling is typically simplistic and fails to leverage inter-object differences, thereby limiting overall tracking robustness. To address these challenges, we propose a cognition-inspired framework, termed contextual multi-modal fusion with centroid-aware query for 3D multi-object tracking. It enhances the representation of key target features and improves association stability. First, we design a contextual aggregation with centroid-aware query module. It reconstructs centroid voxels and guides context aggregation. This achieves fine-grained cross-modal feature interaction and enhances the mining of multimodal information. Subsequently, we introduce a multi-modal adaptive hierarchical fusion module. It employs a dual-domain attention mechanism across spatial and channel dimensions to emphasize salient target features. This improves discriminative performance in complex scenes. Finally, we employ the cascaded confidence-aware multi-cost association module to construct a more discriminative association matrix. Based on target quality, we select the optimal association strategy to reduce mismatches and enhance tracking robustness in occlusion and dense scenes. On the KITTI test dataset, our method achieves 81.04% HOTA and 89.46% MOTA on the Car category, demonstrating superior tracking performance.

title: Impact of Temperature on Partial Discharge in Generator Stator Windings
authors: Shin Nakamura, Takashi Kuraishi, Masaki Kanegami, Satoru Miyazaki
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: Partial discharge (PD) diagnosis is widely used to assess the insulation condition of generator stator windings during operation. However, the influence of winding temperature on PD behavior remains insufficiently understood. This study investigates the temperature dependence of PD characteristics in the epoxy/mica-insulated stator windings of hydrogenerators. To elucidate this dependence, PD measurements are performed on two operating hydrogenerators and three types of coil bars at sample temperatures ranging from approximately 25 °C to 90 °C. The results show that the phase-resolved PD patterns of void discharges are unaffected by sample temperature. By contrast, the largest repeatedly occurring PD magnitude (Q<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</sub>) increases or decreases with the temperature of the sample, depending on the degree of insulation degradation. A strong correlation is observed between the temperature dependence of Q<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</sub> and the breakdown voltage of the coil bars. Based on these findings, this study provides a proof-of- concept diagnostic approach that utilizes the temperature dependence of Q<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">m</sub>.

title:
Low-Loss and Ultra-Wideband SAW Filters Using LiNbO
                    <sub>3</sub>
                    /Si Structure
authors: Qiufeng Xu, Junyao Shen, Shouren Xie, Quhuan Shen, Feng Pan
abstract: Wideband devices are increasingly needed with the development of wireless communication. To simplify the structures of wideband SAW devices and further explore the bandwidth limit, this work researches on ultra-wideband SAW devices using simplified bilayer structures. <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$X$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-cut LiNbO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/Si structure is proposed and resonators with different propagation directions are simulated. <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$X$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$175^{\circ }Y$</tex-math> </inline-formula> LiNbO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/Si is the optimized design when strengthening the main mode and suppressing the spurious mode are well balanced. SAW resonators with different thicknesses of LiNbO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub> and Cu electrodes are theoretically investigated, and resonators with different wavelengths are fabricated. Results show that the electromechanical coupling factor of the resonators can reach about 29% and the admittance ratio can be larger than 70dB. Meanwhile, the spurious mode is sufficiently suppressed. Ultra-wideband and low-loss SAW filters are designed and fabricated. An ultra large bandwidth of 31.92% is achieved. Atotally flat passband and a minimum insertion loss of 0.99dB prove that the filters have high performances but not only a large bandwidth. This work further extends the bandwidth limit of SAW devices and reveals the application possibility of simplified bilayer structure <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$X$</tex-math> </inline-formula>-<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$175^{\circ }Y$</tex-math> </inline-formula> LiNbO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>/Si, effectively and economically developing the technology of wideband SAW devices. [2025-0244]

title: Decoupled Multimodal Fusion Network Based on Peripheral Physiological Signals
authors: Tianqi Fan, Sen Qiu, Yongzhen Wang, Zhelong Wang, Hongyu Zhao, Junhan Jiang, Junnan Xu, Tao Sun, Fuze Tian
publish: IEEE Transactions on Computational Social Systems
abstract: Multimodal peripheral physiological signal fusion for emotion recognition seeks to perceive or recognize human emotions using peripheral modalities such as electromyography, electrodermal activity, and respiratory wave. Previous approaches to multimodal fusion primarily focus on emotion-sensitive signals such as electroencephalogram (EEG), often overlooking the potential value of peripheral physiological signals in emotion recognition. Moreover, the inherent heterogeneity among different modalities continues to pose challenges to fusion quality. In this article, we propose a multimodal decoupled multimodal fusion (DMF) to address these issues, enabling flexible feature decoupling, cross-modal feature interaction, and relational knowledge learning. Specifically, each modality’s representation is first decoupled into two components: common features and modality-specific features; second, the DMF employs progressive cross attention to facilitate the exchange of modality-specific features across different modalities; and finally, it uses relational knowledge to learn multimodal spliced features, embedding both inter-modal and intra-modal feature relationships. DMF offers a dynamic multimodal emotion recognition framework that leverages the emotional information contained in diverse modalities. Experimental results demonstrate that the DMF method consistently outperforms previous approaches and provides a viable solution for multimodal peripheral physiological signal fusion.

title: Powering Reliability, Engineering Resilience: Industry Transformation [Guest Editorial]
authors: Marianna Vaiman, Farrokh Aminifar, Qiuhua Huang, Bo Chen

title:
Relaxation Process of Polypropylene and Polyolefin Elastomer Composites with Al
                    <sub>2</sub>
                    O
                    <sub>3</sub>

                    <sub>2</sub>
                    Nanoparticles
authors: Xuening Wang, Zeqin Liu, Xuanhe Liu, Rujia Men, Wanting Jiang, Vladislav Popovtsev, Yuanyuan Li, Zhipeng Lei
publish: IEEE Transactions on Dielectrics and Electrical Insulation
abstract: In order to obtain polypropylene-based cable insulation materials with excellent dielectric properties, polypropylene composites with different Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>@SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> contents and elastomers with fixed mass fractions are prepared. The change of the complex permittivity of polypropylene composites with frequency and temperature by the addition of core-shell nanoparticles is studied by frequency domain dielectric spectroscopy at multiple temperatures. To characterize the α and β relaxation processes, the triple Havriliak-Negami (HN) function model based on conductivity contribution is used to fit the dielectric spectrum curve, and the characteristic parameters such as dielectric relaxation intensity and relaxation time constant are obtained. Then, the Kirkwood–Fröhlich equation and Arrhenius' temperature-dependent law are used to analyze the dipole relaxation and activation energies reflected in the β relaxation process. The results show that the addition of Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>@SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> has an inhibitory effect on the local segment movement of polypropylene at high temperature, and increases the activation energy of β relaxation. The higher the content of nanoparticles, the more obvious the inhibitory effect. The activation energy of of 1wt% Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>@SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> is 0.219 eV, while that of the non-addition is only polypropylene composites 0.184 eV. However, filling a small amount of Al<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub>O<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sub>@SiO<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sub> in polypropylene composites hardly has any effect on a relaxation.

title: Historical Overview of Public Distance Education in Brazil: 1996-present
authors: Raquel Machado Leite, Jonas SzutkoskI, Raquel Salcedo Gomes, Alba Valéria de Sant'Anna de Freitas Loiola, Andreia dos Santos Sachete, Rodrigo Ghirardelli
abstract: None

title: Domain Adaptation for Speaker Verification Using Optimal Transport with Pseudo Label
authors: Wenhao Yang, Jianguo Wei, Wenhuan Lu, Lei Li, Xugang Lu
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Domain gap often degrades the performance of speaker verification (SV) systems when the statistical distributions of training data and real-world test speech are mismatched. Channel variation is a primary factor causing this gap, including bandwidth changes, background noise and encoding, etc. Although various domain adaptation algorithms could be applied to handle this domain gap problem, most algorithms could not take the complex distribution structure in domain alignment with discriminative learning. In this paper, we propose a novel unsupervised domain adaptation method for speaker verification, i.e., Joint Partial Optimal Transport with Pseudo Label (JPOT-PL), to alleviate the domain mismatch problem. Leveraging the geometric-aware distance metric of optimal transport in distribution alignment and speaker consistency in speech distribution, we further design a pseudo label-based discriminative learning where the pseudo label can be regarded as a new type of speaker label derived from the optimal coupling. With the JPOT-PL, we carry out experiments on the SV channel and lingual domain adaptation with VoxCeleb, LibriSpeech, CNCeleb, and AISHELL-2. Experiments show our method reduces EER by up to 30% compared with several state-of-the-art domain adaptation algorithms.

title: Achieving Privacy-Preserving and High-Accuracy Collection of Key-Value Data With Local Differential Privacy
authors: Junpeng Zhang, Hui Zhu, Jiaqi Zhao, Mengqian Li, Shuang Zhang, Bin Xie, Hui Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: In the context of the Internet of Things (IoT), the large-scale generation and collection of data can greatly improve the quality of service provided, but they also raise significant concerns about privacy breaches. However, existing privacy-preserving data collection solutions based on local differential privacy (LDP) often struggle to balance security and accuracy when handling composite data types. To address this challenge, in this paper, we propose CSKV, a high-precision and privacy-preserving key-value data collection scheme. Specifically, we first design a padding and sampling protocol to improve data utility. Then, we propose two randomized response mechanisms to safely perturb keys and values in a cohesive and segmented manner. After that, by leveraging the sampling protocol and key-value correlation perturbation, we demonstrate that CSKV can provide secondary privacy amplification. Detailed theoretical analysis verifies the security and effectiveness of CSKV. In addition, extensive performance evaluations are conducted on synthetic and real-world datasets, and the results indicate that our proposed scheme outperforms existing schemes in terms of hit rate and estimation variance.

title: Preface

title: A Case Study on the Factors Influencing College Students' Perception of Using Large Language Model Tools
authors: Xieling Chen, Yuexin Zhou, Xingquan Peng
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the rapid development of generative artificial intelligence, large language model (LLM) tools, such as ChatGPT, are increasingly integrated into higher education, significantly shaping how university students learn and interact with knowledge. This study focuses on undergraduates majoring in Educational Technology at Guangzhou University and employs a framework based on the Technology Acceptance Model (TAM). The model includes four key variables: perceived usefulness, perceived ease of use, perceived risk, and behavioral/cognitive factors. A mixed-methods approach, combining a structured questionnaire and thematic analysis, was used to examine the mechanisms influencing students' usage intentions and subjective experiences. Quantitative results indicate that behavioral and cognitive engagement, along with perceived usefulness, significantly and positively affect students' intention to use LLMs. This suggests that students with more experience are more likely to recognize the benefits of LLMs and continue using them. Perceived risk has a moderate negative influence, while perceived ease of use does not show a significant predictive effect. The qualitative analysis further reveals that students acknowledge the positive roles of LLMs in enhancing learning efficiency, supporting academic tasks, and expanding knowledge. However, they also express concerns about academic integrity, information reliability, technological dependence, and lack of emotional understanding. This study extends the theoretical application of the TAM in the context of educational AI tools and provides practical insights for guiding the responsible and effective implementation of LLMs in higher education.

title: The Impact of AI Courses on Pre-Service Teachers' Perception of AI Competency
authors: Xiaotian Han, Yue Yang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: UNESCO's sustainable development agenda underscores the critical need to equip pre-service teachers with artificial intelligence (AI) competencies, thereby enabling future generations to attain the global sustainable goal of quality education. This study aims to evaluate the perceptions concerning the AI competencies of pre-service teachers and investigates the impact of AI courses on the AI competency of pre-service teachers. A total of 279 pre-service teachers participated in the survey. Some participants took AI courses, covering topics from foundational AI principles to interdisciplinary applications. Employing a quantitative research design, the study assessed participants' AI competencies, which were evaluated across six domains: AI awareness, basic AI knowledge, basic AI skills, problem-solving, AI teaching practice, and ethics and safety. These results show that the overall AI competency stood at a moderate level. Pre-service teachers have increased awareness of AI and ethics while they exhibit lower in problem-solving, basic AI skills, and AI teaching practice. Secondly, the greater the number of AI courses taken, the more significant is the improvement in AI competencies. Based on these findings, it can be determined that the role of well-structured AI courses enhancing the development of pre-service teachers' AI-related capabilities is mostly supported. Further research is needed to determine the long-term effect of these AI training on classroom practices and teaching effectiveness.

title: Implementation Method of Deep Semantic Representation and Scoring Algorithm Model for English Composition Correction
authors: Yuanyuan Huo
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: As the frequency of revision for English composition submissions gradually increases, the traditional manual scoring can no longer meet the requirements of high efficiency and accuracy. This paper explores an automated composition correction method based on deep semantic representation and scoring algorithm models. Through methods such as dynamic word vectors, dependency syntactic analysis, and topic modeling, the deep semantics of English compositions are interpreted. Moreover, based on the evaluation algorithms in deep learning, the features, paragraphs, and even sentences of the compositions are accurately evaluated and located. Through the real-time feedback system, students can obtain targeted revision suggestions. The experimental results show that the proposed scoring model has significant improvements in parameters such as MAE and MSE compared with the benchmark model. The MAE is 0.38, the MSE is 0.27, the correlation coefficient is 0.91, and the accuracy rate reaches 86.5%. These results show that the proposed model has obvious advantages in improving the efficiency and accuracy of English composition correction.

title: Activation Desensitization Based on Dynamic Noise Injection in Split Learning
authors: Jiasheng Gu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Activation desensitization, where the goal is to encrypt the activation values to prevent the client from accessing the user's raw data, has been a critical aspect of ensuring data privacy in split learning. Several approaches have been developed to address the problem. However, unlike existing methods which are either cumbersome in process or have weak encryption capabilities, we use a more lightweight approach. In this paper, we propose a novel activation desensitization by integrating lowrank matrices with dynamic noise injection. This new method consists of two important stages: a low-rank noise multiplication phase that filters out some sensitive information while preserving features that are key to the classification task, and a dynamic noise injection phase that add random perturbations to the compressed features to disrupt the recognizability of the remaining contours. We also compare our method with nonencryption and homomorphic encryption from three aspects: privacy protection, prediction loss, and prediction accuracy. The Experimental results confirm that our approach can achieve the effect of enhanced encryption while preserving the accuracy.

title: Towards Precise Growth: An Empirical Study on Digital Classroom Observation Tools Empowering Novice Teachers' Reflective Practice
authors: Mengtao Yang, Yu Wang, Dao Liu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This mixed-methods study explores how CSMS, a data-driven classroom-analysis tool, enhances novice geography teachers' reflection. Two beginning teachers teaching “Urban Internal Spatial Structure” were tracked via CSMS metrics and reflective journals. CSMS precisely diagnosed deficits in classroom structure (excessive teacher talk, weak student agency), interaction design (superficial exchanges, few student questions), and higher-order thinking (limited critical reasoning). Post-intervention, reflections expanded from 2-3 superficial aspects to four or more key domains, shifting from vague impressions to evidence-based analyses linking outcomes to quantified behaviours. Findings highlight CSMS's capacity to convert reflection into proactive, evidence-informed professional growth for early-career geography educators.

title: Research on the Construction of Digital Collaborative Learning Space from the Perspective of Social Interaction Theory
authors: Jie Gao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This article focuses on the current pain points of digital learning is insufficient in depth and the rising dropout rate, revealing that the root cause lies in the fact that the longdistance knowledge transmission mode full of loneliness is difficult to meet the needs of socialized learning. Based on the perspective of social interaction theory, the research constructed an embodied collaborative support framework and innovatively designed an immersive collaborative learning space with embodied experience. Through empirical data verification, this space effectively improves the quality of knowledge co-creation for learners and enhances the emotional connection in the collaboration process. This result not only provides a theoretical paradigm for the development of educational technology in the intelligent era, but also opens up a practical path for constructing a new type of learning space that combines knowledge production and emotional immersion.

title: Problem-Based Learning Practice in Higher Education in Latvia: Perspective from STEM Students
authors: Inese Lūsēna-Ezera, Andrejs Stupāns, Pāvels Maksimkins, Mihails Gorobecs
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Problem-Based Learning (PBL) is becoming widely used in STEM education in Europe, yet it still remains underexplored topic in Latvia. The study examines student perception of PBL approach in Latvian higher education institutions. The data is based on a survey from 74 STEM students from four universities. Participants are from various levels of higher education, including bachelor’s, master’s, and doctoral studies. Results show moderate integration of PBL in Latvia, with most presence in course initiation and implementation stages, and least in assessment stage. Differences were observed between different study years, where first-year students reported significantly lower satisfaction than other groups. The study also explores the experience of other European countries with implementing PBL in STEM education. Findings highlight the need for more consistency and support with integration of PBL approach in Latvia.

title: An Optimized PP-HGNetV2-Enhanced Lightweight YOLOv11 for Robust Object Detection in VisDrone UAV Scenes
authors: Zhongpu Xu, Gang Ma, Rui Gao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the challenges of high computational cost, poor detection accuracy for small objects, and complex background interference encountered by YOLOv11 in unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) object detection scenarios, this paper proposes a lightweight YOLOv11 architecture enhanced with an optimized PP-HGNetV2 backbone. First, the original YOLOv11 backbone is replaced with PP-HGNetV2, whose HG-Blocks are redesigned using lightweight convolutions (GhostConv, RepConv, DWConv, and LightConv) to effectively reduce parameters and computational complexity while preserving feature extraction capability. Second, tailored modifications to the neck and head modules are introduced to better accommodate the dense distribution and large scale variation of small objects in the VisDrone dataset. Extensive experiments conducted on the VisDrone-2021 benchmark demonstrate that the proposed method reduces GFLOPs by 42.3 % and parameters by 38.7 %, while improving mAP@0.5 by 1.8 percentage points compared with the original YOLOv11. Particularly, the detection performance on small objects such as pedestrians and electric vehicles improves significantly. Overall, the proposed model achieves a favorable trade-off between accuracy and lightweight design, offering an effective solution for real-time UAV-based object detection.

title: Research on a Dual-Model Framework for Hierarchical Probability and Quantitative Prediction based on Logistic Regression Algorithm
authors: Yihuang Peng, Ren Huang, Jingbo Fu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Predicting hierarchical outcomes in competitive scenarios is a critical challenge in statistical modeling. This study proposes a dual-model framework combining logistic regression and boxplot analysis to address binary classification problems, which include hierarchical probability and quantitative prediction. The Hierarchical Probability and Quantitative Prediction (HPQP) model integrates six features, including historical performance and event interactions, to achieve hierarchical probability aggregation and L2-regularized feature engineering. Additionally, the Probability and Event Correlation (PEC) model utilizes boxplot analysis to quantify the interplay between event participation breadth and success likelihood. Validated on empirical datasets, the hybrid framework demonstrates ideal accuracy in binary classification and count prediction. The study also provides an in-depth analysis of prediction errors, identifying the impact of outlier performance and variable interactions. Key innovations include a modular framework for hierarchical prediction and interpretable parameter optimization.

title: Ultrasound-Based Thyroid Nodule Classification Using GoogleNet and Xception Ensemble with CNN-LSTM Hybridization
authors: Smitirekha Behuria, Sujata Swain
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: The early and accurate classification is obligatory as the prognosis and effective treatment of one of the most frequent endocrine cancer, the thyroid cancer, is possible. This study proposes a deep learning technique to classify thyroid cancer with Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Long short-term memory (LSTM) networks, GoogleNet, Xception, and similar hybrid models. CNNs extracted features in our ultrasound images and LSTMs have been used to learn the location correlations to improve the classification performance. The introduced model is pre-trained and tested based on a curated dataset, with the help of preprocessing methods, including image augmentation and normalization, toward a more robust approach. Measures of performance lie in analyzing the performance measure in order to verify the effectiveness of the model. Results from experiments show that our method performs better than conventional machine learning models, obtaining better generalization and high classification accuracy. This study contributes to the advancement of automated diagnostic systems, offering a potential tool for assisting radiologists in detecting and classifying thyroid nodules with greater reliability.

title: A Novel Hybrid Prediction Model of Stock Closing Price Based on CEEMDAN-SE-VMD and ARIMA-CNN-BiLSTM-Attention
authors: Peiyao Wang, Qiang Dai, Zhongying Qiu, Xinyu Deng
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the development of the world, the stock market plays an important role in the national economy. Investors are still paying attention to effective strategies which can increase investment returns and reduce risks. The stock market time series data is dynamic, non-stationary and complex, so predicting the stock closing price is a big challenging task. Therefore, this paper proposes a CEEMDAN-SE-VMD and ARIMA-CNN-BiLSTM-attention novel hybrid prediction model. Firstly, the time series data are decomposed into IMFs through CEEMDAN. The sample entropy is used to statistically classify each component, dividing it into high-frequency and low-frequency components. Secondly, for high-frequency components, the VMD is combined to decompose further and extract refined features, and the CNN-BiLSTM-Attention model is used for prediction. For low-frequency components, the ARIMA model is used for prediction. Finally, the final stock closing price prediction value is obtained by integrating the results. Compared to some classic prediction models in this area, this paper's experimental results have shown that the new hybrid prediction model exhibits excellent performance in evaluation indicato rs such as RMSE, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{R}^{2}$</tex>, MAE, MAPE, and 1-WMAPE, reaching 11 12.9924, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.9876,838.5309,1.2040$</tex> and 0.9882 respectively, and significantly improves the accuracy of stock closing price prediction. This method can offer investors a fresh perspective.

title: GC-YOLO: An Improved YOLOv10n for Small-Object Detection in UAV Aerial Imagery
authors: Liyang Tang, Qishen Li, Hua Huang, Qiufeng Li, Xiong Ying
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the low detection accuracy caused by the prevalent small object scales and highly dense distributions in UAV aerial imagery, this paper proposes an improved YOLOv10n-based small-object detection algorithm for aerial-view scenarios, termed GC-YOLO. In terms of network design, we develop a Global Path Aggregation Feature Pyramid Network (GPAFPN), which strengthens information interaction among multi-scale features and effectively enriches spatial localization cues and fine-grained details for small objects. In addition, we introduce a ConvFormer module to construct C2f-ConvFormer, which enables more effective deep feature extraction and enhances the representation of fine details. Experimental results on the VisDrone2019-DET dataset show that GC-YOLO achieves 38.5% mAP50 and 22.5% mAP50-95, delivering substantially higher detection accuracy than existing mainstream methods. Compared with the baseline YOLOv10n, GC-YOLO improves mAP50 and mAP50-95 by 4.0% and 2.7%, respectively. These results demonstrate the effectiveness and practical utility of the proposed method for small-object detection in UAV aerial-view settings.

title: Beyond Standard English: Jamaican Experiences with Voice-Enabled AI Technologies
authors: Howard Reid, Glenville McLeod
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Collaboration and Internet Computing (CIC)
abstract: This paper explores the experience and trust of voice-enabled artificial intelligence (AI) technology with Jamaican users, paying close attention to the intersection of dialect recognition and trust. Drawing on data from a national survey conducted by the Sir Arthur Lewis Institute of Social and Economic Studies (SALISES) at the University of the West Indies, Mona, the study analyzes the responses of individuals who reported both awareness of AI and experience with voice technologies such as Siri, Alexa, and ChatGPT. While many respondents expressed concerns that AI tools fail to accurately recognize their dialect, this experience did not strongly correlate with reduced trust or lower adoption rates. That disconnect raises important questions about user expectations, adaptation, and the normalization of linguistic bias within digital systems. Descriptive statistics and recoding techniques were used to identify patterns across variables like education, trust, and AI interaction.The findings suggest a complex interplay between experience and trust, one that warrants closer investigation in contexts where language is a key barrier to inclusion. This paper puts forward the argument that persistence of voice AI use, despite ongoing issues with dialect misrecognition, highlights not just a technical shortfall, but also a social dynamic shaped by possible necessity, familiarity, and broader digital optimism.

title: Computer-Aided Numerical Simulation Study on Stress Variation of Intercalated Gangue in Composite Coal Seams
authors: Zaijun Xue, Tiangui Xie, Zhen Wang, Yude Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Under the action of multi-source stress, the intercalated gangue structure of composite coal seams exhibits typical irregular reaction characteristics, and traditional analytical methods are difficult to accurately predict its internal stress transmission and failure characteristics. A three-dimensional model including geological conditions, physical property conditions and boundary action stress is established by using computer-aided numerical simulation technology, and combined with intelligent algorithms, structural identification, path evolution simulation and time series stress prediction are realized. The research established an integrated framework of visualization and parallel simulation, significantly enhancing the accuracy of stress analysis in complex coal seams and the efficiency of parallel computing, providing support for the stability evaluation and risk prediction of structures under deep conditions.

title: Turbidity estimation in Paraná River middle basin using remote sensing techniques
authors: Víctor Gauto, Enid Utgés, Daniela Tenev, Anabella Ferral, Osvaldo Cardozo, Mauricio Acosta, Víctor Gómez, Vera Geneyer, Matías Bonansea
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Combining sampling campaigns with satellite spectral data a single band algorithm was developed to retrieve water turbidity, in Paraná River middle basin, North-East Argentina. Water samples were collected and characterized by in situ and laboratory physicochemical measurements. ACOLITE software was used to atmospherically correct Sentinel-2 products, obtaining spectral water surface reflectance. Several models were trained and tested, calculating performance metrics as the selection criteria. The best model was a linear expression between turbidity and spectral band at 704 nm, both transformed by natural logarithm. Turbidity maps followed the same sediment gradient across Paraná River, with high values in the West shore and low presence in the East side. A turbidity regression model can be used to describe water quality, a relevant parameter for water potabilization.

title: A Hybrid Simulated Annealing Genetic Algorithm for BI-Objective Optimization
authors: Wenchao Fang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the development of the economy, enterprises are paying more and more attention to sustainable development. Sustainable development of enterprises requires them to actively undertake corporate social responsibility while obtaining economic benefits. Therefore, achieving the bi-objective optimization of corporate social responsibility and economic benefits has become a current research hotspot. This paper applies the hybrid simulated annealing genetic algorithm to optimize the bi-objective of corporate social responsibility and economic benefits. Corresponding computation results are obtained through numerical simulations and analyzed. The research results show that compared with the standard genetic algorithm, the optimal solution obtained by the hybrid simulated annealing genetic algorithm is better. On this basis, this paper explores the impact of two different cooling strategies, an aggressive-to-conservative cooling schedule strategy and a conservative-to-aggressive cooling schedule strategy, on algorithm solving performance. Simulation results show that the convergence speed of the optimal solution obtained by using the aggressive-to-conservative cooling schedule strategy is faster, but the optimal solution obtained by using the conservative-to-aggressive cooling schedule strategy is better.

title: Snake Optimization Algorithm Based on Dynamic Reverse Learning Strategy and Subtraction Average Optimizer Strategy
authors: Zhikai Zhuo, Hao Wang, Xin Fan
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: The Snake Optimization (SO) algorithm has been recognized as a potent tool for solving a wide range of engineering optimization challenges. However, the conventional mechanisms employed by existing SO for searching and updating often fall short in efficiently locating the optimal solutions. To overcome these shortcomings, this study introduces a novel Snake Optimization Algorithm based on Dynamic Reverse Learning and Enhanced Subtraction Average Optimizer (RSSO). This enhanced algorithm integrates a reverse learning strategy based on chaotic mapping, and an improved subtraction averaging optimizer to refine the optimization process. To ascertain the efficacy of the RSSO, comparative experiments were conducted against ten benchmark optimization algorithms across CEC2005 test set. The results from these experiments clearly demonstrate that RSSO significantly surpasses the comparative algorithms in terms of convergence speed and accuracy.

title: A Novel Task Allocation Method for UAVs to Maximize the Number of Assigned Tasks
authors: Jihua Xu
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: The inherent flexibility of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) combined with their line-of-sight (LoS) communication capabilities has established them as an effective solution for emergency service provision in numerous practical applications. This paper proposes a LEPI algorithm, developed through systematic enhancements to the conventional PI algorithm. The proposed LEPI algorithm comprises three distinct operational phases: 1) task inclusion phase, 2) conflict removal phase, and 3) task reorganization phase, which explicitly incorporates critical constraints including task deadlines and UAVs' limited flight endurance. Comprehensive numerical simulations demonstrate that the implemented LEPI algorithm significantly improves task allocation efficiency, achieving an increase in successfully assigned tasks compared to baseline methods under equivalent operational conditions.

title: Design and Implementation of a General Process Tracking Middleware
authors: Ning Long, Jiguang Liu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Process tracking, the bedrock of production automation, ultimately dictates how mature and efficient the entire automated system can become. For years, such systems have been built to order: although tailor-made solutions meet immediate needs, they are inherently rigid, hard to extend, painful to upgrade and expensive to maintain–quickly becoming a straitjacket when production lines or business models change.To break this deadlock, we mined a broad sample of real-world tracking systems, distilled their common DNA into a generic E-R model and delivered it as a plug-in-based middleware platform. By decoupling business logic from the underlying engine and enabling modules to be snapped in or swapped out on demand, the system offers a standardized, highly adaptive software backbone for smart manufacturing.

title: Shuffle Attention Enhanced YOLOv11n for Cotton Weed Detection
authors: Ao Li
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Weed infestations pose a serious threat to cotton production by competing with crops for essential resources, leading to substantial yield losses. While precision spraying robots offer a promising solution for targeted herbicide application, accurate weed detection remains a critical challenge. In this work, we propose YOLOv11n-SA, a Shuffle Attention-enhanced version of YOLOv11n, specifically designed for detecting common weeds in cotton fields. By integrating the Shuffle Attention module into the backbone network, the model improves feature representation and detection performance while incurring minimal increase in network complexity. Experimental results demonstrate that YOLOv11n-SA outperforms the baseline YOLOv11n, achieving a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP} {@} 0.5$</tex> of 81.0%, compared to 79.1% for the original model, highlighting its effectiveness for weed identification in agricultural scenarios.

title: Advances in Generative AI for Visualizing Classical Chinese Poetry: Prompt Design and Multimodal Evaluation
authors: Hung-Cheng Chen, Kiu-Kit Tung, Huai-Chih Hsu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study examines how generative AI visualizes classical Chinese poetry through systematic prompt engineering, analyzing three representative works across various poetic styles. For Li Bai's “The Difficult Road to Shu,” we developed baseline prompts using classical translations, mood-enhanced prompts with parameter adjustments, and style-transfer prompts leveraging ChatGPT's text reinterpretation, demonstrating AI's capacity for diverse artistic expression. With Li Qingzhao's “A Dreamy Chant,” we refined prompt design strategies through verse-by-verse visualization, emphasizing scene construction, cultural element integration, and compositional control to capture the subtle emotional atmosphere of graceful Song-dynasty lyrics. The study's most significant contribution stems from Su Shi's “Ode to the Red Cliff,” where we established a multimodal evaluation framework that integrates assessments from literature experts, painting specialists, and ChatGPT. This framework reveals profound cognitive divergences: ChatGPT prioritizes technical metrics and stylistic completeness, literary experts emphasize emotional depth and philosophical contemplation, while painting specialists focus on traditional technique authenticity. These findings illuminate the subjective nature of art evaluation and validate an iterative “creation-evaluationoptimization” model for human-AI collaboration. By demonstrating how large language models can facilitate complex knowledge analysis through strategies like knowledge prompting and chain-of-thought prompting, this research provides a viable framework for integrating AI with literature and art education, transforming abstract literary concepts into visual experiences while advancing multimodal teaching methodologies.

title: SSINA: An In-Network Aggregation Architecture with Pattern-Adaptive Congestion Control
authors: Zhi Li, Fan Yang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: In-Network Aggregation (INA) significantly accelerates distributed machine learning training but faces severe performance bottlenecks due to coarse-grained congestion control and heterogeneous traffic interference in modern data centers. Traditional mechanisms rely heavily on Explicit Congestion Notification (ECN), which often triggers unnecessary collective rate reductions and fails to distinguish between transient microbursts and persistent elephant flows. To address these challenges, this paper proposes Super-Spine In-Network Aggregation (SSINA), a converged architecture that couples a scalable Super-Spine topology with a Pattern-Adaptive Congestion Controller (PACC). Leveraging P4 programmable switches, PACC implements a subRTT perception engine that identifies congestion patterns using slope detection and dual-threshold logic. We introduce a differentiated response mechanism: a “Reroute-without-Slowdown” strategy for microbursts and malignant flows by offloading traffic to the Super-Spine layer, and standard rate regulation for benign persistent congestion. Furthermore, a Dynamic Checkpoint Mechanism (DCM) is designed to ensure stable path recovery and prevent routing oscillations. We evaluate SSINA on the NS3 platform, demonstrating its superior resilience to network congestion. Results show that SSINA reduces training communication time by $\mathbf{3 0. 2 \% - 5 5. 3 \%}$ compared to SwitchML and Ring AllReduce. Furthermore, it enhances network fairness by shortening the Flow Completion Time (FCT) of background short flows by approximately $2 \times$ under high loads.

title: Temporal Constrained Retrieval of Soil Dielectric Constant Using CyGNSS Data: Validation with In-Situ Measurements
authors: Javier Arellana, Mariano Franco, Francisco Grings
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: In this work, we present a time-constrained approach for the estimation of soil dielectric permittivity ($\varepsilon$) in agricultural fields in central Argentina. The method combines a two-layer scattering model (based on the Fresnel approximation, which accounts for vegetation attenuation and surface roughness) within a Bayesian inference scheme. To constrain the retrieval, we introduce a semi-empirical prior distribution for $\varepsilon$ on day $t+1$, based on its value on day t and derived from in-situ measurements. This distribution automatically incorporates the asymmetrical dynamics of soil permittivity. Validation against ground measurements shows that the proposed method is able to reproduce short-term temporal trends of soil moisture, while remaining robust even after strong perturbations in the reflectivity data. Nevertheless, systematic differences with in-situ values are observed, which can be attributed to scale mismatches, model simplifications, and occasional data anomalies. Additional filtering of CyGNSS observations is shown to improve the consistency of the retrievals. Overall, the results demonstrate the potential of integrating GNSS-R data into soil monitoring applications, while also highlighting the need for further refinement and the combination with complementary datasets such as polarimetric SAR.

title: Blockchain-based Points System Smart Elderly Care Financial Model: Theoretical Architecture, System Design, and Performance Evaluation
authors: Yan Zhuang, Yixuan Du
publish: Proceedings of the International Conference on Digital Economy and Information Technology

title: Practical Limits of Ionic Magnetophoresis for Rare-Earth Separation
authors: Petru Andrei
abstract: Magnetic separation has been proposed as an attractive alternative to the current chemistry-based purification processes of rare-earths because it is envisioned to be much more environmentally friendly. To evaluate its practical viability, we present a physics-based assessment of the magnetic separation of rare-earth elements (REE). Our approach is based on a standard quantum-statistical model for paramagnetic ions combined with transport estimates and magnetostatic simulations of permanent magnets, superconducting coils, and high-gradient magnetic separation (HGMS) matrices. When these methods are applied to REE ions, we find the level of enhancement and the separation time are impractical even for the highest fields (over 30 T) and most magnetic ions (Dy<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3+</sup> and Ho<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3+</sup>). In terms of time, HGMS provides the best-case scenario, but even here the degree of enrichment remains well below the sub-percent range. These realities dictate that for magnetic separation to be useful in REE processing it must be pursued in hybrid strategies that also involve controlled clustering, selective adsorption, or other chemical and physical processes.

title: Descriptor: ViewPoints Network Graph Structure Analysis Dataset (VPN)
authors: Guangxu Mei, Hongchang Zhang, Xueshen Li, Shijun Liu, Li Pan, Sheharyar Khan
abstract: This article presents the ViewPoints Network (VPN) dataset, a fine-grained academic graph dataset designed to support the analysis of viewpoint-level structures within scientific literature. Existing academic graph datasets mainly operate at the paper or author level and therefore capture only coarse relational patterns such as citations or co-authorship, leaving the semantic evolution of scientific ideas insufficiently represented. The VPN dataset addresses this gap by extracting 1,474,680 viewpoints from publication abstracts and encoding them as individual nodes with high-dimensional sentence embeddings. Similarity-based edges are constructed using multiple thresholds, enabling flexible formation of viewpoint graphs tailored to different analytical needs. The dataset spans eight research domains in computer science and provides structured metadata, embedding files, and multi-threshold adjacency matrices to facilitate diverse graph learning and semantic analysis tasks. All data are processed through a transparent pipeline that includes cleaning, viewpoint extraction using large language models, embedding generation, and similarity computation. By offering a more granular representation of academic discourse, the VPN dataset supports research on viewpoint clustering, semantic interaction, idea evolution, and fine-grained network analysis across scientific domains.

title: Leveraging Transformer Networks for Enhanced Detection of Hardware Trojans via Power Side-Channel Analysis
authors: Ayman Elshenawy, Khalil M. Abdelnaby, Abdurrahman A. Nasr, Mohamed Zaki
abstract: The proliferation of Hardware Trojans (HTs) poses a critical threat to the reliability and security of integrated circuits (ICs), particularly in safety and mission-critical domains. Conventional detection approaches either rely on destructive imaging or trusted golden chips, both of which are costly and limited in scalability. This study introduces a golden-chip-free, non-destructive framework for Hardware Trojan Detection (HTD) based on transformer architecture applied to Power Side-Channel (PSC) signals. The research contribution presents the first systematic performance comparison between decoder-only (GPT-like) and encoder-only (BERT-like) models, as well as full encoder–decoder transformer models, for time-series PSC analysis. To adapt these models to the hardware security domain, side-channel traces are normalized, reduced via principal component analysis, and embedded with positional encodings to preserve temporal structure. The framework is evaluated on the publicly available Trojan Power & EM Side-Channel dataset using 10-fold cross-validation. Results show that GPT-based and BERT-based models achieve 66.32% and 79.41% accuracy, respectively. While the full transformer network significantly outperforms them with 92.43% accuracy, 92.44% precision, 92.43% recall, and an F1-score of 92.31%. These findings demonstrate the potential of attention-based architectures to capture subtle variations in PSC signals without requiring a golden reference chip. The study contributes a reproducible and comparative benchmark for transformer-based HTD, offering a scalable pathway toward more robust and interpretable hardware security solutions.

title: A 6-Gsps/lane MIPI C-PHY Receiver with Glitch Noise-Free Clock Recovery Circuit
authors: Dong-Seob Shin, Jong-Ho Park, Jaeho Bae, Young-Chan Jang
abstract: A mobile industry processor interface (MIPI) C-PHY receiver is proposed that includes a clock recovery circuit to eliminate glitch noise generated during the process of recovering the clock through edge-combining three signals with three voltage levels received from the MIPI C-PHY. The proposed clock recovery circuit removes glitch noise from the recovered clock by adaptively controlling the delay of the pulse generator based on the results of sampling even and odd data on both edges of the recovered clock. The proposed MIPI C-PHY receiver is implemented in a 65 nm CMOS process with a 1.2 V supply voltage and occupies an active area of 0.105 mm². It supports 6 Gsps per lane, recovers a 3 GHz clock, and outputs even and odd data at 3 Gsps using the recovered clock. The measured peak-to-peak time jitter and RMS time jitter of the recovered 3 GHz clock are 20 ps and 2.4 ps, respectively. The 6 Gsps MIPI C-PHY receiver supports an effective bandwidth of 13.68 Gbps, achieving an energy efficiency of 1.64 pJ/bit.

title: Biomimetic Visual Perception Network for Industrial Image Anomaly Detection
authors: Siang Tang, Hua Yang, Jiankui Chen, Zhouping Yin
publish: IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering
abstract: Anomaly detection in the industrial manufacturing process is important for controlling product quality. In real-world scenarios, anomalies manifest in a number of diverse and unforeseen ways and can be categorized into two main types: structural anomalies, such as scratches and breakages, and logical anomalies, such as missing components and dislocations. Current mainstream methods tend to focus on knowledge generalization but are inadequate for detailed and logical detection tasks. To address this issue, this study proposes Biomimetic Visual Perception Network (BVPN), a biomimetic design-based novel paradigm that simulates biological vision and human logical discrimination with detailed distillation dependencies and semantic component information. BVPN introduces three convolution-based networks to extract information from different regions, imitating biological central, para-central, and peripheral vision, and facilitates the integration of disparate information through the process of knowledge distillation. On the basis, BVPN develops two modules based on the student-teacher network: Focus Module, for local detailed detection, and Peripheral Module, for global context detection. Moreover, BVPN proposes the Logical Module, a clustering-based segmentation branch, assessing logical anomalies by the number and color information of segmentation components. Through a fused structure highly aligning with the perception mode of human system, BVPN achieves excellent performance in the detection of structural and logical anomalies. Extensive experiments with mainstream anomaly detection datasets and real-world inkjet printing dataset demonstrate that BVPN outperforms state-of-the-art competitors in accuracy.

title: Enhanced Displacement Sensing via Selective Excitation of Higher-Order Modes in a Cylindrical Dielectric Resonator
authors: Premsai Regalla, Mohsen Koohestani
abstract: This paper presents the systematic design and characterization of a cylindrical dielectric resonator (CDR)-based displacement sensor operating under dual excitation modes. To begin with, the CDR is investigated through radar cross-section analysis to identify the natural resonant modes. Each mode is subsequently excited by using a simple microstrip feed line to evaluate its suitability for displacement sensing in terms of resonant frequency, sensitivity, and dynamic range. Numerical simulations identify the TE<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">011+δ</sub> and HEM<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">12δ</sub> modes are found to exhibit superior sensing capabilities compared to all other supported modes. Further, a transmission-mode sensor configuration is then proposed, wherein both modes are simultaneously excited using a pair of 180° aligned offset microstrip lines integrated within the same CDR structure, allowing to compare the sensitivity performance of the higher-order mode with respect to the fundamental mode. This dual-mode excitation enables the sensor to harness the complementary features of both modes simultaneously. Comprehensive full-wave simulations are carried out in HFSS to validate the concept, and a prototype is fabricated and experimentally characterized. The measured results show good agreement with simulations, confirming that the proposed sensor achieves a nearly threefold improvement in sensitivity with the higher-order mode, though at the expense of reduced dynamic range.

title: Protecting consumer healthcare imaging data from AI-tampering via blind reversible watermarking
authors: Wei Min, Abdelkader Laouid, Mohammad Hammoudeh, Brahim Ferik, Farag Azzedin
abstract: Medical images are increasingly processed across consumer-facing healthcare pathways—teleradiology portals, mobile viewers, MIoT and edge gateways. These images are vulnerable to tampering, privacy abuse, and AI-driven manipulation, such as GAN forgeries and adversarial perturbations, that can mislead diagnosis. We propose a blind, reversible watermarking framework that employs a rotation-invariant biometric fingerprint while preserving diagnostic fidelity through exact reconstruction. The approach generates an Orientation-Guided BSIF (OG-BSIF) signature, protects it with a lightweight FlexenTech permutation, and embeds it in the integer wavelet domain using an adaptive spread-spectrum strategy that combines STDM for robustness with Local Histogram Shifting (LHS) for bit-exact recovery. Experiments on multi-modal datasets, including CT, MRI, ultrasound, and X-ray, achieve high imperceptibility, with average PSNR > 54 dB, SSIM ≈ 0.997, and perfect reversibility, with a reconstruction PSNR of 90.28 dB. OG-BSIF preserves > 99.9% of features under severe rotation, enabling reliable authentication, and the framework remains robust under FGSM and PGD perturbations, with subsecond execution supporting real-time deployment in consumer and point-of-care settings.

title: Hybrid Downlink Beamforming with Outage Constraints under Imperfect CSI using Model-Driven Deep Learning
authors: Lukas Schynol, Marius Pesavento
publish: IEEE Open Journal of the Communications Society
abstract: We consider energy-efficient multi-user hybrid downlink beamforming (BF) and power allocation under imperfect channel state information (CSI) and probabilistic outage constraints. In this domain, classical optimization methods resort to computationally costly conic optimization problems. Meanwhile, generic deep network (DN) architectures lack interpretability and require large training data sets to generalize well. In this paper, we therefore propose a lightweight model-aided deep learning architecture based on a greedy selection algorithm for analog beam codewords. The architecture relies on an instance-adaptive augmentation of the signal model to estimate the impact of the CSI error. To learn the DN parameters, we derive a novel and efficient implicit representation of the nested constrained BF problem and prove sufficient conditions for the existence of the corresponding gradient. In the loss function, we utilize an annealing-based approximation of the outage compared to conventional quantile-based loss terms. This approximation adaptively anneals towards the exact probabilistic constraint depending on the current level of quality of service (QoS) violation. Simulations validate that the proposed DN can achieve the nominal outage level under CSI error due to channel estimation and channel compression, while allocating less power than benchmarks. Thereby, a single trained model generalizes to different numbers of users, QoS requirements and levels of CSI quality. We further show that the adaptive annealing-based loss function can accelerate the training and yield a better power-outage trade-off.

title: IEEE Transactions on Industrial Informatics Information for Authors
authors: N/A

title: Rotor Position Fault Detection Design for PMSM Drive System with Incremental Encoder
authors: Pengfei Tao, Xiaoyuan Zhu
publish: 2025 IEEE Vehicle Power and Propulsion Conference (VPPC)
abstract: This paper aims to show the effectiveness of a robust position fault detection design with guaranteed sensitivity in a PMSM drive system using encoder. Firstly, a linear parametervarying (LPV) model is developed for permanent magnet synchronous motor (PMSM) drive system, in which PMSM model is described in the α - β frame. And sensor fault, parameter variations as well as external disturbances are all considered. Then, a fault detection method based on the H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">∞</inf>/H<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">_</inf> observer is designed to simultaneously fulfill the robustness of disturbances and sensitivity of sensor faults for PMSM drive system. Finally, both simulation and experimental validation tests are conducted, and the results demonstrate the effectiveness and practicality of the proposed method.

title: A Feature-Aided Adaptive IMM-SRCKF Algorithm for Projectiles Attitude Estimation under Magnetometer-Infrared Sensor Faults
authors: Yang Song, Xiongzhu Bu, Yihan Cao, Miaomiao Xu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This paper proposes a feature-aided adaptive Interacting Multiple Model (IMM)-Square Root Cubature Kalman Filter (SRCKF) projectile attitude estimation algorithm to address complex sensor fault scenarios involving magnetometers and infrared (IR) sensors. First, a four-model set, including normal, magnetometer bias, and IR/mag fault models, is established. To overcome the observability deficiency under sensor fault conditions, a heuristic pitch angle derived from the nominal trajectory is introduced as a virtual measurement. Second, a decoupled probabilistic intervention mechanism based on statistical features of a residual sliding window is designed. This mechanism assists the IMM algorithm in rapidly and accurately switching between different fault modes by modifying the model likelihoods through the statistical feature. Finally, SRCKF is employed as the sub-filter, combined with an adaptive measurement noise covariance matrix. Simulation results demonstrate that the proposed algorithm can maintain high-precision roll/pitch estimation and mitigate yaw drift even in compound fault scenarios, exhibiting stronger robustness compared to single SRCKF and traditional IMM-SRCKF algorithms.

title: Designing a Structured Educational Data for AI: A Dataset for Outcome-Based Learning and Assessment
authors: Javed Khan Sheikh, Hemant Kumar Soni
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: Outcome-Based Education (OBE) emphasizes measurable learning outcomes through structured assessments. This research introduces an automated evaluation framework aligned with OBE principles to address challenges in traditional subjective assessments, such as evaluator bias, time constraints and limited scalability. A novel algorithm, SREP (Structured Rubric-based Evaluation Pipeline), is proposed to generate a standardized dataset comprising 140 student responses to domain-specific questions in Data Structures. Each answer was annotated with semantic similarity scores and rubric based ratings across five dimensions: Explanation, Comparison, Terminology, Logical Flow, and Use Case, mapped to Bloom’s Taxonomy. To validate the dataset, a hybrid evaluation model was implemented by combining Sentence-BERT embeddings with a Multi-Output K-Nearest Neighbors (SBERT+KNN) classifier. Experiment were conducted using an 80-20 train-test split, and performance was measured with Accuracy, Precision, Recall and F1-score. Results demonstrated accuracies of 79%, 70%, 68%, 57% and 55% across five key dimensions: Logical Flow, Comparison, Explanation, Use Case and Terminology respectively. The framework supports scalable, fair and insightful assessments while identifying weak pedagogical areas for targeted interventions.

title: Research on Urban Real Estate Price Forecasting Model Based on Multi-Source Data Fusion and Artificial Intelligence Algorithms
authors: Bao Cuiju, Ding Huanhuan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Fluctuations in urban real estate prices have profound economic and social impacts, but traditional forecasting methods are limited by data homogeneity and insufficient dynamic modeling. To address these challenges, this study proposes an AI-based hierarchical multi-source data fusion framework that integrates spatiotemporal data, economic indicators, and emerging data sources, effectively combining structured and unstructured information to mitigate data heterogeneity. Based on this framework, the ST-GATAD (Spatio-Temporal Graph Attention with Anomaly Detection) model architecture is constructed. Combining a graph attention network (GAT), a long short-term memory network (LSTM), and an anomaly detection mechanism, it leverages the strengths of graph neural networks (GNNs) and attention mechanisms in modeling spatial dependencies, temporal changes, and sudden events (such as policy shocks), improving dynamic forecasting capabilities and adaptability. Utilizing SHAP value analysis and attention weight visualization, the model's interpretability is significantly enhanced, enabling the quantification of the impact of key factors on housing prices. Experiments using multi-source data from Beijing's Haidian and Chaoyang districts from 2018 to 2023 showed that an AI-based multi-source data fusion approach significantly improved model performance. The ST-GATAD model demonstrated excellent prediction accuracy and anomaly detection, achieving a low mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 9.6% and an F1 score of 79.8% for event detection. The model also demonstrated rapid response to dynamic events. Explanatory analysis further revealed the mechanisms by which various factors influence real estate prices and policy regulation.

title: Optimization Design of Wideband Current Injection Probe
authors: Zihan Zheng, Hongxu Zhao
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: This paper addresses the specific requirements of radiated susceptibility simulation tests for airborne equipment. Taking the core frequency range of 100 MHz–1000 MHz specified for radiated tests in RTCA DO-160G as a reference, an optimized design study of a simulation model for a current injection probe is conducted. Focusing on three key aspects—magnetic core, winding structure, and shielding enclosure—the influence of critical parameters, such as structural configuration and the distributed capacitance introduced by gaps between structures, on the insertion loss characteristics of the probe is systematically analyzed. Through analytical calculations and iterative optimization based on multiple sets of parametric simulations, a current probe simulation model with a frequency range of 100 MHz–1000 MHz and a relatively smooth insertion loss response is ultimately developed. The proposed model can effectively support radiated electromagnetic compatibility simulation tests for airborne equipment within the corresponding frequency band.

title: A Polar LEO Satellite Wireless Channel Modeling Method Based on Adversarial Learning
authors: Hua Li, Jia'ao Sun, Shuangjia Wang, Chengyan He
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate modeling of narrowband satellite communication channels in polar low Earth orbit (Polar LEO) systems is essential for system design and performance evaluation. Polar LEO channels exhibit severe fading, atypical amplitude distributions, and nontrivial correlation characteristics, which challenge conventional modeling methods under limited measurement data. To address these challenges, this work proposes a Wasserstein generative adversarial network with gradient penalty (WGAN-GP)-based approach to learn the statistical characteristics and underlying distributions of narrowband Polar LEO channels. The proposed method generates high-fidelity channel samples that closely match measured data while avoiding complex analytical assumptions. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed approach achieves accurate channel characterization with reduced modeling complexity, enabling efficient system-level analysis and design of Polar LEO satellite communication systems.

title: Lightweight Machine Learning-based Intrusion Detection Under Class Imbalance in Smart Grids
authors: Eman Saleh, Hayretdin Bahsi, Andrii Chub
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Intrusion detection is crucial in smart grids, where operational traffic must be monitored at substations and edge devices with tight latency and resource limits. While machine learning (ML) is appealing for this purpose, actual implementation necessitates lowering feature dimensionality and dealing with substantially imbalanced, multiclass attack data in which infrequent but safety-critical events are underrepresented. This study systematically evaluates five ML classifiers combined with three filter-based feature selection (FS) methods-Fisher Score, Mutual Information, and ANOVA across three contemporary smart grid/ICS datasets. Filter methods are used because they are model-agnostic and compute-efficient, fitting low-footprint, low-latency deployments. We jointly assess detection under controlled multiclass imbalance and deploymentoriented efficiency (mean wall-clock training/inference time and serialized model footprint), guiding real-time, resourceconstrained environments.
