Under varying imbalance ratios, treebased models emerge as consistently robust, and filter-based FS maintains competitive accuracy while reducing latency and model size, supporting practical ML-based IDS in smart-grid settings. No earlier study in this domain has combined a simple, deploymentfriendly filter FS with a multidataset, multiclass, controlledimbalance evaluation, as well as end-to-end efficiency measures.

title: An Intelligent Approach for Arch Height Index Measurement Based on Machine Vision
authors: Wenjie Chen, Xiaoming Li, Zhengjun Li, Haiyan Chen, Bin Lu, Zhengrui Zhang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: The arch height is a crucial indicator for assessing foot health, and the Arch Height Index (AHI) is a widely recognized method for measuring arch height. The current AHI measurement method mainly involves using calipers to measure the foot, but this method has issues such as complex operation and difficulty in implementation. Therefore, this study proposes a new automatic AHI measurement method based on image segmentation. This method uses a semantic segmentation model to extract key structures of foot images, automatically obtains key parameters such as arch height and truncated foot length, and directly calculates the AHI value. This study collected 46 side images of the foot to verify the algorithm. By comparing with the measurement results of professional instruments, it was found that the AHI value calculated by this intelligent method and the actual AHI value had a Pearson correlation coefficient of 0.9911, and the <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{p}$</tex>-value was calculated by <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathbf{t}$</tex>-test to be 0.945, which was greater than the significance level of 0.05, indicating that the AHI value calculated by this intelligent algorithm is very close to the real AHI value. The above results show that the AHI intelligent measurement method based on image segmentation proposed in this study can effectively obtain the AHI value of the foot, providing a reliable basis for clinical diagnosis and treatment. This study provides a new method for the AHI measurement field, which has the advantages of simple operation and high accuracy, and is worth further promotion and application in the field of telemedicine.

title: MAPAQUALI – Modular system for continuous monitoring of water quality by remote sensing
authors: Claudio Barbosa, Daniel Maciel, Evlyn Novo, Vitor Gomes, Rejane Paulino, Rogério Flores Júnior, Vitor Martins, Gilberto Ribeiro
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: Biannual or quarterly monitoring at some sampling stations provides relevant water quality (WQ) management information on the watershed scale. However, it does not provide sufficient spatial coverage and temporal frequency of data to support effective public policies for the sustainable use of local aquatic systems. Given the high variability in the optical properties of these aquatic systems, the monitoring frequency should allow for continuous tracking of WQ temporal and spatial dynamics, enabling regulatory agencies to issue alerts to managers when necessary. Despite the availability of orbital optical sensor constellations for systematic remote sensing of aquatic systems, there is currently no platform for continuous and automated WQ monitoring. This paper introduces the prototype of the MAPAQUALI platform, a modular continuous monitoring platform that integrates three aquatic systems.

title: Research on Robot Autonomous Inspection Visual Perception System Based on Improved YOLOv8 and SLAM
authors: Peiyao Hong, Yulong Zhang, Yibo Feng, Huiting Tan, Danyao Huang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Road manhole covers are critical to urban traffic safety, but manual inspection of their potential hazards suffers from low efficiency and high costs. Additionally, robot autonomous detection systems in complex scenarios face challenges like visual blind spots, dynamic environmental interference, and insufficient algorithm robustness. To address these issues, this study proposes a novel robot autonomous inspection visual perception system integrating MCPH-YOLO (Manhole Cover Potential HazardYOLO) and an improved SSD-LIO-SAM framework. First, MCPH-YOLO, derived from YOLOv8n, enables fast and accurate identification of manhole cover hazard status. For the SSD model, we optimize its structure by introducing the MobileViT lightweight architecture and Cross-Attention feature fusion module, achieving a 40% detection speed increase (52 FPS) on the Jetson AGX embedded platform while maintaining a mAP@0.5 of 91.8%. Second, a dynamically enhanced semantic LIO-SAM framework is designed: dynamic obstacle masks are generated based on detection results, and combined with a temporal feature matching optimization strategy, reducing the absolute trajectory error (ATE) to 0.1m and constructing a 3D point cloud map with equipment status semantic information. Third, a reinforcement learning-based task-adaptive collaborative mechanism is proposed to dynamically adjust detection resolution and frequency according to SLAM positioning and environmental complexity, cutting system resource consumption by 50%. Experiments on a self-built industrial inspection dataset demonstrate that compared with the baseline (SSD + Cartographer), the system improves small-target detection accuracy (AP-S) by 20.5% and SLAM mapping consistency score by 25.3%. It exhibits high efficiency in autonomous path planning, equipment status recognition, and closed-loop task anomaly alarming, providing a reliable real-time solution for manhole cover hazard detection in complex scenarios.

title: Exploration and Practice of a Multi-Dimensional Evaluation Mechanism for Open-Ended Assignments Empowered by AI
authors: Shangjun Hou, Ruixia Cao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study addresses the core limitations of traditional open-ended assignment evaluation in higher education: inefficiency, lack of authenticity, and limited educational value. It leverages artificial intelligence (AI) to establish the APST (AI Peer - Self - Teacher) four-dimensional collaborative evaluation mechanism and explores a practical multi-dimensional framework. Using Android development assignments from the course “Mobile Internet Application Development Technology” as an empirical scenario (experimental group, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{n}=125$</tex>; control group, <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\mathrm{n}=120)$</tex>, the research adopts a methodology comprising “theoretical construction - framework design - empirical verification”. First, based on theories such as Intelligent Agent Theory and Social Constructivism, the functional boundaries and collaboration processes among the four evaluative entities are clarified. Second, a “three-stage, seven-step” implementation pathway is designed, supported by four types of dedicated tools—including AI initial evaluation prompts and peer review questionnaires. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed mechanism is validated through a pre-test/post-test controlled experiment. The results demonstrate that, compared with traditional single-teacher evaluation, the new mechanism effectively alleviates the three major shortcomings of conventional approaches. The findings provide a transferable practical solution for evaluating different types of open-ended assignments (e.g., text composition, creative design) in tertiary education and enrich both theoretical and practical aspects of AI-empowered educational evaluation.

title: Automated Level Control System development for Harbor Tank Storage for Smart Cities
authors: Nadezda Kunicina, Roberts Grants, Mārtiņš Bisenieks, Valdis Balodis
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: This paper presents a modular, PLC-centric architecture for automated level monitoring and control in a harbor tank farm in Liepāja, Latvia. The solution integrates a Siemens S7-1200 controller with ET200SP remote I/O with HART, a normalized SQL data store, and a WinCC HMI/SCADA layer. Each tank is realized as an ISA-88 equipment module with parameterized user-defined types, deterministic conversion of 4$\mathbf{2 0 ~ m A}$ /HART to engineering units, and consistent alarm model featuring hysteresis and on-delay. The four-layer design-field instrumentation, deterministic control, data lake, and visualization/integration-keeps interlocks and timing-critical logic in the PLC while exposing canonical, analytics-ready data upstream via OPC UA and MQTT. Implementation artifacts include reusable engineering blocks, an idempotent SQL schema with quality flags, and HMI faceplates for overview, detail, alarms, and maintenance. Validation with PLCSIM and system-level tests demonstrates sub-second operator-screen updates under nominal load; the measurement chain targets $\pm 0.25 \%$ of span, and the core control/HMI bill of materials is approximately € 3.2 k excluding field sensors. Safety and cybersecurity include an independent High-High level switch, OT/IT segmentation, encrypted transport, and audit logging. Fault tolerance uses buffering and idempotent writes to prevent duplicates and enable catch-up after outages. The approach scales by instantiating additional tanks without refactoring shared logic or database structures, and it improves data quality for inventory reconciliation through traceable scaling and time synchronization. By bridging operational and information technology with open interfaces and repeatable design patterns, the proposed architecture provides a practical migration path from isolated gauges to resilient, real-time operations suitable for smart-city platforms.

title: Dynamic Geometric Calibration on Visual Odometry Performance for Autonomous Drone Navigation
authors: Fabiano Da Cruz Nogueira, Felipe Pacheco De Almeida Euphrásio, Douglas Damião De Carvalho Honório, Marco Antonio Pizani Domiciano, Alexandre David Caldeira, Elcio Hideiti Shiguemori
publish: 2025 Latin American GRSS &amp; ISPRS Remote Sensing Conference (LAGIRS)
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach based on deep learning techniques for dynamic geometric camera calibration in the context of autonomous aerial navigation. Unlike traditional offline calibration methods, the proposed technique leverages convolutional neural networks (CNNs) to adjust intrinsic parameters in real time, thereby adapting to environmental and mechanical variations that occur during flight. Experimental results demonstrate that this adaptive calibration framework significantly enhances the performance of monocular visual odometry by reducing cumulative trajectory errors in dynamic environments. The findings indicate that continuous calibration driven by deep learning models is a promising solution for improving the robustness and autonomy of vision-based navigation systems in unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). These results also reinforce the growing potential of deep learning to replace static and manual calibration procedures in embedded real-time applications.

title: An Implementing Traffic Light Signal Control Verilog-Based System
authors: Xinyang Liu
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: With the continued expansion of urban intersections, flexible and reliable traffic-signal control has become essential for improving traffic flow and maintaining orderly intersection operations. Field-programmable gate array (FPGA)-based traffic-signal systems are well suited to these requirements, but conventional FPGA design workflows typically require manual digital-circuit design and Karnaugh-map (K-map) logic minimization-tasks that are both technically demanding and time-consuming. To accelerate the development process, this paper presents a Verilog generation framework implemented in Python and integrated with the Vivado toolchain. Given a user-specified truth table, the framework automatically produces synthesizable Verilog module files, corresponding simulation testbenches, and waveform outputs within Vivado, thereby obviating the need for manual logic simplification and low-level circuit design. By reducing implementation complexity and lowering the required learning investment, the proposed approach substantially shortens development time and improves engineering efficiency. The framework was validated through three iterative design-verification cycles: in each cycle, the generated simulation waveforms were checked against the original specifications and found to satisfy the design requirements. Experimental results indicate that the system can effectively reduce development difficulty and time cost for FPGA-based traffic-signal systems, offering practical support for the rapid deployment of intersection control infrastructure.

title: The Impact of a “GenAI+Metaverse” Based Intelligent Learning Environment on Undergraduate Students' Metacognition
authors: Qingyuan Yang, Shuhan Zhong, Yu Zhou, Zhemin Qian, Xi Xu, Yuhui Yang, Cui Huang, Huaizhong Lin
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The rapid development of emerging technologies, such as generative artificial intelligence (GenAI), has led to a profound transformation in societal demands for talent. In addition to the increasing emphasis on technical training, core competencies, such as problem-solving, are recognized as crucial for the cultivation of high-quality talent. At present, learning environments supported by new technologies are widely used to promote educational reform, with particular focus on the impact of intelligent environments on metacognitive processes. However, research indicates that environments driven by a single technology often suffer from limitations, such as a lack of interactivity and insufficient generativity, which are significant but short-lived. This study focuses on an intelligent learning environment built upon the fusion of “GenAI+Metaverse” as the core variable, exploring its effect on the metacognitive abilities of college students. Using a four-dimensional metacognitive frameworkcomprising “awareness, planning, cognitive strategy, and monitoring”-this study compares the effects of traditional teaching, GenAI-supported teaching, and “GenAI + Metaverse” integrated teaching on students' metacognitive development. The results show that the integrated intelligent environment leads to positive changes across all metacognitive dimensions, with particularly significant improvements in task understanding and strategy execution. Furthermore, metacognitive ability is found to depend on the coordinated support of the technological environment, structured instructional design, strategic guidance, and reflection mechanisms. The study deepens the understanding of the interaction between intelligent technologies and metacognition, providing empirical evidence for the optimization of intelligent teaching environments in higher education and for the cultivation of interdisciplinary talent.

title: Design of an Energy-Efficient Hybrid-Precision Neural Network Accelerator with a Unified Memory-Compute Architecture
authors: Feng Jiao, Siying Bi, Shu Wang, Zhong Ma, Jin Huang, Siwei Xiang, Chen Yang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of intelligent technologies and their widespread deployment in edge scenarios, energy efficiency has become a critical challenge for computing platforms. To address the difficulty of improving energy efficiency, this paper proposes a high-efficiency neural network accelerator architecture with dedicated designs for storage and computation, tailored for hybrid-precision workloads. A tightly interleaved storage architecture is developed to enable compact storage and low-latency transmission of multi-precision data, proportionally reducing bandwidth pressure and memory access power consumption under low-bitwidth configurations. Furthermore, a reconfigurable hybrid-precision processing engine (HPPE) is designed, which supports 1/2/4/8-bit dynamic precision through bit-extension and sign-bit compensation techniques, enabling efficient parallel multiplication. The coordinated design of storage and compute subsystems helps mitigate performance bottlenecks. Experimental results on a Xilinx ZCU102 FPGA demonstrate a synthesized frequency of 300 MHz. Compared to a fixed 8-bit design, resource usage increases by only 0.5 % to 23.3 % across different resource types. Running the VGG-16 model on the ImageNet dataset, the proposed design achieves <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$1.05 \times$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$7.38 \times$</tex> energy efficiency improvement in 8-bit mode compared to similar accelerators. In 1-bit mode, the compute throughput reaches 10,069.8 GOPS—an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$8.21 \times$</tex> super-linear improvement over its own 8-bit mode—and offers <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$49.12 \times$</tex> to <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$338.71 \times$</tex> energy efficiency improvement over comparable designs. This work provides a high-efficiency computing foundation for edge-side intelligent systems with dynamic task adaptability.

title: CyberLog Analyst: A Real-Time Security Log Analysis Pipeline Powered by LLM - Architecture, Implementation, and Evaluation
authors: Mouhamadou Mame DIOP, Mamadou BA, Khalifa SYLLA, Samuel Ouya
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: System log analysis is critical for detecting anomalies and ensuring cybersecurity, yet traditional methods struggle with large, heterogeneous, and evolving data. CyberLog Analyst is a modular real-time platform powered by Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate interpretation and generate contextual diagnostics from diverse logs. Tested in a simulated SOC with synthetic attack scenarios, it achieved 91 % recall, 84 % precision, and an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$87 \% ~\mathrm{F} 1$</tex>-score, outperforming rule-based detection in noisy conditions. The system offers improved accuracy, readability, and response time, while challenges remain regarding inference cost, log format sensitivity, and robustness to adversarial inputs.

title: Temporal and Causal-Aware Summarization of Legal Public Opinion With Large Language Models
authors: Wei Zhang, Yang Kou
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Automatic summarization of legal public opinion texts poses unique challenges due to complex temporal dynamics and nuanced causal relationships inherent in legal events discussed in news and social media. Existing summarization approaches, including powerful large language models (LLMs), often fail to capture these critical temporal sequences and causal links, resulting in summaries that lack coherence and narrative logic. To address this, we propose a novel summarization framework that explicitly integrates temporal and causal relationship modeling into an LLM-based architecture, specifically finetuning the Qwen language model with extracted event timelines and causal connections. Evaluations conducted on the publicly available CAIL-2022 legal public opinion dataset, demonstrate that our proposed method significantly outperforms traditional summarizers and baseline LLMs across metrics such as ROUGE1, ROUGE-2, ROUGE-L and BERTScore. Experimental analysis further confirms that the generated summaries exhibit enhanced chronological coherence and accurately reflect causal event structures. This study provides compelling evidence that embedding temporal and causal reasoning into LLM-based summarization substantially improves the quality and informativeness of summaries in the legal public opinion domain, offering promising directions for future research and practical applications in public opinion analysis.

title: Empowering GenAI with SMCKI Collaborative Script in CSCL to Enhance Undergraduate Students' Collaborative Problem-Solving Ability and Learning Performance
authors: Ziwen Wei, Yi Zhang, Shuangting Ding, Dengkang Chen, Xingyi Guo, Yaping Guo
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Cultivating collaborative problem-solving (CPS) ability is a critical objective in higher education. However, collaborative learning in practice is often hindered by uneven participation and superficial interaction. While GenAI demonstrates strong potential to facilitate collaborative processes, its educational use remains constrained by the absence of structured pedagogical guidance. To address this gap, the present study designed a collaborative script (CS) grounded in the Spiral Model of Collaborative Knowledge Improvement (SMCKI) and integrated it with GenAI. The design employed macro-level and micro-level scaffolds to support students' engagement in CSCL with GenAI. A quasi-experimental study was conducted in a nineweek Educational Research Methods course at a Chinese university. Two intact classes were assigned as the experimental group (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=46$</tex>) and the control group (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$n=48$</tex>). The experimental group engaged in collaborative learning with GenAI under the guidance of the SMCKI-based script, whereas the control group used the same GenAI tool without script support. Findings revealed that: (1) CS-GenAI group achieved significantly higher individual academic performance than T-GenAI group; (2) the group work quality of CS-GenAI group was significantly superior to that of T-GenAI group; and (3) the CPS ability of CS-GenAI group, including its social, cognitive, and emotional dimensions, was significantly higher than that of T-GenAI group. These results suggest several practical implications for the design of GenAIsupported CSCL.

title: The Role of Local Governance in Developing ValueOriented Energy Communities in Europe: A Review
authors: Irina Antoskova, Anna Mutule, Roberts Lazdins
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: Energy communities are recognised as a means to empower citizen participation in the energy transition while placing social values in the forefront of the process. Due to their generally place-based nature, it is widely assumed that local authorities, specifically municipalities, will both create enabling conditions for grassroots initiatives and lead their own projects. This review explores the diverse roles of municipalities in supporting the development of energy communities, as well as the factors that hinder their more active involvement. We analyse municipal participation through the lens of value creation. We note that while active member participation often remains limited, successful energy communities place strong emphasis on local values in their agendas.

title: Multi-Scale Dilated Convolution Attention Network for Click-Through Rate Prediction
authors: Qihai Xie, Jun Li, Yan Niu
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Click-through rate (CTR) prediction is crucial for online advertising and recommender systems. Existing methods face challenges in modeling cross-domain feature interactions and capturing multi-scale temporal dependencies. This paper proposes a novel CTR model with two key innovations: (1)Triple Cross Attention Mechanism (TCAM) that explicitly models interactions among user, item, and context domains through multi-head crossattention, and (2) Multi-Scale Dilated Convolution Network (MSDC) that captures temporal patterns at multiple granularities using parallel convolution branches with different dilation rates. Experiments on two real-world datasets (Criteo, Frappe) demonstrate state-of-the-art performance with 81.76% and 97.23% AUC. Ablation studies validate the effectiveness of both components.

title: Innovative Strategies for Intelligent Device Management in the Construction of Power Grid Infrastructure Empowered by Internet of Things Technology
authors: Jinxiu Zhang, Zhipeng Li, Xiyu Chen, Hewen Kang, Yaojia Wang
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: In response to the problems of untimely status perception, weak fault prediction, and difficult system integration of intelligent power equipment, this paper proposes an intelligent equipment management strategy based on the Internet of Things and artificial intelligence. A closed loop of “collection - identification - decision-making - deployment” has been constructed, and edge computing and deep learning models are adopted to achieve state identification and maintenance decision-making. Through empirical analysis of typical scenarios, this system has good engineering application value in terms of accuracy, response speed and adaptability.

title: Research on Broadband Passive Spectral Estimation Direction Finding Algorithm Based on Long Short Baseline Interferometer
authors: Yan Zhao, Jianyu Yu, Yuyang Chang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: A multi-target DOA estimation method is proposed to address the problem of interference direction finding being affected by the synthesis of multiple signal electric field vectors and unable to achieve multi-target resolution in scenarios where active decoys and protected targets are concentrated and distributed simultaneously on the same frequency. This method combines the prior angle of the interferometer with the MUSIC algorithm. Firstly, through interferometer direction finding simulation, it is verified that the direction finding results only reflect the direction of the synthesized vector and cannot distinguish individual targets when multiple targets are stacked. Subsequently, interferometer direction finding was used to determine the concentrated distribution angle interval between the target and the decoy as prior decision information, providing range constraints to solve the periodic pseudo peak phenomenon in wideband passive DOA estimation. The MUSIC algorithm was introduced to verify the effective resolution ability of the MUSIC algorithm based on prior angle constraints for concentrated distribution of multiple targets. Finally, the experimental data of multiple incident signal patterns were verified, and the results showed that the proposed method can accurately achieve DOA resolution of targets and decoys in multiple concentrated distributions on the same frequency based on the prior information of the angle range obtained by the interferometer, improving the direction finding accuracy and resolution performance in complex multi-target scenes.

title: Scaffolding in Action: A Turn-by-Turn Analysis of how a Tutor Chatbot Elicits and Fails to Elicit Higher-Order Thinking
authors: Ziyan Che, Chee Kit Looi, Longkai Wu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This study examines how a scaffolding tutor chatbot elicits higher-order thinking (HOT) in a graduate-level course. We coded 50 anonymized chatbot–student turns for tutor prompt type, and HOT type (Critique and Transfer), and depth (low/high for student's response). Prompt categories were derived inductively and include Social Greeting, Invite Experience Recall, Invite Problem Identification, Invite Critical Reasoning, Invite Contextual Application, Invite Solution Generation, and Invite Summary Synthesis. Results show that not all prompts equally lead to high-quality HOT: Invite Critical Reasoning (Type 4) and Invite Solution Generation (Type 6) reliably produced Critique_High and Transfer_High responses; Invite Experience Recall (Type 2) frequently produced rich, high-level transfer; Invite Summary Synthesis (Type 7) also elicited deep, comprehension responses. By contrast, Invite Contextual Application (Type 5) often remained at low-level. We suggest that dialogue design shapes HOT depth. Practical implications are proposed, such as keeping the seven types of prompts and adding more scaffolding to Invite Problem Identification and Invite Contextual Application. Future work should broaden the dataset and examine more HOT indicators.

title: A Reliability Model for Surgical Skill Assessment in Surgical Training
authors: Zhipu Yu, Jin Zhang
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: In surgical training, the typical approach involves completing actual training with traditional simulators and then providing quantitative results using appropriate metrics. However, evaluating the ability to complete specific training tasks within a set time and under certain conditions requires considering the cumulative effects of uncertainty factors such as fatigue. This Reliability model simulates training paths, constructs a state function using Euclidean distance, and estimates skill Reliability via Monte Carlo method. In the experiment of Knot-Tying training, the model not only enhances the training efficiency of traditional simulators but also provides a theoretical foundation for more comprehensive surgical skill assessments, offering significant research and practical value.

title: Optimization of Quality Inspection Strategy for Multi-stage Manufacturing Systems Based on Recursive Modeling and Simulated Annealing Algorithm
authors: Jingyi Bai, RuiHe Zhao, Jiawei Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: In multi-stage assembly manufacturing systems, formulating the optimal quality inspection strategy to balance upfront costs and later risks is the core challenge in maximizing production profit. This paper proposes an innovative approach that integrates recursive cost modeling with simulated annealing optimization to solve such combinatorial optimization problems characterized by deep nested structures. Computational results on simulation datasets demonstrate that, compared to traditional dynamic programming methods, the proposed method possesses a significant advantage in computational efficiency, and the optimization results align closely with the global optimal solution. Furthermore, the method consistently generates high-quality decisions across different production parameter scenarios, providing a scalable and robust solution for real-time production planning in complex manufacturing environments. These findings provide an efficient computational intelligence solution for real-time production planning in complex manufacturing environments.

title: Personalized Search of Heterogeneous Educational Data for IoT Based on DBSCAN and RCNN
authors: Peng Dong
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: Traditional data search methods rely on simple statistical principles, which have problems such as low efficiency, limited coverage, and difficulty in handling complex heterogeneous data. Therefore, this study proposes a personalized search method that integrates DBSCAN clustering and RNN learning. Firstly, standardize the preprocessing of heterogeneous educational data in the Internet of Things, use the DBSCAN algorithm for initial clustering, and identify data intensive areas and outliers. Subsequently, the RNN model is used for deep learning and prediction of clustering results to improve search accuracy; Finally generate a personalized recommendation list. The experiment shows that this method maintains efficient search performance even when the data volume increases, with excellent acceleration ratio, scalability, and parallel processing capability. The average search coverage exceeds 97.5%, and it demonstrates higher accuracy and comprehensiveness in complex heterogeneous educational keyword processing.

title: A Mission-Centric Review of Tactical Energy Innovations for Distributed Military Operations
authors: Heiki Jakson, Vladislav Musakko, Ahto Pärl, Ivo Palu
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: As modern military operations increasingly take place in distributed, infrastructure-degraded, and multi-domain environments, tactical energy systems must evolve beyond legacy diesel-based generators that are inefficient, logistically vulnerable, and acoustically and thermally conspicuous. This paper presents a comprehensive review of next-generation military power architectures emphasizing modularity, hybridization, and interoperability. Key technologies covered include hybrid power generation systems, mobile microgrids, modular energy storage units, hydrogen and fuel cell platforms, renewable integration, and mobile nuclear power. Drawing from field-validated programs such as AMMPS, MEHPS, HEROS, and TGER, the review highlights technologies that enhance energy resilience, mission continuity, and operational autonomy in contested environments. Rather than comparing technologies directly, the review organizes them by operational function and deployment maturity to reflect their practical role in current and emerging tactical contexts. This review aims to inform NATO-aligned modernization efforts by identifying scalable, mission-specific energy stacks that reduce logistical burdens and serve as strategic enablers across both defense and humanitarian operations.

title: Performance Evaluation of YOLOv11 for Ship Recognition in Complex Sea Surfaces
authors: Hanqing Xue, Chao Chen, Xiaowei Shao, JunXiang Zhao, XiaoMing Dou, Hejia Wang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: To enhance the real-time recognition level of ship targets in remote sensing images under complex scenarios such as variations in lighting, background interference, and changes in target size, this study employs an intelligent recognition algorithm based on the YOLOv11 architecture. Tested and validated on typical maritime ship recognition datasets, the algorithm achieves a 2.3% to 5% improvement in mAP50 compared to classic detection algorithms like YOLOv5 and YOLOv8. Effectively balancing recognition accuracy and speed, the algorithm holds broad application value for the deployment in typical embedded scenarios such as space-based autonomous target recognition, maritime monitoring, and emergency rescue.

title: Gamification and Game-Based Learning as Applied Educational Discourses of Game Studies
authors: Anton Sukhov
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This paper builds a theoretical model of gamification and game-based learning as applied educational technologies of modern game studies. The first part of the paper identifies key methodological approaches to game studies (narratological, ludological, and non-reductionist approaches) and defines theoretical and practical discourses of game studies. The second part of the paper substantiates the specifics of gamification, its classical models (based on the principles of extrinsic motivation), identifies criticism of such gamification and a response to it in the form of the latest frameworks (based on the principles of intrinsic motivation), and their applied educational significance (with analysis of relevant examples). The third part of the paper defines the essence of game-based learning, its difference from gamification and explores the educational possibilities of game-based learning based on a detailed analysis of numerous video games (using simulation video games as the relevant case study).

title: Dunhuang Element Segmentation and Script Mapping Algorithm Based on Self-Attention Mechanism
authors: Zhang Yuan
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: In this paper, a self-attention mechanism based algorithm for Dunhuang element segmentation, as well as script mapping, is proposed. Based on a transformation encoder, it combines a self-attention mechansim layer, as well as attention gating layers, to accomplish pixel-level segmentation of complex cultural elements from mural images, as well as precise mapping of ancient script characters to modern Unicode. The approach can well model global dependencies as well as local details via an encoder-decoder structure. The segmentation task module properly harnesses skip connection attention gates to filter background noise, while the mapping task module employs crossattention to match source characters to corresponding characters. The experimental results indicate the proposed algorithm can obtain a high mIoU of 0.892 on the segmentation task, greatly surpassing the performance of state-of-the-art networks, including U-Net, DeepLabV3+, as well as Swin-transformer, especially on small targets as well as blurred regions. In the script mapping task, the character error rate is reduced to 3.8%, and the word error rate is reduced to 9.2%. This algorithm provides an efficient framework for the digital analysis of Dunhuang cultural heritage and has high academic value and application potential.

title: Efficient DDoS Detection and Mitigation in Decentralized SDN Environments via Zero-Training Large Language Model and Port-Level Features
authors: Mohammed N. Swileh, Shengli Zhang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: Decentralized Software-Defined Networking (dSDN) addresses the scalability and reliability challenges of centralized SDN (cSDN) by distributing control across multiple local controllers. However, dSDN remains vulnerable to Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) attacks. This paper introduces a novel framework for detecting and mitigating DDoS attacks in dSDN environments. The framework utilizes lightweight port-level statistics combined with prompt engineering and in-context learning, enabling the DeepSeek-v3 Large Language Model (LLM) to classify traffic as benign or malicious without the need for model retraining or fine-tuning. Once an anomaly is detected, mitigation is enforced directly at the source, blocking malicious traffic and preserving normal network operations. The system achieves 99.99% accuracy, 99.97% precision, 100% recall, 99.98% F1-score, and an AUC of $\mathbf{1. 0}$ in experimental evaluations, demonstrating a scalable and efficient defense mechanism. This approach provides a proactive solution for securing dSDN infrastructures against DDoS threats, maintaining high efficiency while reducing system complexity.

title: Exploring the Empowerment of Undergraduate Art and Design Education through Artificial Intelligence Technology
authors: Fei Qi, Tsen-Shu Tsaur, Kuo-Shun Sun, Tony Yun-chung Li, Yu-Tong Qiu, Yung-Chieh Tsao
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: This empirical study investigates the integration of artificial intelligence in undergraduate art and design education through 586 valid questionnaires. Key findings reveal that generative AI reduces creative solution generation time by over 80%, while parametric tools enable simultaneous 3D modeling and structural optimization. Although 92.66% of users acknowledge enhanced design innovation, only 23.5% can fully leverage AI capabilities. Technical proficiency emerges as crucial, increasing work completion rates by 37%. The research proposes establishing an integrated “traditional techniques + AI tools” curriculum system, creating AI design laboratories, implementing teacher AI capability certification, and developing localized AI design material libraries. These recommendations provide practical implementation pathways for educational reform in the AI era, addressing both opportunities and challenges in digital transformation.

title: A Transformer-Based Power Combining Technique for a 60-GHz CMOS Power Amplifier
authors: Kuei-Cheng Lin, Chun-Lin Ko, Po-Chang Wu, Hann-Huei Tsai, Ying-Zong Juang
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: A broadband and low-loss power amplifier (PA) implemented in 40-nm CMOS is presented. The PA employs a differential transmission-line transformer (DTLT) to achieve wideband impedance matching and efficient power combining. The proposed DTLT provides impedance transformation to <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$50~\Omega $</tex-math> </inline-formula> over a 13-GHz bandwidth. Measurement results show that the PA achieves a gain of 17.6 dB around 60 GHz, with a 3-dB bandwidth from 53 to 67 GHz, covering all channels defined in the IEEE 802.11ad standard. The PA achieves an output 1-dB gain compression point (OP<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{1\text {dB}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 18 dBm and a saturated output power (P<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">${}_{\text {sat}}$</tex-math> </inline-formula>) of 18.2 dBm, with power-added efficiencies (PAEs) of 19.2% and 20%, respectively.

title: Fuzzy Decoupled Adaptive Impedance Control for a Five-Wheel Wall-Climbing Robot on Discontinuous Wall Surfaces_supp2-3670440.mp4
authors: Lei Chen
abstract: Aiming at the problems such as insufficient driving force, body oscillation, and contact instability that easily occur when wheel-based wall-climbing robots operate on discontinuous wall surfaces (such as corners and obstacles), this paper proposes an adaptive control strategy integrating joint impedance control and fuzzy decoupling compensation for a Five-Wheel Wall-Climbing robot(FWWC-robot). This strategy optimizes impedance parameters (mass, damping, and stiffness) online through an adaptive parameter update mechanism based on force error to minimize magnetic gap fluctuations and ensure stable adsorption. Meanwhile, a Mamdani fuzzy controller is used to dynamically compensate for the coupling interference of the front and rear swing arms, realizing real-time adjustment of joint compliance and improving the robot’s motion stability and dynamic adaptability when encountering abrupt changes in wall structures. Simulation and experimental results show that, compared with the traditional impedance control, this method reduces the attitude error by 60%, controls the magnetic gap fluctuation within 0.3 mm, achieves a 100% success rate of obstacle crossing under the working conditions of 63° corner and 30 mm obstacle, and keeps the attitude stability within ±1.2°. It provides an efficient and robust solution for the compliant motion of robots in complex wall environments, and can directly support the automated applications in high-risk operation scenarios such as ship derusting and large oil tank inspection.

title: Trajectory Tracking of a WMR Using a Neuro-Adaptive MRAC Control with Dynamic Uncertainties
authors: Jorge Uliarte, Eduardo Oliveira Freire, Fernando Ulloa Vázquez, Federico Camargo, Humberto Secchi, Ricardo Carelli, Francisco Rossomando
abstract: This work presents a Neuro-Adaptive Model Reference Adaptive Control (NA-MRAC) strategy for trajectory tracking in wheeled mobile robots (WMRs) subject to nonlinear dynamics, load-dependent variations, and unmodeled uncertainties. The proposed approach integrates an inverse-kinematics controller with a dynamic NA-MRAC structure that merges a radial-basis-function neural network and a regressor-based adaptive mechanism, enabling simultaneous compensation of parametric deviations and nonlinear residual dynamics. A complete stability analysis of the cascade kinematic–dynamic architecture is provided, establishing bounded-error behavior under the derived adaptation laws. The method is assessed in simulation using a Pioneer 3DX platform in MobileSim, including scenarios with sudden payload changes, nonsmooth trajectories with directional discontinuities, and a comparative evaluation against a non-adaptive MRAC implementation and a regression-based MRAC controller. The results show that the NA-MRAC provides improved robustness and disturbance rejection, with faster transient response and reduced tracking error in the evaluated scenarios, compared to the other approaches.

title: Conditional Self-Supervised Learning for Few-Shot Learning
authors: Yuexuan An, Hui Xue, Xingyu Zhao
publish: IEEE Transactions on Circuits and Systems for Video Technology
abstract: Few-shot learning seeks to emulate humans by grasping a new concept with a few examples. However, it is often a tricky problem to completely learn a new concept and avoid falling into overfitting with few examples. Recently, self-supervision has been introduced as an auxiliary pretext task to enhance the generalization of few-shot learning models. However, in few-shot scenarios, self-supervised learning may easily learn undesirable shortcuts and biased representations due to insufficient information, negatively impacting the decision boundary of few-shot models. In this paper, we propose a new learning paradigm called conditional self-supervised learning (CSS), where the blind self-supervised tasks are constrained. Specifically, we first utilize the embedding knowledge of supervised learning as a condition to guide the representation learning process of self-supervised tasks. In order to optimize the overall objectives, we formulate the CSS as a multiple objective optimization problem to find Pareto optimal solutions. Moreover, we combine the meaningful information extracted from both supervised and self-supervised learning into a unified distribution, further enriching the original representation. Extensive experiments show that our method, without any fine-tuning, significantly improves accuracy in few-shot tasks compared to state-of-the-art methods.

title: Empowering IoT Security: Automated Identification of Standard Library Functions in RTOS Firmware with LLM and RAG
authors: Zhihan Zheng, Yu-an Tan, Zihan Chen, Zheng Lu, Weizhi Meng, Shuo Wang
abstract: Reverse engineering embedded firmware for Real-Time Operating Systems (RTOS) is a formidable challenge in Internet of Things security. The common practice of stripping symbolic information from firmware to optimize performance and storage makes identifying standard library functions exceptionally difficult, creating a significant bottleneck for functional analysis and vulnerability discovery. This paper introduces a novel, automated method for identifying these functions in RTOS firmware by leveraging Large Language Models. Our approach operates directly on raw binary code, requiring no symbolic or debugging information, and demonstrates broad generalizability across diverse hardware architectures and compilers. At its core, the method combines Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to enhance identification accuracy with a newly designed Adaptive Iterative Screening Algorithm (AISA), which optimizes analysis efficiency by prioritizing candidate functions based on a weighted score of call frequency, call depth, and address proximity to reduce token costs. We validated our method through rigorous experimentation on Zephyr RTOS firmware spanning nine architectures (e.g., ARM, MIPS, RISC-V). The results are compelling: our approach achieves a 90.59% accuracy rate in identifying standard library function names. Moreover, its application to commercial IoT firmware confirms its high efficiency, identifying functions significantly faster and more economically than traditional heuristic techniques. This work contributes a powerful, general-purpose, and cost-effective solution for automated firmware analysis.

title: An Integrated Data Engineering and Machine Learning Framework for Energy Prediction and Fault Detection in Smart Buildings
authors: Shashank Kammanahalli Chandra Sekhara, Akshith Nukala, McAndrew Okwei, Yesem Kurt Peker, Mohamed Riduan Abid, Praveen Ashok Kumar
abstract: Given the rise in energy consumption across the globe, efficient management of building energy usage has become increasingly critical. Faults in energy consumption, when left unchecked, can lead to excess costs or operational failures. By leveraging machine learning techniques, we can predict and classify faults in energy consumption, providing actionable insights to proactively mitigate them. In this paper, we propose a data engineering and machine learning framework deployed in the cloud to predict energy consumption across multiple building types. The dataset includes hourly energy consumption, weather conditions, and occupancy data. From outlier detection to model prediction and fault detection, this paper provides a detailed comparative analysis on methodologies for implementing a scalable and efficient framework for buildings’ energy management systems. Overall, the resulting framework is adaptable and can be seamlessly applied across a wide range of organizational infrastructures, highlighting its flexibility and relevance to diverse energy-optimization applications. Experimental results show that XGBoost delivers the best forecasting accuracy across building types, while a Bollinger-Bands-based detector effectively captures abnormal fluctuations via deviations and volatility around the moving average, supporting the effectiveness of our cloud-deployed framework.

title: Risk-Aware Prediction-Optimization Integrated Method for Day-ahead Microgrid Operation
authors: Hongzhang Sheng, Yan Xu, Dunjian Xie
abstract: This letter proposes a risk-aware prediction-optimization integrated method for day-ahead operation of a microgrid under uncertain electricity prices. The operation problem is formulated in a bilevel structure: the lower-level optimization model determines the dispatch decisions, while the upper-level prediction model minimizes the decision loss induced by price forecast errors. A training loss function is developed to explicitly capture prediction-induced economic risks, which first approximates the decision loss via a convex and differentiable surrogate, and then reweights training samples using a spectral risk measure. By training the prediction model to minimize the risk-weighted loss, both prediction and optimization objectives can be aligned in an end-to-end pattern, thereby achieving risk-aware microgrid operation. Case studies on a real-world dataset demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed method.

title: Precise RF-Vision Fusion UAV Positioning and Identification for 6G Spectrum Security
authors: Yiyao Wan, Hongtao Liang, Guangyu Wu, Fuhui Zhou, Bruno Crispo, Qihui Wu
publish: IEEE Journal on Selected Areas in Communications
abstract: Precise positioning and identification of unauthorized unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) are of crucial importance for spectrum security and privacy protection in future intelligent networks. Although various single-modality approaches have been investigated, their performance degrades under the sensor-specific noise, resulting in suboptimal performance and robustness. To address these security challenges, we propose a multi-layer radio frequency (RF)-vision fusion framework that synergistically exploits temporal-spectral features of UAV RF signals and spatial-visual information to achieve precise and robust UAV positioning and identification. Moreover, a corresponding unified RF-Vision fusion Network (RFViNet) is designed to exploit the RF-vision cross-modal complementary and semantic synergy. Specifically, by leveraging the novel RFinformed proposal generation, RF-enhanced feature modulation, and RF-guided semantic query modules, the RFViNet effectively exploits the complementary strengths of RF and visual modalities. Furthermore, a practical RF–vision platform is developed to evaluate the performance of our method under various challenging conditions. Experimental results on the real-world dataset demonstrate that the proposed method achieves a competitive 85.8% average precision AP<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">50</sub>, highlighting its potential for enhancing the spectrum security in future intelligent wireless networks.

title: High-Performance Elliptic Curve Point Addition on Versal AI Engine for Multi-scalar Multiplication
authors: Ayumi Ohno, Kotaro Shimamura, Shinya Takamaeda-Yamazaki
abstract: Multi-Scalar Multiplication (MSM) is a primary computational bottleneck in modern cryptographic applications, especially zero-knowledge proofs. The Pippenger algorithm parallelizes MSM by decomposing it into numerous elliptic curve point additions (PADDs), but accelerating these operations on novel hardware like the Versal ACAP presents a significant challenge. This work explores the acceleration of PADDs on the Versal ACAP’s spatial array of 400 AI Engines (AIEs). While the SIMD-VLIW architecture of AIEs is ideal for the multiplication-heavy workloads in PADD, the complex 377-bit modular arithmetic, particularly carry propagation, demands architecture-aware optimization. We propose two key contributions: (1) algorithmic optimizations for carry propagation employing a carry-save-like technique to exploit VLIW and SIMD capabilities, and (2) a comparison of spatial mapping strategies and modular reduction algorithms to enhance intra- and intertask parallelism. Our approach achieves 567× speedup over the integrated CPU on the AIE evaluation board, utilizing 51.1% of the theoretical memory bandwidth.

title: Multi-Scale Adaptive Clustering and Local Consistency Learning for Unsupervised Clothing-Changing Person Re-Identification
authors: Yongkang Ding, Zi Ye, Ivonne Xu, Shuangquan Lyu, Liyan Zhang
publish: IEEE Transactions on Information Forensics and Security
abstract: Clothing-changing person re-identification (CC-ReID) aims to address cross-camera person identification challenges caused by variations in pedestrian attire, making it a highly valuable research area within computer vision. Due to the high cost of labeling data, unsupervised learning methods have gained significant attention for CC-ReID tasks. However, existing unsupervised methods frequently suffer from high pseudo-label noise and reliance on complex preprocessing (e.g., human parsing) or multi-encoder architectures, resulting in increased computational overhead and deployment difficulties. To tackle these challenges, this paper proposes an end-to-end unsupervised CC-ReID framework named Multi-Scale Adaptive Clustering and Local Consistency Learning (MALC). Utilizing a single CLIP Vision-Encoder as its backbone, MALC discards such intricate procedures. Its core innovations include a multi-scale adaptive density clustering (MS-ADC) strategy to improve pseudo-label quality, and a local consistency learning (LCL) approach that imposes constraints on local region features to enhance robustness against clothing variations. Through the joint optimization of global and local losses, the model learns a highly discriminative and robust feature representation. Experimental results demonstrate that MALC, employing only RGB images, substantially outperforms comparable unsupervised approaches that rely on additional parsing information, showcasing notable advantages in identification accuracy and ease of deployment. The code will be made available at: https://github.com/ykding666/MALC.

title: Artificial Intelligence for Echocardiographic Assessment of Pulmonary Hypertension and Right Heart Function: Advances in Intelligent Diagnosis and Risk Stratification
authors: Yan Fang, Fengshi Jing, Rongfeng Cheng, Yihan Cheng, Weibin Cheng, Hao Ren, Weitao Cai, Wen Jin
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
abstract: None

title: A Survey of Feature Detection and Matching Methods Based on Deep Learning in Visual SLAM
authors: Shizu Wei, Bo Xie, Pengju Hou, Ruifeng Li, Na Yang, Jing Guo, Haoyang Liu
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
abstract: None

title: A Rapid Prediction Method for Rotary Kiln Head Temperature Based on Residual TCN-GRU-Self Attention Integration
authors: Ya Mao, Yuhang Li, Rui Huang
publish: Proceedings of the 2026 International Conference on Human-Computer Interaction, Neural Networks and Deep Learning
abstract: None

title: Research on Dynamic Node Replacement Technology based on Cloud Computing Platform
authors: Fei Xing, Zhe Sun, Xicheng Qian
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: As cloud computing environments grow in complexity and scale, hardware failures have become a key factor impacting system high availability and reliability. This paper focuses on the research and implementation of dynamic node replacement technologies in cloud computing environments, aiming to address node replacement and seamless service takeover after hardware failures. Existing node replacement mechanisms rely on health status checks to trigger processes–simple to use, but heavily dependent on network conditions, leading to misjudgments during network fluctuations. To resolve this, we propose a Baseboard Management Controller (BMC)-based dynamic node replacement mechanism: leveraging BMC's realtime hardware monitoring capabilities, it tracks changes in motherboard-specific attributes (e.g., serial number, slot ID) and only initiates replacement when substantive changes to hardware identifiers are detected, migrating services from faulty nodes to standby nodes to avoid network-induced erroneous operations.

title: Predictive Modelling of Cardiovascular Disease Risk Based on Lifestyle Using Machine Learning Techniques
authors: Fardeen Mansoori, Harminder Kaur, Khushi Chauhan, Isha Jakhad
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: cardiovascular diseases (CVD) continue to be a major cause of premature death globally and present a significant health challenge worldwide. With the increasing burden of non-communicable diseases, the need for early and non-invasive methods to predict CVD risk has become more urgent. Traditional risk assessment methods, which rely on fixed heuristics, often fall short when dealing with complex and multifactorial health data. This research investigates the application of machine learning (ML) techniques to forecast outcomes CVD risk by analyzing various lifestyle and physiological factors such as age, BMI, blood pressures, cholesterol levels, glucose levels, smoking habits, alcohol consumptions, and physical activities. The analysis is derived from actual healthcare data that was obtained from a publicly accessible dataset on kaggle. Three different machine learning models XGboost, lightGBM, and logistic regression were trained and evaluated using various performance measures, including precision, recall, f1 score, and AUC-ROC. The outcomes prove the ability of such models, especially in performing well in dealing with structured health information. This study highlights the potential of ML-based risk prediction models in facilitating early detection of CVD, especially in low resource healthcare contexts.

title: Frequency-Domain Compact Thermal Modeling for Chiplets with Temperature-Dependent Thermal Conductivity
authors: Chongming Wang, Bowen Jia
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Accurate thermal modeling is needed in 2.5D chiplet-based systems, where high power density and stacked materials create strong vertical and lateral temperature gradients. Existing frequency-domain kernel-based compact models speed up prediction by learning spatial convolution kernels from simulation data, but they usually assume temperature-independent thermal conductivity and therefore tend to underestimate hot spots and smooth out temperature gradients when conductivity actually decreases with temperature. We propose a frequency-domain compact thermal model motivated by temperature-dependent conductivity: it starts from a Green's-function-based linear formulation under a reference constant conductivity, fits layer-wise kernels in the frequency domain using complex-valued ridge regression, and then applies a simple feedback term on the chip-layer self-kernel as a phenomenological correction that implicitly accounts for conductivity degradation while preserving the FFT-based structure and low runtime. Experiments on representative 2.5 D chiplet layouts show that the method predicts temperature within a few degrees Celsius of HotSpot and achieves more than three orders of magnitude speedup, making it practical for fast thermal estimation in chiplet-level design flows.

authors: Manoj Kumar Gupta, Bhavnesh, Baltej Singh Sidhu, Amrit Singh, Sanjay Kumar Singh, K. S. Kahlon
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: This study presents a theoretical and experimental investigation of radiation interaction parameters in functional ceramics, focusing on high-temperature superconductor YBCO (Yttrium Barium Copper Oxide) and Zirconium dioxide (ZrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf>) irradiated with Americium-241 (59.5 $\mathrm{keV} \gamma$-rays). The effective atomic number and electron density of YBCO are determined from its atomic constituents (Yttrium, Barium, Copper, and Oxygen), offering insights into its radiation response. Complementary experiments are conducted using an Am-241 source and a XPIPS detector, where the attenuation of gamma radiation in ZrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> is measured and compared with computational results from WinXCOM. The study characterizes ZrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> in terms of effective atomic number, electron density, jump ratio, and jump factor, for the knowledge of radiation shielding, dose calculation, and material identification. Moreover, findings highlight ZrO<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</inf> ‘s behavior at 60 keV, supporting advancements in medical imaging and radiation therapies. The integration of computational and experimental methods ensures reliability, deepening understanding and enabling future applications.

title: Sequence Recommendation Model Based on the Cooperation of Time-Domain Attention and Frequency-Domain Filtering
authors: Sumin Li, Yingmei Zhang, Qian Li, Yijin Gang, Siyao Hong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Recommender systems have been an important area of research. However, the traditional sequence recommendation algorithm based on Transformer architecture has the problem of over-smoothing caused by the attention mechanism. Therefore, a sequential recommendation model based on time domain attention and frequency domain filtering (TF-SR) is proposed. The model retains the ability of attention mechanism to effectively capture low-frequency signals in the time domain, and the frequency domain filtering adaptively attenuates the noise information in the frequency domain. Experiments on two benchmark datasets show that compared with the baseline model, the proposed method achieves significant performance improvement, especially in the NDCG index, the maximum improvement reaches 14.53%. This study verifies the key role of frequency domain tuning sequential recommender systems.

title: Vehicle State Parameter Estimation Based on Adaptive Unscented Kalman Filtering
authors: Chunliang Li, Zheng Xu, Xulong Wang
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: To address the decline in estimation accuracy of the traditional Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) under time-varying noise, this paper proposes an Adaptive Unscented Kalman Filter (AUKF) algorithm based on the Sage-Husa theory. A three-degree-of-freedom vehicle model is established, and an online correction mechanism for noise covariance matrices is designed. Verification is conducted through co-simulation using CarSim and MATLAB/Simulink. The results show that the proposed algorithm effectively enhances both the estimation accuracy and adaptability of the yaw rate and the sideslip angle.

title: Grid-Level Energy Storage Systems: Financial Modeling Framework and Policy Recommendations for Revenue Stacking
authors: Ahto Pärl, Ivo Palu, Heiki Jakson, Sulabh Sachan
publish: 2025 IEEE 66th International Scientific Conference on Power and Electrical Engineering of Riga Technical University (RTUCON)
abstract: The increasing penetration of variable renewable energy sources (RES) poses substantial challenges to power system stability, reliability, and flexibility. Energy Storage Systems (ESS), particularly Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), have emerged as essential enablers to address these challenges by providing multi-scale grid services such as frequency regulation, energy arbitrage, congestion management, and capacity firming. This paper presents a comprehensive review of the technical, economic, and regulatory factors influencing ESS deployment from investors’ perspective. We synthesize recent literature and market developments to identify critical elements of ESS financial modeling, focusing on revenue stacking, capital expenditure, operational considerations, and regulatory frameworks. Key barriers such as high upfront costs, uncertain revenue streams, and market design limitations are discussed along with policy recommendations aimed at enhancing revenue visibility, simplifying market participation, and incentivizing the full spectrum of ESS benefits, including those traditionally undervalued. The paper also highlights emerging opportunities through hybrid storage solutions, aggregation of distributed resources, and advanced market models to facilitate large-scale, economically viable ESS integration. These insights provide valuable guidance to investors, lenders, commercializes, developers, regulators, and policymakers striving to accelerate the transition to a flexible, resilient, and green power system.

title: Impact of Milking Animals on Socio-Economic and Ethical Dimensions of Society
authors: D.K. Chaturvedi, Achal Mogla
publish: 2025 8th World Engineering Conference on Contemporary Technologies (WECON)
abstract: In India, milking animals (i.e. cows, goats, buffaloes, and camels) hold a unique socio-economic and cultural significance. As per Hindu culture milking animals specifically cow is considered to be a sacred animal. They also play their respective productive roles in providing milk, draft power, and manure. However, a significant challenge arises with unproductive cattle – those that are old, infirm, diseased, or have ceased to be economically viable for their owners. The care and management of these animals present a complex issue with ethical, economic, and environmental dimensions. Objective of this paper is to explore the current state of unproductive cattle management in India, the challenges associated with it, and potential pathways towards more humane and sustainable solutions. This paper presents a unique case study of unfertile cows being treated and looked after till the last stage of their life cycle. This case study acts as a practical finding and can be implemented as a role model in other parts of the country where milk production and cattle population is high.

title: Prediction of the OODA Heterogeneous Network’s Tipping Point Strike Intensity Based on GIN-GRU Architecture
authors: Qihang Sun, Jian Pan, Xue Zheng, Kebin Chen
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on High Performance Computing, Big Data and Communication Engineering (ICHBC)
abstract: This paper addresses the critical challenge of predicting sudden functional collapses in OODA Heterogeneous Networks (OODA HetNet) under sustained strikes. Traditional methods fall short in providing accurate, quantitative, and early warnings for these network-wide failures. To overcome this, we propose a novel deep learning approach that integrates a Graph Isomorphism Network (GIN) with a Gated Recurrent Unit [4] (GRU). The key advantage of GIN-GRU model is its ability to predict the critical strike intensity using only node state time-series data, eliminating the dependency on complete and often unavailable network topology information. Our results confirm the feasibility of this topology-agnostic prediction, offering a technical pathway for assessing network resilience in battlefield characterized by incomplete information.

title: Demand Analysis and Optimization Strategies of Intelligent English Learning Tools for College Students
authors: Fang Rui, Li Ping, Wu JiaBei, Wang YaXin, Pei YiNan
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: With the rapid advancement of internet technology and artificial intelligence, English Learning Tools have become increasingly valuable resources for learners, offering unparalleled convenience, interactivity, and contextualized support, evolved into increasingly indispensable resources for learners, offering unparalleled convenience, interactive engagement, and contextualized support tailored to diverse learning scenarios. However, as the market for such intelligent language tools expands, a host of challenges have emerged, including widespread functional homogenization, inconsistent user experiences, technical limitations in advanced language processing, low long-term user engagement, and unclear monetization strategies—all of which hinder their ability to meet learners' evolving needs. To better understand college students' needs regarding intelligent language learning tools, identify existing challenges, and explore potential directions for growth and innovation, we distributed a questionnaire to college students. Our findings indicate that many AI-powered tools face issues such as functional homogenization, inconsistent user experiences, technical limitations, low user engagement, and unclear monetization strategies. This paper offers targeted recommendations for technological, content-related, and service design improvements to enhance their market competitiveness and inform the development of next-generation AI-driven English learning solutions.

title: Cross-Domain Rotated Patch Mixing for Semi-Supervised Medical Image Segmentation
authors: Jin Song, Tao Wang, Tong Tong
publish: 2025 5th International Conference on Electronic Information Engineering and Computer Communication (EIECC)
abstract: Semi-supervised medical image segmentation (SSMIS) trains models using a small amount of labeled data and a large volume of unlabeled data, which is well-aligned with the practical clinical scenario of scarce annotations. However, it still faces two core challenges: the global image transformation adopted by existing methods fails to fully exploit local features, and the inefficient knowledge transfer between labeled and unlabeled data leads to the accumulation of pseudo-label errors. To address these issues, we proposes the Cross-domain Rotated Patch Mixing (CRPM) framework. Specifically, CRPM enhances the robustness of local features through rotation-augmented image patches, facilitates knowledge transfer via cross-domain patch exchange, and optimizes global predictions using consistency constraints. Experimental results on two benchmark datasets demonstrate that CRPM achieves superior performance compared to the state-of-the-art methods in the field of SSMIS, effectively resolving the two core problems of neglected local features in global transformation and accumulated pseudo-label errors.

title: Crack Defect Recognition Technology for Photovoltaic Panels Based on the YOLOv8 Target Detection Algorithm
authors: Congcong Wang, Jingrao Xu, Yulu Ding, Ruihang Jiang, Xiaoming Song
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: To efficiently identify crack defects in photovoltaic panels, this paper proposes a photovoltaic system fault detection method based on the YOLOv8 detection model. It establishes a multi-type image library of damaged photovoltaic modules and pre-labels them. A lightweight convolutional neural network is used to accurately extract the information from the photovoltaic module damage image library. By adjusting the loss function and updating the decoupling detection head scheme, Improve the accuracy of fault identification for photovoltaic modules. Experiments show that this method demonstrates excellent performance in multiple aspects such as recognition accuracy, recall rate, and <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$\text{mAP50}$</tex>, providing reliable support for the operation and maintenance of photovoltaic systems.

title: A Practical Recipe for Structured Pruning of MotionGPT: Dependency-Graph Pruning and FFN Channel Reduction
authors: Shijun Chen
publish: 2025 2nd International Conference on Computer Communication, Networks and Information Science (CCNIS)
abstract: Human motion generation from natural language has become an important research challenge in computer vision and natural language processing, with applications in animation, robotics, and immersive VR/AR systems. Recent advances such as MotionGPT treat motion as a language sequence, enabling unified text-to-motion generation, captioning, and prediction across datasets like HumanML3D. However, the large size of such models limits their deployment in real-world settings. This paper presents a practical and reproducible pruning framework for MotionGPT, a state-of-the-art multimodal generative model that treats human motion as a foreign language. Two complementary structured pruning strategies are proposed: (1) dependency-graph pruning, a global, structure-aware method implemented with Torch-Pruning that removes channels consistently across residual connections, tied projections, and multi-head attention; and (2) FFN channel pruning, a local procedure tailored to T5-style DenseReluDense feed-forward blocks, which shrinks the dominant intermediate dimension. Experiments on HumanML3D show that the pruned model reduces parameters by ~14% while largely preserving semantic metrics such as Matching Score and R-Precision. Although FID increases (<tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.22 \rightarrow 0.86$</tex>) and trajectory errors (ADE/FDE) worsen moderately, motion diversity remains high. Compared to prior models such as MDM, the pruned MotionGPT maintains competitive or superior alignment scores while being more efficient. These findings demonstrate that structured pruning provides a viable path to making large motion-language models lighter and more accessible.

title: Multi-Information Fusion Knowledge Graph Representation Learning Method Based on Large Language Model
authors: Junjie Ma, Ruiyang Shang, Junpeng Bao, Yifeng Huang
publish: 2025 IEEE 8th International Conference on Pattern Recognition and Artificial Intelligence (PRAI)
abstract: Knowledge graph has been widely applied in many artificial intelligence fields, but most of the current knowledge graph representation learning methods rely on graph structure information, and do not fully exploit the entity semantic background information and hierarchical structure information contained in the graph. As a result, when we face complex problems, there are issues such as high computational complexity, lack of interpretability, and low reasoning accuracy. Aiming at the problem of how to effectively utilize the entity semantic background information and hierarchical structure information in the knowledge graph, this paper proposes a multi-information fusion knowledge graph representation learning method LHKGE based on a large language model. The LHKGE enriches the semantic representation of entities in the knowledge graph by using the knowledge of the large language model itself. Our experimental results on popular knowledge graph datasets demonstrate that the proposed method significantly improves accuracy in link prediction tasks, showcasing its superiority.

title: A Multimodal Feature Fusion Based Action Normality Matching Algorithm for Online Sports Education
authors: Jie Liu, Fengyang Fu
publish: 2025 International Conference on Educational Technology Management (ICETM)
abstract: The traditional online sports education action normality matching algorithm relies only on image data and is susceptible to environmental interference, resulting in misjudgments and omissions. To this end, an algorithm based on multimodal feature fusion is proposed. The algorithm first performs multimodal feature extraction on sports movements, integrating multiple data sources such as image data and sensor data. The image data includes spatial features such as joint position, body contour, posture, limb angle, and motion trajectory, which are obtained through color images and depth infrared cameras of Kinect devices; The sensor data includes time features such as acceleration, angular velocity, force, and motion rhythm, which are collected by the built-in sensors of the Kinect device. The weighted fusion method is used to fuse the features, assign weights, and then evaluate the normality of the actions through keyframe matching. In the experimental stage, two mainstream indoor action recognition datasets, NTU RGB+D 60 and NTU RGB+D 120, were selected to select three sets of actions with similar joint coordinates to construct a similar action dataset for verification. The experiment shows that the algorithm can more accurately identify and match joint standard actions without misjudgment or omission.
